title: compose.yaml: agent-vault named volume mounted at /vault/file but image stores vault at /data — credential-loss on recreate
description:
Canonical lead-shop ops compose.yaml template (shop-templates 0.16.0) mounts the agent-vault named volume {{OPS_SLUG}}-agent-vault-data at target /vault/file, but infisical/agent-vault:latest stores its vault at /data (VOLUME /data, HOME=/data, no storage-path flag). The persistent named volume is mounted at an unused path; the broker writes its vault to image-default /data which (unmounted) is an ephemeral per-container anonymous volume. Result: all provisioned credentials lost on docker compose down/up, container recreate, or restart-with-recreate. Fresh adopter loses vault on broker 2nd start, silently breaking the brokered fleet.

Fix: mount named volume at target /data (as pre-0.16.0 poured compose did); keep long-form entry + source {{OPS_SLUG}}-agent-vault-data; only target is wrong. Lands in canonical ops/compose.yaml + re-pour.

Vehicle: request_bugfix dispatched to shopsystem-templates (this work_id). Discriminator: existing capability misbehaving vs evident intent; target-path UNPINNED by lead-held scenarios. Scenario 170 (8fcf898fdeebc6be) pins volume NAME only; agent-vault-broker/12 (9bf7669e98c01448) pins restart-survival intent this bug violates. Scope lead-held-only. May warrant new mount-target pinning scenario (lead-po follow-up, non-blocking).

Empirical (ADR-018, no BC code read): docker inspect infisical/agent-vault:latest Volumes={/data:{}}, HOME=/data; running broker live vault at /data/.agent-vault/, CA /data/agent-vault-ca.pem; bin/agent-vault-provision step6 r/w /data/agent-vault-ca.pem; pre-0.16.0 compose mounted agent-vault-data:/data.

Blocks lead-hzzv (host 0.16.0 re-pour/migration — HALTED until this releases). Context lead-4him/lead-d9zq (cold-start CA scenario 192, shipped).
close_reason: RECONCILED + CLOSED (artifact surface, ADR-018; no BC code read). work_done from shopsystem-templates: canonical src/shop_templates/templates/ops/compose.yaml agent-vault service mount target /vault/file -> /data (matches infisical/agent-vault:latest VOLUME /data); long-form entry + type: volume + source {{OPS_SLUG}}-agent-vault-data unchanged. scenarios: [] honored. RE-VERIFIED no lead-held hash changed: scenario 170 (8fcf898fdeebc6be, pins volume NAME not target) and agent-vault-broker/12 (9bf7669e98c01448, restart-survival intent) both recompute to pinned values via scenarios verify/hash (exit 0). Nothing retired/edited. Accepted Implementer gated-loop proof: red 9e57042 -> green 36a2392, no-ff merge origin/main 3a45518, 70 ops/compose/agent-vault tests pass. Restores conformance to agent-vault-broker/12. BC filed BC-local tmpl-5nc for a future target-pin scenario; lead-po follow-up to pin target==/data is DEFERRED/non-blocking. Fix on origin/main but NOT yet published — delivery to host/adopters requires a release >0.16.0 (Task 2 follow-up bead).

title: shop-shell CA self-source is mis-gated AND donor-recovery is not cold-start-correct -> empty AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM -> SSL cert-verify exception on claude startup (adoption blocker)
description:
POURED bin/shop-shell (shipped by shopsystem-templates) blocks the adopter from starting a brokered lead/shell: claude startup throws an SSL certificate exception. RE-DISPATCHED to shopsystem-templates as request_bugfix work_id=lead-7gvm (revised fix direction delivered via nudge — see notes).

ROOT CAUSE: shop-shell sources broker creds from (1) .env (operator-recorded) and (2) donor-recovery from a running BC via docker inspect + jq, gated "if [ -z $AGENT_VAULT_TOKEN ]" (fires only when token absent). When .env supplies AGENT_VAULT_TOKEN but NOT AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM (common case — the multi-line CA PEM cannot live in .env; the source-1 line-parser truncates it), source 2 is SKIPPED, AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM launches EMPTY, no CA into system trust, agent-vault MITM TLS untrusted, first HTTPS call (api.anthropic.com via HTTPS_PROXY) throws SSL cert-verify. The exact silent-broken (token present, CA empty) state scenario 183 @scenario_hash:2adc62a25c401e4b forbids.

REVISED FIX DIRECTION (cold-start-correct — see notes for empirical finding): source AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM from the BROKER shop-shell just started (agent-vault ca fetch against the agent-vault container, multi-line PEM captured intact, ungated by token-presence) BEFORE the docker run; donor-BC recovery is only a lower-priority fallback; a fully-cold start (broker up, zero BCs) MUST succeed; error only if no token resolvable after all sources.

DOCTRINE: poured instance furniture; fix lands in shopsystem-templates canonical + re-poured here; do NOT hand-edit the lead copy as canonical. Router applied a temp local .env workaround — REVERT after re-pour. Governing 183 @scenario_hash:2adc62a25c401e4b (conformance); additive to 172 @scenario_hash:5335c39eb06f7493 and 134 @scenario_hash:bd4c9e3f3e391736 (unchanged, lead-held-only scope). RE-DISPATCHED — awaiting work_done.
notes:
nudge: reason=general work_id=lead-7gvm at=2026-06-22T18:25:48.559384+00:00
RE-DISPATCH DELIVERED (2026-06-22) via 'shop-msg send nudge --bc shopsystem-templates --work-id lead-7gvm --reason general'. RATIONALE: the original request_bugfix (BC inbox row id 632) is still UNCONSUMED, and 'shop-msg send' refuses to overwrite an existing inbox entry under the one-row-per-(bc,work_id) collision invariant (features/templates/122; no --force on send -- the --force affordance in 118 is respond-side only). Per 122, direction='nudge' is the sanctioned channel that sidesteps that invariant and coexists with the original dispatch. The general nudge carries the full REVISED fix direction (broker-fetch, cold-start-correct) as its --note, superseding the original fix direction while the original request_bugfix body + dispatch lifecycle stay intact (nudge does not mutate dispatch_state per ADR-015). BC will pick up both: the original request_bugfix and the superseding nudge note. Bead stays OPEN/dispatched awaiting work_done.
close_reason: RECONCILED + CLOSED 2026-06-22. work_done from shopsystem-templates: warm-path donor-recovery conformance DONE. Hash verification (ADR-018 artifact surface, scenario-block-only canonicalization): 183=2adc62a25c401e4b MATCHES recorded tag (text unchanged, no register drift); 172=5335c39eb06f7493 unchanged; 134=bd4c9e3f3e391736 unchanged. No lead-held hash retired/superseded; scope additive lead-held-only. Donor block now fires on ANY empty {TOKEN,CA_PEM,ADDR,VAULT}, backfills empty fields only, preserves .env. Outbox row consumed (--bc shopsystem-templates --work-id lead-7gvm --message-type work_done). BC merge/test claims (red de8e8d4->green 4c73bc3, merge b622aa7, close 6a81925, 26 shop-shell tests) accepted as BC gated-loop evidence per matrix. COLD-START GAP (broker-fetch not pinned; fail-loud on zero-donor cold start) is NOT a conformance defect of 183 (183 only PERMITS broker-fetch + allows fail-loud) -> tracked as SCENARIO GAP on lead-4him (P1, ready, related-dep on lead-7gvm), requires lead-po authoring pass. REMINDER: revert the router's temp local .env CA workaround once cold-start fix re-pours.

title: WS-2: bin/agent-vault-provision omits claude-api (+platform/mcp/github-api) broker service mappings -> Claude 401 in BC (PDR-018 cond 6/9 wall, iter-6)
description: Empirically surfaced by dummyco spike iter-6 (lead-jdfb). The shop-templates-rendered bin/agent-vault-provision only runs ONE 'vault service add' (github.com basic, line 135). The live shopsystem 'fleet' vault carries FIVE services: github-git, github-api, claude-api (api.anthropic.com bearer CLAUDE_OAUTH), claude-platform, claude-mcp-proxy. With CLAUDE_OAUTH stored but NO claude-api service, the broker never attaches the OAuth bearer to api.anthropic.com requests, so the BC's Claude calls return 401 'x-api-key header is required' (verified via curl through the broker proxy, and reproduced as 'Please run /login - API Error: 401 Invalid bearer token' in the live BC agent). The brokered clone (github) worked; only the Claude leg was unprovisioned. UNBLOCKED IN-SPIKE generically via the agent-vault tool (3x 'vault service add' for claude-api/platform/mcp), NOT a code hand-edit. Owner: shopsystem-templates, request_bugfix: provision must register the claude-api/claude-platform/claude-mcp-proxy (bearer CLAUDE_OAUTH) and github-api services mirroring the live fleet vault, so a fresh product's BC can reach Claude through its own broker. This is the WS-2 provisioning-completeness gap. DISPATCHED via request_bugfix -> shopsystem-templates 2026-06-15, with fold-ins lead-eqzi (CLAUDE_OAUTH human-gate prompt casing) + lead-71tq (.env writeback AGENT_VAULT_VAULT + AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM) named for joint reconcile.
close_reason: RECONCILED complete (origin/main 4797c35). Rendered bin/agent-vault-provision now registers ALL FIVE live-fleet broker services: github-git (basic, GITHUB_USERNAME/GITHUB_TOKEN, preserved clone svc), github-api (bearer GITHUB_TOKEN), claude-api (api.anthropic.com bearer CLAUDE_OAUTH — THE missing mapping that 401'd fresh BCs), claude-platform, claude-mcp-proxy. claude-* adds run unconditionally (declare host->CLAUDE_OAUTH; value via human gate). Reconciled github cred keys GITHUB_PAT_USER/GITHUB_PAT -> live vault's authoritative GITHUB_USERNAME/GITHUB_TOKEN (still SCREAMING_SNAKE). Slug-clean. test(red 42e03ca)->green(ffbb165), zero newly-failing. E2E pin (fresh BC reaches Claude no-401) = iter-7 live validation. Rides templates v0.9.0.

title: WS-1.1: shop-msg storage.py SYSTEM_SLUG hard-coded 'shopsystem' defeats distinct-product address projection (PDR-018 cond 2/5 wall, iter-6)
description: Empirically surfaced by the dummyco spike iter-6 (lead-jdfb). shop_msg/storage.py:222 defines module-level SYSTEM_SLUG='shopsystem'; _abstract_address_for() (L226-240) and the registry SQL CASE (L276-278) use it unconditionally and do NOT read SHOPMSG_SYSTEM_SLUG from the env. bc-launcher correctly INJECTS SHOPMSG_SYSTEM_SLUG=dummyco into the BC container (lead-53y0 unification reached the launcher), but the messaging BC's storage never CONSUMES it. Result: registering dummyco-product/dummyco-greeter projects to shopsystem/lead and shopsystem/dummyco-greeter, and an assign_scenarios dispatch deposits at abstract address 'shopsystem/dummyco-greeter' (verified via shop-msg dump). Transport still round-trips because BOTH lead and BC mis-project consistently, but condition 2 (distinct identity) / condition 5 (deposit to a dummyco/<name> address, NOT shopsystem/<name>) is DEFEATED — a real second product on a shared DB would collide on the shopsystem/lead sentinel. Owner: shopsystem-messaging, request_bugfix: make SYSTEM_SLUG read SHOPMSG_SYSTEM_SLUG (env > default 'shopsystem'), threading it through _abstract_address_for, LEAD_ABSTRACT_ADDRESS, and the registry SQL. This is the forecast WS-1.1 P0 wall. DISPATCHED via request_bugfix -> shopsystem-messaging 2026-06-15.
close_reason: RECONCILED complete (origin/main b2a3ec5). THE gate-blocker (review finding 2). storage.py's two remaining frozen SYSTEM_SLUG sites now consult _get_system_slug() at OPERATION time: (1) lead sentinel via new _lead_abstract_address() (LEAD_ABSTRACT_ADDRESS kept as back-compat default), (2) registry addressing-migration UPDATE CASE built per-connect via _registry_address_migration_sql(). With SHOPMSG_SYSTEM_SLUG=dummyco -> projects dummyco/<rest> + dummyco/lead; unset -> shopsystem/ (non-regressing, live fleet safe). TDD red(b993b60: 3 dummyco backfill assertions)->green(30175cb), 173 passed incl migration scenario e9a31b6f. Completes WS-1.1 (lead-tgsb left it incomplete). Needs a messaging release + re-pour before iter-7 can project dummyco/<name> cleanly. Unblocks PDR-018 conditions 2/5.

title: WS-2 spike wall (iter-4): rendered bin/agent-vault-provision uses kebab-case credential keys (github-pat / github-pat-user) — agent-vault 0.32.0 vault credential set REQUIRES SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE; provision fails before fleet mint + human gate
notes:
DISCOVERED-FROM: lead-jdfb (WS-0 dummyco spike, iter-4) + lead-l7uz (WS-2 bootstrap path). This is the wall that lead-beym's own close-reason FORECAST: "BC verified CLI SYNTAX vs agent-vault --help; END-TO-END provision against a live broker is the dummyco-spike iter-4 validation — iter-4 is the real test of this fix." iter-4 ran that real test and the syntax-only verification proved insufficient one layer deeper.

WALL (iter-4, against the LIVE dummyco-agent-vault broker, agent-vault 0.32.0 commit e01a925):
The rewritten provision (lead-beym/v0.7.0) gets FURTHER than ever — owner `auth register` SUCCEEDS ("Owner account created"), `vault create` SUCCEEDS (real vault id 63bf3f59-...). It then fails at `vault credential set`:

  Error: Invalid credential key "github-pat": must be SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE (e.g. STRIPE_KEY)

The rendered script (bin/agent-vault-provision lines ~125-141) uses kebab-case credential keys `github-pat-user` and `github-pat`, and references them via `--username-key github-pat-user` / `--password-key github-pat`. agent-vault 0.32.0 `vault credential set` enforces SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE keys (confirmed against `vault credential set --help` examples: STRIPE_KEY, SLACK_BOT_TOKEN). Because the script is `set -euo pipefail`, a supplied GITHUB_PAT aborts here BEFORE the fleet-token mint and BEFORE the Claude-OAuth human gate.

FIX (grounded against the live 0.32.0 CLI surface — single coherent edit in shop-templates' bin/agent-vault-provision template):
Rename the two credential keys to SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE and update the service-add references accordingly:
  - `github-pat-user` -> `GITHUB_PAT_USER`
  - `github-pat`      -> `GITHUB_PAT`
  - `vault service add ... --username-key GITHUB_PAT_USER --password-key GITHUB_PAT`
(The shell env var GITHUB_PAT_USER already uses that casing; only the vault credential KEY names + the --*-key references are kebab. Note the shell var GITHUB_PAT and the vault key GITHUB_PAT can share the name without conflict.)

EMPIRICAL PROOF THE FIX IS COMPLETE (iter-4 contract-surface probe against the live broker — NOT a hand-patch of the rendered script; ADR-018 contract-surface evidence):
With SCREAMING_SNAKE keys, the ENTIRE rest of the flow works end-to-end on the live dummyco broker:
  - `vault credential set GITHUB_PAT_USER=... GITHUB_PAT=... --vault dummyco`  -> exit 0 (both creds set)
  - `vault service add --name github --host github.com --auth-type basic --username-key GITHUB_PAT_USER --password-key GITHUB_PAT --vault dummyco` -> exit 0 (service added)
  - `agent create dummyco-fleet --token-only --vault dummyco:proxy` -> mints a real av_agt_... token (exit 0)
So the key-casing rename is the SOLE remaining wall between the provision script and a fully-clean E2E run through fleet-mint + .env writeback + the Claude-OAuth human gate. No further provision wall lies behind it.

OWNER / VEHICLE: shopsystem-templates (provision script render) -> request_bugfix. Cut a templates patch release + re-pour onto the lead host, then re-run iter-5 from empty to validate (the fix is validated by the clean re-run, per gate condition 8 / ADR-031 D2).

SEVERITY: P0 BLOCKING — the GitHub-PAT arm of the one human gate + the fleet-token mint + BC launch are all downstream of this. (The Claude-OAuth dashboard arm is reachable only on the no-PAT path, same shape as iter-3's nuance, because pipefail aborts here when a PAT is supplied.)

DISPATCH: request_bugfix sent to shopsystem-templates 2026-06-12 (work_id lead-l95x). dispatch_state=dispatched. Rides templates v0.8.0 + re-pour (router cuts after work_done); iter-5 validates E2E to the human gate. Bead stays open/dispatched.

The proven-last provision wall (iter-4 corrected-casing probe already validated the rest of the flow mints a real av_agt_ fleet token). title: WS-2 spike wall: rendered ops compose agent-vault service is hashicorp/vault, not the infisical/agent-vault broker — provision+proxy cannot work
description:
Surfaced by dummyco instantiation spike iter-2 (lead-jdfb), 2026-06-12, against v0.5.0-poured ops (findings/dummyco-spike-iter-2.md). The rendered compose.yaml 'agent-vault' service uses image hashicorp/vault:latest. That is HashiCorp Vault, NOT the agent-vault broker. Three coupled defects in the rendered agent-vault service vs the live broker (infisical/agent-vault:latest):
(1) WRONG IMAGE: rendered=hashicorp/vault:latest; required=infisical/agent-vault:latest. hashicorp/vault has /bin/vault but NO 'agent-vault' CLI, so bin/agent-vault-provision's 'docker exec <broker> agent-vault put' fails: exec: agent-vault: executable file not found in $PATH.
(2) MISSING ENV: live broker reads AGENT_VAULT_MASTER_PASSWORD from env (auto-unseal, ADR-028 D1). Rendered service sets none.
(3) MISSING PORTS: live broker publishes 14321 (API) + 14322 (proxy). Rendered service publishes none, so shop-shell's HTTPS_PROXY=agent-vault:14322 and provision/check have no broker endpoint.
FIX (owner shopsystem-templates ops render, render_ops_template): the agent-vault compose service must render image infisical/agent-vault:latest (product-neutral / parameterized as the live fleet references it), an AGENT_VAULT_MASTER_PASSWORD env wired to the instance .env, and the broker ports (slug-derived, override-able, mirroring the postgres 5714 derivation; default fleet 14321/14322). This is THE blocking wall for the dummyco spike Step 3/4 — upstream of the human credential gate.

lead-pwl8 (P1) FOLDED IN (same render_ops_template surface): bootstrap renders no .env.example though Brief 011 Step 2 says 'cp .env.example .env'. Fix renders a placeholder-only .env.example carrying AGENT_VAULT_MASTER_PASSWORD + broker addr/token placeholders, additive to the six ops-tool files (174 non-regressing — .env.example is a top-level scaffold file OUTSIDE the six-file ops-tool set).

Live broker shape grounded via docker inspect shopsystem-agent-vault-1 (lead artifact surface): infisical/agent-vault:latest, AGENT_VAULT_MASTER_PASSWORD, ports 14321/14322.

title: EPIC: progressive disclosure for LLM artifacts (ADR/PDR/brief) — tiered format + task-conditioned access patterns to prevent context bloat and contradictory decisions
description:
## Context
Requested by David 2026-07-04 as the next important issue. Problem: the artifact set (adr/ pdr/ briefs/ features/ findings/) has grown; unstructured access means lead-po/lead-architect and BC architects don't reliably reference existing decisions before making new ones, producing contradictory decisions unnoticed, and pulling whole documents wastes context. Goal: a progressive-disclosure system so an agent (lead or BC) pulls only the disclosure LEVEL relevant to its current activity.
design: Target: multi-level disclosure per artifact. For an ADR: L0 = id+title+one-line description (relevance triage); L1 = the DECISION statement (+status/supersedes) — the tier distributed to BCs that must CONFORM; L2 = full document incl. context/alternatives/tradeoffs/consequences — used by lead-architect AUTHORING new ADRs that must account for existing ones. Formalize existing ADR metadata into a required YAML frontmatter schema (id,title,status,description,decision,supersedes,superseded-by,depends-on,tags) and generate the tiers + indexes from the ONE source (single-sourcing, to avoid summary/full drift — same failure class as the scenario hash/gherkin drift already fought; nbx5 single-source precedent). Generate a machine+human index (llms.txt / OKF index.md style). Distribute the L1 'decisions digest' to BCs via the skills-style pour or shop-msg, keeping L2 lead-only. Add a decision-coherence GATE (analogous to scenarios --aggregate and ADR-047 coherence gate) that flags supersede-conflicts / contradictory active decisions. Reference frameworks: Google Open Knowledge Format (OKF, GoogleCloudPlatform/knowledge-catalog); llms.txt (Answer.AI); Anthropic Agent Skills progressive disclosure; MCP resources; Diataxis; MADR; RAPTOR/GraphRAG/contextual-retrieval. Full research report delivered in-session 2026-07-04. Recommended first step: a PDR formalizing the frontmatter schema + disclosure tiers + access-pattern gates, then an ADR, then a 'decisions' CLI tool (sibling to scenarios) emitting --level card|decision|full + index + coherence gate.
acceptance_criteria: A PDR/ADR formalizes the artifact frontmatter schema and the disclosure tiers (L0/L1/L2); a generator produces tiers+index from a single source (no hand-maintained summaries); BC distribution ships only the conforming tier; a coherence gate detects contradictory/superseded active decisions; lead-po/lead-architect prompts carry a 'consult the decision index before authoring' gate.
notes: 2026-07-04: RESEARCH-FIRST correction (David) — no PDR before the research finding; the epic's 'in-session research report' was never a durable artifact. Running the fabro-style experimental protocol (PDR-016): research fan-out -> synthesis -> throwaway experiment -> implementation recommendation, output to findings/progressive-disclosure/ (00-plan.md seeded). Launched as background multi-agent Workflow (run wf_9632879f-404). Process-formalization bead: lead-x2rl (proving case = this epic). PDR/ADR authoring DEFERRED until the finding + verdict land. Do NOT author scenarios/PDR until 08-recommendation.md verdict is reviewed.

title: DDD bounded-context review: ubiquitous language + scope per BC, drift audit (system-as-defined vs behaviors-assigned-over-time), resolve overloads (templates; build-pipeline mgmt vs execution)
description: David 2026-07-02: our domain breakdown into bounded contexts has blurred — behaviors assigned to BCs ad hoc over time. Approach via improved DDD: (1) current-state inventory (what BCs exist + behaviors each was assigned); (2) ubiquitous language + scope definition PER BC (PO-authored, David is product authority on vocab/boundaries); (3) drift review (defined vs assigned) surfacing misplacements — templates is overloaded; build-pipeline MANAGEMENT vs EXECUTION placement (where dagger CI / publish-bc-base / launch / fabro-orchestration belong) is an instance; (4) target context map + rehoming plan -> ADRs + re-dispatch mis-homed capabilities. Subsumes the dagger-CI-placement question (was lead-fzxt #4/merge decision). Open scoping Qs to David: whole-system vs hotspots-first; where per-BC context artifacts live (new bounded-context canvas vs ADR/PDR); existing source of truth for intended scope (shop-card?). Lead-shop initiative: PO=language/scope, architect=boundaries/drift/ADRs; product-authority discovery gate applies (conduct vocab/scope dialogue with David).

title: BUG (x3) fabro residuals found dogfooding on real work: F1 classify pinned to sonnet-4-5 (429) blocks deliverable, F2 drain consumes dispatch on blocked run, F3 --orchestrator fabro launch doesn't return
description: Found dogfooding fabro building the dagger CI module (findings/dagger-spike/04a-dogfood-dispatch.md; fabro instance shopsystem-bc-launcher-dagger, run 01KWJ2XQCJ...). Fabro booted+engaged+drained correctly + fail-closed (ADR-051 held), but blocked at classify. FIXES (all in bc-launcher's fabro impl): F1 (BLOCKING) src/bc_launcher/assets/fabro-def/workflow.fabro ~L84 pins .coding/.review (incl classify) to claude-sonnet-4-5, no retry=, fallbacks:[]; sonnet-4-5 is 429 on the agent-vault wire (haiku-4-5=200 via 'fabro model test') -> any sonnet 429 deterministically fail-closes the whole deliverable at node 1. Fix: route light classify off .coding to haiku + retry/backoff on 429 + optional sonnet->haiku fallback chain (keep fail-closed, don't let ONE transient 429 block the run). F2: native arm/drain CONSUMES the dispatch even on a blocked/fail-closed run -> lead-6tks assign_scenarios lost, re-run needs re-deposit. Fix: don't consume-and-lose on fail-closed (defer consume until terminal complete / re-queue on block) so blocked runs are retriable. F3 (non-fatal): --orchestrator fabro launch holds 'fabro server --foreground' + never returns (had to background). Fix: launch returns after engage (detach the server) like the tmux path. On green cut v0.3.49. Parent lead-fzxt (dogfood); vehicle request_bugfix to shopsystem-bc-launcher (real, tmux — do NOT dogfood fabro to fix fabro).

title: BUG Defect D (final): engage exports ANTHROPIC_API_KEY AFTER fabro install spawns the serving daemon -> keyless server -> fabro run 'No LLM providers configured'
description: Clean v0.3.47 e2e (findings/fabro-spike/09-launcher-e2e-v0347.md): Fix#4 A/B/C all automatic (server boots, fabro run engages+loads 23-node loop, settings schema-valid), S3 cred path + S6 fail-closed GREEN automatic, invariants held. ONE remaining defect: 'fabro install ... --github-strategy token --github-username x' STARTS the serving daemon (127.0.0.1:32276) BEFORE the engage exports ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, so the running server is keyless; the redundant 2nd 'fabro server start' (with key) can't bind ('Server already running') + no-ops; 'fabro run' -> preflight FAILS 'No LLM providers configured'. VERIFIED FIX (by-hand: loop then ran end-to-end through emit_r): export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (dummy), SSL_CERT_FILE=/home/vscode/.config/agent-vault/ca.pem, ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL BEFORE 'fabro install' (so its spawned daemon inherits the key) + DROP the redundant 2nd server start. Additive; on green cut v0.3.48. NOTE: a 'complete' (vs 'blocked') demo also needs the classify model available (external sonnet-4-5 429 is out of scope; loop correctly fail-closes to blocked when a node model is unavailable). Parent lead-kqgp; FINAL launcher fix.

title: BUG Fix#4 incomplete: bc-container --orchestrator fabro engage server-bootstrap broken (3 sub-defects) — blocks automatic launch->work_done
description: Clean e2e on v0.3.46 (findings/fabro-spike/08-launcher-e2e-clean.md): Fixes #1/#2/#3 + S3 cred path GREEN automatic, but the engage server-bootstrap (_fabro_engage_script / _fabro_server_install_argv) fails 3 ways so fabro server never starts + fabro run never engages -> NO automatic work_done. Sub-defects: A) 'fabro install --non-interactive --skip-llm --overwrite-settings' aborts on fabro 0.254.0 ('requires --github-strategy'); VERIFIED minimal recipe: GH_TOKEN=x fabro install --non-interactive --skip-llm --overwrite-settings --github-strategy token --github-username x. B) trailing '&' backgrounds the whole 'cd /workspace/.fabro && install && ... && nohup server' list so cd runs in subshell, parent cwd stays /workspace, and 'fabro run workflow.fabro' resolves /workspace/workflow.fabro -> not found; fix = run the engage so cwd is /workspace/.fabro for fabro run (cd in the same shell as fabro run; only nohup the server), or pass an absolute def path. C) --skip-llm skips server provider registration so 'fabro model test'=not configured even with server up; ensure the anthropic provider is registered at the server (pointing at the shim base_url http://127.0.0.1:8788/v1 + dummy ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) so the loop's agent nodes can call the model (workflow-level settings alone was insufficient per the run). Additive; scn 73-77 green; tmux default unchanged. On green cut v0.3.47 re-release + report digests/tag/run-id. Vehicle: request_bugfix to shopsystem-bc-launcher. Parent lead-kqgp. NOTE: 4th round of live-only launcher bugs — see lead-fzxt (local-build) escalation.
notes: dispatch request_bugfix -> shopsystem-bc-launcher (lead-8q2x)
close_reason: Superseded by lead-sp2m (Fix C correction). Fixes A+B from 8q2x were sound; C fixed in sp2m; all shipped in v0.3.47.

title: BUG (x4) bc-container --orchestrator fabro launch path defects block a clean fabro-orchestrated launch (found live at end-to-end)
description:
Live end-to-end (findings/fabro-spike/07-launcher-e2e.md) PROVED the fabro runtime GREEN (S3 cred path, S5 loop->work_done, S6 fail-closed, all 5 invariants) — but the released v0.3.45 launcher has 4 real integration bugs that block the automated bc-container launch --orchestrator fabro (workspace-mount) path (each independently blocking):
BUG#1: fabro def/shim/settings placement is inside the 'if repo_url and not workspace_mount' clone-guard (controller.py ~L1815/L2227/L2291) so it's SKIPPED on --workspace-mount, yet _start_agent_session still runs the fabro engage -> fails (no /workspace/.fabro). FIX: move def/shim/settings placement OUT of the clone-only guard; run on BOTH clone + workspace-mount paths.
BUG#2: placed workflow.toml [run.environment.env]/[run.inputs] hardcode BC_NAME=fabro-throwaway/WORK_ID=fabro-spike-demo-3 (byte-verbatim from the bundle); native script= nodes (arm/armed/worktree/integ/wdg_r/emit_r) read / from that overlay; -I only overrides [run.inputs] for agent PROMPTS, NOT the native command sandbox env. FIX: launcher rewrites the placed workflow.toml [run.environment.env] (+[run.inputs]) BC_NAME/WORK_ID to the actual bc_name/work_id (same as it rewrites settings.toml).
BUG#3: shim + engage started via non-login /bin/sh -c so SSL_CERT_FILE (exported by /etc/profile.d/agent-vault-ca.sh login profile, not docker ENV) is empty -> shim urllib doesn't trust the agent-vault MITM CA -> upstream POST to api.anthropic.com via HTTPS_PROXY fails CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED (502). FIX: launcher exports SSL_CERT_FILE=/home/vscode/.config/agent-vault/ca.pem explicitly on the shim + fabro engage exec env (parallel to how the clone path sets GIT_SSL_CAINFO for the same reason).
BUG#4: engage runs 'fabro server start --foreground --no-web' but launcher writes only workflow-level /workspace/.fabro/settings.toml, never a SERVER-level ~/.fabro/settings.toml -> server aborts 'server.auth.methods: field is required'; fabro run then can't reach it. FIX: engage first bootstraps the ephemeral server (fabro install --non-interactive --skip-llm --overwrite-settings + register the anthropic provider at the shim base_url + dummy ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in server env) before fabro run.
All 4 STRUCTURALLY verifiable by the BC (placement runs on both paths; workflow.toml env rewritten; exec env includes SSL_CERT_FILE; engage issues the server-bootstrap commands). Vehicle: request_bugfix to shopsystem-bc-launcher (capability pinned by scn 73-77, runtime impl broken). Then re-release v0.3.46, lead re-upgrades + re-runs end-to-end. Parent lead-kqgp.
notes: dispatch request_bugfix -> shopsystem-bc-launcher (lead-ze4w)
close_reason: COMPLETE: 4 fabro-launch-path bugs fixed (f82d9cc, reviewer-verified, 296 tests, additive scn 73-77 green) + v0.3.46 shipped GREEN. bc-base:latest v0.3.46 sha256:0dfa8d06 (off 4a4d2993); bc-lead:latest v0.3.46 sha256:d5057a72; publish run 28552359196. Lead bc-container upgraded to 0.3.46. Fixes proven by the clean end-to-end re-run (dispatched).

title: EPIC Fabro spike: BC under in-container ephemeral fabro orchestration (same launch interface as bc-container), executes assign_scenarios via shop-msg — agent-vault creds NOT fabro secrets
description: ACTIVE EXPERIMENT 2026-07-01 (David, overnight autonomous). Goal: a BC comes up orchestrated by an ephemeral LOCAL fabro server INSIDE the container and executes work per the shop-msg protocol (handle assign_scenarios end-to-end: build->review->work_done), with the SAME launch interface as bc-container. title: bc-launcher launch ABORTS when /var/lib/bc-launcher diagnostic dir is not writable — _write_launch_diagnostic mkdir PermissionError (fresh-adopter shop-shell)
description: OPERATOR E2E 2026-06-30 (David, fresh adopter testproduct3, AFTER approve-claude succeeded). bin/shop-shell -> bc-container launch crashed: bc_launcher/controller.py _start_agent_session -> _write_launch_diagnostic -> driver.py:735 write_launch_diagnostic does p.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True) on /var/lib/bc-launcher/bc-testproduct3-lead/... and dies with PermissionError [Errno 13] on /var/lib/bc-launcher (root-owned, not writable by the shop-shell user). A best-effort LAUNCH DIAGNOSTIC write should never be FATAL to the launch, and its path should not require a root-writable /var/lib. TWO-PART FIX (BC to decide): (a) make the diagnostic write best-effort/non-fatal (try/except -> warn, continue launch); and/or (b) target a user-writable location (workspace/state dir under the invoking user) instead of /var/lib/bc-launcher. ADR-018: lead cannot read bc-launcher source; verify pre-state against contract/artifact surface and dispatch request_bugfix to shopsystem-bc-launcher. Function-BLOCKING the fresh-adopter bootstrap path (relates l7uz WS-2 E2E).

ADR-041 PRESERVATION (verified against lead artifact surface per ADR-018, no BC source read): D1 PRESERVED — diagnostic is still produced, host-readable, and cause-marked on the normal failure path (work_done attests git diff for the feature is EMPTY; the try/except only changes the degraded UNWRITABLE-dir case from fatal-abort to warn-and-continue, which IS the intent: a best-effort diagnostic write must never be fatal to the launch). D2 PRESERVED — the new user-writable path is host-discoverable via a pure launch_diagnostic_path lookup with no tmux attach; the concrete path is BC-owned per D2 (ADR defers concrete path to the BC, requires it documented + echoed in work_done — satisfied). title: approve-claude (v0.47.0) breaks halfway on a missing env var, half-completes, and re-run is broken — needs upfront precondition check + idempotent re-run / update-tokens-after-the-fact
description: Product authority 2026-06-29, tried the LATEST release (shop-templates v0.47.0 / the 08136da oauth-tokens path): bin/agent-vault-approve-claude breaks PARTWAY THROUGH when it can't find an env var it needs. It tells the operator to retry, BUT it had already HALF-COMPLETED the work, and re-running is BROKEN (not idempotent — the partial state from the first run breaks the second). REQUIRED FIX: (1) VERIFY PRECONDITIONS UP FRONT — check every required env var / input / reachable endpoint / owner cred BEFORE doing any mutating step, so the script either runs the FULL process or fails fast with a clear message and ZERO partial state. (2) IDEMPOTENT / UPDATE-AFTER-THE-FACT — a re-run after a partial or successful run must work, and it should be possible to UPDATE the tokens later (the oauth-tokens endpoint supports re-POST; the script should expose that cleanly). Likely the missing var is something the new login->/v1/credentials/oauth/tokens path needs (owner cred / broker addr / ops-coordinate). Routes to shopsystem-templates. Follow-on to lead-al1r (the approve-claude oauth fix); this is a robustness gap in that shipped script.
notes:
Follow-on to lead-al1r. Priority: this breaks the JUST-SHIPPED v0.47.0 approve flow for adopters, so it's the most urgent of the current batch.
dispatch request_bugfix -> shopsystem-templates (lead-m1dc)

Red/green differential demonstrated for each: 219 zero-partial-state (red 4363865 made_any_mutating_call=True / proposal approved before gap caught -> green: precondition gate moved UP-FRONT, zero mutating calls on every precondition gap); 220 idempotent create-or-reuse (red 0ab5e6f errored 'proposal already approved' -> green: harness broker genuinely rejects re-approve so create-or-reuse is the only passing path); 221 update re-POSTs NEW token material acc-NEWER/ref-NEWER, owner-login, framed 'updated' (red 3bd37a7 read 'populated'). Directly fixes the v0.47.0 break the product authority hit (missing owner-password now caught up-front, zero partial vault state). Coexistence: 202 (1c054dfdc468860a) + 217 (5cf88671d3fab25b) recompute green, untouched; footing pins 74d0086b/d8422606/e59b29a6 not regressed; nothing retired; only suite reds are 12 pre-existing baseline failures identical at parent 7c958be. The DSN must be rendered/exported into the lead shell env by the bootstrap/footing flow (shop-templates). Routes to shopsystem-templates (bootstrap/footing/shop-shell render). Pre-state to verify empirically: which rendered artifact is supposed to export SHOPMSG_DSN and why it's absent on the fresh path. Related arc: bootstrap (lead-z3e2/b2iz).

dispatch request_bugfix -> shopsystem-templates (lead-j8so)
RECONCILED 2026-06-29: work_done from shopsystem-templates reconciled clean. Scenario 214 block-only hash recomputes via scenarios hash to a0342b1700fe27e7 = BC-reported (no drift; implemented as authored). Coexistence pins recompute+preserved: 200=7ce09202755b0503, 201=0789db8bb7f3bc73, 211(Scenario Outline)=0a3a8267109b5792 — none retired/mutated. Acceptance demonstrated: TDD red 52dca4f (impl absent) -> green bed7589; -e SHOPMSG_DSN process-env form on BOTH docker run blocks (launcher+attach), not --env-file; non-empty + ops-coordinate-derived (real 6246 vs stubbed 5439 proves derivation, zero product literal). bc-emit false-refuse wrinkle is the already-tracked lead-s8j2 class (stale 1758fe166d638cda in bc_implementer_sections.feature) — cross-linked there, no duplicate. Scenario 214 is now a live BC-implemented pin. Outbox row consumed.

title: [architect] obtain shopsystem-templates scenario register for 3 approve-claude pins (lead-al1r residual #3) — vehicle gap
description:
Resolve register-visibility gap from lead-al1r reconciliation (lead-oxd8 item #3). Need the shopsystem-templates BC's authoritative @scenario_hash register for the approve-claude / agent-vault-credential surface — specifically the location/text + canonical block-only hashes of three pins the BC's lead-al1r mechanism_observation named as 'lead-held in this BC features/': d8422606299d8819, 74d0086b73d4e477, e59b29a6fc34f60a. Empirically verified (2026-06-29): NONE of the three is present in this lead's features/ tree (grep -r @scenario_hash features/); the work_done register was scenario_hashes:[]. Per ADR-018 the authoritative set = lead features/ + BC mailbox register; reconciles against neither. Goal: locate/import the three pins so the lead features/ mirror reconciles and the PO supersession in lead-oxd8 item #2 binds the real pin 74d0086b73d4e477.

CONTRACT GAP (blocker): the role-catalogue vehicle 'request_scenario_register' (and 'request_shop_card') is NOT implemented in the installed shop-msg (messaging v0.4.2). 'shop-msg send' accepts only {request_maintenance, assign_scenarios, clarify_response, request_bugfix, request_completion_journal, nudge}; both 'request_scenario_register' and 'request_shop_card' error 'invalid choice'. Neither vehicle is pinned in this lead's features/messaging/ catalog. The only register-adjacent vehicle that exists, request_completion_journal, returns a BARE set of block-only canonical hashes with NO per-entry text or file location (features/messaging/27,29) — so it cannot deliver the text/locations needed to import the three pins; it can at most confirm which of the three the BC currently holds as completed (live vs retired). Cannot dispatch the requested vehicle and must not hand-write mailbox YAML or substitute a wrong-intent vehicle.

PATH FORWARD (needs router/PO judgment — scope/contract): either (a) accept request_completion_journal as a partial measure to confirm live-vs-retired of the three hashes now, accepting that text/locations still won't return; or (b) treat the missing request_scenario_register as a messaging-BC capability gap and route a PO-authored assign_scenarios/request_bugfix to the messaging BC to add the vehicle, then dispatch it here. Until resolved, lead-oxd8 PO authoring cannot cleanly import/supersede the named pins.
notes:
dispatch request_scenario_register -> shopsystem-templates (lead-igzz)
dispatch request_scenario_register -> shopsystem-templates (lead-igzz)
RESOLVED-VIA-CLARIFY 2026-06-30 (architect reconciliation). The shopsystem-templates BC could not route request_scenario_register (its installed shop-msg predates v0.4.3 — propagation gap filed lead-r46p) but ANSWERED THE INTENT in its clarify: its live approve-claude register = 1c054dfdc468860a(202), 5cf88671d3fab25b(217), 3b7e07095a354e0a(219), 9aa82d211517155d(220), 45dc18d4b0d1730e(221). Goal of igzz (locate/supersede the 3 named pins d8422606299d8819 / 74d0086b73d4e477 / e59b29a6fc34f60a) is now ANSWERED: those 3 appear in NEITHER ADR-018 authoritative surface — not in lead features/ (grep -r @scenario_hash features/ : absent) and not in the BC's live register (clarify). They are not live pins; the lead-al1r mechanism_observation that named them as 'lead-held in this BC features/' was a transient claim that reconciles against nothing. The refuted KEY=VALUE pin 74d0086b73d4e477 is therefore already retired everywhere. Empirically VERIFIED (ADR-018 D2) via the installed scenarios CLI: all 5 live register hashes reproduce exactly from this lead's features/templates blocks (block-only canonicalization, comments+tags stripped) — scenarios verify --hash matched 5/5. Both authoritative surfaces now reconcile to the identical set. Clarify consumed (shop-msg consume outbox). No re-dispatch (vehicle unroutable BC-side; intent already satisfied).
close_reason: Closed

title: [PO] author refreshable-CLAUDE_OAUTH pin + supersede refuted approve-claude pin (lead-al1r residual)
description:
lead-architect reconciliation of lead-al1r (shopsystem-templates work_done, fix path (a) additive: bin/agent-vault-approve-claude now drives POST /v1/auth/login + POST /v1/credentials/oauth/tokens). Three lead-po authoring items surfaced, none authorable by the architect:

1) NEW PIN UNAUTHORED. The dispatch (request_bugfix, scenarios:[]) deliberately left the new behavior pin to the PO. Author a @scenario_hash pin for the corrective behavior: the rendered approve-claude step populates a REFRESHABLE CLAUDE_OAUTH via the supported oauth-tokens endpoint such that credential_oauth.refresh_token_ct is non-NULL and connected_at is set (broker-refreshable). 202 (1c054dfdc468860a, script-layer non-blank writeback) is NECESSARY-BUT-INSUFFICIENT and stays green; the new pin tightens the premise it left open.

2) SUPERSEDE A REFUTED PIN. BC mechanism_observation reports a lead-held approve-claude pin @scenario_hash:74d0086b73d4e477 whose Then asserts the 'proposal approve <n> KEY=VALUE' form makes the credential refreshable — REFUTED by DB proof (agent-vault silently drops the oauth override; refresh_token_ct stays NULL). PO must supersede/retire that pin so the register stops asserting a mechanism the broker drops.

3) REGISTER-VISIBILITY GAP. The BC names three approve-claude pins (d8422606299d8819 / 74d0086b73d4e477 / e59b29a6fc34f60a) as 'lead-held in this BC features/', but NONE is present in this lead's features/ tree and the work_done register was scenario_hashes:[]. Per ADR-018 the authoritative set = lead features/ + BC mailbox register; these reconcile against NEITHER. PO/architect should request a scenario register dump (or shop card) from shopsystem-templates to locate/import these pins before authoring, so the supersession in (2) binds the real pin.

Reconciled-clean facts (no PO action): preserved pins recompute exactly — 202=1c054dfdc468860a, broker/11=72af524bca85f59c, broker/13=1d08c456af08d577; none mutated. Fix landed origin/main merge 08136da.
notes:
RECONCILED + CLOSED 2026-06-30 (architect, via lead-igzz clarify reconciliation). All three items resolved without new PO authoring:

ITEM #2 (supersede refuted pin 74d0086b73d4e477): ALREADY SATISFIED. The refuted pin (Then asserts 'proposal approve <n> KEY=VALUE' makes CLAUDE_OAUTH refreshable — refuted by the lead-al1r DB proof) is present in NEITHER ADR-018 authoritative surface: not in this lead's features/ (grep absent) and not in the BC's live approve-claude register (igzz clarify). The refuted KEY=VALUE mechanism was REPLACED by the oauth-tokens-endpoint path (POST /v1/credentials/oauth/tokens) pinned by the lead-m1dc robustness family 219/220/221.

ITEM #1 (author refreshable-CLAUDE_OAUTH pin): ALREADY SATISFIED by the lead-m1dc family (authored 2026-06-29, after oxd8 was filed). The 'refreshable CLAUDE_OAUTH via the supported oauth-tokens endpoint, refresh-token present + connected' behavior oxd8 wanted is pinned by: 220 (9aa82d211517155d) Then = 'populated, refreshing CLAUDE_OAUTH — non-empty token material, refresh token present, connected'; 221 (45dc18d4b0d1730e) Then = 'remains in a refreshing/connected state'; 219 (3b7e07095a354e0a) Given pins the POST /v1/credentials/oauth/tokens path with access+refresh; 217 (5cf88671d3fab25b) is the doctor-side assertion that CLAUDE_OAUTH is present AND refreshable/connected. 202 (1c054dfdc468860a, non-blank writeback) stays GREEN as necessary-but-insufficient — exactly as oxd8 specified and as each of 219/220/221's headers confirm ('does NOT retire 202'). title: [BUG] approve-claude oauth-credential seed is structurally wrong: agent-vault 'proposal approve KEY=VALUE' silently drops oauth overrides -> blank CLAUDE_OAUTH
description: The rendered bin/agent-vault-approve-claude (correct as of shop-templates v0.46.0) seeds the Claude OAuth token by calling 'agent-vault vault proposal approve <n> CLAUDE_OAUTH=<json> --yes'. agent-vault applies a KEY=VALUE override ONLY for type=static credentials and SILENTLY DROPS it for type=oauth. Result: the CLAUDE_OAUTH oauth slot is created (token_url+client_id from the proposal) but token material never lands: credentials.ciphertext len 0 and credential_oauth.refresh_token_ct / token_expires_at / connected_at all NULL. Credential reads back blank -> adopter's 'tokens not in the UI' symptom. The script-layer fix (lead-dui6, @scenario_hash:1c054dfdc468860a) is necessary but insufficient because the fault is BELOW the script in agent-vault. Dispatched as request_bugfix to shopsystem-templates.
acceptance_criteria: After the chosen approve-claude flow, for CLAUDE_OAUTH: credential_oauth.refresh_token_ct is non-NULL and connected_at is set, and the broker can refresh the credential. Must preserve agent-vault-broker/11 (72af524bca85f59c) and broker/13 (1d08c456af08d577).
notes: OPERATOR E2E PASS 2026-06-30 (David, fresh adopter): bin/agent-vault-approve-claude ran the populate-step path and the approval WORKED — CLAUDE_OAUTH credential landed (leg-2 mechanism satisfied). Both acceptance legs now complete (leg-1 release 08136da/v0.47.0 pulled+verified; leg-2 operator-verified). Closing. Minor ergonomics note split to a P3 follow-up (script requires env vars that already live in .env).
close_reason: Closed

description: ACTIVE DEBUG THREAD (product authority, 2026-06-29). The prior fresh-bootstrap failure was root-caused to a STALE OLD container/image that shop-shell reused (old shop-templates -> no AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM, hardcoded shopsystem-product); user force-pulled the latest (bc-base/bc-lead v0.3.37 = shop-templates 0.46.0) and that part is fixed. BUT now on the latest, the APPROVE-CLAUDE CREDENTIALS issue (the lead-dui6 class: approve-claude writes a blank/missing Claude credential) is BACK. lead-dui6 fixed the approve-claude blank-token at shopsystem-templates origin/main 993b4f7 (shipped in v0.46.0), so either that fix is insufficient for the real flow OR a different approve-claude path regressed. INVESTIGATION LEADS for next session: (a) v0.46.0 ships a SEPARATE script bin/agent-vault-approve-claude (proposal-create + operator-approve flow: 'vault proposal create' makes an OAuth-typed CLAUDE_OAUTH credential SLOT, then STOPS for the operator to approve at a printed URL pasting the real token) — the lead-dui6 fix was in agent-vault-provision's inline approve-claude block, NOT necessarily bin/agent-vault-approve-claude; verify which path the user's bootstrap uses. (b) Inspect the v0.46.0 rendered scripts at /tmp/.../scratchpad/render46/bin/agent-vault-approve-claude + /bin/agent-vault-provision and compare to the dui6 fix (993b4f7). Related: lead-dui6 (CLOSED — approve-claude blank-token fix), lead-b2iz (stale-image-verify), bootstrap arc.

title: [bug] bin/bootstrap does not verify the pulled image's baked shop-templates version — a stale/cached :latest silently renders an ancient shop (no AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM, hardcoded product) -> fresh bootstrap broken
description: Empirical (product authority, 2026-06-29): a fresh adopter bootstrap of testproduct3-lead from dstengle/shopsystem-starter rendered an ANCIENT shop-templates (.env header 'shopsystem-product' hardcoded not testproduct3; pre- style; NO AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM line) instead of v0.46.0 — so shop-shell could not deliver the broker MITM CA to the leaf, agent-vault did not connect, 'very few environment variables set'. ROOT CAUSE: bin/bootstrap (starter) floats BC_BASE_IMAGE/BC_LEAD_IMAGE on :latest and 'docker pull >/dev/null' them (bin/bootstrap L85, L103), but PROCEEDS regardless of whether the pull actually fetched the current image — a stale cached :latest (or a silent pull/auth failure falling back to cache) makes the in-image 'shop-templates bootstrap' render an ancient shop. Verified the CURRENT v0.3.37 image (=:latest) bakes shop-templates 0.46.0 and renders CORRECTLY (testproduct3-lead .env.example WITH AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM + ops-coordinates + parameterized); the user just didn't get that image. FIX: bin/bootstrap must assert the resolved image's baked shop-templates version (e.g. docker run pip show shop-templates) against an expected/floor version and FAIL LOUDLY on a stale pull, rather than silently rendering an ancient shop. Connects to the stale-:latest-cache class (cf. earlier bc-container stale-image gap). Owning BC: shopsystem-bc-launcher (bin/bootstrap is rendered from templates/ops; render owner is shopsystem-templates — architect to pin owner). BLOCKS reliable fresh adopter bootstrap.
notes: dispatch request_bugfix -> shopsystem-templates (lead-b2iz)
close_reason: Reconciled work_done from shopsystem-templates: bin/bootstrap baked-version verify gate (scenarios 226: 4457c8c280d4fbf4 proceed-at-or-above-floor + e9d64a8acc917efb refuse/exit-1/no-render-below-floor). Both 226 block-only hashes recompute-match via scenarios hash. Coexistence pins 187/174/184 (7 hashes) recompute clean, preserved-not-retired. Acceptance: NO-RENDER-ON-STALE (docker-invocation-log: exit 1, zero shop-templates bootstrap on refuse path), VERSION-READ via docker image inspect label + printenv fallback (no pip/python), FLOOR-TRACKS-FRAMEWORK (currency-guard fails if EXPECTED_MIN 0.48.0 lags pyproject), DISCRIMINATION mutants caught, TDD red ca886c2 -> green 6020656/8f0b6ca. Delivery leg is lead-x4mk (starter re-render) — adopters get the fix only after x4mk; x4mk kept OPEN. Scenario 226 merged-but-unreleased past shop-templates v0.48.0, accumulating with 225 (lead-s8j2) toward a v0.49.0 consolidated release + image bump.

title: [delivery] bc-launcher image bump -> bake shop-templates v0.46.0 (v0.3.37) so one bc-base/bc-lead image carries BOTH the launcher clone-path fixes AND the v0.46.0 bootstrap fix-set
description: Empirical (2026-06-28): bc-base:latest (v0.3.36) bakes shop-templates 0.45.0 (verified: docker run bc-base pip show shop-templates => 0.45.0), so NO single image yet carries both the launcher clone-path fixes (F1/F2/F3, v0.3.36) AND the v0.46.0 bootstrap fix-set (b6f2 gh-cred, dui6 approve-claude, ml51 shop-shell zero-literal param, 0act bin/ops-coordinates render, jonx skill-doc, 8vny gate, mab1 reviewer, 3rda wrapper-scope). A fresh adopter bootstrap pulls bc-base and uses its BAKED shop-templates, so until the image bakes v0.46.0 a fresh bootstrap does NOT get the v0.46.0 fixes. FIX: bump SHOP_TEMPLATES_VERSION/the baked shop-templates pin to v0.46.0 and cut bc-launcher v0.3.37 (rebuild+publish bc-base + bc-lead GHCR images). Same pattern as the session-start lead-4wik (bump -> bake v0.45.0). Acceptance: new tag > v0.3.36; bc-base bakes shop-templates 0.46.0 (docker run bc-base pip show shop-templates => 0.46.0); publish-bc-base.yml run success; bc-base/bc-lead :tag==:latest GHCR digests. Owning BC: shopsystem-bc-launcher. This is the complete-image delivery for the bootstrap. Note: bc-launcher work_done mentioned an async scheduled poll may also do this version-bump-then-rebuild; dispatching explicitly for determinism.
notes:
dispatch request_maintenance -> shopsystem-bc-launcher (lead-zi6b)
Dispatched 2026-06-28 via shop-msg send request_maintenance --bc shopsystem-bc-launcher --work-id lead-zi6b. Vehicle: request_maintenance (discriminator Q3 = flat: bump baked shop-templates pin v0.45.0->v0.46.0 + rebuild/republish bc-base+bc-lead images; no new scenarios, no behavior change). Pre-state (ADR-018 contract/release surface): latest bc-launcher tag v0.3.36 (ls-remote 89b30542, no v0.3.37); bc-base:latest bakes shop-templates 0.45.0 (empirical image check); shop-templates v0.46.0 released (ls-remote c65fd4cc, 8 bootstrap commits). Acceptance: tag>v0.3.36; bc-base bakes shop-templates 0.46.0; publish-bc-base.yml success; :tag==:latest GHCR digests for bc-base AND bc-lead. Read-back confirmed valid in BC inbox. DOWNSTREAM (record, do not act): after v0.3.37 work_done, docker pull bc-base+bc-lead :latest on lead host so lead launches use complete image; bc-launcher agent historically does not pause on release publish, so expect no inject-unblock, but router injects go-ahead if it stalls in-inbox with no work_done.
close_reason: COMPLETE IMAGE DELIVERED + VERIFIED. bc-launcher v0.3.37 cut (tag d888d7a, commit cf976fb): Dockerfile SHOP_TEMPLATES_VERSION=v0.46.0 + self-pin @v0.3.37, publish-bc-base.yml run 28340099468 SUCCESS, GHCR bc-base/bc-lead :v0.3.37==:latest. Lead pull-side acceptance PASSED: docker pull bc-base:latest + bc-lead:latest; 'docker run bc-base pip show shop-templates' => 0.46.0 (was 0.45.0). So v0.3.37 bc-base/bc-lead is the SINGLE image carrying BOTH the launcher clone-path fixes (F1/F2/F3, retained from v0.3.36) AND the full v0.46.0 bootstrap fix-set (b6f2/dui6 creds, ml51 shop-shell param, 0act ops-coordinates render, jonx/8vny/mab1/3rda). A fresh adopter bootstrap from v0.3.37 gets everything baked.

title: [delivery] cut shopsystem-bc-launcher v0.3.36 release containing F3 grep-validation fix 35be394 (lead-eqao); rebuild+publish bc-base/bc-lead GHCR images baking new bc-container
description: DELIVERY of lead-eqao (launcher F3 grep-validation fix — the FINAL piece of the launcher clone-path fix). The fix is on bc-launcher origin/main (35be394, ancestor of main HEAD 46386c9 — compare 35be394...main => status=ahead ahead_by=1 behind_by=0) but NOT in any release: latest tag v0.3.35 (peeled 4fcfc15) LACKS 35be394 (GitHub compare 4fcfc15...35be394 => status=ahead ahead_by=3 behind_by=0). WHERE THE FIX SHIPS: the fix is in controller.py _clone_ca_materialize_script (exec'd controller.py:1476) — i.e. the bc-container PIP PACKAGE (CLI/controller). The clone runs INSIDE the launched container, where bc-container is the version BAKED into the bc-base image (scn 42 bc-base installs deps in vcs-pin shape; scn 53 rebuilt bc-base latest carries released version; scn 59 newer dep release bumps pin then rebuilds+republishes latest). So F3's runtime validation ships in BOTH the bc-container pip package AND the bc-base IMAGE that bakes it. Delivery therefore REQUIRES a release whose CI (publish-bc-base.yml) rebuilds+publishes the GHCR bc-base/bc-lead images baking the new bc-container — a pip/pyproject bump ALONE is insufficient. Vehicle: request_maintenance (flat delivery; release-cut/publish capability already pinned by features/bc-launcher scn 37/53/59; NO new scenarios, NO behavior change, NO @scenario_hash retired). DOWNSTREAM (router, post-release): docker pull bc-base + bc-lead :latest AND pip-upgrade bc-container to v0.3.36 (the fix is in controller.py so the pip package matters too), then flagless-launch a BC and verify the clone SUCCEEDS end-to-end (F1+F2+F3, no 'error setting certificate file', no 'grep: unrecognized option') — the final launcher verification.

title: [bug] launcher F3 STILL fails (v0.3.35): CA materialized correctly but agent-vault-ca.sh BEGIN-CERTIFICATE validation uses grep PATTERN with leading dashes -> grep parses it as options -> false 'missing BEGIN CERTIFICATE' -> fail-loud refuses clone. One-char fix (grep --); test-fidelity gap persists
description: Empirical (product authority, 2026-06-28) on a REAL flagless v0.3.35 launch of shopsystem-test-harness (bc-base image fd00496d pulled, bc-container 0.3.35). The F3 fail-loud now works correctly (refuses rather than silently proceeding) and the CA IS materialized correctly: /home/vscode/.config/agent-vault/ca.pem exists, vscode-owned, 574 bytes, first line is the BEGIN CERTIFICATE marker. The ONLY remaining bug is the post-write VALIDATION: agent-vault-ca.sh runs grep with the BEGIN-CERTIFICATE marker as the PATTERN (leading dashes), so grep parses it as options and errors ('grep: unrecognized option ...'), the script concludes the CA is invalid ('agent-vault CA file missing BEGIN CERTIFICATE'), and refuses to point git at the (actually-valid) CA -> clone never runs. REPRODUCED in-container: grep with the dash-prefixed pattern -> 'unrecognized option'; grep with a leading -- (end-of-options) -> matches the line. FIX (one line, bc-launcher agent-vault-ca.sh): use grep with end-of-options (-- before the pattern) or grep -F -e, OR anchor differently (e.g. head -1 comparison). TEST-FIDELITY GAP (3rd F3 cycle): scenario 69 passed in the BC suite but the REAL agent-vault-ca.sh grep fails — the test validates the CA differently than the shipped script. The pin/test must exercise the ACTUAL agent-vault-ca.sh validation against a real cert so a grep-option bug RED-tests. Launcher clone-path STILL blocked on F3. Re-opens lead-z0v2 F3. Related: lead-uiwu, lead-z0v2, lead-boa3/lead-37zx (deliveries), ADR-045.

title: [bug] launcher FACET-3 CA materialization is NON-FUNCTIONAL in the real container (v0.3.34): logs 'Materialized CA' but no ca.pem written, git sslCAInfo+AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM unset -> clone STILL fails TLS; fake-driver test (scn 68) didn't catch it
description: Empirical (product authority, 2026-06-28) on a REAL flagless v0.3.34 launch of shopsystem-test-harness after pulling the v0.3.34 bc-base image (digest a10bd472). lead-uiwu shipped F1+F2+F3 but only F1/F2 actually work in the real container; F3 is broken. EVIDENCE: launch logged 'Resolved repo remote from bc-manifest.yaml' (F1 OK), '/workspace vscode:vscode' confirmed (F2 OK, new image Dockerfile chown), 'Materialized the agent-vault broker MITM root CA into the container trust store before the clone' (F3 CLAIMED) — but the clone FAILED: 'error setting certificate file: /home/vscode/.config/agent-vault/ca.pem'. In-container check: /home/vscode/.config/agent-vault/ DOES NOT EXIST (no ca.pem), 'git config --global http.sslCAInfo' is EMPTY, AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM env is EMPTY. So F3 points git at a CA path it never creates. The BC's 257 tests passed because scenario 68's FAKE-DRIVER models CA materialization but does NOT verify the real container writes the file + sets git trust — a test-fidelity gap. FIX (bc-launcher): F3 must ACTUALLY (a) write the broker MITM CA content to a real file before clone (cf. working operator workaround: 'agent-vault ca fetch > ca.pem'), and (b) configure git to use it (git config http.sslCAInfo / GIT_SSL_CAINFO) OR install into system trust; AND scenario 68 must be SHARPENED to pin the real observable (ca.pem exists + non-empty + git sslCAInfo set + a proxied clone actually succeeds end-to-end), not just the fake-driver model. Clone-path STILL broken on F3 => bootstrap still blocked for clean bring-up. Re-opens lead-uiwu F3. Related: ADR-045/lead-lu91 (CA-inline), lead-uiwu, lead-boa3 (v0.3.34 delivered F1/F2).

title: [bug] bc-container launch clone-path BROKEN (regression): no-repo-url silently yields EMPTY /workspace; root-owned workspace fails non-root clone; agent-vault MITM CA not trusted by clone -> blocks ALL BC bring-up
description:
CRITICAL bootstrap blocker (product authority, 2026-06-28). bc-container launch (shopsystem-bc-launcher) clone path is broken THREE ways; reproduced launching shopsystem-templates. A 5-day-old running BC (messaging) has a correct clone, so this is a REGRESSION in the current bc-base image/launcher (v0.3.33).

FACET 1 — silent empty workspace: 'bc-container launch shopsystem-templates' with NO --repo-url and NO --workspace-mount started the container + agent but left /workspace EMPTY (not a git repo) with NO clone attempt and NO error. The agent then flails on an empty /workspace (no CLAUDE.md). The bc-manifest.yaml carries the remote (https://github.com/dstengle/shopsystem-templates) and its own header says it is 'the source of remote URLs when launching BCs' — so launch MUST resolve the repo URL from the manifest and clone it, or FAIL LOUDLY; it must never silently launch an empty /workspace.

FACET 2 — root-owned workspace blocks non-root clone: with --repo-url passed, the clone is ATTEMPTED but fails: 'git clone failed: Cloning into /workspace ... /workspace/.git: Permission denied'. /workspace is created root:root (drwxr-xr-x), but the agent + clone run as user vscode (uid 1000). The WORKING messaging container has /workspace owned vscode:vscode. Fix: the launcher/bc-base must create /workspace owned by the agent user (uid 1000), not root.

FACET 3 — agent-vault MITM CA not trusted by clone: after chown vscode /workspace, the clone (routed via HTTPS_PROXY=http://av_agt_...@agent-vault:14322) fails 'SSL certificate problem: unable to get local issuer certificate'. git in the container does not trust the agent-vault MITM proxy CA. Fix: materialize the broker MITM CA into the container's git/system trust BEFORE the clone (connects to ADR-045 / lead-lu91 CA-inline contract; the leaf must trust the proxy CA).

OPERATOR WORKAROUND THAT UNBLOCKED templates (all three applied manually): docker exec -u root chown vscode:vscode /workspace; then as vscode: agent-vault ca fetch > /tmp/av-ca.pem; GIT_SSL_CAINFO=/tmp/av-ca.pem git clone <remote> .; then bc-container start-agent (recovered agent against the now-cloned workspace). templates is now running + draining its inbox.

Owning BC: shopsystem-bc-launcher (bc-container launch orchestration + bc-base image workspace setup + CA materialization). NEEDS regression guards for all three facets. BLOCKS all BC bring-up via the clone path => bootstrap-critical. Related: lead-lu91/ADR-045 (CA-inline), lead-ngzl (flagless launch), bring-up-bc skill.
notes:
dispatch request_bugfix -> shopsystem-bc-launcher (lead-uiwu)
DISPATCHED 2026-06-28 (lead-architect): shop-msg send request_bugfix --bc shopsystem-bc-launcher --work-id lead-uiwu, carrying 4 scenarios (65 @bdec2754d9135086 FACET1+; 66 @0b50d090c9cc3c45 FACET1-; 67 @4154b0ea63d0516b FACET2; 68 @0d29c76818a323a1 FACET3) + coexistence enumeration + empirical repro + operator workaround. VEHICLE=request_bugfix: clone-path capability pinned by scn 02 @62e1e005e9ece9f3 and worked 5 days ago (messaging BC) => REGRESSION/tightening of unpinned facets, not net-new. All 4 PO hashes reproduced block-only via scenarios hash; coexist anchors (02/01/bc-manifest06/44-family/templates200) verified live post lead-glel cleanup; phantom 18/19/20 excluded. Read-back OK: message_type=request_bugfix, @bc:shopsystem-bc-launcher on all 4. shop-msg=shopsystem-messaging v0.4.2 (no-clobber). Critical bootstrap blocker.
RECONCILED 2026-06-28 (lead-architect): work_done lead-uiwu accepted. origin/main 4c5d42b, 257 passed +4. All 4 hashes reproduce BLOCK-ONLY via 'scenarios hash' over lead-held features/bc-launcher/65-68: 65=bdec2754d9135086, 66=0b50d090c9cc3c45, 67=4154b0ea63d0516b, 68=0d29c76818a323a1 — all MATCH embedded authoring tags AND work_done. ADDITIVE: 65-68 are net-new files (commit 840f370); coexistence anchor scn 02 @62e1e005e9ece9f3 intact; NO @scenario_hash retired. Per-facet revert-teeth verified RED in work_done (F1+ neutralize _resolve_manifest_remote; F1- silent-success; F2 remove/root chown; F3 CA-after-clone); d64 recursive -R chown invariant preserved. ADR-018 artifact-surface only. BC fix COMPLETE+VERIFIED; delivery (release containing 4c5d42b) tracked on follow-up delivery bead.

title: [bug] lead's own bin/shop-shell missing ADR-045 CA-inline fix -> launched leaf gets path-string trust anchor (MITM-TLS failure); lead-side instance of lead-lu91
description: Function-BLOCKING (product authority flagged 2026-06-27: drift that blocks shop-shell proper function is >= P1). The lead's own committed bin/shop-shell was STALE and lacked the ADR-045 CA-inline fix that lead-lu91 pinned+delivered for adopters: line 73 did 'grep -E ^AGENT_VAULT_[A-Z_]+= .env > --env-file' with NO CA exclusion, so AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM (which .env records as a PATH string) was passed into the launched leaf as the env VALUE -> leaf trust anchor = the literal path string, not the certificate -> silent MITM-TLS validation failure in EVERY leaf this shop-shell launches. This is the lead host's OWN instance of lead-lu91; shop-templates update could not deliver the canonical fix because bin/shop-shell is drift-protected (see lead-q14e). FIX APPLIED (this commit): surgically ported the canonical v0.45.0 CA-inline block into bin/shop-shell — (1) exclude AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM from the --env-file grep, (2) read the CA file CONTENT into the launcher PROCESS env (export AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM=$(cat file)), (3) pass -e AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM on BOTH docker runs. Slug-neutral port, no ops-coordinates dependency. bash -n clean. VERIFICATION STILL NEEDED (operator, ADR-018-empirical, cannot run a leaf launch from here): launch via shop-shell and confirm in the leaf that 'cat ~/.config/agent-vault/ca.pem' (and NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS) begins with -----BEGIN CERTIFICATE----- and MITM-TLS validates. title: [bug] bd update --claim with --set-metadata/--append-notes clobbers title/type/priority/description (shop-msg bd-integration); recurring, needs source fix
description: Recurring tooling bug. When the lead-architect runs 'bd update <id> --claim' combined with --set-metadata/--append-notes during a request_bugfix dispatch, the bead's title/type/priority/description get CLOBBERED to an auto-synthesized 'dispatch request_bugfix -> ...' stub (type bug->task, priority P1->P2, description replaced). Observed THREE times now: lead-lu91 (see its NOTES 'Field-restore: title/type/priority/description were clobbered to a dispatch stub by the shop-msg bd-integration and restored here'), and both lead-b6f2 and lead-dui6 this session (2026-06-27). Each time the architect detected and restored the four fields verbatim, but the restore is manual toil and a data-loss risk if missed. ROOT CAUSE (suspected): the shop-msg bd-integration / bd update path writes a synthesized dispatch-stub record over the existing bead instead of a metadata-only/notes-only patch. FIX: make the dispatch-side bd update a strict additive patch (metadata + notes only), never overwriting title/type/priority/description. Likely owned by the bd-dolt / shop-msg integration (cf. lead-kf36 bd-fork track). Workaround until fixed: capture the four fields BEFORE any --claim+metadata update and restore after.
notes:
OWNER VERDICT (lead-architect, 2026-06-27): shopsystem-messaging BC (shop-msg bd-integration). EMPIRICAL EXPERIMENT: created throwaway bead lead-r11o (bug/P1/distinctive title+desc), ran DIRECT 'bd update lead-r11o --claim --set-metadata probe_key=probe_val --append-notes ...' (NOT via shop-msg). RESULT: NO clobber — title/type=bug/P1/description all intact; only status->in_progress, assignee, notes, metadata changed as intended. Throwaway deleted. CONCLUSION: the bd/bd-dolt write path is INNOCENT (combines with prior evidence that plain 'bd update --priority 1' also did not clobber). The clobber occurs ONLY when triggered through shop-msg send, so root cause = shop-msg bd-integration in shopsystem-messaging (NOT the bd-fork track, contrast lead-kf36). MECHANISM (suspected, per scenario features/templates/119): shop-msg send Step-1 'bd create --metadata' upserts a pre-existing work_id bead with a synthesized dispatch stub, overwriting the 4 identity fields. ROUTE: request_bugfix to shopsystem-messaging, BUT BLOCKED on a PO-authored pinning scenario first — existing scenario 119 pins 'bd create' at Step 1 (hash e73037244a92b1a4) and the 3-step protocol (hash 98c6065aca2b54c6) but does NOT pin the additive-only invariant; in fact 119 is the OPPOSITE behavior for pre-existing beads. PO must author: 'when the work_id bead already exists, the dispatch-side bd write is a strict additive metadata/notes-only patch; title/type/priority/description are NEVER overwritten.' The eventual request_bugfix must enumerate conflicting hashes e73037244a92b1a4 and 98c6065aca2b54c6 (amend/coexist). NOT dispatched now (no pinning scenario exists). NEXT STEP: PO round-trip to author the pinning scenario, then architect dispatches request_bugfix to shopsystem-messaging. INTERIM lead-side hardening filed separately.
FIX DISPATCHED (lead-architect, 2026-06-27): request_bugfix dispatched to shopsystem-messaging via work_id lead-yyr9, carrying PO scenario 206 @scenario_hash:ea2d453f88110e82 (strict additive patch on pre-existing work_id bead; never clobbers title/type/priority/description). Conflicting-hash decision: e73037244a92b1a4 coexists unchanged; 98c6065aca2b54c6 amended/scoped (Step-1-create qualified to absent-bead case, not superseded). Confirmed live by this very dispatch: lead-yyr9 was clobbered (title->stub, P1->P2, description dropped) and restored verbatim. Awaiting shopsystem-messaging work_done; reconcile on arrival.
SOURCE FIX DELIVERED AT MESSAGING (lead-architect reconcile, 2026-06-27): the clobber root fix landed on shopsystem-messaging origin/main at 0329a2b via lead-yyr9 — shop-msg send dispatch-side bd write is now a strict additive metadata/notes-only patch (scenario 206 @scenario_hash:ea2d453f88110e82, verified on lead artifact surface; 98c6065aca2b54c6 amended->8edfb82fc6a07184 per BC; e73037244a92b1a4 untouched). 444 tests pass; revert-teeth confirmed. WHAT REMAINS: the fix is NOT yet in the LEAD's running shop-msg (installed shopsystem-messaging v0.4.0 at /usr/local/bin/shop-msg lacks 0329a2b) — proven empirically: the lead-yyr9 dispatch via that v0.4.0 STILL clobbered. So the clobber remains LIVE in this lead shop until a messaging release containing 0329a2b is cut AND the lead upgrades. Tracked by lead-zqub (now blocks this bead). Keep IN_PROGRESS; do NOT close until lead-zqub lands and no-clobber is confirmed live. Interim capture/restore SOP (lead-od0o) stays in force until then.
2026-06-28 LEAD UPGRADED to shopsystem-messaging v0.4.2 (contains the additive _patch_dispatch_bead fix; lead-zqub delivery CLOSED). Fix installed by verified construction. STAYS OPEN pending one behavioral LIVE no-clobber confirmation: the NEXT real 'shop-msg send' onto a pre-existing work_id bead must leave title/type/priority/description intact WITHOUT the capture/restore SOP. Plan: on the next dispatch, apply the lead-od0o SOP one last time as a SAFETY net but compare pre/post — if identity survives natively, retire lead-od0o and close this.
close_reason: RESOLVED + LIVE-CONFIRMED. Source fix shipped in shopsystem-messaging v0.4.2 (additive _patch_dispatch_bead, scenario 206); lead upgraded 0.4.0->0.4.2 (lead-zqub). Behavioral confirmation: the first real shop-msg send post-upgrade (lead-ngzl request_bugfix to bc-launcher, 2026-06-28) did NOT clobber the work_id bead — title/type/priority/description all intact with NO capture/restore needed. The recurring identity-field clobber is gone.

title: Bootstrap: approve-claude token step writes a BLANK Claude credential (manual web-interface entry works)
description: Empirical finding from product-authority bootstrap run (2026-06-27). The 'approve-claude' token step (logic lives in bin/agent-vault-provision per grep on the lead host; NOT a separate script) does not put in a valid token — the resulting credential looks BLANK. Doing the same approval MANUALLY through the web interface works fine, which isolates the defect to the script's token-capture/writeback, not the upstream issuance. SYMPTOM (product authority): credential comes out empty after the scripted approve-claude flow. FIX DIRECTION: trace the approve-claude token capture in bin/agent-vault-provision — the token is not being captured/written into the credential value (suspect a blank/empty-string writeback, cf. the path-vs-inline class of bug in lead-lu91). Owning source: shopsystem-templates (rendered bin/* scripts). Related: lead-mdng (agent-vault substrate initiative), lead-lu91 (sibling provision/writeback contract bug). Architect verifies pre-state empirically + applies discriminator after PO pins the scenario.

Discriminator: capability exists (bin/agent-vault-provision approve-claude logic, shop-templates-rendered, provision-level pinned by agent-vault-broker/13) but the non-empty writeback is unpinned/broken -> request_bugfix. title: Bootstrap: leaf agent gets no dummy GH credential — gh fails until operator exports GH_TOKEN=placeholder
description: Empirical finding from product-authority bootstrap run (2026-06-27). In the agent-vault proxy model the proxy injects the REAL credential on the wire, so the leaf only needs a non-empty placeholder token present in env for gh to attempt the call. The bootstrap/provision path does NOT set up any dummy GH credential, so gh fails in the freshly-launched leaf agent. WORKAROUND (operator-confirmed working): export GH_TOKEN=placeholder => gh works fine. FIX DIRECTION: the credential-bootstrap path (bin/agent-vault-provision and/or the rendered leaf env) should seed a dummy GH_TOKEN (e.g. 'placeholder') so the proxy can swap in the real credential. Owning source: shopsystem-templates (bin/* are lead-repo scripts rendered from templates). Related: lead-h755 (gh presence-on-PATH, distinct), lead-mdng (agent-vault substrate initiative). Discriminator/dispatch is architect's call after PO pins the scenario.
notes: 2026-06-27 architect dispatch: request_bugfix -> shopsystem-templates, work_id lead-b6f2, reproducing @scenario_hash:0789db8bb7f3bc73 (verified block-only via scenarios hash CLI; reproduces PO-authored tag). Discriminator: capability exists (bin/shop-shell, shop-templates-rendered, pinned by templates/172) but unpinned/broken -> request_bugfix. Owning BC justification: scenario 201 pins the bin/shop-shell body (shop-templates surface); precedent scenario 200/lead-lu91 routed the shop-shell bringup transport to shopsystem-templates. Enumeration: retires/supersedes nothing; coexists with templates/172 @scenario_hash:725562869d9df919 (no-host-cred-mount, env vars are not cred mounts). Residual cross-BC question encoded in dispatch: propagation of GH_TOKEN through inner 'bc-container launch'/bc-base entrypoint is shopsystem-bc-launcher's surface; BC instructed to file a sibling bc-launcher bugfix if the inner launch drops GH_TOKEN. Verified deposited via shop-msg read inbox.
close_reason: gh dummy-credential fix complete at templates origin/main 6989f8e: rendered bin/shop-shell seeds a non-empty placeholder GH_TOKEN (${GH_TOKEN:-gh-broker-placeholder-token}) into the leaf launch env-file so gh works out of the box (broker substitutes the real token on the wire). Scenario 0789db8bb7f3bc73 verified (teeth: seed-removed/empty-default both fail; no real-PAT prefix), coexists 172, additive (0 deletions). Lead-side delivery to the lead's drifted bin/shop-shell rides the re-pour reconciliation (lead-q14e) once a templates release ships it. BC flagged a pre-existing unrelated red (bc-base literal generification, their bd tmpl-2iu) — not part of this work.
