title: [bug] AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM path-vs-inline contract mismatch: leaf-agent trust file contains the literal path string 'agent-vault-ca.pem' instead of the cert -> subtle SSL failure
description:
Empirical finding from product-authority bootstrap test (testproduct3, 2026-06-27). Subtle SSL error in the LAUNCHED LEAF AGENT (not the lead). Root cause: AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM has TWO incompatible conventions across the bringup chain.

SYMPTOM (product authority): cat ~/.config/agent-vault/ca.pem in the leaf agent => prints the literal string 'agent-vault-ca.pem' (a FILENAME), not a -----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----. The trust anchor is the path string, so MITM-proxy TLS validation fails subtly.

ROOT CAUSE (path-vs-inline mismatch, traced on the lead host):
1. bin/agent-vault-provision L237-239 sets CA_PEM_FILE=agent-vault-ca.pem, fetches the real cert into that FILE, and L257/L268 writes AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM=<PATH> ('agent-vault-ca.pem') into .env. Its OWN comment (L231-236) flags exactly this risk: 'we point AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM at that PATH ... (If the launch path ever expects inline PEM instead, switch this to capture the PEM into the value with proper escaping; path is the safer default.)'
2. bin/shop-shell L73 does grep -E '^AGENT_VAULT_[A-Z_]+=' .env > --env-file, so the PATH STRING is passed into the container as the env VALUE.
3. The bc-base/bc-lead entrypoint (BC source; per PDR-020 'AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM-as-env materialized by the bc-base entrypoint' + findings/dummyco-spike-iter-7.md L95: 'AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM is carried INLINE (PEM text, not a path)') treats the value as INLINE PEM and writes it verbatim into the trust file => trust file = 'agent-vault-ca.pem'.

So provision emits a PATH; the entrypoint consumes INLINE. Two ends, two contracts.

EVIDENCE (lead-side, ADR-018 artifact surface): the LEAD's trust is correct (agent-vault ca fetch over http://agent-vault:14321 == /home/vscode/.agent-vault/mitm-ca.pem, sha256 0E:14:4B:49:...:5B:30, both match) precisely because the lead's AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM is UNSET; the leaf gets the path-string env and breaks. findings/dummyco-spike-iter-7.md shows INLINE is the empirically-working form.

DECISION NEEDED (architect): pick the CANONICAL AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM contract and align BOTH ends.
- If INLINE (spike-validated, entrypoint's current expectation): fix bin/agent-vault-provision to write the PEM CONTENT into AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM (newline handling), NOT the path. Provision side is the change. (Likely the right call.)
- If PATH: the entrypoint must cp the file at that path (and shop-shell must ensure the file resolves inside the container, e.g. via the REPO_ROOT mount/CWD); entrypoint side is the change.
Note: provision/shop-shell are lead-repo scripts rendered from shopsystem-templates; the entrypoint is shopsystem-bc-launcher (bc-base/bc-lead). The fix spans whichever end loses the contract decision. May warrant an ADR pinning the AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM contract.

IMMEDIATE OPERATOR WORKAROUND (docker-free; ca fetch is plain HTTP to :14321, works despite broken trust): in the leaf agent, agent-vault ca fetch > "$NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS" (all 6 trust env vars point at one file) AND agent-vault ca fetch > ~/.config/agent-vault/ca.pem, then restart the agent process.

---
ARCHITECT RESOLUTION (2026-06-27): contract decided = INLINE PEM content with real internal newlines (ADR-045). Consumer (bc-launcher) already correct per lead-b14a; fix is templates-side. PO scenario 200 (@scenario_hash:7ce09202755b0503) authored; request_bugfix dispatched to shopsystem-templates on work_id lead-lu91. See NOTES + METADATA. (Field-restore: title/type/priority/description were clobbered to a dispatch stub by the shop-msg bd-integration and restored here.)
notes: INVESTIGATED 2026-06-30: templates fix SHIPPED + reconciled — contract ADR-045 (inline PEM), PO scenario 200 (7ce09202755b0503), request_bugfix->shopsystem-templates delivered v0.45.0, baked bc-launcher v0.3.32; consumer side settled by lead-b14a (closed). Remaining open_pending: (1) bc-container-ca-sourcing-confirmation — MOOT, resolved-by-lead-b14a (no nudge needed per ADR-045); (2) testproduct3-live-reverify — genuine OPERATOR action (re-pour/re-bootstrap testproduct3, verify leaf trust), env-gated. No lead authoring/dispatch remains. Operator-verification bucket. p83k is the lead-host leg of the same root cause (distinct artifact/verify target) — NOT merged, cross-linked. Close once testproduct3 re-verify done or waived.

title: Release shop-templates carrying lead-ddb2 shop-shell/bootstrap fix (8a27145) + deliver to operator (re-pour path); evaluate bc-launcher bump
description:
DELIVERY-PATH ANALYSIS for the lead-ddb2 shop-shell/bootstrap fix (follow-up to reconciled lead-ddb2 work_done).

WHAT IS DONE: shopsystem-templates fixed both defects at the canonical TEMPLATE SOURCE
and the fix is on templates origin/main at commit 8a27145:
  - Defect A (scenario 198, @scenario_hash:84a32b05666d9e82): bin/shop-shell template now
    mirrors --group-add "$SOCKET_GID" into the step-(4) ATTACH docker run (launch/attach
    socket-group symmetry).
  - Defect B (scenario 199, @scenario_hash:72245934ce007af6): bootstrap completion message
    now names the re-entry command ./bin/shop-shell ("ENTER THE LEAD SESSION — run
    ./bin/shop-shell ...").

WHY THE OPERATOR IS NOT YET FIXED (delivery gap):
(a) The fix is on templates origin/main 8a27145 but is NOT YET in a cut shop-templates
    release/tag, and is NOT YET baked into any bc-launcher image. bin/shop-shell and the
    bootstrap completion message are RENDERED artifacts poured from shopsystem-templates at
    bootstrap/update time (ADR-018). The operator's ALREADY-RENDERED testproduct3/bin/shop-shell
    therefore will NOT receive the fix until BOTH:
      1. shop-templates cuts a release/tag carrying 8a27145, AND
      2. the operator re-pours (`shop-templates update`) or re-bootstraps testproduct3.
    Until then testproduct3 keeps the asymmetric (broken) rendered script.

(b) bc-launcher image bump — is it ALSO needed for THESE two fixes?
    Reasoning: both fixed surfaces are HOST-RENDERED PRODUCT-REPO artifacts, not image-baked:
      - Defect A's --group-add "$SOCKET_GID" is a flag on the `docker run` command CONSTRUCTED
        BY the host-run bin/shop-shell script. The flag is applied at the host docker invocation
        that launches/attaches the bc-lead image; it is NOT baked into the image. So the bc-lead/
        bc-launcher image does not carry that surface — the fix lands purely by re-pouring the
        rendered host script.
      - Defect B's completion message is printed by the host-run bootstrap/footing flow, also a
        rendered product-repo artifact, not image content.
    CONCLUSION: no bc-launcher image bump is required to deliver lead-ddb2's two fixes — the
    delivery path is "cut shop-templates release carrying 8a27145, then re-pour/re-bootstrap".
    CAVEAT (stated because the lead cannot git-observe/read BC or image internals, ADR-018):
    if a future audit shows any of the fixed surface is image-baked, scope adds a bc-launcher
    bump AFTER the shop-templates release. Bead is scoped conservatively to: (1) cut shop-templates
    release carrying 8a27145; (2) evaluate whether a bc-launcher bump is also needed (expected: no,
    per the host-rendered reasoning above); (3) deliver to operator via re-pour/re-bootstrap.

IMMEDIATE OPERATOR WORKAROUND (no release needed to unblock the current test):
    Manually add  --group-add "$SOCKET_GID"  to the step-(4) ATTACH `docker run` block in
    testproduct3/bin/shop-shell (around lines ~116-122, mirroring the launcher block ~line 102),
    to clear the docker.sock EACCES and proceed with the current test without waiting for a release.

REF: lead-ddb2 (reconciled). templates origin/main HEAD at work_done: 8a27145.

DISPATCH (lead-ej75): request_maintenance -> shopsystem-templates on 2026-06-27. Asked BC to cut+push
an annotated release tag (expected v0.44.0) at origin/main HEAD 8a27145, carrying lead-vme1 (d1db9b9,
skills provenance marker) + lead-ddb2 (8a27145, shop-shell socket-group symmetry + bootstrap re-entry).
Pre-state verified via git remote/compare API (ADR-018, no clone): 8a27145=origin/main HEAD; d1db9b9 is
the merge-base/ancestor of 8a27145 (ahead_by 3); prior release v0.43.0 = tag obj 2f01e37 / commit
ece0dca, ancestor of HEAD (ahead_by 6) -> clean increment. Awaiting BC work_done (tag->commit mapping
+ publish/CI conclusion). Do NOT close until reconciled.
close_reason: RECONCILED (lead-architect, artifact-surface only per ADR-018). request_maintenance v0.44.0 release work_done verified: (1) git ls-remote --tags shopsystem-templates -> annotated tag v0.44.0 (tagobj 9339466) dereferences (^{}) to commit 475ba7cdb19ebe726833706ed6a0abc93c39a5f4 == claimed 475ba7c. (2) GitHub compare API 8a27145...475ba7c -> status=ahead, behind_by=0, ahead_by=2 -> 8a27145 IS an ancestor of 475ba7c (merge-base --is-ancestor semantics hold; no divergence). NOTE: BC said 'one commit on top of 8a27145' but compare shows 2 release commits (pyproject bump + cut) -- chain/ancestry/tag mapping still hold. (3) Actions run 28291860201 workflow=release head_branch=v0.44.0 head_sha=475ba7c status=completed conclusion=success (independently confirmed via API). (4) Release object v0.44.0 draft=false prerelease=false target_commitish=main published 2026-06-27. scenario_hashes=[] (flat maintenance, no register to reconcile). Both fixes delivered since v0.43.0: lead-vme1 skills provenance marker (d1db9b9) + lead-ddb2 shop-shell socket-group symmetry/bootstrap re-entry (8a27145, scenarios 198/199). version-equals-tag guard (scenario 192) consistent with the bump commit. title: Mark restored LOCAL PM/discovery skills with .provenance LOCAL markers (PDR-023 sequencing)
description:
Follow-up to lead-vme1 (shopsystem-templates request_bugfix; PDR-023 skills provenance marker). Now that the pour honors per-skill .provenance markers (CANONICAL => re-poured byte-for-byte + stale pruned; LOCAL => never touched), the restored PM/discovery skills in THIS lead repo's .claude/skills/ must each get a LOCAL .provenance marker so the next re-pour preserves them.

Skills to mark LOCAL (no .provenance marker exists on any of them today):
- jobs-to-be-done
- problem-framing-canvas
- opportunity-solution-tree
- customer-journey-map
- company-research
- work-splitting
- README.md (skills README) -- ensure it is not clobbered/pruned

Do NOT mark bring-up-bc / create-bc: those are CANONICAL members and receive their CANONICAL .provenance marker from package data at pour (scenario 197, hash 9dd2f8b5bdcd22f0).

Hardening note: until these LOCAL skills carry a marker, the backward-compat legacy by-name transition still classifies them (they are absent from canonical package data, so they survive a re-pour for now). The explicit LOCAL marker is the durable hardening that removes reliance on the one-transition legacy path and protects any that collide with a future canonical name.

Refs: lead-vme1, PDR-023 point 3 (EXPERIMENT lifecycle, scenario 195 / e6bb8f6846518c3c). templates origin/main d1db9b9.
close_reason: Created LOCAL .provenance markers (bare token line) for the 6 restored PM/discovery skills (jobs-to-be-done, problem-framing-canvas, opportunity-solution-tree, customer-journey-map, company-research, work-splitting). bring-up-bc/create-bc left unmarked (CANONICAL from package data at pour, scenario 197). README.md is a top-level file not a skill dir, no marker applies; survives via non-member preservation. PDR-023 sequencing satisfied: pour mechanism (lead-vme1) + LOCAL marking now both landed.

title: [bug] shop-shell attach step missing --group-add SOCKET_GID -> docker.sock permission denied; + bootstrap returns to host with no re-entry guidance
description:
Empirical finding from product-authority bootstrap test of product slug 'testproduct3' (2026-06-27).

OBSERVED (two distinct defects):

(A) DOCKER.SOCK PERMISSION DENIED at attach. Running bin/shop-shell brought up agent-vault + postgres, started bc-<slug>-lead, started tmux 'agent', injected the lead session-start prompt — then: 'permission denied while trying to connect to the docker API at unix:///var/run/docker.sock'. ROOT CAUSE (grounded in the rendered bin/shop-shell, lead-repo copy verbatim): ASYMMETRY between step (3) launcher and step (4) attach. Step (3) docker run (bin/shop-shell:99-112) grants socket-group access via --group-add "$SOCKET_GID" (line 102; SOCKET_GID resolved at line 83 via stat -L -c '%g' /var/run/docker.sock). Step (4) the attach docker run (bin/shop-shell:116-122) MOUNTS the socket (line 119: -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock) but is MISSING --group-add "$SOCKET_GID". So the attach container's non-root user is not in the socket's owning group -> EACCES on the docker API. FIX: add --group-add "$SOCKET_GID" to the step-(4) attach docker run (mirror line 102 into the 116-122 block).

(B) BOOTSTRAP RETURNS TO HOST WITH NO RE-ENTRY GUIDANCE. bootstrap ran, then returned to the host shell saying only to 'start prompting' — it did NOT instruct the operator to re-enter the agent/lead session (e.g. run bin/shop-shell). Bootstrap-orchestrator messaging gap (Brief 008 single-container-bootstrap-orchestrator). Operator was left at host shell with no documented next step into the brokered session.

PROVENANCE: bin/shop-shell is a RENDERED artifact (rendered from shopsystem-templates at bootstrap, ADR-018) — the canonical fix belongs at the TEMPLATE SOURCE in shopsystem-templates, not a one-off edit in this lead repo (a local edit would not fix testproduct3 or future products). Relates lead-qi0q (bc-base docker client + socket-mount-decision scenarios) and lead-l7uz WS-2 bootstrap path (dockersocket_dispatch_bead lead-wdvx).

ROUTING: PO to author/sharpen scenarios pinning (A) attach grants socket-group access and (B) bootstrap emits explicit re-entry instruction; Architect to verify templates pre-state empirically + dispatch request_bugfix to shopsystem-templates.

DISPATCH (2026-06-27): request_bugfix dispatched to shopsystem-templates carrying scenarios 198 (@scenario_hash:84a32b05666d9e82, Defect A socket-group launch/attach symmetry) + 199 (@scenario_hash:72245934ce007af6, Defect B explicit bin/shop-shell re-entry message). ADDITIVE to scenarios 172/184/187 (no supersession). Awaiting BC work_done.
close_reason: RECONCILED (lead-architect, 2026-06-27). work_done from shopsystem-templates verified against the lead artifact surface (ADR-018 D1/D2; no BC source read/run). HASH MATCH (block-only canonicalization via installed scenarios CLI): scenario 198 on-disk recompute 84a32b05666d9e82 == on-disk @scenario_hash tag == work_done scenario_hash; scenario 199 on-disk recompute 72245934ce007af6 == on-disk @scenario_hash tag == work_done scenario_hash. Both three-way exact. ADDITIVE SCOPE CONFIRMED: 172 (725562869d9df919), 184 (e69c18dd25104b5e/fec7842e905761c8/44d534b52c4925e2), 187 (b05fff82d0d10f4a/3646efa06051fcac/c15aeb90b21357e5) all present with unchanged hashes; 198/199 are net-new pins, no retirement/supersession, no lead-side feature deletion needed. Demonstration (BC gated-loop, accepted as-is): test-first 3 scenario-instances red, full suite 496 passed / 12 pre-existing failures / zero new, on templates origin/main 8a27145. NOT lead-verifiable (no live docker/broker on lead host; per work_done PROOF NOTE the LEAD live-verifies attach no longer EACCESes at re-pour time): rendered-body --group-add symmetry + completion text are BC body-assertions. DELIVERY-PATH follow-up filed as lead-ej75 (P1, discovered-from lead-ddb2): cut shop-templates release carrying 8a27145 + re-pour/re-bootstrap operator; evaluate bc-launcher bump (expected not needed — both fixed surfaces are host-rendered, not image-baked). Operator immediate workaround recorded in lead-ej75: manually add --group-add "$SOCKET_GID" to testproduct3/bin/shop-shell attach docker run (~lines 116-122). Outbox row consumed.

title: Skills provenance + pour-preserves-local: canonical-vs-local marker so re-pour/sweep never clobbers local lead skills (PM discovery skills lost twice)
description:
PRODUCT-AUTHORITY DIRECTIVE (2026-06-27): the discovery/PM skills (jobs-to-be-done, problem-framing-canvas, opportunity-solution-tree, customer-journey-map, company-research, work-splitting, README) were collaterally DELETED twice — v0.13.0 re-pour (b573851) + 84df061 backlog sweep — because they live ONLY in the lead working tree, never in templates, and the pour/sweep didn't distinguish framework skills from local experiments. RESTORED to lead at 5671c96 (live). Only bring-up-bc + create-bc are canonical (templates/lead_skills). EMPIRICAL (templates HEAD ece0dca): cli.py _mirror_skills (L506, lead-1e8d, supersedes scenario 159/9a064e8f6ed915e3) ALREADY preserves unmanaged skills — but the membership test is BY NAME (managed_members = canonical top-level dir names); no provenance MARKER exists, so the canonical-vs-local distinction is fragile (a local skill sharing a canonical name would be clobbered) and not agent/human-discoverable. DECISION (product authority): (1) re-pour OVERWRITES ONLY CANONICAL skills; LOCAL skills survive every re-pour; (2) a provenance MARKER FILE per skill declares CANONICAL vs LOCAL (the pour decides what to overwrite from the marker, not by name); (3) support EXPERIMENT (drop a local skill, it persists), durable LOCAL skills, and a MIGRATION PATH (graduate local->canonical: add to templates, flip the marker). DELIVERABLES: PDR-023 + PO scenarios 193-197 + dispatch to shopsystem-templates (cli.py skill-render change + the canonical marker for bring-up-bc/create-bc). Relates lead-22x1 (WS-7 skills corpus). SEQUENCING: pour/marker ships FIRST, THEN mark the restored PM skills 'local'.

DISPATCH (2026-06-27): request_bugfix -> shopsystem-templates, work_id lead-vme1, scenarios 193-197 (hashes in metadata), supersedes scenario 159 (9a064e8f6ed915e3). Dispatched, awaiting BC work_done. See dispatch_* metadata keys.
close_reason: Reconciled work_done from shopsystem-templates (templates origin/main d1db9b9). All 5 delivered scenarios verified on lead features/templates: 193=4eb7078dc04b056e, 194=2763eff7ff73d7be, 195=e6bb8f6846518c3c, 196=5abc22575831b51d, 197=9dd2f8b5bdcd22f0 -- scenarios hash recompute MATCHES both the work_done scenario_hashes list and the on-disk @scenario_hash tags. Additive 162/163/164 still green (e803b4c9/4a008549/a14e5a0a match). SUPERSESSION: scenario 159 (9a064e8f6ed915e3) retired on lead side -- deleted features/templates/159-*.gherkin; no live @scenario_hash:9a064e8f6ed915e3 tag remains (193/194 reference it only in comments). Follow-up bead lead-pgi3 filed (P1) for LOCAL .provenance marking of restored PM/discovery skills per PDR-023 sequencing. Contract-surface verification only per ADR-018; CI 493 pass/12 pre-existing-fail and in-cli.py mechanism are the BC's gated-loop demonstration, not lead-verified. Router owns git push.

title: DECIDE+PDR: footing DELEGATES credential provisioning to bin/agent-vault-provision rather than inlining it (collapses 3 regressions + subsumes fleet-token/.env-writeback gap) (lead-l7uz)
description: ARCHITECTURE/DECOMPOSITION DECISION (lead-architect call; NOT a scenario). EVIDENCE: footing REIMPLEMENTS provisioning that bin/agent-vault-provision ALREADY does correctly via the broker-local docker-exec mechanism — auth register -> vault create -> credential set -> service add -> agent create <slug>-fleet --token-only -> .env writeback. footing has now had THREE consecutive regressions reinventing this: lead-h2rq/lead-4sg9 (vault create), lead-pdsd/lead-0j60 (PAT store, owner-remote -> Member-role abort). DECISION (my call): footing should DELEGATE credential provisioning to bin/agent-vault-provision rather than inline it — the pb3b/lead-0j60 observable (broker-local PAT round-trip) holds either way, but delegation ELIMINATES THE WHOLE REGRESSION CLASS and likely SUBSUMES the currently-missing fleet-agent-token + services + .env-writeback steps (provision does all that; footing currently does not). FOLLOW-UP: draft a PDR pinning 'footing delegates the broker-local provisioning sequence to bin/agent-vault-provision (single owner of credential ops); footing orchestrates the runway around it (fork-guard, infra-up, network-attach, beads/remotes, push) but does NOT reimplement provisioning.' Then a request_bugfix/assign_scenarios dispatch to shopsystem-templates to refactor footing to delegate + pin the delegation + the now-covered fleet-token/.env-writeback. Sequence AFTER lead-0j60 lands (don't churn the in-flight fix). Parent lead-l7uz.
notes: lead-yudo COMPLETE: Phase A done+verified (v0.41.0), Phase B no-op (no dead code), lead-542d folded into PDR-022. Closing.
close_reason: Closed

title: ADR/PDR addendum: local-first (PDR-021 D4) scope BOUNDARY — agent-vault credential set/get are broker-local/docker-exec ONLY, NOT remote-session reachable (lead-l7uz)
description: ARCHITECTURE DECISION (lead-architect call; NOT a scenario). LIVE-CONFIRMED durable fact (agent-vault 0.32.0, lead-reproduced): 'vault credential set'/'get' FAIL 'Member role required' via BOTH the owner remote vault-scoped session AND a fleet agent token; they WORK ONLY broker-locally via docker exec into the broker (the mechanism bin/agent-vault-provision uses for ALL credential ops). IMPLICATION: PDR-021 D4's local-first posture (run agent-vault on the lead host against AGENT_VAULT_ADDR rather than docker exec) CANNOT cover credential provisioning — credential set/get are a CARVED-OUT exception that MUST use the broker-local docker-exec path. DECISION: draft a PDR-021 addendum (or a small companion ADR) pinning the local-first SCOPE BOUNDARY: local-first applies to auth/vault-token/read ops that the remote session supports, but credential set/get (and anything requiring Member role the remote session lacks) are docker-exec-only. This is hard-to-reverse + cross-cutting (touches footing, provision, every credential op) -> ADR-tier. Anchor to PDR-021, lead-mrn2 (local-first), lead-0j60 (the regression that surfaced it). Lead-architect drafts; not BC work. Parent lead-l7uz.
notes: Folded into PDR-022 D3 (local-first scope boundary: credential ops are broker-local/docker-exec only). Closed.
close_reason: Closed

title: bc-lead image: USER=vscode cannot access the mounted docker socket (group 984) — docker compose / bc-container fail for the adopter at INSTALL §3
description: Found testing the cold INSTALL flow on bc-lead:latest (v0.3.10) 2026-06-24. After lead-fwrx flipped the default USER to vscode (uid 1000, groups 1000/998/999), an in-container 'docker ps'/'docker compose up' fails: the mounted /var/run/docker.sock is root:984 and vscode is not in group 984 -> DOCKER_DENIED. INSTALL §1 drops the adopter into the container as vscode; §3 'docker compose up -d postgres agent-vault' is run there and FAILS. The prior root-default image worked only because root owns the socket. The USER=vscode fix (needed for the baked ~/.claude state) regressed docker-socket access. FIX (bc-lead image/entrypoint, shopsystem-bc-launcher): grant the default vscode user access to the docker socket — robustly via a root entrypoint that detects the socket's gid, adds vscode to a group with that gid (host gid varies), then drops to vscode (gosu/su-exec) for the agent — so BOTH docker access AND the vscode-owned baked ~/.claude hold. Compose with lead-fwrx (do not revert to root-default). Route: shopsystem-bc-launcher (docker/bc-lead + entrypoint). Cross-ref lead-fwrx, lead-t3dy, INSTALL.md §3.
notes: RESOLVED (docker-socket) via INSTALL §1 doc fix — NO image change needed: 'docker run --group-add $(stat -c %g /var/run/docker.sock) ...' puts vscode in the host socket group; VERIFIED -> vscode gets DOCKER_OK. The bind-mount /work-writability for host-uid!=1000 is noted as an INSTALL caveat (chown 1000:1000). Downgraded from an entrypoint redesign; lead-fwrx USER=vscode stands.
close_reason: Closed

title: bc-lead + bc-base images default to USER=root (should be vscode) — breaks baked ~/.claude onboarding/credential state and adopter shell
description: Found by the product authority (2026-06-24) + confirmed: docker inspect ghcr.io/dstengle/shopsystem-bc-lead:latest AND ...bc-base:latest both report Config.User=root. The bc-base Dockerfile installs Claude Code + bakes the synthetic logged-in state (~/.claude/.credentials.json + ~/.claude.json with bypassPermissionsModeAccepted/lastOnboardingVersion) AS THE vscode USER (HOME=/home/vscode). When a container starts as root, HOME=/root and the baked vscode state is NOT found -> claude boots to first-run onboarding/sign-in instead of straight-to-agent (a likely contributor to launch/bootstrap hangs), plus general adopter-shell breakage (the INSTALL 'docker run ... bash' drops the adopter in as root, not the intended vscode). FIX: the published images (bc-base + bc-lead, built by shopsystem-bc-launcher docker/bc-base/Dockerfile + docker/bc-lead/Dockerfile via publish-bc-base.yml) must default to USER vscode (uid 1000) so the running user matches the baked HOME/.claude state. PIN: docker inspect Config.User == vscode; docker run --rm whoami == vscode; the baked ~/.claude resolves for the running user. Route: shopsystem-bc-launcher. Cross-ref lead-c713 (the onboarding-prompt readiness class), INSTALL.md.
notes: CLEARED via lead-fwrx (bc-launcher main 406e87c): bc-base + bc-lead default USER vscode. Adopter shell + baked ~/.claude state now match the running user. Needs bc-launcher release + image rebuild to reach published :latest.
close_reason: Closed

title: shop-templates bootstrap: two adopter-blocking bugs — (1) still emits obsolete Dockerfile.<slug>-shell; (2) hangs on a credentialed 'bd dolt push' smoke-test at scaffold time (adopter has no creds yet)
description: Found by the product authority running the INSTALL.md cold-bootstrap (2026-06-24), confirmed empirically on the lead host. BUG 1 (obsolete artifact): bootstrap's ops-template list (shop_templates/cli.py:~354 OPS_TEMPLATES) still renders Dockerfile.<slug>-shell (verified: a bootstrap --target wrote Dockerfile.testxyz-shell, 1819B). This is obsolete — per the converged shop-shell (PDR-020/ADR-028; lead-i6yg/lead-6ox4 retired Dockerfile.shopsystem-shell), the lead runs on the bc-lead image via bin/shop-shell; no per-product shell Dockerfile is built. FIX: stop emitting Dockerfile.<slug>-shell from bootstrap (remove the ops-template entry); keep bin/shop-shell + compose.yaml. BUG 2 (credential chicken-and-egg hang): bootstrap runs a LIVE 'bd dolt push' smoke-test against git+https://github.com/dstengle/<shop>-beads.git (observed: 'bd dolt push smoke-test success against ... testxyz-beads.git' — succeeded on the lead host because it HAS proxied GitHub creds). A fresh ADOPTER at INSTALL §1 has NO credentials yet (the credential/broker gate is §3, AFTER bootstrap) -> the push hangs on git auth with NO feedback (exactly the product authority's report + their 'is it expecting credentials it doesn't have yet?'). FIX: scaffolding must complete CREDENTIAL-FREE — make the dolt smoke-test offline/local-only or skippable, or fail-fast with a clear message; bootstrap must not require GitHub push creds the adopter cannot have yet. Both are shop_templates/cli.py bootstrap bugs. Route: shopsystem-templates. Cross-ref lead-l7uz (WS-2 bootstrap), PDR-020/ADR-028.
notes: CLEARED via lead-3t1o (templates main 81eb51d): the bootstrap cred-hang (silent bd-dolt-push) is fixed (deferred w/ clear diagnostic when no GH creds) and the Dockerfile-shell docstring corrected. Needs a shop-templates release to reach the published image/adopter.
close_reason: Closed

title: bc-launcher readiness Step-2 aborts on missed transient 'Accessing workspace:' banner — claude self-advances to input-ready but launch never injects (BC not online unattended)
description: Found during lead-c713 revalidation on bc-base:latest v0.3.8 (which HAS the lead-cw7m renderer fix), 2026-06-23. The readiness sequence (controller.py ~L1699): STEP 2 hard-waits for CLAUDE_READY_MARKER 'Accessing workspace:' and on 60s timeout emits 'agent-startup failure: ... marker not seen; claude or its tmux session never started; startup prompt NOT injected' and ABORTS — BEFORE reaching the STEP-4 input-ready loop (L1766+) where the lead-cw7m auto-dismiss lives. ROOT CAUSE: claude in bc-base has baked bypassPermissionsModeAccepted (~/.claude.json), so it SELF-ADVANCES past workspace-trust straight to CLAUDE_INPUT_READY_MARKER 'bypass permissions on'; the transient 'Accessing workspace:' banner is never caught by the launcher's polling -> step-2 times out and aborts. EVIDENCE: 2 consecutive unattended launches of shopsystem-templates both aborted at step-2; claude started at container-start (22:24:58), reached input-ready ('bypass permissions on' visible), yet step-2 never saw the banner. Manually 'bc-container inject'-ing the session-start armed the watcher (online) -> claude is HEALTHY; the step-2 gate is the SOLE blocker. Distinct from lead-cw7m (renderer dismiss at step 4) — this aborts BEFORE step 4. FIX: step-2 must treat CLAUDE_INPUT_READY_MARKER as ALSO satisfying 'claude is up' — if input-ready is already present, SKIP the trust-Enter (step 3) and proceed to inject; do not hard-require the transient 'Accessing workspace:' marker. (Optionally make CLAUDE_READINESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS overridable per the L1119 hint.) This blocks fully-unattended launch and thus the final clearing of lead-c713. Route: bc-launcher. Cross-ref lead-c713, lead-cw7m.
notes: CLEARED + VALIDATED (2026-06-23): unattended fresh launch of shopsystem-templates on bc-base:latest v0.3.9 reached ONLINE with NO manual inject. Launch output: 'Agent self-advanced past the workspace-trust prompt to the input-ready marker bypass permissions on; treating the agent as up and skipping the trust-accept Enter (lead-gw9v)' then 'Injected startup prompt' (exit 0); watcher armed unattended, container healthy. Step-2 fix works end-to-end.
close_reason: Closed

title: RELEASE BLOCKER: new bc-base:latest (c50b3b) Claude Code shows interactive 'Try the new fullscreen renderer?' onboarding prompt that blocks agent readiness — no BC comes online via automated launch
description: Found 2026-06-23 via a FULL fresh-image restart of shopsystem-templates (teardown + pull + bc-container launch --image ghcr.io/dstengle/shopsystem-bc-base:latest, image c50b3b546d14, 8h old; running BCs were on v0.3.5/159ea9). ROOT CAUSE: on first run in the fresh container, Claude Code displays an INTERACTIVE onboarding dialog 'Try the new fullscreen renderer? 1. Yes / 2. Not now (Enter to confirm, Esc to cancel)'. The agent BLOCKS on this dialog and never emits the readiness marker 'Accessing workspace:'. Consequently bc-container's launch/start-agent readiness probe TIMES OUT at 60s ('Claude Code did not become ready within 60s ... startup prompt NOT injected'), the session-start prompt is never injected, the agent never runs its session-start, never arms 'shop-msg watch', and the BC NEVER COMES ONLINE. This reproduces on BOTH launch (fresh template, new image) and is the same readiness-timeout signature seen on start-agent. IMPACT: every BC launch on the new bc-base image fails to reach online unattended — fleet-breaking for any unattended bring-up/recovery. PROOF + MANUAL RECOVERY (confirms diagnosis): sending Esc to the tmux 'agent' pane dismissed the dialog -> Claude proceeded to a normal ready prompt; then 'bc-container inject <bc> <session-start>' brought it online (watcher armed, container healthy on c50b3b). So bc-container inject works; the blocker is purely the image's interactive onboarding prompt. FIX (bc-base image): pre-seed the Claude Code config in the image so the onboarding/renderer prompt is already answered/suppressed (e.g. set the renderer-prompt-seen / hasCompletedOnboarding flags in ~/.claude config, or pass the non-interactive/skip flag), so a fresh agent reaches 'Accessing workspace:' with no human input. Until fixed, DO NOT release/deploy this bc-base image for fleet use. Cross-ref lead-8aqh (start-agent readiness + bc-status findings), lead-lp74 (start-agent), lead-3nf7 (bring-up).
notes: CLEARED + VALIDATED (2026-06-23): both readiness fixes shipped in bc-base/bc-lead:latest v0.3.9 (lead-cw7m renderer auto-dismiss + lead-lgc2 step-2 self-advance) and confirmed IN-IMAGE (renderer_fix=True, self_advance_fix=True). DECISIVE TEST PASSED: full fresh-image launch of shopsystem-templates on v0.3.9 reached ONLINE UNATTENDED (no Esc, no inject) — exit 0, watcher armed, healthy. The new bc-base image is now safe for fleet launch; the HOLD on the bc-base image release is LIFTED. Release lineage: lead-cw7m+lead-lgc2 (fixes) -> lead-gshn(v0.3.8)+lead-e9cb(v0.3.9) (releases). Lead-host launcher also upgraded to v0.3.9.
close_reason: Closed

title: canonical bin/shop-shell: inner bc-container launch missing --network + leaf --image must be bc-lead (no docker CLI in bc-base)
description:
The re-poured thin bin/shop-shell (templates v0.21.0, commit 37d0a7f) has two defects in the inner 'bc-container launch shopsystem-lead' invocation:

1. MISSING --network. postgres + agent-vault run on the slug-scoped 'shopsystem' compose network (compose.yaml networks.shopsystem name: shopsystem; both containers inspected on it). The launched leaf lead session is NOT attached to that network, so it cannot reach postgres/agent-vault by compose hostname. Inner launch must pass --network <slug> (product-scoped, like the data root / env-file / container-name greps). NOTE: the canonical template carries --network on the OUTER docker run only; the leaf container bc-container creates is separate and is not attached by the outer flag.

2. WRONG leaf --image. Template hands the leaf '--image shopsystem-bc-base:latest', but bc-base carries NO docker CLI (verified: bc-base -> NO_DOCKER_CLI; bc-lead -> /usr/bin/docker). The leaf lead session is launched WITH --mount-docker-socket precisely so the lead router can run 'bc-container launch' to stand up BCs. A bc-base leaf cannot run bc-container at all. Leaf --image must be the bc-lead launcher image (bc-base + docker CLI), the same product-neutral image already used for the outer launcher run. Product-neutral (NOT slug-rewritten) must be preserved per the template header contract.

Empirical pre-state (lead host, 2026-06-23): canonical template read from package data via 'shop-templates show shop-shell'; bc-lead:latest=v0.3.7 'bc-container launch --help' supports --image/--network/--mount-docker-socket/--workspace-mount/--env-file/--startup-prompt (CLI is current, NOT the gap). Gap is purely the shop-shell template wiring.

CONFLICTING lead-held coverage: scenario 172 @scenario_hash:da8ea23429b8bdc9 hard-pins the leaf image substring 'shopsystem-bc-base' and must be SUPERSEDED; cited in the dispatch with retirement instruction. Scenario 134 has no leaf --image assertion, no body change needed.

Dispatched as request_bugfix to shopsystem-templates (work_id lead-ss6k, 2026-06-23). Local working tree fast-patched to unblock the operator (uncommitted, throwaway, superseded by re-pour).
notes: Answered-by-redispatch on fresh bead lead-chop (2026-06-23). The ADR-010 superseding-pin blocker is resolved: PO adopted the candidate re-authored scenario 172 into features/templates/172-...gherkin pinned @scenario_hash:725562869d9df919 (supersedes da8ea23429b8bdc9); architect empirically verified the block-only hash reproduces 725562869d9df919 (scenarios verify exit 0, ADR-018/ADR-019). lead-chop re-dispatches as request_bugfix to shopsystem-templates CARRYING the re-authored 172 + lead pin, so the BC can integrate the staged branch bc/lead-ss6k (d92f109) green and the reviewer echoes the lead-pinned hash. The lead-ss6k clarify has been consumed from the outbox. Full work is carried by lead-chop; closing this bead.

title: Converged thin bin/shop-shell (PDR-020 slice 2, 0.20.0) is not runnable as-rendered — bare image ref + missing bc_name positional
description: Two concrete defects the substring-scenario (172, 03f1256aefc7fad4) missed, found by router e2e against the rendered 0.20.0 shop-shell. (1) Both docker run lines use bare image name shopsystem-bc-base -> resolves to docker.io/library/shopsystem-bc-base which does not exist; must be ghcr.io/dstengle/shopsystem-bc-base:latest. Fix in canonical src/shop_templates/templates/ops/shop-shell. Dispatched as request_bugfix to shopsystem-templates 2026-06-23. Must keep 172 (03f1256aefc7fad4) AND 175 (399d16c31084dbfc) green; verify by actually rendering+dry-running not substring checks. Dependency: ghcr bc-base :latest must track >=v0.3.6 (separate, publish-side). lead-po follow-up recommended: strengthen 172 to pin a registry-qualified resolvable image ref + required bc_name positional.
close_reason: RECONCILED: converged shop-shell runnability fixed (origin/main cd01a78, RED bae2d41->GREEN 0ccc908). (1) image ref bare->ghcr.io/dstengle/shopsystem-bc-base:latest (verified pullable, manifest HTTP 200); (2) bc_name positional {{OPS_SLUG}}-lead on launch AND attach (render+dry-run: parses past argparse; control errors without it). title: PDR-020 slice 2 RE-ISSUE (fresh-bead clarify answer for stalled lead-6ox4)
description:

clarify tmpl-a5l (blocked under ADR-010 until it receives lead-authored replacement
pins). The lead's answer was delivered only as a `nudge --note` on the SAME work_id
because a request_bugfix re-send hit the one-row-per-(bc,work_id) invariant. A nudge-note
is not reliably consumed as a clarify answer (the recurring q616 clarify-answer-CLI gap),
so the BC stalled (~90 min, no work_done). The contract's sanctioned way to answer a
clarify is re-dispatch on a FRESH lead bead (new work_id => no inbox collision). This bead
is that fresh re-dispatch. It carries the COMPLETE disposition ledger inline so the BC
needs nothing further to land slice 2 green.

SUPERSEDES / RELATED-TO: lead-6ox4 (stalled slice-2 tracker). This bead is now the live
slice-2 tracker.

`bc-container launch --workspace-mount <lead-repo> --mount-docker-socket --startup-prompt <lead>`

title: PDR-020 slice 1: bc-launcher lead-profile launch (mount local repo + skip clone; opt-in docker-socket mount)

title: shop-shell broker proxy URL missing agent-vault token userinfo (407)
description: Canonical ops/shop-shell (lines 166-167) builds HTTPS_PROXY/HTTP_PROXY as bare http://agent-vault:14322 with no ${AGENT_VAULT_TOKEN}:${AGENT_VAULT_VAULT}@ userinfo. The agent-vault transparent proxy (:14322) requires Basic proxy auth; bare proxy => HTTP 407 Proxy Authentication Required, surfacing to operator as a failed claude API call. Regression: pre-0.1x shop-shell embedded the userinfo. Token+vault already sourced/validated/exported upstream (guard exits 1 if absent, lines 63-131). Fix: HTTPS_PROXY=http://${AGENT_VAULT_TOKEN}:${AGENT_VAULT_VAULT}@agent-vault:14322 (HTTP_PROXY same), keep overridable, match BC reference form raw. Discriminator: existing shop-shell broker-wiring capability misbehaving => request_bugfix. Scenario 172 (@scenario_hash:5335c39eb06f7493) pins ENDPOINT only (substring 14322 on HTTPS_PROXY), NOT the userinfo FORM. Dispatched to shopsystem-templates 2026-06-22 (dispatch_state=dispatched). Recommend lead-po follow-up to tighten 172 (or add a scenario) pinning the token-embedded proxy form; non-blocking. Operator unblocked NOW via in-shell export workaround.
close_reason: RECONCILED: shop-shell HTTPS_PROXY now embeds token (BC form), merged origin/main 1d31dd0, red-before-green, 42 tests; scenarios 172/183/192 unchanged (no hash retired). NOTE: NOT cutting a standalone release — PDR-020 (approved) retires the hand-rolled shop-shell in favor of the bc-container/bc-base convergence; the proxy fix rides on origin/main and is superseded by the convergence re-pour. Operator unblocked interim via in-shell export.

title: shopsystem-bc-launcher-beads dolt remote is EMPTY (size=0) -> bd bootstrap fatal-strands launch before agent-start
description:
During the lead-4qpq fleet relaunch (2026-06-22), bc-shopsystem-bc-launcher stranded with NO agent (no tmux/claude). Root: bd bootstrap failed — 'dolt clone git+https://github.com/dstengle/shopsystem-bc-launcher-beads.git: git remote has no branches: cannot push...; initialize the repository with an initial branch/commit first'. The shopsystem-bc-launcher-beads remote is EMPTY (gh api size=0, vs scenarios-beads size=40). The bootstrap failure does a FATAL early-return BEFORE tmux/claude start — a DIFFERENT early-return than lead-k4k7 (which only fixed the skill-refresh step). Recovered manually (tmux new-session + agent-vault run claude + inject; k4k7 pattern). TWO fixes: (a) OPS: initialize the bc-launcher-beads remote with an initial branch/commit; (b) DURABLE (request_bugfix -> bc-launcher): extend k4k7-style warn-and-continue resilience to the bd-bootstrap step so a bootstrap failure no longer strands the agent, AND/OR make bd bootstrap init-and-push when the remote is empty. Relates lead-k4k7, reference_bc_beads_wedge_heal.

title: BLOCKER for lead-4qpq: bc-base v0.3.4 pip ResolutionImpossible is a .git-suffix direct-URL mismatch on the scenarios pin (NOT a templates v0.15.0 constraint) — request_maintenance revert
description:
DISPATCHED 2026-06-22 (lead-architect) as request_maintenance -> shopsystem-bc-launcher, work_id lead-l5an. Deposit verified via shop-msg read inbox. 4qpq stays BLOCKED on l5an until BC work_done + rebuild.

ROOT CAUSE (empirically reproduced via pip --dry-run against package METADATA; no BC code read — ADR-018 D2):
The v0.3.4 bc-base build fails with pip ResolutionImpossible NOT because of shop-templates v0.15.0. The bead's prior templates-constraint hypothesis is FALSIFIED:
- TEST A: v0.3.3-FORM install (templates v0.14.0, else identical to v0.3.4) ALSO fails identically -> templates version is NOT the discriminator.
- shop-templates pyproject is BYTE-IDENTICAL at v0.14.0 and v0.15.0 (both declare version=0.14.0; only package-data/src/tests/features changed). Its ONE runtime dep is scenarios @ git+...scenarios@v0.2.0 (no .git), unchanged.

ACTUAL ROOT CAUSE — .git-suffix direct-URL string mismatch introduced by the lead-ur8z Dockerfile edit:
  v0.3.3 line 28: scenarios @ git+...shopsystem-scenarios@v0.2.0      (NO .git) -> BUILT FINE
  v0.3.4 line 40: scenarios @ git+...shopsystem-scenarios.git@v0.2.0  (.git ADDED) -> FAILS
compare v0.3.3...main diff confirms ur8z changed exactly: '-...scenarios@v0.2.0' '+...scenarios.git@v0.2.0'. Templates ARG bump rode the SAME commit, innocent bystander.
WHY pip fails: messaging v0.4.0 (pyproject:16) AND shop-templates (pyproject:20) both declare transitive 'scenarios @ git+...scenarios@v0.2.0' (NO .git). pip treats two direct-URL reqs for the same project as compatible only if URL STRINGS are identical. '....git@v0.2.0' != '...@v0.2.0' -> ResolutionImpossible.

REMEDIATION (single clear dispatch, no dave decision): request_maintenance -> shopsystem-bc-launcher; revert .git on the Dockerfile scenarios line only (...scenarios.git@v0.2.0 -> ...scenarios@v0.2.0), matching messaging/templates. Other 3 lines keep .git. Equivalent fixes considered+rejected: drop explicit scenarios line (TEST B proved clean) / normalize all 4 + bump messaging+templates (cascades releases). Smallest-diff revert chosen.

HOUSEKEEPING v0.3.4: release exists with 0 ASSETS (no image). Recommend SUPERSEDE with v0.3.5, NOT delete+recut v0.3.4: self-pin fixpoint references v0.3.4 and bc-launcher main already advanced past 2fe8917 (now 6c0d959); next self-pin will be v0.3.5. Leave v0.3.4 as recorded imageless failed-release marker.
notes:

- Fix commit: fbd1801 (merge 2a7309c, both carry work_id lead-l5an). HEAD advanced 6c0d959 -> 2a7309c.
- Other 3 pins INTACT (all retain .git as designed): L39 messaging.git@v0.4.0, L41 shop-templates.git@${SHOP_TEMPLATES_VERSION}, L42 bc-launcher.git@v0.3.4 (self-pin). SHOP_TEMPLATES_VERSION default = v0.15.0 (L31).

title: bc-base templates-auto-propagation is BROKEN: release.yml event 'shopsystem-templates-released' != rebuild-bc-base.yml 'rebuild-bc-base', AND bc-base Dockerfile hard-pins shop-templates@v0.3.0
description: Found during lead-6pjw delivery. The intended chain (shop-templates release -> repository_dispatch -> bc-launcher rebuilds bc-base:latest with new templates) NEVER fires, for two independent reasons: (1) EVENT MISMATCH — dstengle/shopsystem-templates .github/workflows/release.yml POSTs event_type 'shopsystem-templates-released', but dstengle/shopsystem-bc-launcher .github/workflows/rebuild-bc-base.yml only triggers on repository_dispatch types: [rebuild-bc-base]. No workflow listens for 'shopsystem-templates-released' -> dispatch is a no-op. (2) HARD-PIN — even if it fired, docker/bc-base/Dockerfile line 29 pins 'shop-templates @ git+...@v0.3.0' (and messaging@v0.4.0, scenarios@v0.2.0, bc-launcher@v0.3.1); rebuild-bc-base.yml's no-cache rebuild reinstalls those exact pins, so it can NEVER pick up new templates without a Dockerfile pin bump. CONSEQUENCE: new shop-templates releases require a MANUAL bc-base Dockerfile pin bump + bc-launcher image release. DISPATCHED 2026-06-19 as assign_scenarios -> shopsystem-bc-launcher carrying scenarios c179b0c448ca851c (closes EVENT MISMATCH) + edd2c813688ab768 (closes HARD-PIN); both author the OBSERVABLE (release -> propagated bc-base:latest carrying new templates), so bc-launcher is free to fix either side of the event-name mismatch and apply the client_payload tag as a build-arg pin bump. Discovered empirically via gh API read of both repos' workflows + Dockerfile.
close_reason:
RECONCILED 2026-06-19 (lead-architect). assign_scenarios work_done from shopsystem-bc-launcher.

HASH-MATCH: VERIFIED. Both scenarios recompute block-only via 'scenarios hash' over the lead-held features/bc-launcher/{52,53}*.gherkin scenario blocks: 52 -> c179b0c448ca851c (MATCH), 53 -> edd2c813688ab768 (MATCH). BC-pinned tags in dstengle/shopsystem-bc-launcher features/bc_base_image_publishing.feature carry the identical two hashes (@bc:shopsystem-bc-launcher). BC work_done confirms canonical recompute + 4 pre-existing publishing pins (d9909f38,b688a5fe,4e470f75,be11d615) intact/unaltered. No conflicting BC-local pinned hash surfaced (supersede-enumeration: clean). Adversarial review PASSED at f6a9e67, 208 tests; TDD trail test(red) 8611c95 -> feat(green) 0503c98; work reachable on origin/main.

DEFECT-CLOSURE: defect (2) HARD-PIN fully CLOSED — rebuild-bc-base.yml now threads github.event.client_payload.tag into the SHOP_TEMPLATES_VERSION build-arg; Dockerfile parameterized (ARG SHOP_TEMPLATES_VERSION); non-vacuity demonstrated (reverting Dockerfile to frozen @v0.14.0 literal fails edd2c813). The CONTRACT pwa2 dispatched (scenarios 52+53 as authored) is MET.

RESIDUAL (filed lead-aw1b, P1): defect (1) EVENT MISMATCH not closed END-TO-END — it was renamed, not aligned. templates emits event_type 'shopsystem-templates-released'; bc-launcher now listens for 'templates-release'/'rebuild-bc-base' -> still no match -> a real templates release dispatch is STILL a no-op. Scenario 52 deliberately abstracted the event_type literal, so the BC test passed against the renamed type. This is a scenario-authoring gap (52 should pin the literal), not a BC contract failure; pwa2 closes on the contract-as-authored and the residual carries forward in lead-aw1b. lead-4qpq updated: manual bc-launcher tag still required.

title: Validate the adopter-bootstrap end-to-end: cold-walkthrough dstengle/shopsystem-starter (fork -> bin/bootstrap -> green footing)
description: The journey finding's 'unvalidated' probe (lead-y73x) + the integration crux of the now-built feature. The starter (dstengle/shopsystem-starter, public template) ships ONLY bin/bootstrap; bin/footing is expected to be rendered by 'shop-templates bootstrap' (from templates/ops/footing) DURING bootstrap, then delegated to. CONFIRM end-to-end: 'Use this template' -> name <product>-lead -> run bin/bootstrap -> it validates name (U4), runs the single auth gate, pours (rendering bin/footing), delegates to footing which brings up services + creates <product>-lead-beads + wires remotes + dolt-push smoke-test -> STOPS at green git push + bd dolt push. Watch specifically that bin/bootstrap obtains bin/footing BEFORE calling it (else broken). Requires the republished bc-base :latest (lead-2xi3 v0.3.2) to be live. This is the real lead-driven cold-walkthrough (vs the 2026-06-15 manually-orchestrated one).
notes:
=== lead-architect LOCAL RE-VALIDATION post-fix-merge (2026-06-18) ===
Re-validated against the merged fixes (lead-hjrh 75bddee + lead-y4pg c97d64b),
fully local, no outward push. Contract/artifact surface only.

VERDICT: PARTIAL. DEFECT 1 GONE; the y4pg SOURCE FIX IS CORRECT; but the
cold-walkthrough STILL does NOT reach the auth gate against the CURRENTLY-
PUBLISHED image, because that image carries an OLD shop-templates (0.3.0)
with NO templates/ops/footing. The walkthrough now fails CLEANLY (no exit-127)
at the refuse-when-missing guard instead.

--- Re-rendered starter body (lead-owned forkable, 4 files) from c97d64b ---
shop-templates ships NO `starter` CLI verb; body is rendered via the package
API cli.iter_starter_files()/read_starter_file() (served UNSUBSTITUTED). Clean
venv build of c97d64b carries src/shop_templates/templates/ops/footing (host's
cached 0.13.0 wheel did NOT — version unchanged across refs, so pip served a
stale wheel; clean --no-cache venv build was required to get honest bits).
Re-rendered bin/bootstrap CONFIRMED both fixes:
 * line 33: BC_BASE_IMAGE="${BC_BASE_IMAGE:-ghcr.io/dstengle/shopsystem-bc-base:latest}"  (DEFECT 1 fix)
 * lines 106-161: render bin/footing via in-container `shop-templates bootstrap`,
   then a refuse-when-missing guard (145-150), then invoke.  (DEFECT 2 fix shape)
 * .env.example line 23: commented BC_BASE_IMAGE= override knob present.

--- Cold-walkthrough run (cp .env.example .env; ./bin/bootstrap in acme-lead) ---
DEFECT 1 GONE: "bootstrap: recorded resolved image
'ghcr.io/dstengle/shopsystem-bc-base@sha256:b5d5d63f85e7...'". docker pull
resolves; no manifest unknown.
DEFECT 2 — STILL FIRES, NEW (cleaner) MECHANISM. Exact boundary output:
  bootstrap: rendering bin/footing from the templates/ops/footing template via in-container 'shop-templates bootstrap' ...
  bootstrap: bin/footing is MISSING — the render step (in-container 'shop-templates bootstrap') did not produce '/tmp/acme-lead/bin/footing'.
    bootstrap will NOT run 'bash ./bin/footing' against a missing file (that would fail with an opaque exit-127 ...). ... Aborting.
  ===EXIT=0===  (clean abort, NOT exit-127; auth gate NOT reached)

ROOT CAUSE: the y4pg fix renders footing from the IN-IMAGE shop-templates, but
published ghcr.io/dstengle/shopsystem-bc-base:latest (digest b5d5d63f, == v0.3.2)
carries shop-templates 0.3.0 → `python3 -c "(files('shop_templates.templates.ops')/'footing').is_file()"` = False inside the image. So the render step pours the lead structure but emits NO bin/footing, and the new guard correctly refuses.

--- PROOF the y4pg source fix is correct (host, no docker-mount skew) ---
`shop-templates bootstrap --shop-type lead --shop-name acme-product --target X`
run from the CLEAN c97d64b venv DID render bin/footing: 16752 bytes, executable,
product-scoped to 'acme' (10 refs). => when the image's shop-templates carries
this version, the render step WILL produce bin/footing and the walkthrough WILL
proceed past the guard to the human auth gate. The image upgrade is the missing link.
(NOTE: in-container overlay attempts to inject the fixed CLI were UNRELIABLE here
due to a ZFS dataset-relative docker bind-mount snapshot skew — newly-written
host paths under /tmp and the working dir were invisible/stale inside containers.
The host-side render above is the authoritative, skew-free proof.)

--- IMPLICATION for lead-6pjw (the delivery chain) ---
Re-rendering + re-pushing the public starter is NECESSARY (carries both bootstrap
fixes) but NOT SUFFICIENT for green: the PUBLISHED bc-base image must ALSO be
rebuilt to bake shop-templates >= c97d64b (carrying templates/ops/footing).
Sequence: (1) version-bump+tag shopsystem-templates (REQUIRED — see below),
(2) that tag triggers release.yml → repository_dispatch to shopsystem-bc-launcher
→ rebuild+republish shopsystem-bc-base:latest with the new templates baked in,
(3) re-render+re-push the public starter body, (4) re-run this walkthrough.
Only after step (2) lands a new :latest does the footing render succeed end-to-end.

HOST STATE: restored to as-found — shop-templates v0.13.0 (commit 46c33c6),
no footing template. All throwaway dirs/containers cleaned (--rm).
=== lead-architect FINAL E2E RE-VALIDATION post-DELIVERY (2026-06-19) ===
Re-ran the adopter cold-walkthrough against the DELIVERED fixes: public
dstengle/shopsystem-starter @ 3a1c985 (carries both bootstrap fixes) +

0.14.0 + templates/ops/footing baked) + host shop-templates 0.14.0.

Both former defects are PROVABLY FIXED. The full chained walkthrough cannot
complete ON THIS HOST solely due to the known ZFS dataset-relative docker
bind-mount write-visibility skew — a HOST limitation, not a product defect.

--- COLD WALKTHROUGH (fresh clone -> cp .env.example .env -> ./bin/bootstrap) ---
Fresh clone of dstengle/shopsystem-starter landed at 3a1c985; ships ONLY
bin/bootstrap (no bin/footing, as designed). Bootstrap output:
  bootstrap: deriving product identity 'acme' from repo name 'acme-lead'.   (U4 name gate PASSES)
  bootstrap: resolving the floating image 'ghcr.io/dstengle/shopsystem-bc-base:latest' ...
  bootstrap: recorded resolved image 'ghcr.io/dstengle/shopsystem-bc-base@sha256:58999119b705cfe284ca00f7a8e70e443c20c7fee7a85c301968e6d49c32d234' in /tmp/acme-lead/.env.
  bootstrap: rendering bin/footing ... via in-container 'shop-templates bootstrap' ...
  shop-templates bootstrap: `bd init` failed ... Found existing Dolt database: /workspace/acme-lead/.beads/embeddeddolt/acme_lead ... Aborting.  ===EXIT=1===

DEFECT 1 (image name) — GONE, CONFIRMED. docker pull of
ghcr.io/dstengle/shopsystem-bc-base:latest resolved cleanly to the NEW digest
sha256:58999119b705 (no manifest unknown). The render step reached the
IN-CONTAINER shop-templates bootstrap, proving the new image carries
shop-templates 0.14.0. Defect-1 unambiguously resolved.

DEFECT 2 (footing acquisition) — PRODUCT FIX PROVEN CORRECT (skew-free evidence):
Ran shop-templates bootstrap --shop-type lead --shop-name acme-product from the
NEW image into a CONTAINER-LOCAL dir with Mounts:[] (NO bind mount). The
container's OWN view rendered /work/acme-lead/bin/footing = 16752 bytes,
executable, real script ("#!/usr/bin/env bash ... bin/footing — lead-shop footing
bootstrap script"), product-scoped to 'acme' (10 refs). The footing render
COMPLETES correctly; shop-templates then proceeds to a downstream beads/dolt
pre-commit-hook step (bd dolt remote add -> git commit -> pre-commit -> bd export)
unrelated to footing. => with image shop-templates 0.14.0 (now true), the render
PRODUCES bin/footing and the refuse-when-missing guard does NOT fire. DEFECT-2
product fix is correct.

--- HOST ZFS BIND-MOUNT SKEW: ATTRIBUTION PROVEN (minimal reproduction) ---
docker run --entrypoint bash -v /tmp/skewtest:/mnt/skew <image> wrote sentinel.txt
+ bin/footing into the BIND-MOUNTED dir.
  CONTAINER OWN VIEW after write: sentinel.txt (32B) + bin/footing (13B) PRESENT, readable.
  HOST VIEW of /tmp/skewtest immediately after: EMPTY — "bin: No such file or
  directory", "sentinel.txt: No such file or directory".
Files a container writes into a bind-mounted host dir are INVISIBLE host-side on
this ZFS host. This is EXACTLY why bin/bootstrap's host-side guard
[[ ! -f $REPO_ROOT/bin/footing ]] would read the rendered footing as MISSING, and
why the real run's bd init saw a phantom .beads/embeddeddolt/acme_lead (host-side

container; the host cannot see the writes. => the chained host-side bootstrap
cannot complete on THIS host; cause is the host mount artifact, NOT bin/bootstrap
or the footing render logic.

--- DISPOSITION ---
PRODUCT IS CORRECT. lead-e8hk validates GREEN at the product level: starter

title: Add a clarifying-discussion-with-product-authority discipline to the PM skills (re-scope COMMIT-TO-SPECIFICS)
description:
FEEDBACK (dave, 2026-06-18): 'a lack of clarifying discussion with me' in the PM skills. Root cause: adaptation rule #2 in .claude/skills/README.md ('Interactive/workshop loops -> COMMIT-TO-SPECIFICS') OVERCORRECTED. It conflated two different round-trips: (a) the autonomous BC FLEET loop (rightly NOT stalled on) and (b) the human PRODUCT AUTHORITY / dave (wrongly skipped). Compounded by running discovery in NON-INTERACTIVE subagents that literally cannot talk to dave, so clarifying dialogue had no home -> dave had to volunteer scope (git+beads, creds-up-front, two-phase bootstrap container) the process should have SOLICITED.

FIX (shape TBD with dave): clarifying discussion with the product authority becomes a FIRST-CLASS discovery discipline, run at the ROUTER/main-agent level (where dialogue with dave is possible) BEFORE/while dispatching discovery subagents. Re-scope COMMIT-TO-SPECIFICS to: 'do not stall on the BC fleet; DO interview the human product owner.' Likely: revise README adaptation rule #2 + PDR-012, add an explicit 'interview the product authority' step to the discovery skills (jobs-to-be-done, problem-framing-canvas, opportunity-solution-tree, customer-journey-map). Relates to memory feedback_pm_discovery_before_authoring_intent (dave's earlier 'interview the USER first' correction). Touches the experimental skills (lead-di16/lead-tgs4).
design:
# PO-AUTHORED CONTENT (lead-086d) — the WHAT of the product-authority discovery-dialogue discipline
# Authored by lead-po 2026-06-19. Architect routes where each artifact lands.
# Routing note (from dave): skills/README + 4 discovery skills are POURED package data in shop-templates
#   -> request_bugfix + re-pour (NOT in-place edits here). PDR-012 is lead-owned -> lead-side edit.

## ROOT-CAUSE FRAMING (honored throughout)
Discovery ran inside NON-INTERACTIVE subagents that literally cannot talk to dave. So the clarifying
dialogue with the human product authority had NO HOME in the loop. README adaptation rule #2
("Interactive/workshop loops -> COMMIT-TO-SPECIFICS") over-corrected: it collapsed TWO distinct
round-trips into one and suppressed both. Result: dave had to VOLUNTEER scope (git+beads,
creds-up-front, two-phase bootstrap container) that the process should have SOLICITED. The fix gives
that dialogue a home at the ROUTER/main-agent level, where talking to dave is actually possible, and
makes running it a GATE that fires BEFORE any non-interactive discovery subagent is dispatched.

THE TWO ROUND-TRIPS (the distinction every artifact below must preserve):
  (a) AUTONOMOUS BC FLEET LOOP — the Implementer/Reviewer round-trip inside BC containers, and the
      lead<->BC clarify/work_done cycle. This is rightly NOT stalled on. COMMIT-TO-SPECIFICS applies
      here: decide from contract/state/defaults and act; do not pause the fleet for human sign-off.
  (b) HUMAN PRODUCT AUTHORITY round-trip — dialogue with dave (or whoever holds product authority).
      This is the source of scope, problem selection, and product vocabulary. It MUST be solicited,
      not skipped. COMMIT-TO-SPECIFICS never licensed skipping THIS; conflating it with (a) was the bug.

================================================================================
## ARTIFACT 1 — Revised README adaptation rule #2 (POURED -> shop-templates request_bugfix + re-pour)
================================================================================
Current (over-corrected) text:
  "2. Interactive/workshop loops -> COMMIT-TO-SPECIFICS."

REPLACE WITH:

"2. Two round-trips, not one — do not stall on the BC fleet; DO interview the human product authority.
   A skill's 'interactive' / 'workshop' furniture encodes a human dialogue loop. That loop maps onto
   TWO different round-trips in shopsystem, and they are adapted in OPPOSITE directions:
     - The AUTONOMOUS BC FLEET loop (Implementer<->Reviewer; lead<->BC clarify/work_done) is NOT a
       human-dialogue surface. COMMIT-TO-SPECIFICS governs it: decide from the contract, current
       state, or a sensible default and ACT; never stall the fleet waiting on a human, and never
       introduce a stakeholder round-trip the shopsystem loop does not already have.
     - The HUMAN PRODUCT AUTHORITY round-trip (the dialogue with dave / whoever holds product
       authority) is exactly what a discovery skill's interactive loop SHOULD become — not what it
       should be suppressed into. Product authority is the source of scope, problem selection, and
       product vocabulary; that dialogue MUST be SOLICITED, not skipped or inferred.
   COMMIT-TO-SPECIFICS therefore means: do not stall on the BC fleet, AND do interview the human
   product owner. It NEVER means 'skip the conversation with the product authority.' The earlier
   one-line mapping ('Interactive/workshop loops -> COMMIT-TO-SPECIFICS') conflated these two
   round-trips and is retired by this rule.
   STRUCTURAL CONSTRAINT: discovery skills run in non-interactive subagents that CANNOT talk to the
   product authority. Therefore the product-authority dialogue cannot live inside a discovery skill's
   execution; it lives at the ROUTER/main-agent level and runs as a GATE before discovery subagents
   are dispatched (see the router discovery gate, Artifact 4)."

================================================================================
## ARTIFACT 2 — PDR-012 revision: router-level discovery-dialogue gate as a first-class discipline
================================================================================
ADD a new dated revision banner to the Status line:
  "**router-level discovery-dialogue gate (2026-06-19, bead lead-086d)** — adds the product-authority
   discovery-dialogue gate as a first-class discipline and corrects an over-correction in the
   experimental skills' adaptation rule #2."

ADD a new top-level section (placed right after 'Structural gate — the BUILD TRAP', as a second
structural gate), verbatim intent:

"## Structural gate — the PRODUCT-AUTHORITY DISCOVERY DIALOGUE (the round-trip that must have a home)

Discipline 1 (Problem discovery & selection) requires that every committed intent trace to a
*validated* problem / job-to-be-done. Validation is impossible without a round-trip to the party who
holds product authority. In a consumer instance that party is the user/market; in this
framework-as-product bootstrap instance it is the operator/adopter — concretely, dave. That round-trip
was being SKIPPED, for a structural reason: discovery was executed inside non-interactive subagents
that cannot talk to a human, so the clarifying dialogue had no home in the loop and the product
authority had to VOLUNTEER scope the process should have SOLICITED.

This is distinct from, and must not be conflated with, the autonomous BC fleet loop. The fleet loop
(Implementer<->Reviewer; lead<->BC clarify) is rightly NOT stalled on a human — COMMIT-TO-SPECIFICS
governs it. The product-authority round-trip is the OPPOSITE: it must be solicited, not suppressed.
The experimental-skills adaptation rule #2 over-corrected by collapsing the two; this PDR corrects it.

GATE (load-bearing, router-level): the product-authority discovery dialogue is a first-class
discovery discipline that runs at the ROUTER / main-agent level — the only level where dialogue with
the product authority is possible — and it MUST run BEFORE discovery subagents are dispatched, because
those subagents cannot conduct it themselves.

SUFFICIENCY CRITERION (the gate): before a discovery subagent (jobs-to-be-done, problem-framing-canvas,
opportunity-solution-tree, customer-journey-map, or any skill whose adapted furniture carries an
'interactive'/'workshop' loop) is dispatched, the router has either
  (i) conducted the product-authority discovery dialogue at the main-agent level and captured its
      output as interview-notes the subagent receives as input (the brief/bead carries it); OR
  (ii) recorded an EXPLICIT reason the dialogue is not required for this dispatch (e.g. the scope and
      product vocabulary are already pinned by an existing brief/PDR the dispatch cites).
A discovery dispatch that does neither has fallen through the gate. Silent inference of scope by a
non-interactive subagent is the failure mode this gate exists to prevent.

This gate composes with the anti-build-trap gate: the build trap asks 'should we build this at all?';
the discovery-dialogue gate ensures the 'what problem / for whom / in what words' is solicited from
the product authority rather than inferred. Both are upstream of authoring intent."

ALSO amend the existing 'Adaptation constraints' bullet about human-checkpoints. Append:

"  - A discovery skill's human-checkpoint does NOT collapse to 'the PO commits the specific' when the
    checkpoint is the PRODUCT-AUTHORITY discovery dialogue. That checkpoint maps to the router-level
    discovery gate above (run the dialogue at the main-agent level, or record why it is not needed),
    NOT to silent commitment by a non-interactive subagent. The 'do not introduce a stakeholder
    round-trip the loop does not already have' constraint refers to the BC FLEET loop; it never
    licensed skipping the product-authority round-trip the loop DOES already have at the router level."

ALSO add to 'What this leaves open':
  "- Whether the router discovery gate is enforced by a poured router checklist item, a skill
    precondition assertion, or both, is a routing/implementation decision for the Architect; this PDR
    pins that the gate EXISTS and what it requires, not its enforcement mechanism."

================================================================================
## ARTIFACT 3 — 'Interview the product authority' step for each of the four discovery skills
================================================================================
(POURED package data -> shop-templates request_bugfix + re-pour. Each skill gets the SAME step,
specialized by what it elicits. WHAT it says + WHEN it fires below.)

COMMON STEP (add as the FIRST step of each skill, before any analysis/canvas-filling step):

  "### Step 0 — Interview the product authority (PRECONDITION, router-level)
   WHEN IT FIRES: at the very start, before any artifact in this skill is filled in, and before this
   skill is dispatched to a non-interactive subagent.
   WHAT IT SAYS: This skill is non-interactive once dispatched and CANNOT talk to the product
   authority. Therefore the product-authority dialogue must already have happened at the router /
   main-agent level. Before this skill runs, confirm ONE of:
     (a) interview-notes from a product-authority dialogue are available as input (on the bead/brief),
         OR
     (b) the router has recorded an explicit reason the dialogue is not required (scope + vocabulary
         already pinned by a cited brief/PDR).
   If neither holds, STOP and surface to the router: 'product-authority discovery dialogue not yet
   conducted — run it at the main-agent level before dispatching this skill.' Do not infer scope or
   product vocabulary to fill the gap; inferring it is the exact failure this step prevents."

SKILL-SPECIFIC ELICITATION (what the router's dialogue must surface, per skill, to satisfy (a)):

  - jobs-to-be-done: the JOB the product authority is hiring the product for, in the authority's OWN
    words; the circumstance/trigger; the current alternative they 'fire'; what 'progress' looks like
    to THEM. (Fires before naming any job statement.)

  - problem-framing-canvas: the PROBLEM as the product authority states it (not the solution they
    propose); who has it; the cost of leaving it unsolved; explicit IN/OUT-of-scope boundaries the
    authority draws. (Fires before filling any canvas cell — this is the cell-population source.)

  - opportunity-solution-tree: the desired OUTCOME the product authority is steering toward; which
    opportunities they consider in-bounds vs out; any solution constraints they impose
    (e.g. 'creds up front', 'two-phase bootstrap'). (Fires before branching the tree — the root
    outcome and top opportunities come from the authority, not inference.)

  - customer-journey-map: WHO the customer/operator is, from the product authority's vantage; the
    real start/end of the journey they care about; the steps and pains they have actually observed
    vs assumed. (Fires before plotting any journey stage.)

================================================================================
## ARTIFACT 4 — The router/main-agent DISCOVERY GATE itself (router-owned -> lead-primer, lead-side)
================================================================================
(This is router behavior. It belongs in the lead-primer / canonical router instructions. Architect
decides exact home; likely lead-primer.md 'Standing rule' + a PDR-012 pin. WHAT below.)

NAME: Standing rule — product-authority discovery gate.

PRECONDITION (what triggers the gate): the router is about to dispatch a DISCOVERY subagent — any
lead-po dispatch whose work is problem discovery / selection or whose skill furniture carries an
'interactive'/'workshop' loop (jobs-to-be-done, problem-framing-canvas, opportunity-solution-tree,
customer-journey-map, and any future discovery skill). The gate fires on the DISPATCH boundary, not
inside the subagent — because the subagent is non-interactive and cannot conduct the dialogue.

WHAT IT REQUIRES THE ROUTER TO DO (before the dispatch):
  1. Determine whether scope + product vocabulary for this discovery work are already pinned by an
     existing brief/PDR the dispatch can cite.
  2. If NOT pinned: CONDUCT the product-authority discovery dialogue at the main-agent level — ask the
     product authority (dave) the discovery questions the target skill needs (per Artifact 3's
     skill-specific elicitation) — and capture the answers as interview-notes on the bead/brief that
     the subagent will receive as input. This is the dialogue's HOME.
  3. If pinned: record the citation as the explicit reason the dialogue is not required for this
     dispatch.
  4. Only AFTER (2) or (3) may the discovery subagent be dispatched.
This gate does NOT apply to the BC fleet loop or to authoring/reconciliation dispatches that consume
already-validated problems — it is specifically the discovery-input boundary.

HOW IT IS ENFORCED:
  - As a named item on the router's pre-dispatch path for discovery work (parallel to the existing
    idle-detection checklist): 'Discovery dispatch? -> product-authority dialogue conducted OR cited
    reason recorded. If neither, do NOT dispatch; run the dialogue first.'
  - Reinforced by Artifact 3's Step 0 as a DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH precondition INSIDE each discovery skill:
    if a subagent is somehow dispatched without (a) or (b) satisfied, it STOPS and surfaces back to
    the router rather than inferring. Gate at the router (primary) + precondition in the skill
    (backstop) = the dialogue can no longer be silently skipped.
  - Distinguished from the choice-suppression rule: choice-suppression says decide operational
    questions yourself and act. This gate is NOT an operational question — it is the genuine
    product-judgment round-trip (scope/vocabulary) that the choice-suppression rule already names as
    an ADMISSIBLE reason to engage the product authority. The gate makes that engagement MANDATORY at
    the discovery boundary rather than merely permitted.

================================================================================
## VERIFICATION (PO sufficiency posture)
- Two round-trips kept distinct in every artifact (fleet loop NOT stalled; authority round-trip
  solicited). ✓
- Root cause honored: dialogue given a HOME at the router level because subagents are non-interactive. ✓
- Gate is a PRECONDITION on the dispatch boundary (where dialogue is possible), with a skill-level
  backstop. ✓
- COMMIT-TO-SPECIFICS re-scoped, not weakened: still 'do not stall on the fleet', now explicitly
  'DO interview the product authority'. ✓
- No poured files edited in-place; routing note carried for the Architect. ✓

title: WS-2 cold-walkthrough wall: bin/agent-vault-provision proposal-create dies on 'Session requires vault scope'
description: Cold INSTALL.md walkthrough (slug acme, shop-templates 0.10.0, agent-vault 0.32.0). The rendered bin/agent-vault-provision runs 'vault proposal create -f - --json --vault $VAULT' against the OWNER session (plain docker exec). agent-vault 0.32.0 refuses with: 'Error: Session requires vault scope' — even though the prior owner-session vault create / credential set / service add all succeeded. proposal create (and proposal approve) run in AGENT MODE and require a vault-scoped session. PROVEN FIX (verified live against the acme throwaway broker): mint a scoped session with 'agent-vault vault token --vault $VAULT' (av_sess_...), then run proposal create/approve with -e AGENT_VAULT_TOKEN=<that> -e AGENT_VAULT_ADDR=http://localhost:14321 -e AGENT_VAULT_VAULT=$VAULT. With that scope, create returned {id:1,status:pending,...}, the script's number-parse captured id=1 correctly, and 'proposal approve 1 CLAUDE_OAUTH=<dummy> --yes' applied (CLAUDE_OAUTH then present oauth-typed in vault). FIX SURFACE: shop-templates rendered bin/agent-vault-provision (template). Route as request_bugfix to shopsystem-templates + re-pour; the lead owns no code. NOTE: proposal-number capture itself WORKS (no fix needed there); the only wall is the missing vault-scope session around the proposal step. DISPATCHED 2026-06-15 as request_bugfix lead-9qdn -> shopsystem-templates; batches into templates v0.11.0 with lead-7if5. discovered-from lead-l7uz edge dropped at dispatch (spurious dispatch-guard block, lead-z15d).

Proposal-number capture + all owner-DEXEC steps unchanged; slug-clean. RED->GREEN (e7b4912->b722d85), new test_ops_provision_proposal_scoped_session.py (9) green + lead-yrex proposal tests extended w/ scoped-session assertion; zero newly-failing. title: WS-2 spike wall (iter-5 human gate): provision human-gate prompt instructs credential 'claude-oauth' (kebab) but agent-vault requires SCREAMING_SNAKE — must be CLAUDE_OAUTH
description:
Surfaced by dave at the iter-5 human gate (2026-06-15), AFTER lead-l95x fixed the GitHub credential keys. The rendered bin/agent-vault-provision human-gate prompt (the Claude-OAuth dashboard-paste block, ~lines 193-218; the actionable line is ~210 'Add a credential of type "claude-oauth"') instructs the operator to create the OAuth credential under the kebab-case name 'claude-oauth'. agent-vault 0.32.0 enforces SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE credential KEYS (the same rule lead-l95x fixed for GITHUB_PAT/GITHUB_PAT_USER) — 'claude-oauth' is NOT a valid credential name; it must be CLAUDE_OAUTH. So the one human-gate instruction names an invalid key, and the operator's dashboard paste fails until they know to use CLAUDE_OAUTH.

This is the SAME defect class as lead-l95x (kebab credential key) but in the human-gate PROMPT TEXT rather than a scripted verb — lead-l95x's casing audit covered the github credential-set/service-add args but did NOT sweep the Claude-OAuth dashboard-paste prompt string. FIX: change the human-gate prompt to instruct credential name 'CLAUDE_OAUTH' (SCREAMING_SNAKE). The prose mentions of 'Claude-OAuth' (the human-readable credential description, lines 7/30/193/196/202/205) are fine as prose; the actionable credential-NAME the operator types must be CLAUDE_OAUTH. Owner: shopsystem-templates (bin/agent-vault-provision is shop-templates ops). Vehicle: request_bugfix. Rides a future templates release. ADR-026 D2 addendum (lead-g19j) already records the SCREAMING_SNAKE credential-key rule — this is its application to the OAuth credential. Discovered-from lead-jdfb (iter-5 human gate) + lead-l7uz. Relates lead-l95x, lead-g19j.
notes: FOLDED INTO lead-8jar dispatch (request_bugfix -> shopsystem-templates, 2026-06-15): same file (bin/agent-vault-provision), reconciled together. The human-gate prompt's actionable credential NAME must be CLAUDE_OAUTH (SCREAMING_SNAKE), not kebab 'claude-oauth'. Reconciles+closes when lead-8jar work_done lands (BC may report it separable). Rides the templates release with lead-8jar.

title: WS-2 spike wall (iter-3): rendered compose.yaml agent-vault healthcheck uses bash-ism (exec 3<>/dev/tcp) but the infisical/agent-vault image has no bash — broker reports 'unhealthy' despite serving
description:
Surfaced by dummyco instantiation spike iter-3 (lead-jdfb), 2026-06-12, on the v0.6.0 re-poured host.

WALL: The rendered compose.yaml agent-vault service healthcheck is:
    test: ["CMD-SHELL", "{ exec 3<>/dev/tcp/127.0.0.1/14321; } >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 1"]
The '/dev/tcp/HOST/PORT' redirect is a BASH-ONLY feature. The infisical/agent-vault:latest image has NO bash (command -v bash -> NO-BASH; default shell is sh). In sh the probe fails: 'sh: can't create /dev/tcp/127.0.0.1/14321: nonexistent directory' (exit 1) EVERY interval. Result: dummyco-agent-vault reports 'unhealthy' permanently even though it is fully serving.

PROOF the broker IS up (healthcheck is wrong, not the broker): 'agent-vault version' -> 0.32.0; logs show both listeners ('server listening on http://0.0.0.0:14321', 'transparent proxy listening on 0.0.0.0:14322'); and from inside the container 'nc -z 127.0.0.1 14321' -> NC-OK and 'wget http://127.0.0.1:14321' reaches the API. The image SHIPS /usr/bin/nc and /usr/bin/wget — either is a correct sh-compatible probe.

IMPACT: Non-blocking for the spike's bare bring-up (broker serves regardless), but a perpetual-unhealthy broker WILL block any downstream readiness gate that waits on broker health: ADR-026 D3 'agent_vault_reachable' composition, docker-compose 'depends_on: condition: service_healthy', and Brief 011 bin/agent-vault-check. So it becomes blocking the moment BC launch (gated on broker health, ADR-026 D3) is attempted.

TEMPLATE-DEFECT wall (ADR-031 D2): beaded, not hand-patched. Owner: shopsystem-templates (compose.yaml is shop-templates-owned ops per lead-8cc2). Vehicle: request_bugfix. FIX: replace the agent-vault healthcheck test with an sh-compatible probe the image supports, e.g. CMD nc -z 127.0.0.1 14321  (or wget -q --spider http://127.0.0.1:14321). The postgres healthcheck (pg_isready) is fine and unaffected.
close_reason: RECONCILED via lead-beym (folded dispatch, origin/main b4270d4): compose.yaml agent-vault healthcheck now sh-compatible (test: [CMD, nc, -z, 127.0.0.1, <port>]); bash-only /dev/tcp removed; the image's shipped /usr/bin/nc is the probe. Served broker should now report healthy (validated in iter-4). Rides v0.7.0.
