description: User correction 2026-06-12: 'The lead shop is an instance of shopsystem, it does not own any code. Any modifications here would not make it into a new product instance. Everything must go into shop-templates.' VERIFIED: shop-templates ships templates/ops/{compose.yaml,shop-shell,Dockerfile.shopsystem-shell}; this repo's compose.yaml + bin/shop-shell are POURED INSTANCE COPIES. ROOT CAUSE of confusion: (1) files physically present in instance look editable-in-place; (2) mislabeled them 'ADR-028 lead-host supporting-services' (ADR-028 = broker is a SERVICE, not lead owns compose code); (3) applied ADR-037 self-hosting to role-templates/spec but NOT to ops/ infra. CORRECT MODEL: lead owns documents (briefs/PDR/ADR/spec/scenarios/findings) + instance config (manifest entries/.env/.beads); shop-templates owns ALL code/infra (ops/, claude/ role templates, skills/); BCs own implementation. Then release+re-pour reconciles the instance (WS-4 pour-back pattern). GOING FORWARD: edit templates FIRST; the lead never edits code. Extend/cross-link ADR-037.
notes: STRANDED-EDIT PORTING COMPLETE (2026-06-12): all this session's in-place ops/ edits are now in shop-templates via lead-faua (compose+shop-shell+Dockerfile+healthcheck+POSTGRES_PASSWORD+broker-wiring) and lead-w87b (agent-vault-provision+check). title: no first-class lead->BC 'answer a clarify' vehicle — shop-msg respond is BC->lead only; lead must re-dispatch (heavyweight) or nudge (transmission-only, not consumed)
description:
MECHANISM FINDING (surfaced answering shopsystem-bc-launcher's clarify on lead-uol6, 2026-06-12).

EMPIRICAL CLI BEHAVIOR:
- shop-msg respond REFUSES a lead-side caller: 'respond' is BC->lead only (BC answers a lead, or emits work_done/clarify TO the lead). The lead has no 'respond clarify' path to answer a BC's clarify.
- The only lead->BC paths that exist are: (a) RE-DISPATCH a fresh work_id carrying the refined directive (heavyweight: new bead, full request_bugfix/assign_scenarios, supersedes the prior work_id); (b) NUDGE (ADR-015) — transmission-only, general (not bound to a work_id), and may never be consumed by the BC's gated loop.
- In lead-uol6 we used both: a nudge was sent first (best-effort), then the authoritative answer was re-dispatched as lead-53y0 (the refined WS-1.3).

QUESTION FOR THE FRAMEWORK:
Is re-dispatch-with-refined-scope the INTENDED clarify-answer mechanism — i.e. the lead REFINES (authors a sharper directive) and never 'chats' a free-text answer back to the BC — making 'no lead->BC clarify-response' a deliberate design property? OR is a first-class lead->BC clarify-response message type MISSING from the §5.3 message-type catalogue (BC asks, lead answers in-band on the same work_id, BC's gated loop consumes the answer and proceeds without a new bead)?

Tension: re-dispatch is correct when the clarify reveals genuinely underspecified scope (lead-uol6 case — the answer changed the contract). But for a pure information clarify ('which env var?', 'does X exist?') re-dispatch is heavyweight and nudge is unreliable (not guaranteed consumed). A bounded in-band lead->BC answer would close that gap.

RELATES: §5.3 message-type catalogue / lead-vqg9; ADR-015 (nudge).
ROUTE NOTE: if a first-class vehicle is wanted, it is a messaging-BC capability -> assign_scenarios -> shopsystem-messaging (BC has no such message type today; new behavior). If the answer is 're-dispatch IS the intended mechanism', this resolves as a doctrine/§5.3 note, no code.
notes:
STRONG FRESH EVIDENCE (2026-06-23 overnight): this gap forced TWO fresh-bead re-dispatches during the PDR-020 slice-2 convergence (lead-6ox4 clarify -> nudge-answer NOT consumed -> re-issue as lead-mgwb; lead-mgwb clarify -> nudge NOT consumed -> re-issue as lead-4c3z). Each clarify cost a full fresh-bead request_bugfix because (a) shop-msg respond is BC->lead only, (b) a same-work_id re-send hits the one-row-per-(bc,work_id) invariant, (c) a nudge --note is transmission-only and the BC loop does NOT consume it as the clarify answer. Net: answering a clarify is heavyweight + lossy. Prioritized P1. Fix needs design (first-class lead->BC clarify-answer vehicle that the BC loop consumes); pairs with lead-fnj5 gate-bug. Flag for product authority.
=== ARCHITECT PROPOSAL (2026-06-23, contract-surface analysis) ===

RECOMMENDATION: Build a first-class lead->BC clarify-answer vehicle, but as a BOUNDED in-band primitive that resolves the tension — NOT a general re-litigation of ADR-009/ADR-027.

WHY THIS IS NOT ALREADY SETTLED: ADR-009 + ADR-027 already pin re-dispatch as the clarify-resolution vehicle, and ADR-027 explicitly says the lead REFINES via a fresh catalog dispatch and never uses `respond`. That doctrine is CORRECT for the SCOPE-CHANGE clarify (the lead-uol6 case: the answer changed the contract -> a new scenario set genuinely belongs on a fresh work_id). The gap ADR-009 itself left open is path (a) — the native `clarify_response` primitive — DEFERRED to lead-ox8 (P3) because "operational pressure had not yet exceeded the friction of path (b)." The PDR-020 slice-2 evidence (lead-6ox4 -> lead-mgwb -> lead-4c3z, TWO fresh-bead re-dispatches forced by nudge-answers not being consumed) is exactly that pressure crossing the threshold. This proposal RE-PRIORITIZES lead-ox8 from P3->P1 and SHARPENS its semantics, rather than inventing a new initiative.

THE DISCRIMINATOR (resolves the tension the bead names):
  - SCOPE-CHANGE clarify (answer changes the contract / adds or tightens scenarios) -> STILL re-dispatch (ADR-009 layer b / ADR-027). Unchanged. This is the lead REFINING.
  - INFORMATION clarify (pure fact: "which env var?", "does X exist?", "socket or network?") — answer does NOT change scenario coverage -> the new bounded `clarify_response` vehicle. Lead answers in-band on the SAME work_id; the BC's gated loop consumes the answer and proceeds; NO new bead.
  The discriminator is whether the answer carries scenario_hashes. If it would -> it is a re-dispatch (assign_scenarios/request_bugfix), by definition. If it carries only free-text resolution and NO scenario state -> it is clarify_response.

ROUTE: shopsystem-messaging capability, NEW behavior (the BC has no such message type today — verified against §5.3 catalogue: the eight types carry no lead->BC `clarify_response` row; ADR-027 confirms no lead->BC `respond` row exists). Vehicle = assign_scenarios to shopsystem-messaging. This is the lead-ox8 scope.

MESSAGE-TYPE SEMANTICS SKETCH (sharpening lead-ox8 / ADR-009 path a):
  1. CLI: `shop-msg send clarify_response --bc <name> --work-id <id> --resolution <text>` (NO --scenario-file; if scenarios are needed it is a re-dispatch, not a clarify_response — this is the bounded constraint that keeps the two cases distinct).
  2. Schema: new ClarifyResponse Pydantic class; carries work_id + resolution text; MUST NOT carry scenario_hashes (mirrors the nudge no-scenario-state constraint, ADR-015 decision 7). This is what mechanically enforces the discriminator.
  3. Storage: lead->BC send path opts into allow_multi_type=True for clarify_response (storage.py allow_multi_type flag per ADR-009) so the clarify_response row COEXISTS with the original assign_scenarios/request_bugfix row on the same (bc, work_id) — does NOT collide with the one-row-per-(bc,work_id,message_type) invariant.
  4. BC-loop consumption (the load-bearing decision, ADR-009 design-question #3): receipt of clarify_response RE-OPENS the original dispatch for the BC's gated loop to resume on the SAME work_id — it is the answer to the clarify the BC already emitted, not a standalone pinned doc. The BC loop consumes it as the clarify-answer and continues; no fresh bead, no supersession.
  5. Precondition: clarify_response is only valid when a prior BC clarify exists on that (bc, work_id). Absent one -> exit non-zero (a clarify_response with nothing to answer is operator error).

WHY NOT "re-dispatch IS intended, close as doctrine": that answer is already RECORDED (ADR-027 + ADR-009 layer b) and the live evidence shows it is insufficient for the INFORMATION case specifically — re-dispatch is heavyweight (full fresh bead + payload) and nudge is lossy (ADR-015 decision 6/7: transmission-only, not loop-consumed, no work_id-bound answer semantics). Keeping ONLY re-dispatch leaves the information-clarify case paying scope-change cost. The bounded vehicle closes precisely that gap WITHOUT eroding the refine-not-chat discipline (because scope-change clarifies still re-dispatch).

PAIRS WITH lead-fnj5: every forced re-dispatch in the q616 evidence ALSO had to pass the dispatch-gate, which is itself buggy (fnj5). The two compound: clarify answering is both heavyweight (q616) AND gate-blocked (fnj5). Landing clarify_response reduces the NUMBER of fresh dispatches that must clear the gate at all.

CAVEAT (ADR-018): the storage.py line refs above are carried from ADR-009's prior record; the lead host has no repos/shopsystem-messaging source to re-verify them. The BC owns confirming the exact storage mechanism at implementation.
MECHANISM_OBSERVATION 2026-06-23 from shopsystem-messaging (via lead-r8di adversarial review, provenance 166a51c) — LOAD-BEARING for q616-family gate-bug reconciliation: The q616/fnj5/r8di gate-bug recurrence was NEVER a source defect. src/shop_msg/bd_facade.py NON_GATING_DEPENDENCY_TYPES ALREADY includes 'relates-to' (+ related, discovered-from, caused-by, validates, supersedes, tracks) and list_depends_on already excludes those from gating — so the LIVE-consultation contract (scenario 48ade065ce073a54) was ALREADY correct in source. The live violation we observed this session (reclassify-to-relates-to did NOT clear the gate while full 'bd dep remove' DID) was a STALE site-packages SHADOW: a frozen non-editable shop_msg/catalog copy carrying an outdated {parent-child}-only NON_GATING set shadowed the editable /workspace/src checkout. Same failure mode as shopsystem-messaging-c1f, now guarded by the conftest collection-time editable-install guard (origin/main 1fba988 / 71cf840). DISPOSITION: treat the q616-family gate-bug as CLOSED at the source/contract level (always correct there). Any FIELD recurrence = install-shadow issue (re-run pip install -e), NOT a gate-logic defect. STOPGAP RETIRABLE: any lead-side doctrine that treated reclassify-not-clearing as a source gate defect is now retired — the diagnostic flips to 'check for stale install shadow' first.
close_reason: RESOLVED-BY lead-ox8 (2026-06-23). The architect proposal recorded on this bead (bounded first-class lead->BC clarify_response vehicle, discriminator = whether the answer carries scenario_hashes) was authored as features/messaging/30-33, dispatched as assign_scenarios -> shopsystem-messaging (lead-ox8), and SHIPPED: shopsystem-messaging work_done reconciled, all 4 scenario hashes verified against lead-held features. The design question q616 raised is answered (bounded in-band vehicle, NOT a re-litigation of ADR-009/ADR-027: scope-change clarifies still re-dispatch; information clarifies use clarify_response, loop-consumed on same work_id, no new bead) AND the vehicle is implemented. Gate-bug subthread (fnj5/r8di) already dispositioned via the recorded mechanism_observation: gate logic was always correct at source; field recurrences = stale install shadow, re-run pip install -e. HOST-CLI CAVEAT: lead-host 'shop-msg send' does not yet expose clarify_response (stale install, same as lead-vu8u read-outbox lag) — operationally usable from this host only after the messaging release is re-poured; cross-linked lead-vu8u. dep edge to lead-0lml is relates-to (closed, non-gating) — no dep-remove needed.

title: WS-1.3 (refined): bc-launcher RESOLVES system slug from manifest product: and INJECTS -e SHOPMSG_SYSTEM_SLUG into the launched container (ADR-038); supersedes lead-uol6
description:
Re-dispatch of WS-1.3 as the CLI-sanctioned answer to shopsystem-bc-launcher's clarify on lead-uol6 (shop-msg respond is BC->lead only; a clarify is answered by RE-DISPATCH with refined scope). EDGE-FREE: references lead-uol6 / lead-t12k / ADR-038 by text only.

CLARIFY CONTEXT (lead-uol6): bc-launcher noted SHOPMSG_SYSTEM_SLUG does NOT exist in its code (it lives in messaging, lead-tgsb); so 'make product: the system-slug default' was undefined at its BC boundary. The original lead-uol6 acceptance conflated the launcher's resolution job with messaging's consumption job. This refined directive resolves that.

REFINED CHANGE (shopsystem-bc-launcher, request_bugfix):
- bc-launcher RESOLVES + INJECTS, does NOT consume. Precedence: SHOPMSG_SYSTEM_SLUG env (launcher invocation) > manifest product: > hard default 'shopsystem'.
- INJECT the resolved slug into the launched BC container's docker run as -e SHOPMSG_SYSTEM_SLUG=<resolved>, mirroring SHOPMSG_DSN injection. CONSUMER is the BC's own shop-msg at runtime (messaging / lead-tgsb), NOT bc-launcher.
- Unify all three identity surfaces on the ONE product: resolver: docker network name, BC-name-shape prefix, AND injected system slug (env override on top).
- Schema/doc: add product: to the manifest schema + document (ADR-038 D1).

ACCEPTANCE PIN:
- FakeDockerDriver recorded docker run carries -e SHOPMSG_SYSTEM_SLUG=dummyco when product: dummyco, no override.
- SHOPMSG_SYSTEM_SLUG env override wins over manifest product:.
- product: absent or 'shopsystem' (no override) injects 'shopsystem' (or omits) WITHOUT regressing current behavior (additive; honors lead-xntx default-slug guarantee).

PRE-STATE (ADR-018 D1, contract/artifact surface, 2026-06-12):
- SHOPMSG_DSN injection precedent: features/bc-launcher/13 + /14 pin 'the container has SHOPMSG_DSN set'.
- Observable surface: features/bc-launcher/18-network-naming-01 asserts FakeDockerDriver records '--network shopsystem-product' from manifest product: — same recorded-docker-run surface.
- @scenario_hash retirement enumeration: EMPTY (grep -r @scenario_hash over features/bc-launcher/13,14,18-26 returns none; BC register scenario_hashes:[]). Additive; nothing retired/superseded/contradicted.

DISPATCH: request_bugfix -> shopsystem-bc-launcher, work_id lead-53y0, 2026-06-12. Landed in BC inbox, read-back verified, scenarios:[]. Awaiting work_done to reconcile. SUPERSEDES lead-uol6 for completion tracking.
close_reason: Closed

title: WS-1.3: bc-launcher reads manifest product: as the product-identity source (ADR-038) — derive slug/network/name-prefix from it, env overrides on top
description:

The BC's clarify revealed the original scope was underspecified at the bc-launcher boundary: SHOPMSG_SYSTEM_SLUG does not exist in bc-launcher (lives in messaging/lead-tgsb), so 'derive system slug end-to-end' conflated bc-launcher's RESOLVE+INJECT job with messaging's CONSUME job. Refined directive re-dispatched as lead-53y0 (bc-launcher RESOLVES product slug + INJECTS -e SHOPMSG_SYSTEM_SLUG into the launched container; messaging consumes at runtime). Clarify answered by re-dispatch (shop-msg respond is BC->lead only; lead cannot respond to a clarify — must re-dispatch). Completion tracking moves to lead-53y0.

[Original description retained in history.] Per ADR-038, WS-1.3 of lead-t12k externalize-identity-constants; closes the WS-1 set.
notes:
CLARIFY RESOLVED 2026-06-12 (delivered as general nudge vs work_id lead-uol6 — lead cannot 'shop-msg respond'; CLI refuses lead-side respond and directs send/nudge; no re-dispatch, existing work_id unblocked).

Options (a) new SHOPMSG_SYSTEM_SLUG resolution+inject, (b) treat as same name-shape slug, (c) out of scope.

ANSWER: option (a) refined — bc-launcher RESOLVES system slug from product: and INJECTS -e SHOPMSG_SYSTEM_SLUG=<resolved> into the launched container; does NOT consume it (messaging consumes at runtime). Decomposition sound at contract surface: mirrors existing SHOPMSG_DSN injection (features bc-launcher/13,/14 pin 'container has SHOPMSG_DSN set'); observable on FakeDockerDriver recorded docker run, the same surface bc-launcher/18 asserts '--network shopsystem-product'. Refined acceptance relayed: (1) precedence SHOPMSG_SYSTEM_SLUG env>product:>'shopsystem'; (2) observable = -e SHOPMSG_SYSTEM_SLUG on docker run; (3) unify network + name-shape + injected-slug on ONE product: resolver; (4) schema/doc add product: (ADR-038 D1); (5) default preserved/additive. NOT (b), NOT (c).

description: Refinement of lead-rfc5 (WS-1 BC_NAME_RE genericity). lead-rfc5 BC self-blocked: wiring the formerly-dead BC_NAME_RE into validate() newly REJECTED default-slug entries validate() previously accepted (empirical: acme-widget default slug -> ok=True before, ok=False after), regressing the additive-only / shopsystem-accepted-set-unchanged pin. description: Surfaced by WS-0 spike iter-2 (2026-06-12, empirical vs installed bc_launcher controller.py — ADR-018 D2 tool surface). bc_launcher/controller.py:32 BC_IMAGE='ghcr.io/dstengle/shopsystem-bc-base:latest' is a hard module constant, doubly product-locked (dstengle namespace + shopsystem name); controller.py:726 launch_image = BC_IMAGE hard-uses it. bc-container launch exposes --repo-url/--shopmsg-dsn/--network/--agent-vault-broker/--env-file/--startup-prompt but NO --image flag and reads no BC_IMAGE env. title: using-git-worktrees skill places worktrees OUTSIDE /workspace (../worktrees/<id>) — stalls containerized BCs at the out-of-project permission wall
description:
EMPIRICAL REPRODUCTION (2026-06-12): the shopsystem-messaging BC stalled mid-dispatch on lead-tgsb, parked at a Claude-Code permission prompt probing 'touch ../_wtest'. Root cause: the bc-implementer canonical template directs the BC to 'operate inside the work_id worktree the router created' (using-git-worktrees skill), and that skill creates the per-work_id worktree at the convention path ../worktrees/<id> — the PARENT of /workspace, OUTSIDE the project root. In a containerized BC (/workspace is the bind-mount), any write outside /workspace trips a hard Claude-Code permission prompt EVEN IN BYPASS MODE, and the container root is not writable by the vscode user. The ../worktrees/<id> convention is host-developer-shaped and breaks in-container. I manually steered the messaging BC to an in-/workspace path to unblock lead-tgsb; THIS bead is the durable fix.

FIX: the using-git-worktrees skill (and any bc-implementer/bc-reviewer template prose that names the worktree path) must place worktrees INSIDE /workspace — e.g. /workspace/.worktrees/<work_id> — and ensure /workspace/.worktrees/ is gitignored so the isolated worktree does not pollute the main checkout.

ACCEPTANCE: a containerized BC creates the work_id worktree under /workspace (e.g. /workspace/.worktrees/<work_id>), .worktrees/ is gitignored, and the BC never attempts a write outside /workspace.

Dispatched to shopsystem-templates via request_bugfix (tightening unpinned skill/template behavior). No Gherkin: no lead-held scenario pins implementer worktree placement (spike-lifecycle 02/03 pin spike-scratch teardown/isolation only).
close_reason: Closed

title: FINDING: PO in-file @scenario_hash pins computed over MULTI-LINE tag layout don't match dispatch wire hashes (single-line tags, body-only) — features/messaging/26-29 + features/scenario-journal/09-10
description:
Observed while dispatching lead-f1ui (messaging) and lead-8z3f (scenarios) on 2026-06-12.

ROOT CAUSE: 'scenarios hash' strips a tag block ONLY when all tags sit space-separated on a SINGLE line directly above 'Scenario:'. When @bc and @scenario_hash are on SEPARATE lines (as the PO authored features/messaging/26-29 and features/scenario-journal/09-10), 'scenarios hash' over the full file does NOT strip them — the @bc line is folded into the hash input. So the PO's in-file @scenario_hash pins are hashes of (@bc + body):
  - 26->1a74780383687ce6  27->761fb4d48a4aa2bc  28->1d2fd105fec8bacc  29->7013321b3d8d8e1f
  - 09->fb4b2e113070fc1e  10->8e4a5e72eb8e220c

The dispatch CLI ('shop-msg send assign_scenarios --scenario-file') emits both tags on ONE line in the deposited gherkin (the wire form), which 'scenarios hash' DOES strip -> body-only hash. Those are the hashes that actually landed and that the BCs will reconcile against:
  - 26->65c85ffce8f88507  27->7afa72ede6099ee1  28->79dd275d8584c8fc  29->5138b2335150db4c
  - 09->60403e1ba2035031  10->79acbeb40154dfed

CONSEQUENCE: each deposit is internally self-consistent (deposited hash == scenarios hash of deposited gherkin), so the BCs are NOT broken — but the canonical features/*.gherkin @scenario_hash tags DRIFT from the landed/register hashes. Reconciliation of lead-f1ui/lead-8z3f work_done must expect the body-only family, not the PO-pinned family.

FIX OPTIONS (PO/Architect): (a) re-pin the features/ @scenario_hash tags to the body-only/wire hashes so canonical source matches the register; OR (b) standardize tag layout to single-line so 'scenarios hash' strips uniformly and PO pin == wire hash; OR (c) make 'scenarios hash' strip own-line tags too (scenarios-BC change — would be assign_scenarios/request_bugfix). Recommend (a)+(b): a PO maintenance pass to put @scenario_hash + @bc on one line and recompute pins to the stripped/body hash.

RELATES: lead-f1ui, lead-8z3f (the two dispatches carrying the drift); ADR-010 (hash discipline).
notes: RESOLVED 2026-06-12 via fix option (a)+(b) [recommended in this bead]: single-lined @bc @scenario_hash and re-pinned @scenario_hash to the body-only/wire-form hashes in all six drifted files. features/scenario-journal/09 -> 60403e1ba2035031, 10 -> 79acbeb40154dfed; features/messaging/26 -> 65c85ffce8f88507, 27 -> 7afa72ede6099ee1, 28 -> 79dd275d8584c8fc, 29 -> 5138b2335150db4c. Verified each: 'scenarios hash' over body == in-file @scenario_hash pin (pin==body, all six OK). Canonical features/ pins now match the landed BC registers (lead-8z3f reconciled cleanly; lead-f1ui messaging 26-29 will reconcile cleanly when it returns). Closing.
close_reason: Closed

title: WS-4 part 2: make canonical role templates self-contained (inline doctrine, demote/drop §x citations) + extract situational guidance to skills (ADR-037)
description:
Per ADR-037 (adr/037-framework-spec-is-system-construction-not-shipped-to-products.md) D2/D3. Tracking parent WS-4 = lead-hyxx (referenced by TEXT, NOT a bd edge, to keep this bead edge-free so it dispatches without a guard workaround; tracked in lead-j4ne).

D2 (self-contained): the canonical role templates shipped by shopsystem-templates cite framework-spec sections (§3.2, §3.4, §6, §4.4) and a prototype finding (findings-from-prototype-1.md §5) that do NOT resolve in a freshly-instantiated product. Inline the operative doctrine each citation refers to (templates already largely restate it), demote §x refs to bare provenance footnotes OR drop them — they must NOT be load-bearing. The findings-from-prototype-1.md §5 ref: inline the durable rule, drop the prototype-narrative footnote.

D3 (situational->skill): extract genuinely situational guidance — notably the turn-limited PO<->Architect decomposition exchange (lead-architect §3.4 guidance) — into a PDR-014 skill-group skill, loaded on demand, rather than ambient template prose.

Targets: canonical lead-architect.md and lead-po.md (and any other role template carrying dangling §x/findings citations).

Acceptance: a fresh product's role templates carry no load-bearing external §x/findings citation; situational guidance is a loadable skill.

Dispatched to shopsystem-templates as request_bugfix 2026-06-12 (dispatch_state=dispatched).
close_reason: Reconciled WS-4 part 2 (ADR-037 D2/D3). work_done: role templates made self-contained on shopsystem-templates origin/main edca834 — D2 swept all role templates (lead-architect ×6, lead-po ×2 incl §3.3, bc-reviewer §4.4 ×2; bc-implementer already clean), dangling §x/findings citations dropped/demoted to non-load-bearing; D3 turn-limited PO<->Architect decomposition exchange extracted to loadable PDR-014 skill po-architect-decomposition-exchange/SKILL.md. TDD red->green, gate clean, no scenario-hash (request_bugfix, no new scenarios). Contract-surface verification via installed shop-templates show is BLOCKED by package-lag (installed v0.2.0 predates edca834; lead-5wky) — verified against work_done demonstration + mechanism_observation per ADR-018 fallback. Both outbox rows (work_done + mechanism_observation) consumed. Follow-up lead-n4r2 filed for the version-bump+tag delivery step (mechanism_observation point 3).

title: ADR: framework spec is a system-construction artifact, not shipped to product instances; product templates self-contained, situational doctrine -> skills
description: User principle (2026-06-12): untangle 'constructing products WITH the system' from 'constructing the system itself'. The framework spec §1-6 describes the SYSTEM's own design; a product built with the system needs self-contained role templates + skills, NOT the framework's self-description. Decision: (1) framework spec §1-6 stays in the framework/lead repo; (2) product role templates inline their operative doctrine (no load-bearing external §x citations — provenance footnotes at most); (3) conditional/situational guidance lives in skills. Drives WS-4 Part 2 (lead-hyxx). Author: lead-architect. System-global tier per ADR-034/035 hierarchy.
notes: ADR-037 DRAFTED (adr/037-framework-spec-is-system-construction-not-shipped-to-products.md). D1: spec §1-6 stays in framework repo, NOT shipped to products (reverses ship-the-spec). D2: product role templates self-contained — inline doctrine, §x citations are provenance-only/dropped. D3: situational guidance -> skills. D4: author test (operative=inline / situational=skill / system-self-description=framework spec). System-global tier. WS-4 Part 2 = templates request_bugfix to make templates self-contained (Part 3 absorbed). Nit flagged: ADR-034 cites (PDR-003) where it means PDR-002 -> separate one-line touch-up.
close_reason: Closed

title: shop-msg dispatch guard treats parent-child epic edge as a gating predecessor — blocks dispatching any child of an open epic
description:
The shop-msg dispatch guard (PDR-010 / ADR-013 decision 4) reads the bd dependency graph and refuses to dispatch a bead if any predecessor is not in a dispatched/closed state. The guard currently gates on ALL dependency edges including dependency_type: parent-child edges. A parent-child edge to an epic is a CONTAINER relation, not a dispatch predecessor — an epic is open for its entire lifetime by design, so gating on its parent-child edge makes EVERY child of an open epic permanently undispatchable.

DEFECT: the guard must gate ONLY on true depends-on / blocks edges and MUST EXCLUDE parent-child (epic-container) edges from the predecessor set.

EMPIRICAL REPRODUCTION (contract surface, ADR-018): shop-msg send request_bugfix for work_id=lead-tgsb was refused with refusing to dispatch ... predecessor 'lead-wm2r' is at dispatch_state='open'. lead-tgsb's SOLE dependency edge to lead-wm2r is parent-child (epic container), confirmed via bd dep list lead-tgsb -> lead-wm2r ... (open) via parent-child. lead-wm2r is the WS-0..WS-9 epic; it is open by container design. Interim workaround: reparent-remove -> dispatch -> reparent-restore. That workaround must not be the standing path — the guard itself is wrong. NOTE: dispatch of lead-j4ne itself also hit this defect via its relates-to edge to lead-wm2r; worked around with bd dep unrelate -> dispatch -> bd dep relate restore. So the over-gating applies to relates-to edges too, not only parent-child.

DISPATCHED 2026-06-12 as request_bugfix -> shopsystem-messaging, work_id=lead-j4ne.
close_reason: Closed

title: WS-0 wall #1: registry projects every shop under hard-coded shopsystem/ slug — dummy product cannot get a distinct ADR-020 address (defeats gate condition 2)
description:
WALL surfaced by the WS-0 dummy-product instantiation spike (lead-jdfb / PDR-018), iteration 1, conditions 1-3.

DUMMY IDENTITY USED: system slug 'dummyco', org 'acme', one BC 'acme-widget'.

DOCUMENTED PATH FOLLOWED (no hand-edits, no shopsystem/dstengle hard-codes typed in):
  1. shop-templates bootstrap --shop-type lead --shop-name dummyco-product --target /tmp/spike-dummyco-lead  -> rc=0, scaffolds lead structure, briefs/adr/pdr/features EMPTY (gate condition 1 PASS).
  2. shop-msg registry add acme-widget  -> rc=0.
  3. shop-msg registry list  -> 'acme-widget shopsystem/acme-widget bc'.

THE WALL (empirical, contract surface per ADR-018):
The dummy BC's ADR-020 abstract address projects as 'shopsystem/acme-widget' — under the hard-coded 'shopsystem' SYSTEM slug — NOT under the dummy product's slug. There is NO documented surface to set the system segment:
  - 'shop-msg registry add' flags are only [name] [--lead-shop]; no --system/--slug.
  - env override does not help: 'SYSTEM_SLUG=dummyco shop-msg registry add acme-widget3' still projected 'shopsystem/acme-widget3'.
This is the SILENT cross-product routing failure ADR-020 + WS-1.1 forecast: a message addressed to the dummy BC would deposit under shopsystem/<name>, an address a genuinely-distinct dummy product BC would not read.

GATE CONDITION DEFEATED: PDR-018 condition #2 (distinct identity end-to-end; discriminating check = abstract address must project under the dummy slug, not shopsystem/<name>). Conditions 1 PASSED; 3 not reachable past this.

MAPS TO: WS-1.1 (lead-t12k bundles it). Per WORKPLAN + PDR-018 WS-1.5 surface, the messaging slug constant is at shopsystem-messaging storage.py:222 (_abstract_address_for) — BC source, NOT on lead host (ADR-018); wall observed at the contract surface (registry list projection) only.

ROUTING RECOMMENDATION (for the router to decide): fix is BC-owned -> shopsystem-messaging, request_bugfix. The behavior (abstract addressing) EXISTS and is pinned by features/messaging-registry/ scenarios (ADR-020) but the SYSTEM segment is an un-externalized constant -> tightening existing unpinned behavior = request_bugfix, not assign_scenarios. Derive the system slug from the registered lead's system name / env (SHOPMSG_DSN-pattern), not a module constant.

ISOLATION (ADR-030): probe registry entries acme-widget/2/3 were removed after observation; shared registry verified clean + intact. Scratch lead at /tmp/spike-dummyco-lead (spike-prefixed, throwaway).

ADDITIONAL OBSERVATIONS (collateral, not this bead's scope — noted for WS-1.2/1.3/WS-2):
  - bc-container launch has NO --image/--bc-image flag (WS-1.2 BC_IMAGE override surface confirmed absent).
  - bc-manifest.yaml has NO product: field; ADR-005 never defines it (WS-1.3 confirmed implemented-but-undocumented-unused).
  - scaffold bakes 'shopsystem' into compose.yaml (POSTGRES_DB/USER/PASSWORD=shopsystem, network name 'shopsystem') and filename Dockerfile.shopsystem-shell (WS-2 bootstrap prereqs + WS-1.4 territory).
close_reason: Closed

close_reason: EPIC COMPLETE 2026-06-15 — Framework genericity: path to a second product ACHIEVED. The MVP acceptance test (the dummy-product instantiation spike, lead-jdfb/WS-0, rec #1) PASSED iter-7: all 10 PDR-018 gate conditions hold; dummyco instantiated E2E as a real 2nd product with a real implemented feature (greet-by-name), distinct identity projecting dummyco/<name>, own postgres+broker+network, Claude via its own broker, §6.4 reconcile clean, live fleet undisturbed. WS-1 (identity constants: SYSTEM_SLUG/BC_IMAGE/BC_NAME_RE/product: under ADR-038) + WS-2 (bootstrap path: ops genericity, provision, Brief 011) surfaced+validated by the spike and shipped across templates v0.5.0-v0.9.0 + messaging v0.4.0. title: dispatch request_bugfix -> shopsystem-bc-launcher: provision bd via bd bootstrap (supersedes lead-kjv7)
description: Re-dispatch launcher bd-provisioning fix with NOW-PROVEN bd bootstrap recipe. Supersedes lead-kjv7 (pull+import failed empirical gate, worsened the wedge). PROVEN empirically: fresh BC clone with committed .beads/issues.jsonl + no pre-existing bd-created Dolt working set -> 'bd bootstrap' imports 21 issues, creates .beads/embeddeddolt/, bd create/ready succeed, fully non-destructive. Recipe: do NOT bd dolt pull first; do NOT bd config set issue_prefix (rejected) or separate bd import (pre-creates DB -> deadlock); ensure committed issues.jsonl present (git checkout HEAD -- .beads/issues.jsonl if gitignored), THEN bd bootstrap; run as vscode (or chown .beads to vscode after); fake-driver BDD must model real bootstrap import flow; empirical gate = lead launches real BC, BC cannot self-verify (no container runtime), defers to lead reconciliation. Conflicting BC-side scenario_hash set: f4ebaa3f7559a84a (bc-launcher/03 'bd dolt pull has been run') CONTRADICTED -> retire/rewrite; 1f1d178bca957fbc (bc-launcher/32 functional-bd, When-step asserts 'bd dolt pull') mechanism SUPERSEDED -> rewrite step. Dispatched via shop-msg; reconcile via real-launch verification.
close_reason: DONE — lead-implemented + LAUNCH-VERIFIED. The BC's bootstrap-first approach was correct but its impl corrupted the repo on real launch (bd bootstrap as root -> root-owned .git -> agent git Permission-denied -> phantom mass deletions; + .beads/.claude re-rooted at bootstrap/refresh time). Per the launcher critical-path mandate (the BC has no container runtime to verify provisioning, so 3 dispatch round-trips shipped broken fixes), the lead implemented the fix directly and verified by real launch: bd bootstrap AS VSCODE on a vscode-owned tree + defensive re-chown -R vscode immediately before bootstrap AND before shop-templates update + bootstrap exit-code check. RESULT (fresh launch, rc=0): .beads vscode-owned, embeddeddolt created, 0 phantom deletions, bd create/ready EXIT 0 with NO manual heal, .git writable, bc-router health skill delivered (11 matches). Commits 2b7ba61 + 2043599; SHIPPED in release v0.2.8 (36b5b8d). lead-kjv7 superseded-by this; lead-vlsu (bd bootstrap is the non-destructive path) confirmed.

title: bc-launcher skill-refresh: invalid 'shop-templates pour --workspace' call + false-success log — lead-80t0 health skill never poured to launched BCs

PROVEN launcher-side root cause: controller.py:895 execs ['shop-templates','pour','--workspace',ws] but shop-templates has NO 'pour' subcommand (valid: list/show/bootstrap/update) and flag is --target not --workspace -> exec FAILS every launch; launcher LOGS FALSE SUCCESS ('Poured ...') without checking result -> skill-refresh has silently NEVER run -> launched BC keeps its committed stale .claude/skills/bc-router/SKILL.md (dstengle/shopsystem-scenarios: 6613 bytes/111 lines/0 health). v0.2.7 image shop-templates is CURRENT (143 lines/11 health, lead-80t0 present). close_reason: EMPIRICAL GATE PASSED (lead ran the real BC launch). Upgraded lead CLI to bc-launcher origin/main HEAD eaaa395 (carries q5k7 fix 5742d3f), launched shopsystem-scenarios fresh. Launch log now: 'Refreshed shop-templates skill-group (shop-type=bc) into /workspace/.claude/skills/' (was the broken 'Poured...'). VERIFIED in-container: poured .claude/skills/bc-router/SKILL.md = 143 lines, 14 health/dolt matches — lead-80t0's health step (auto-heal/validate/test-dolt-push/hard-block) IS NOW DELIVERED to a launched BC, overwriting the repo's committed stale 111-line/0-health copy. Fix: controller.py execs 'shop-templates update --target <ws> --shop-type <bc|lead>' (valid cmd), checks exec result, no false-success log. Ships in the next bc-launcher release (v0.2.8). NOTE: 2 pre-existing suite failures (test_bc_base_framework_cli_pins, test_bc_base_shop_templates_pin) are from MY v0.2.7 SHA pin on shop-templates — fix by bumping that pin to templates v0.3.0 at the v0.2.8 release (tracked on lead-slkk followup).

title: dispatch request_bugfix -> shopsystem-bc-launcher (lead-kjv7)
notes:
EMPIRICAL GATE FAILED (lead ran the real BC launch the dispatch required). Launched shopsystem-scenarios on the kjv7 CLI (origin/main 1613244). Result: NOT write-ready. .beads IS now vscode-owned (D3 ok) BUT issue_prefix is '(not set)' and 'bd create' fails 'database not initialized: issue_prefix config is missing'. ROOT CAUSE — kjv7's explicit 'bd import' (step 4) pushes the embedded-Dolt backend INTO the lead-vlsu DEADLOCK state ('database already exists' + no prefix), where ALL documented non-destructive recovery is blocked: bd bootstrap='nothing to do', bd init=aborts, bd config set issue_prefix=REJECTED ('cannot be set via bd config set'). This is WORSE than the original wedge: the proven manual heal (bd config set side-effect auto-import) only fires on a FRESH truly-empty Dolt; kjv7's import destroys that precondition. Also D1 prefix-read STILL fell back to name-derived 'scenarios' (committed 'shopsystem-scenarios') — the cat/extract returned empty at provisioning time. The ONLY path to write-ready bd found is DESTRUCTIVE: 'bd init --reinit-local --prefix <committed>' (interactive 'will destroy' warning) — then bd create works. CONCLUSION: lead-kjv7 cannot be correctly solved with non-destructive bd commands while the bd init-safety deadlock (lead-vlsu) stands. BLOCKED-BY lead-vlsu. Options: (A) fix lead-vlsu first; (B) launcher adopts non-interactive '--reinit-local' destructive reinit + import (prove a clean recipe); (C) lean on the lead-80t0 heal-skill (lead-q5k7). NOTE the fake-driver BDD passed because it models bd as the BC THINKS it behaves, not real bd — provisioning correctness is untestable without a real container+bd.
SUPERSEDED-BY lead-ezzr (2026-06-11, lead-architect). The pull+import approach in kjv7 failed the empirical gate and worsened the wedge. lead-ezzr re-dispatches the launcher bd-provisioning fix with the NOW-PROVEN 'bd bootstrap' recipe (non-destructive: imports git-tracked committed issues.jsonl, 21 issues, creates embeddeddolt, bd create/ready write-ready). Closed in favor of lead-ezzr.
close_reason: Superseded by lead-ezzr (closed done, lead-implemented bootstrap-as-vscode provisioning, launch-verified, shipped v0.2.8).

notes:

SUPERSEDED-BY lead-q5k7 (2026-06-11, lead-architect). This dispatch was MIS-ROUTED to shopsystem-templates with a WRONG root cause (package staleness). Empirical investigation against the v0.2.7 image PROVED the real cause is a bc-launcher bug: controller.py:895 execs 'shop-templates pour --workspace <ws>' but shop-templates has NO 'pour' subcommand (valid: list/show/bootstrap/update; flag is --target) so the skill-refresh exec FAILS every launch, and the launcher LOGS FALSE SUCCESS without checking the result. HOWEVER this does NOT fix LAUNCH-time delivery: the launcher uses the broken 'shop-templates pour --workspace' invocation (controller.py:895), so a launched BC still gets its committed stale .claude/skills. The launch-delivery fix is lead-q5k7 (launcher: pour->update --target --shop-type + stop logging false success), routed to shopsystem-bc-launcher with an empirical BC-launch acceptance gate. lead-slkk closed as the templates-currency portion; launch delivery tracked in lead-q5k7. title: Canonical shop-shell template must run the agent as non-root (vscode) — Claude Code refuses EUID==0
description:
FINDING (lead-host, this session): bin/shop-shell dropped into a ROOT shell and `claude` refused to start. Two defects in the prototype shop-shell: (1) the docker run omitted --user, so it ran as the image default USER = root (bc-base Dockerfile ends USER root); Claude Code refuses --dangerously-skip-permissions when EUID==0. (2) a hardcoded IMAGE= line dead-lettered the SHOPSYSTEM_SHELL_IMAGE override. Both fixed in this repo's prototype (commits 3a08db4, ddb1c6b): added --user vscode and folded bc-base into the override default.

The prototype shop-shell's own header says the canonical form will be rendered by shopsystem-templates. So OTHER product lead shops will inherit the SAME root-shell defect unless the templates BC's rendered shop-shell pins the non-root agent user. Reference: bc-launcher controller.AGENT_CONTAINER_USER='vscode' runs every agent command via docker exec --user vscode.

DISPATCHED 2026-06-11 as request_bugfix -> shopsystem-templates (work_id lead-2bdq). title: Brokered claude-agent boot is UNVALIDATED/BLOCKED: no-host-mount model leaves claude at its first-run wizard (theme + login-method picker); never reaches the agent
description: Found in the live fleet-flip canary 2026-06-10. In the brokered no-host-mount model, bc-base bakes ~/.claude.json WITHOUT hasCompletedOnboarding/theme (352B) and ~/.claude/.credentials.json = {accessToken:__PLACEHOLDER__}. So a freshly-launched BC's claude boots into the FIRST-RUN WIZARD: (1) theme picker, then (2) 'Select login method' (Claude subscription / Console / 3rd-party). It never reaches 'Accessing workspace:' so the launcher's 60s readiness gate times out and no startup prompt is injected. SOLVED+PROVEN E2E 2026-06-10 (see comments). DISPATCHED to shopsystem-bc-launcher as request_bugfix 2026-06-10 (work_id lead-2s6y), part of v0.2.6 with lead-3q12. Blocks lead-5jbc; relates lead-5jbc/lead-mdng/lead-5fji; ADR-026.
close_reason: DELIVERED + LIVE-VERIFIED v0.2.6 (tag 3b87ca6->fadfcc6, bc-base v0.2.6 on GHCR). Reviewer CLEAN, 167 passing. Scenario reconciliation (ADR-018 D2): the two authorized placeholder-shape scenarios re-pinned to nested claudeAiOauth shape -- 3931e43e01824a3c->8dfad9acd7503b3f (features/bc-launcher/45 block2) and e4348b11e0b38d4f->0c90e2234954ccc4 (features/bc-launcher/50 block1). 'scenarios hash' over the lead-held bodies reproduces both OLD hashes = confirmed pre-state these supersede. No-real-credential invariant (scenario 50) HOLDS: nested accessToken==__PLACEHOLDER__; live image-bake confirmation showed all baked values placeholder/synthetic. LIVE PROOF (router, definitive acceptance): image-level bake confirmed via docker run on v0.2.6 -- ~/.claude/.credentials.json nested claudeAiOauth{accessToken/refreshToken/expiresAt=4070908800000/scopes/subscriptionType:max}, ~/.claude.json {hasCompletedOnboarding:true,theme:dark,bypassPermissionsModeAccepted:true,projects[/workspace]{hasTrustDialogAccepted:true,...}}; plain bc-container launch booted claude straight to agent REPL (no wizard), recognized as Claude Max, answered FLIPREADY = full claude->broker->Anthropic round-trip. First brokered end-to-end claude-the-app boot. Lead features/ bodies still carry old shape (separate lead-side register-sync, non-blocking).

comments.author: David Stenglein
comments.text: SOLVED + PROVEN E2E 2026-06-10 (per dave's pointer: old non-brokered launch cp'd host ~/.claude.json AND ~/.claude dir). Root cause: claude needs a RECOGNIZED logged-in state across BOTH files; the brokered baked .credentials.json={accessToken:__PLACEHOLDER__} is the WRONG SHAPE (claude wants nested claudeAiOauth). FIX (validated live: scenarios booted to the agent REPL brokered and replied 'BROKERWORKS' to a prompt = full claude->broker->Anthropic round-trip with placeholder token substituted): bc-base must bake --- (1) ~/.claude/.credentials.json = {"claudeAiOauth":{"accessToken":"__PLACEHOLDER__","refreshToken":"__PLACEHOLDER__","expiresAt":<far-future e.g. 4070908800000>,"scopes":["user:file_upload","user:inference","user:mcp_servers","user:profile","user:sessions:claude_code"],"subscriptionType":"max"}} (broker swaps the Authorization header on the wire, so placeholder token VALUES are fine — only the SHAPE + far-future expiry matter so claude doesn't try to refresh). (2) ~/.claude.json: hasCompletedOnboarding:true, theme:"dark", lastOnboardingVersion:"<ver>", a placeholder oauthAccount stanza + userID, AND projects["/workspace"]={hasTrustDialogAccepted:true,hasCompletedProjectOnboarding:true,projectOnboardingSeenCount:0,allowedTools:[]}. (3) the Bypass-Permissions-mode acceptance flag (claude --dangerously-skip-permissions warning) — BC to confirm exact ~/.claude.json key. All placeholder/synthetic (no real secrets) so safe to bake into public bc-base. Gates observed in order: theme -> login-method -> folder-trust -> bypass-permissions -> agent REPL. Dispatch to shopsystem-bc-launcher (bake into bc-base), cut release. Blocks lead-5jbc.

title: Brokered launch: container runtime HTTPS_PROXY defaults to :14321 (control API), not the :14322+token MITM proxy — only the clone gets the derived proxy
description: Found in the live fleet-flip canary 2026-06-10 (first brokered relaunch of scenarios on bc-base v0.2.5). bc_launcher/controller.py sets the CONTAINER's persistent HTTPS_PROXY = broker_address (line ~552), defaulting to DEFAULT_AGENT_VAULT_BROKER=http://agent-vault:14321 when --agent-vault-broker is not passed. The lead-5fji fix added _build_clone_proxy_url/_derive_mitm (:14322 + av_agt_<token>:<vault>) but applies it ONLY to the clone exec env (lines ~663-671), NOT the container runtime HTTPS_PROXY. Consequence: auto-clone succeeds but claude-the-agent inherits HTTPS_PROXY=:14321 (control API) and brokered Anthropic calls fail (curl 56 CONNECT 405). Proper fix: derive runtime HTTPS_PROXY from AGENT_VAULT_ADDR/TOKEN/VAULT same as the clone. Runtime-half completion of lead-5fji DEFECT 1. PROVEN+workaround in comments. DISPATCHED to shopsystem-bc-launcher as request_bugfix 2026-06-10 (work_id lead-3q12), part of v0.2.6 with lead-2s6y. Blocks lead-5jbc; relates lead-5fji/lead-5jbc/lead-mdng.

'scenarios hash' over the lead-held body reproduces OLD c4e88075a0b4bd00 = confirmed pre-state. Scope matches dispatch (additive/tightened). LIVE PROOF (router, definitive acceptance): plain bc-container launch on v0.2.6 (env-file ADDR/TOKEN/VAULT + AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM, NO --agent-vault-broker, NO manual seed) set container runtime HTTPS_PROXY to the DERIVED http://av_agt_<token>:fleet@agent-vault:14322 (was :14321) via controller _build_runtime_proxy_url. comments.text: PROVEN 2026-06-10: relaunching scenarios with --agent-vault-broker 'http://av_agt_<token>:fleet@agent-vault:14322' sets the container runtime HTTPS_PROXY to the :14322 MITM proxy, and claude's brokered Anthropic calls then SUCCEED (claude booted + replied to a prompt = full round-trip). title: FINDING: Implementer pre-emit gate FALSE-BLOCKS release/tag-deliverable maintenance + improperly punts BC-repo housekeeping to lead (feeds lead-o6tp/lead-m56e)
description:
Source: lead-if8q work_done reconciliation 2026-06-10 (shopsystem-bc-launcher, request_maintenance to cut v0.2.5 + trigger bc-base rebuild). The BC verified ALL THREE acceptance criteria PASS and the deliverable (v0.2.5 tag + pushed bc-base image with Claude Code) was independently confirmed on the lead-side artifact surface (gh API tag deref, GHCR manifest HTTP 200, gh run isolated-failure log). Yet the work_done came back status=blocked, NOT complete, purely on two Implementer-emitter pre-emit preconditions. This is a FALSE-BLOCK on legitimately-complete work, and it feeds the pre-emit-gate redesign tracked in lead-o6tp (ADR-candidate, move procedural pre-emit checks to CLI-layer enforcement) and lead-m56e (dispatch bc-emit work-done precondition wrapper to shopsystem-templates, ADR-036).

TWO DEFECTS IN THE PRE-EMIT GATE AS APPLIED:

(1) RELEASE/TAG-DELIVERABLE PATH MISSING. The gate's 'work_id reachable from origin/main HEAD' precondition assumes every deliverable is a work_id-attributed COMMIT on HEAD. Release-mechanics maintenance produces a TAG (v0.2.5 -> commit 1b0ae92), and origin/main legitimately advances PAST the tag (here to 430579c with a later HEALTHCHECK). So the work_id is correctly NOT on HEAD — the tag IS the deliverable. The gate flagged this as incomplete. The bc-emit work-done precondition wrapper (lead-m56e, ADR-036 D1) needs an explicit release/tag-deliverable path: when the deliverable is a tag, the reachability check must verify the tag exists + points at the expected commit lineage, NOT that the work_id appears on origin/main HEAD.

(2) IMPROPER PUNT OF BC-REPO HOUSEKEEPING TO THE LEAD. The gate's clean-working-tree precondition tripped on the BC's OWN uncommitted bead state (M .beads/issues.jsonl = its bclaunch-lhh/wuo beads), and the BC's work_done explicitly instructed 'Router must commit/push bclaunch-lhh and reconcile.' This is WRONG on two counts: (a) per ADR-018 the lead never commits BC repos — there is no lead-side BC source to commit; (b) per BC discipline (memory: feedback_bc_self_resolves_procedural_gates) the BC MUST self-resolve its own clean-tree gate before emitting, never punt BC-repo housekeeping to the lead. The precondition wrapper's named-cause error text and the bc-primer/bc-implementer prose MUST NOT instruct or imply punting BC-repo commits to the lead; it must direct the BC to self-resolve (commit its own bead state) before re-emitting.

WHAT THE WRAPPER NEEDS (feeds lead-m56e step 1 / ADR-036 D1):
- A release/tag-deliverable mode where the work_id-reachability check is satisfied by a tag pointing at the expected lineage, not a HEAD commit.
- Clean-tree carve-out / self-resolve guidance so the BC's own bead-tracking churn (.beads/issues.jsonl) either lands in the ambient-artifact carve-out set (cf. lead-8lm .specstory/.scheduled_tasks.lock precedent) OR the error directs the BC to commit-its-own-state-then-re-emit — never to punt to the lead.
- Error messaging that never names the lead as the actor for BC-repo state.

NON-GOAL: this finding does NOT reopen lead-if8q (substantively accepted; tag-as-deliverable attribution accepted by router judgment). It is design input for the pre-emit-gate wrapper. Relates lead-o6tp, lead-m56e.
notes: TRIAGE 2026-06-11 (lead-architect). Discriminator applied vs contract surface (ADR-036, features/templates/105-117, shop-templates show). DISPOSITION: NOT a standalone request_bugfix/maintenance to shopsystem-templates. Both defects (release/tag-deliverable reachability path + clean-tree carve-out/self-resolve, no-lead-punt error text) are DESIGN INPUT to the bc-emit work-done WRAPPER ratified by ADR-036 D1/D2, carried by lead-m56e step 1 (PO-authored assign_scenarios -> shopsystem-templates; NEW capability — no command pins bc-emit today). CONSOLIDATED into lead-m56e as wrapper-scenario requirements: (a) release/tag-deliverable reachability mode (verify tag points at expected lineage, not work_id-on-HEAD); (b) .beads/issues.jsonl ambient-artifact carve-out (cf lead-8lm .specstory precedent); (c) named-cause error text that directs the BC to self-resolve its OWN bead state, NEVER names the lead as actor for BC-repo housekeeping (ADR-018: no lead-side BC commits). NEXT: lead-po authors these into the lead-m56e wrapper scenario set; gated on lead-yxsr (BC idle-park) before dispatch. NOT dispatchable as a bugfix today.
close_reason: RESOLVED — all three design-input defects shipped via the bc-emit work-done wrapper (lead-m56e, reconciled, in templates v0.6.0): (a) release/tag-deliverable reachability mode = scenario 178 (tag lineage not work_id-on-HEAD); (b) .beads/issues.jsonl ambient-artifact carve-out = scenario 176; (c) named-cause error directs BC self-resolve, never names lead as actor = scenario 180. The finding's purpose (feed the wrapper design) is complete; the wrapper enforces all three. No standalone deliverable remained.

comments.text:

title: bc-launcher v0.2.4 auto-clone broker wiring bug (canary-found): HTTPS_PROXY :14321->:14322+token:vault + login-shell CA trust
description: Found by the agent-vault canary 2026-06-10. The brokered model WORKS (manual login-shell clone through broker = exit 0), but bc-container launch's built-in auto-clone FAILS. Two defects in v0.2.4: (1) controller sets HTTPS_PROXY = broker_address verbatim and DEFAULT_AGENT_VAULT_BROKER=http://agent-vault:14321 (control API, no token) — but the MITM proxy is :14322 with token:vault basic-auth (format 'agent-vault run' uses: http://av_agt_<token>:<vault>@agent-vault:14322). Fix: build the proxy URL as :14322 with the agent token + vault from AGENT_VAULT_ADDR/TOKEN/VAULT, not the API addr. (2) the launch-time clone runs in a non-login shell, so it lacks GIT_SSL_CAINFO (set by /etc/profile.d/agent-vault-ca.sh) -> 'unable to get local issuer certificate'. Fix: run the clone with the trust env (source profile.d / login shell, or set GIT_SSL_CAINFO+proxy explicitly for the clone exec). Workaround used for canary: pass the full proxy URL to --agent-vault-broker. Dispatched to shopsystem-bc-launcher (request_bugfix) 2026-06-10; cut v0.2.5. Relates lead-5jbc, lead-mdng.
