title: Reconcile lead-km9: BC delivered only the replacement scenario (lead-b34) — 4 of 5 original scenarios still unpinned in shopsystem-docs
notes:
Reconciliation finding from lead-b34 work_done (2026-05-27).

ORIGINAL DISPATCH (lead-km9, 2026-05-22): assign_scenarios carrying 5 scenarios to shopsystem-docs:
  - 7ac28115fe1a2a33 (entry doc)
  - b0fe0666bcaa2398 (end-to-end runtime — superseded by lead-b34)
  - 6055501e8a23a652 (plain markdown only)
  - 8c55526c7a83bcd0 (agent-consumable walkthrough structure)
  - 3874a7f3c34e4f60 (launcher gap honestly named)

BC's clarify on lead-km9 explained that b0fe0666 was not establishable in its pytest-bdd harness, but said nothing about the other four being blocked. PO + Architect dispatched lead-b34 carrying only the b0fe0666 replacement; the other four were expected to land via the original lead-km9 dispatch.

OBSERVED STATE (BC repo /workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-docs):
  - features/ contains exactly ONE file: adoption_walkthrough_terminal_section.feature, pinning 56babfaeec4514a3 only.
  - grep '@scenario_hash' in BC features/ returns only 56babfaeec4514a3.
  - BC work_done echoes the 4 other hashes, but they are not in the BC's features/ tree.
  - lead-km9 has no recorded work_done in the BC outbox (shop-msg read outbox for lead-km9 returns only the BC's clarify).

INTERPRETATION: per ADR-009 vehicle (b), the BC was expected to emit ONE consolidated work_done covering 56babfaeec4514a3 (the lead-b34 replacement) plus the four unchanged originals — addressed to the lead-b34 work_id. The BC did echo all five hashes in scenario_hashes:, but in fact only landed the replacement; the four others were not implemented. The work_done's hash list misrepresents BC state.

POSSIBLE CAUSES:
  1. BC understood the dispatch as ONLY covering the replacement (lead-b34 description framed it as additive — 'carries only the revised scenario'). BC may have inferred that the other four would be tracked via a separate work_done on lead-km9, and is still in flight on those.
  2. BC mechanically copied the hash list from the lead-b34 dispatch payload into work_done.scenario_hashes without verifying each hash had a corresponding feature file. (ADR-009 v2 candidate: tighten the BC-side requirement that scenario_hashes in work_done must be observably pinned in features/.)

FOLLOW-UP OPTIONS:
  (a) Send request_shop_card to shopsystem-docs to read its authoritative scenario register — confirms what BC believes it has pinned.
  (b) If BC is genuinely still in flight on the four originals, leave lead-km9 open and wait.
  (c) If BC considers lead-km9 closed (because lead-b34 work_done echoed all hashes), the four originals need to be re-dispatched. Architect to decide vehicle (likely a fresh request_bugfix or assign_scenarios depending on whether BC has any partial work).

ALSO: BC's working tree has uncommitted untracked files (features/, src/, tests/, walkthrough/) — orthogonal hygiene concern.

ADR-009 v2 FRICTION: this is the first BC work_done landing against the canonized vehicle-(b) workaround. The friction point: the BC's work_done.scenario_hashes echoed the entire original dispatch's hash list when only one was actually pinned. The pattern as canonized does not unambiguously tell the BC which hashes it must observably pin to declare work_done — particularly when the bugfix dispatch is additive vs. replacement. Candidate for ADR-009 v2: spell out the BC's work_done scenario_hashes contract explicitly (must be a strict subset of BC's pinned @scenario_hash set in features/), and clarify whether work_done on a clarify-resolution bead is allowed to claim coverage of unchanged sibling hashes from the original dispatch.
close_reason:
Superseded by lead-eqx (re-dispatch of the four unpinned scenarios via assign_scenarios, dispatched 2026-05-27) and ADR-010 (canonizes the work_done.scenario_hashes strict-subset rule that prevents the lead-b34 echo pattern in future).

lead-apk's tracking role is closed; ongoing reconciliation responsibility moves to lead-eqx. lead-km9 remains open per ADR-010 consequences (expected closure path: superseded by lead-eqx once its work_done lands).

Template follow-up filed as lead-83l (P2).

title: Future: shop-msg send clarify_response primitive (ADR-009 path a)
description:
Tracks the future implementation of a lead-side 'shop-msg send clarify_response --bc <name> --work-id <id> --resolution <text>' primitive per ADR-009 path (a). NO --scenario-file (bounding constraint).

Scope (coordinated change):
1. New ClarifyResponse Pydantic schema in shopsystem-messaging catalog (MUST NOT carry scenario_hashes).
2. The lead-to-BC send path opts into allow_multi_type=True for clarify_response (storage.py:210-223) so the row can coexist with the original assign_scenarios/request_bugfix/request_maintenance row on the same work_id.
3. BC-side semantics: receipt of clarify_response RE-OPENS the original dispatch on the SAME work_id for the BC's gated loop to consume (no new bead).
4. shop-msg CLI surface: 'shop-msg send clarify_response' subcommand with --bc/--work-id/--resolution; NO --scenario-file.
5. BC-side read path: 'shop-msg pending inbox' surfaces the clarify_response alongside the original dispatch.
6. Precondition: refused non-zero when no prior BC clarify exists on that (bc, work_id).

THE DISCRIMINATOR (load-bearing): INFORMATION clarify -> clarify_response (in-band, same work_id, loop-consumed, no new bead). SCOPE-CHANGE clarify -> STILL re-dispatch per ADR-009 layer (b) / ADR-027. The absence of scenario state mechanically enforces this (mirrors ADR-015 dec 7 nudge no-scenario-state rule).

NOT blocking ADR-009's operational layer (b) — the de-facto re-dispatch pattern is canonical now and works without this primitive.

SCENARIOS AUTHORED (lead-PO, 2026-06-23) — delivers q616's approved clarify_response vehicle; re-prioritized P3->P1:
- features/messaging/30-...reopens-original-dispatch  @scenario_hash:f5819af74da50561
- features/messaging/31-...coexists-no-overwrite      @scenario_hash:eb96e352471ffd35

All 4 hashes reproduced via 'scenarios hash' (block-only, ADR-018 D2) before send; deposit confirmed via 'shop-msg read inbox --bc shopsystem-messaging --work-id lead-ox8'. Vehicle = assign_scenarios (NEW message type — 'shop-msg send --help' shows only {request_maintenance, assign_scenarios, request_bugfix, request_completion_journal, nudge}; no clarify_response). Awaiting BC work_done -> reconcile register/hashes.

Pairs with lead-fnj5 (dispatch-gate bug). Closes the q616 information-clarify gap WITHOUT eroding refine-not-chat discipline.

title: request_bugfix: shopsystem-templates — canonical settings.json hooks must not embed shop identity
description: The canonical .claude/settings.json templates at repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/claude_settings/{bc,lead}.json must not require shop-identity substitution. Today the BC variant ships 'shop-msg prime --bc {{SHOP_NAME}}'; bootstrap pours it byte-for-byte (cli.py:423), the placeholder is never substituted, and the Claude Code harness executes the literal command — producing 'shop name {{SHOP_NAME}} is not registered'. This bug is currently latent for every BC that hasn't had a Claude Code session opened in its own repo dir; surfaced when starting shopsystem-docs. After B-MESSAGING (implicit CWD resolution) lands, the canonical hook becomes bare 'shop-msg prime' for both shop types. Scenarios to author: (1) canonical bc.json hook command is exactly 'shop-msg prime' with no addressing flags and no placeholders; (2) canonical lead.json hook command is exactly 'shop-msg prime' (currently 'bd prime' — verify whether bd prime stays alongside or is replaced; PO judgment); (3) a poured BC's .claude/settings.json contains no {{...}} placeholders; (4) repour via 'shop-templates update' replaces the old hook content. Discriminator: capability exists (canonical bootstrap pours settings.json) but the hook-content tightening is unpinned. Vehicle: request_bugfix. Anchor scenarios that need re-examination: 2b9bd9c82017b0c6 / cad9ccb5b462978d (byte-for-byte pour — these remain correct; the new scenarios sit alongside them, pinning the body). Depends on: B1 (shop-msg implicit lookup must exist first).

Same failure mode as lead-0bw earlier today. Blocked on lead-qwt: BC must commit and push. Downstream re-pour beads (lead-0u2 / lead-yhr / lead-u16 / lead-8yl) remain blocked.

All 5 scenario hashes verified present in repos/shopsystem-templates/features/canonical_settings_hooks_no_shop_identity_pdr009.feature (0c157533eb3145c8 / 9317b34e56712c7c / d3cc63377ac86cce / e74510bc2af8f058 / ef68be19d7b3a3bb), and 71797e9017c95fed retired in brief_003_event_driven_activation_and_settings_json_bootstrap.feature with the supersession note. title: assign_scenarios: shopsystem-messaging — shop-msg implicit CWD-based shop resolution
description: Add implicit shop resolution to shop-msg: when called without --bc/--lead, walk up from CWD looking for .claude/shop/name.md and .claude/shop/type.md. Read type.md to choose BC vs lead mode; read name.md to resolve the canonical name; use the registry to find the path as today. Explicit --bc <name> / --lead <name> remain unchanged for cross-shop addressing. This eliminates the need for the canonical BC settings.json SessionStart hook to embed the shop name. Anchors: PDR-007 (name-based addressing — this extends it with a CWD-implicit form, does not reverse it). Prior art: bd discovers .beads/ from CWD using the same walk-up pattern. Discriminator: NEW capability — implicit CWD resolution does not exist today; calling shop-msg prime with no addressing flags currently errors via the mutually-exclusive arg group. Vehicle: assign_scenarios. PO must author scenarios pinning: (a) bare 'shop-msg prime' resolves a BC when CWD is inside a BC shop dir, (b) bare 'shop-msg prime' resolves a lead when CWD is inside a lead shop dir, (c) error case when CWD is not inside any shop, (d) explicit --bc/--lead still works unchanged. Suggested generalization to other subcommands (pending, respond, watch) — PO judgment whether to bundle.

title: Bootstrap prerequisite: create shopsystem-docs BC repo + scaffold + register
description: Before any assign_scenarios dispatch for brief 007 (adoption documentation) can be sent to the new shopsystem-docs BC, the BC repo must exist, be bootstrapped with shop-templates, cloned into repos/shopsystem-docs/, and registered with shop-msg. Anchors: brief 007 (resolved Q4 commits new-BC topology; boundary 10 commits v1 plain-markdown publishing), ADR-008 (introduces the docs BC; same precedent as ADR-004's lead-mxy bootstrap blocker). Host-side steps (Decision A: host-operator pattern, same as lead-mxy): (1) gh repo create dstengle/shopsystem-docs --private (matches the existing org convention; shopsystem-bc-launcher is PRIVATE per gh repo view). (2) clone the empty repo into repos/shopsystem-docs/. (3) shop-templates bootstrap --shop-type bc --shop-name shopsystem-docs --target /workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-docs. (4) first commit + push the poured scaffold. (5) shop-msg registry add shopsystem-docs /workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-docs and verify via shop-msg registry list. Brief tracking bead (templates BC tracker): shopsystem-templates-h22.

BC is reachable for assign_scenarios dispatch.

title: Lead subagent templates missing Claude Code YAML frontmatter
description:
The canonical templates for lead-po.md and lead-architect.md (in repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/) lack the YAML frontmatter (---\nname: ...\ndescription: ...\n[tools: ...]\n---) that Claude Code requires to register a markdown file as an invokable subagent. As a result, when shop-templates bootstrap pours a lead shop, the resulting .claude/agents/lead-po.md and .claude/agents/lead-architect.md are present on disk but unregistered: the runtime reports 'Agent type "lead-po" not found' (and likewise for lead-architect) when the router attempts to dispatch.

Empirical demonstration: from /workspaces/shopsystem-product, an Agent invocation with subagent_type=lead-po failed with the registration error above; the available-agents list contained only the runtime defaults (claude-code-guide, Explore, general-purpose, Plan, statusline-setup) and none of the lead subagents.

This blocks the entire lead-shop role-isolation pattern documented in lead-primer.md §'Who you are — router for lead-po and lead-architect subagents.' The router cannot delegate PO or architect work, which means briefs/PDRs/scenarios cannot be PO-authored and request_bugfix/assign_scenarios/request_maintenance dispatches cannot be architect-composed.

The BC subagent templates in the same directory (bc-implementer.md, bc-reviewer.md) DO carry the required frontmatter — they are the pattern to match. The fix is to add an analogous frontmatter block to lead-po.md and lead-architect.md whose name/description fields accurately describe each lead-shop role's invocation criteria.

This bug was likely introduced in commit 88ca83c ('agents: lead-po and lead-architect subagents (PDR-002 path a)').

NOTE on dispatch protocol: this request_bugfix is being filed router-direct rather than architect-composed because the architect subagent is itself one of the unreachable subagents — composing the dispatch through the proper vehicle requires the very capability this bugfix restores. Router-direct is the one-off needed to bootstrap the architect role itself; future dispatches return to architect-composed once shopsystem-templates ships the fix and we re-pour.
notes: Reconciled 2026-05-27: shopsystem-templates work_done verified. .claude/agents/lead-po.md and .claude/agents/lead-architect.md frontmatter (name/description/tools) confirmed well-formed and matching the canonical templates at repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/lead-{po,architect}.md. Re-pour already applied locally via commit 15037cf. No further re-pour to other shops needed (sibling BC repos don't use lead subagents). Outbox row already consumed. No scenarios, no register check; bug fix is doc-shape only.
close_reason: Closed

title: [bug] request_bugfix: messaging test suite clobbers production registry on every run
description: The shopsystem-messaging pytest suite overwrites production shop_registry entries on every run. In tests/conftest.py the session-scoped _ensure_session_lead fixture (lines 87-114) calls registry_add('shopsystem-product', str(root.resolve()), shop_type='lead') and registry_add('shopsystem product', str(root.resolve()), shop_type='lead') with a tmp_path as the shop_root. registry_add uses ON CONFLICT ... DO UPDATE (storage.py lines 630-640), so these calls overwrite existing production entries pointing to /workspaces/shopsystem-product. After any test run, shop-msg registry list shows 'shopsystem product' and 'shopsystem-product' pointing at /tmp/pytest-of-vscode/pytest-NNN/... paths. The teardown at line 128 only removes the random test-lead-session-* entry; the two named entries are never cleaned up. Similarly, the step-def at line 3439 (given_bc_registered) registers any bc_name passed in from a feature file under a tmp_path with no teardown. Operational effect: shop-msg pending outbox --lead 'shopsystem product' silently returns empty; resolve_lead_shop() routes BC responses to a tmp path. Recovery requires manually re-running shop-msg registry add after every test run. Fix: the test suite must not upsert well-known production names. Either (a) use a separate database or schema for tests (SHOPMSG_DSN already supports this via env var), (b) save and restore the original registry values around the session fixture, or (c) add a finalizer that removes the two named entries and re-inserts the original values. Source: tests/conftest.py lines 87-128 and 3439-3448; storage.py registry_add lines 622-642.
notes: Reconciled 2026-05-27: shopsystem-messaging work_done verified. Pre-existing 48 failures (subprocess PATH gap, unrelated). Probes all green. Production registry names shopsystem-product/shopsystem product correctly restored after suite run. Outbox row already consumed prior to this reconciliation session. Mechanism observation: synthetic-name fixtures (bc-alpha/bc-beta/ghost-lead) lack finalizers — filed lead-vvz P3.
close_reason: Closed

title: [bug] request_bugfix: shop-msg pending inbox --bc returns empty despite unconsumed rows
description: shop-msg pending inbox --bc <name> resolves the BC path via resolve_shop_name() (storage.py:732-747) and then runs query_pending_inbox(bc_root) (storage.py:277-302). That query filters by bc = <resolved_path>. When the registry entry for the BC name has been overwritten by test fixtures (Bug D), resolve_shop_name() returns a tmp path and the query finds zero matching rows even though the real inbox row exists under the production path. Additionally, query_pending_inbox uses NOT EXISTS (outbox row) as the pending predicate, but the messages table also has a consumed column; if consumed semantics are used on the inbox side in future this predicate diverges. Observed: shop-msg pending inbox --bc shopsystem-bc-launcher returned empty while DB row (id=7, bc=/workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-bc-launcher, work_id=lead-1wv, direction=inbox, consumed=False) existed. Note: lead-yaj covers only the outbox direction (pending outbox --lead filtering); this is the separate inbox direction bug. Source: src/shop_msg/storage.py lines 277-302 and src/shop_msg/cli.py lines 168-182 (_resolve_bc) and 585-596 (_cmd_pending_inbox).
close_reason: Closed

title: [bug] request_bugfix: shop-msg pending outbox --lead returns empty despite unconsumed rows in DB
description: shop-msg pending outbox --lead <name> calls query_pending_outbox(lead_root) in storage.py lines 305-361. The query fetches ALL outbox rows with consumed=FALSE (lines 338-346), then Python-filters for rows whose bc column starts with lead_root + '/repos/' (lines 356-359). The filter fails when the BC's registered shop_root in the registry does not start with that prefix — for example, when insert_bc_response writes the BC-side outbox marker using the path stored in the registry, and that path is the canonical filesystem path to the BC clone. If the path stored in the registry differs from <lead_root>/repos/<bc-name> (e.g., an absolute path outside that tree), all rows are filtered out silently. Observed: three unconsumed rows (lead-80i, lead-9d3, lead-dzv) were present in the DB with consumed=False; shop-msg read outbox --bc <name> --work-id <id> read them correctly; shop-msg pending outbox --lead returned empty. Fix: query_pending_outbox must either (a) join against shop_registry to enumerate BCs registered under the lead, or (b) use the registry path to correctly scope the bc-column filter.
close_reason: Cannot reproduce 2026-05-29. Empirical check: 'shop-msg pending outbox --lead shopsystem-product' just returned 'lead-cw7-reviewer-recovery mechanism_observation shopsystem-templates' — non-empty, with the expected canonical-name + work_id + message_type triple. The filter-mismatch bug described (DB has unconsumed rows but pending outbox returns empty) is NOT triggering today. The original observation cited rows lead-80i, lead-9d3, lead-dzv as visible via 'shop-msg read outbox' but absent from 'pending outbox' — those rows have since been drained and the current code path is producing rows correctly. Likely root cause: bc-column filter scoping was corrected by a separate commit (probably as a side effect of one of the post-2026-05-21 shop-msg fixes — pending outbox refactor land sequence not enumerated here), OR the registry-path canonicalization that triggered the filter mismatch is no longer in effect on the current data set. If the bug recurs, file a fresh bead with the new observation; do not reopen this one. Related-discoverability note in COMMENTS (unified 'pending --lead' command merging inbox and outbox) is preserved in bd record and is independently worth its own bead if the router-mental-model concern resurfaces.

comments.author: David Stenglein
comments.text: Reconciliation observation 2026-05-22: Monitor stream at session start flushed 'lead-1t2 clarify' but 'shop-msg pending outbox --lead' did not list it. Investigation: lead-1t2 is in 'pending inbox --lead' (a BC->lead clarify routed to lead inbox), NOT in 'pending outbox --lead' (which tracks BC outbox rows). The two commands surface different message-flow directions, which is consistent with the data model — but the router's mental model 'lead-1t2 should be in pending outbox' suggests a follow-up bead may be warranted: a unified 'pending --lead' that merges inbox-clarifies-from-bcs and outbox-responses-from-bcs into one list, so the operator does not have to remember to run two commands. This is orthogonal to lead-yaj's outbox-filter-scoping bug (lead_root + /repos/), filed here as a related-discoverability note.

title: [bug] request_bugfix: shop-msg watch silently continues on LISTEN connection drop without exiting
description: shop-msg watch --lead holds a psycopg3 autocommit connection and blocks in conn.notifies() (storage.py:watch_lead_inbox lines 1013-1029 and watch_inbox lines 1135-1156). If the TCP connection to Postgres drops mid-session, psycopg3 does not raise immediately — the notifies() generator silently starves with no output and no exit. There is no exception handler around the LISTEN loop, no timeout, no keepalive-expiry detection, and no reconnection logic. The watcher appears running (process alive, no stderr) but delivers no notifications. Recovery requires manual monitor restart. Fix: detect connection drop (OperationalError or generator exhaustion) and either exit non-zero with a clear stderr message, or attempt bounded reconnection with per-attempt stdout logging.
notes: 2026-05-29: chain status — lead-7v1 -> lead-m32 -> lead-2ca (BC signed off, NOT on origin/main, entangled with lead-767) -> lead-pfmb (recommit carrier filed). lead-7v1 remains IN_PROGRESS pending lead-pfmb push.
close_reason: Subsumed by carrier lead-xscs (b2649b6). Original LISTEN-drop fix arrives via lead-m32->lead-2ca chain, all carried in b2649b6 on origin/main.

title: [bug] request_maintenance: tighten scenario 72 instruction-substring step-defs to sentence-scoped containment (lead-pn9 §4.4 closure)
description:
§4.4 closure on lead-pn9. The Reviewer signed off 5/6 scenarios (71, 73, 74, 75, 76) but flagged scenario 72 (d87ccb133fa64d2f — 'canonical primer activation instruction names prereqs and failure mode') as having loose step-defs in tests/conftest.py:

- then_body_directs_path_verification (L5451-5479): whole-body 3-token conjunction (verify-verb OR 'command -v') AND ('path' substring) AND ('before' substring + 'monitor' substring), with no proximity/sentence binding.
- then_body_directs_refuse_with_diagnostic (L5487-5530): same whole-body conjunction shape (refuse_verb + diagnostic_marker + missing_marker), no sentence binding.

Reviewer demonstrated false-positive: bare-stripped primer body 'Before you read further: if inotifywait and stdbuf are missing, the router must refuse to arm the Monitor and surface a diagnostic.' still passes because (a) 'sufficiency check' elsewhere supplies has_verify_verb via 'check', (b) 'PATH' satisfies has_path_token via the host-prereq bullet ('packages being present on PATH'), (c) 'before' anywhere + 'monitor' anywhere satisfies has_monitor_temporal.

Fix shape per Reviewer: mirror scenario 71's exclusion-clause step-def at L5398-5439 (sentence-aware regex scan over sentence-broken body, requires the activation-verb + hook-pattern coupling within a single sentence). Tighten both L5451-5479 and L5487-5530 to require their full token-conjunction within a single sentence.

Gherkin text is FINE. Canonical primer is FINE. Only the step-defs are too permissive.

Vehicle: request_maintenance — flat test-infrastructure tightening; no new scenarios; existing scenario 72 pinned behavior unchanged.

Parent: lead-pn9. This dispatch's terminal work_done implicitly closes lead-pn9's chain.

title: brief-003 realization revision: replace SessionStart-hook activation with CLAUDE.md-primer instruction (router arms Monitor at session start)
description: Test-harness BC diagnosed a hook-model mismatch in brief 003's canonical templates: Claude Code awaits SessionStart hooks synchronously, so a foreground 'inotifywait -m' invocation never returns and startup hangs / times out. Schema + host prereqs are fine; the mismatch is structural — synchronous hook delivery is incompatible with a long-running watcher. Brief 003's two invariants (every shop reactive on session start; activation honors brief 001 encapsulation) still hold. The realization changes: in place of a SessionStart hook running inotifywait, the canonical CLAUDE.md primer carries an explicit instruction telling the router (Claude session) to arm the Monitor tool on a 'stdbuf -oL inotifywait -m -e create,moved_to <watch-dir>/' pipeline at session start. Monitor IS the in-session primitive that delivers the 'background process whose stdout streams as notifications' semantic the hook was faking. This bead carries the revision: PO authors superseding scenarios (numbered 71+, tagged @bc:shopsystem-templates) that retire the hook-model-bound scenarios (57/58/59/65/68 wholesale; 70 keeps as schema-conformance with shrunk scope) and pin the new realization (CLAUDE.md primer per shop type contains activation-instruction substring; instruction names host prereqs + failure mode; bootstrap-generated CLAUDE.md contains the instruction). Architect handles the request_bugfix dispatch carrying this round.
notes:
PO authored 6 new scenarios at features/templates/71-76 (Scenario Outlines where shop-type-parametrized, single Scenarios where shop-type-specific). Tagged @bc:shopsystem-templates at dispatch time by Architect. Hash computation deferred to Architect per role contract.

Retire-with-successor map:
- 57 (d4b8aac19b34ea8c, BC-side 1621b59b0ea8b20b) → superseded by 73 (bootstrap-generated CLAUDE.md carries activation instruction); settings.json package-data surface survives but is no longer the activation home.
- 58 (2a09d13c21d48aa0, BC-side 679d227f04533ad4) → superseded by 71 (canonical CLAUDE.md primer contains activation-instruction substring).
- 59 (21769e2a22c36bc7, BC-side c1e7f31eeef73e05) → superseded by 75 (canonical lead settings.json declares 'bd prime' as sole SessionStart inner-hook, no activation hook).
- 65 (a9379ab3a162158d, BC-side a9379ab3a162158d) → superseded by 72 (activation instruction names prereqs + refuse-with-diagnostic failure mode).
- 68 (a3338bb773fda65e, BC-side 287e6a4f31533336) → superseded by 74 (canonical bc settings.json carries no SessionStart entries; subsumes 68's 'no other-shop-type watch target' clause).
- 70 (f2a7de49d80332c1, BC-side f2a7de49d80332c1) → superseded by 76 (schema conformance conditional on SessionStart being present; bc-variant antecedent is vacuously satisfied).

Keep unchanged: 60, 61, 62 (bootstrap/update mechanics for settings.json — still apply byte-for-byte to the new canonical contents pinned by 74 and 75); 63, 64 (CLAUDE.md primer + bootstrap-generated CLAUDE.md name host prereqs — substrings inotify-tools/coreutils/inotifywait/stdbuf still required by router's Monitor invocation, motivation now consistent with 72); 66, 67 (editable-install discipline, unaffected); 53 (CLAUDE.md references bd discipline, parallel structural pin to 73 for activation).

PO stance committed on the open question (canonical bc.json shape): option (b) — bc.json ships as a minimal hooks wrapper with no SessionStart entries. Rationale: preserves managed-update workflow (scenarios 60/61/62 still apply byte-for-byte to the new content); leaves the slot available for future terminate-cleanly BC-side hooks without committing speculative content now; avoids the destructive cost of retiring 60/61/62 wholesale (option a) and avoids the speculative-content failure mode of shipping hooks before a use case exists (option c). lead.json ships with 'bd prime' as the sole SessionStart inner-hook (the prior two-hook composition under scenario 59 collapses to a one-hook shape since the activation hook is gone).

No intent gaps surfaced during authoring. The realization revision is internally consistent: 71+72 pin the new realization home (CLAUDE.md primer), 73 pins it surviving the bootstrap pour, 74+75 pin the now-cleared-out settings.json shape per shop type, 76 carries forward the schema-conformance invariant under the new constraint. Architect handles request_bugfix dispatch carrying this round; the dispatch description should instruct the Implementer to retire the 6 listed BC-side scenarios when landing 71-76, mirror the supersedes-via-Feature-description-block convention established in scenario 69.
close_reason: Brief-003 realization revision complete. New canonical realization: CLAUDE.md-primer-instructs-router-to-arm-Monitor (in place of SessionStart-hook-runs-inotifywait). Architectural diagnosis (test-harness BC: Claude Code awaits SessionStart hooks synchronously; foreground inotifywait -m never returns; startup hangs) confirmed; brief 003's two invariants (every shop reactive on session start; activation honors brief 001 encapsulation) hold under the new realization without regression. title: request_bugfix: brief 003 implementation (12 scenarios 57-68, post-supersession re-dispatch)
description:
Re-dispatch of brief 003's 12 scenarios (57-68) to shopsystem-templates, carried as request_bugfix per BC's earlier vehicle observation (BC already has bootstrap capability; brief 003 extends it).

BACKGROUND
==========
The lead-xkh chain hit a coordination gap. Sequence:

- lead-xkh (assign_scenarios, 12 scenarios 57-68 for brief 003: event-driven shop activation, canonical .claude/settings.json, bootstrap extension) → BC clarified on contradiction with prior-pinned scenario 51 (compound AGENTS.md+settings.json pin in bootstrap_bd_init_side_effects.feature).
- lead-5mz (request_bugfix, carried only scenario 69 — the supersession removing scenario 51's settings.json clause, leaving AGENTS.md-only outcome pin) → BC work_done complete, scenario 1a6e90189f9c2ade attested, supersession landed clean.

But: lead-5mz carried ONLY scenario 69. Brief 003's 12 positive pins (.claude/settings.json writing, prereqs naming, venv-sync, etc.) were never delivered to the BC. lead-xkh still has a clarify in its outbox, so the BC's router will not re-process it. To get brief 003 implemented, the lead must send a NEW dispatch carrying the 12 brief 003 scenarios.

This is a protocol gap shape: the §4.4 closure assumption that lead-5mz's terminal work_done closes lead-xkh's chain doesn't hold because lead-5mz only carried the supersession scenario, not the brief 003 positive pins. Prior chains (lead-bwu→lead-e4g→lead-14e) worked because each follow-up bundled the prior scenarios. The lead-5mz dispatch instruction was wrong on this — it should have bundled.

LINKAGE
=======
- Predecessors: lead-xkh (assign_scenarios for brief 003, clarify-stalled in BC outbox), lead-5mz (closed; clean supersession of scenario 51).
- lead-xkh remains in_progress with a pointer to this new bead. lead-xkh closes implicitly when this new work_id's terminal work_done arrives in the BC outbox.

SUPERSESSION STATE
==================
- Prior scenario 51 (hash 7d64bb8ed5a3f656, compound AGENTS.md+settings.json pin) is retired. Its settings.json clause was removed by lead-5mz.
- New scenario 69 (hash 1a6e90189f9c2ade, AGENTS.md-only outcome pin) lives in BC features/bootstrap_no_top_level_agents_md.feature.
- Brief 003's scenario 60 (bootstrap writes canonical .claude/settings.json per shop type) no longer conflicts with anything in the BC — the contradiction lead-xkh tripped on is resolved.

PAYLOAD (12 scenarios 57-68)
============================
Implements brief 003 items A–E:

- A (canonical .claude/settings.json templates ship from shop-templates): scenarios 57, 68.
- B (SessionStart inotifywait hook, four reference-realization properties): scenarios 58, 65.
- C (lead-shop settings.json composes with existing bd prime hook): scenario 59.
- D (bootstrap/update writes and re-pours .claude/settings.json; CLAUDE.md names activation host prereqs): scenarios 60, 61, 62, 63, 64.
- E (lead-bootstrap installs sibling BC clones editable into product venv; bootstrapped shop resolves BC CLI invocations to live source on session start): scenarios 66, 67.

Scenario files (lead canonical authored locations):
  features/templates/57-canonical-claude-settings-json-templates-ship-from-shop-templates.gherkin
  features/templates/58-canonical-claude-settings-json-declares-sessionstart-hook.gherkin
  features/templates/59-canonical-lead-settings-json-composes-with-existing-bd-prime-hook.gherkin
  features/templates/60-bootstrap-writes-canonical-claude-settings-json-per-shop-type.gherkin
  features/templates/61-update-re-pours-claude-settings-json-as-managed-file.gherkin
  features/templates/62-update-is-idempotent-on-already-current-claude-settings-json.gherkin
  features/templates/63-bootstrap-generated-claude-md-names-activation-host-prereqs.gherkin
  features/templates/64-canonical-claude-md-primer-names-activation-host-prereqs.gherkin
  features/templates/65-canonical-sessionstart-fails-loudly-on-missing-inotifywait.gherkin
  features/templates/66-bootstrapped-shop-resolves-bc-cli-invocations-to-live-source-on-session-start.gherkin
  features/templates/67-lead-bootstrap-installs-sibling-bc-clones-editable-into-product-venv.gherkin
  features/templates/68-canonical-bc-settings-json-does-not-name-lead-watch-target.gherkin

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES (previously prepared, surfaced here since assign_scenarios was retired in favour of request_bugfix and request_bugfix's description IS the carrier for procedural framing)
=========================================================================================================================================================================================

- Scope item E realization framing: scenario 66 is the outcome pin (BC CLI invocations resolve to live source under repos/ on session start). Scenario 67 is the mechanism pin (lead-bootstrap installs sibling BC clones editable into product venv). The two compose: 67 is one valid realization of 66, but 66 is the durable contract the Reviewer attests against, and 67 names the specific mechanism the canonical templates encode today. If a future realization replaces editable installs with a different live-source resolution (e.g., a path-aware shim), 66 remains intact and 67 gets superseded.

- Scenario 65 (loud-fail on missing inotifywait): the canonical SessionStart hook MUST produce a visible failure when inotifywait is missing on PATH; silently degrading to a no-watcher state is the failure mode this scenario pins against. The canonical settings.json should encode this property via the hook's startup command shape (e.g., exit-on-missing-binary), not via a separate runtime probe.

- Scenario 59 (lead settings.json composition): the lead-shop's canonical settings.json composes both the inotifywait activation hook AND the bd prime hook on SessionStart. Concretely the SessionStart entry has multiple hook commands, not a single replaced command. This is so the activation hook does NOT clobber bd prime (operational hygiene).

PROCEDURAL REMINDERS
====================
- Hash discipline via wire-form per lead-wgv: the shop-msg send CLI computes hashes from the wire-form scenario body. Pass --scenario-file flags only; do not pre-hash.
- venv activated per lead-zmi: source .venv/bin/activate before invoking shop-msg.
- Vehicle is request_bugfix per BC's earlier vehicle observation in the lead-xkh clarify: BC already carries bootstrap pins; brief 003 tightens/extends the existing bootstrap behavior with additional managed files and CLAUDE.md content. This is NOT new capability — bootstrap exists and writes managed files today; this dispatch adds .claude/settings.json to the managed set and extends the CLAUDE.md content the bootstrap writes.

EMPIRICAL PRE-STATE VERIFICATION
================================
- Verified BC's bootstrap capability already exists by inspecting BC features/bootstrap_cli_surface.feature, bootstrap_bd_init_side_effects.feature, and bootstrap_no_top_level_agents_md.feature: scenarios for bootstrap writing managed agent files, writing CLAUDE.md, writing .gitignore, and invoking bd init are all pinned and green per lead-5mz's regression pin list. So Q1 (does the BC have the capability?) answer is YES — bootstrap exists, writes managed files; brief 003 extends the managed set.
- Verified BC does NOT pin .claude/settings.json today: grep '.claude/settings.json' across BC features/ produces no hits after lead-5mz landed. So the specific extension brief 003 names is unpinned — Q2 (does any scenario currently pin it?) is NO. Tightening, not flat change.
- Therefore: request_bugfix is the correct vehicle.
close_reason: BC work_done complete: 129/129 BDD passing, all 12 dispatched hashes attested (1621b59b0ea8b20b, 679d227f04533ad4, c1e7f31eeef73e05, f83e03ee69261242, d29cd723439faae1, d3066d4476d0a975, cad1153ef4dfd18c, 6bc3eb5f62115d91, a9379ab3a162158d, 3957f255c35aff60, ff882696856530a4, 287e6a4f31533336). 6 mutation probes confirmed teeth + 3 direct empirical probes (loud-fail hook under PATH=/nowhere, _install_sibling_bc_clones_editable with no repos/, update mtime preservation). Prior pins regression: all 9 examples green. Post-state spot-check empirically verified: claude_settings/{bc,lead}.json package-data exist with stdbuf-wrapped inotifywait pipelines; cli.py carries _CLAUDE_SETTINGS_PKG constant + read_claude_settings_template() + bootstrap pour at line 365 + update re-pour at line 588 + _install_sibling_bc_clones_editable() at line 390; bootstrap probe of fresh BC target produced .claude/settings.json byte-identical to bc.json template.

description:

title: scenarios 57-68: event-driven shop activation, canonical .claude/settings.json + bootstrap extension (brief 003)
description:
Brief 003 (lead-0h1) — Event-driven shop activation. 12 scenarios extending the
shop-templates bootstrap surface to ship a canonical `.claude/settings.json`
per shop type plus host-prereq naming, and to install sibling BC clones
editable from the lead bootstrap.

The brief carries two invariants and five scope items:

- Invariant 1: every shop is reactive on session start (BC watches own
  `inbox/`; lead watches `repos/*/outbox/`).
- Invariant 2: activation honors brief 001's encapsulation — the
  watcher fires a wake signal; reading content still goes through
  `shop-msg`.
- Scope A: canonical `.claude/settings.json` per shop type (scenarios 57, 68).
- Scope B: SessionStart hook content with stdbuf-wrapped inotifywait,
  alias-bypassed flushing filter, merged 2>&1 for startup-chatter
  visibility, monitor-mode persistent arming (scenarios 58, 59, 65).
- Scope C: bootstrap writes settings.json; update re-pours it as a
  bootstrap-managed file (scenarios 60, 61, 62).
- Scope D: prereq naming (`inotify-tools`, `coreutils`, `inotifywait`,
  `stdbuf`) in bootstrap-generated `CLAUDE.md` and the canonical primer
  (scenarios 63, 64).
- Scope E: live-source resolution outcome + lead bootstrap installs
  sibling BC clones editable into the product venv (scenarios 66, 67).

## Empirically validated context

This session validated the stdbuf-wrapped inotifywait pipeline end-to-end
on lead-79q reconciliation: sub-second event-fire on `work_done`
arrival via the reference pipeline named in scope item B. The pattern
works. Scope items A/B/C/D codify the canonical shape so every
bootstrapped shop gets it by default.

## Pre-state cited

Pre-state verification on shopsystem-templates current state:

- Package data at `src/shop_templates/templates/`: 4 role-prompt files
  (`bc-implementer.md`, `bc-reviewer.md`, `lead-po.md`, `lead-architect.md`)
  + 2 CLAUDE.md primers under `templates/claude/` (`bc.md`, `lead.md`) +
  `gitignore.template`. No `.claude/settings.json` template exists.
- `src/shop_templates/cli.py`: bootstrap writes `.claude/agents/*.md`,
  top-level `CLAUDE.md` (from primer + `{{SHOP_NAME}}` substitution),
  top-level `.gitignore`, and invokes `bd init --skip-agents`. No
  `.claude/settings.json` is written. `update` re-pours managed
  agent files only — no settings.json handling.
- Empirical bootstrap of a probe directory
  (`shop-templates bootstrap --shop-type bc --shop-name probe
  --target /tmp/probe-003`) produced exactly: `CLAUDE.md`,
  `.claude/agents/bc-implementer.md`, `.claude/agents/bc-reviewer.md`.
  No `.claude/settings.json`. Exit 0.
- `repos/shopsystem-templates/features/` scan: no existing scenarios
  pin SessionStart hooks, settings.json content, Monitor harness,
  inotifywait, or venv install mode.

All 12 scenarios target net-new capability — `assign_scenarios` is the
correct vehicle.

## Implementation notes (informational; bodies are the contract)

- Scope item E is realization-pinned by the PO via two scenarios:
  scenario 66 pins the outcome (live-source resolution on session
  start) and scenario 67 pins the install-shape (lead bootstrap
  installs sibling clones editable into the product venv). Scenario 67
  alone is the PO's leaning shape; the Implementer is free to add a
  hook-time refresh on top if pre-state on E surfaces drift cases not
  covered by 67 alone, or to treat 67 as sufficient.

- Scenario 65 pins the "loud fail on missing `inotifywait`" property.
  The Implementer must select a hook shape such that a host with no
  `inotifywait` on PATH surfaces a visible diagnostic — not a silent
  no-watcher state. A polling fallback that silently activates
  violates the invariant; if the Implementer commits a fallback, it
  must itself be a visible notification.

- Scenario 59 specifies the canonical lead variant **composes** with the
  existing `bd prime` `SessionStart` hook — two distinct entries under
  `SessionStart`, not a replacement.

- Scope D's prereq naming surface is the Implementer's call between
  `CLAUDE.md` primer (named in scenarios 63 + 64 as the realization)
  vs other places. The scenarios commit primer + bootstrap-generated
  CLAUDE.md as the surface; the Implementer must satisfy both.

## Procedural reminders

- Hash discipline: per `lead-wgv`, verify computed hashes against
  wire form, not against the body source pre-CLI. The `shop-msg send`
  CLI computes hashes from canonicalized wire form; the Implementer's
  BDD harness must read from the same wire form so verification holds.

- Mailbox encapsulation: `shop-msg` is the only sanctioned mailbox
  boundary on both sides. Do not hand-edit mailbox YAML; do not `cat`
  it. If `shop-msg pending` fails on a stale venv, fix the install —
  do not bypass the CLI (per `lead-xq0`).

- Bead ID: `lead-xkh` is the lead-shop work_id for this assignment.
  No dotted suffix; non-dotted lead-shop bead IDs per `lead-4wy`.

title: Brief: inter-shop messaging encapsulation (shop-msg as sole surface; self-contained messages)
description: Lead-shop brief authored from mechanism_observation 'shopsystem-templates-kin' against lead-kq0. Names two invariants (shop-msg is the sole inter-shop messaging surface; inter-shop messages are self-contained), the bd-decoupling corollary, and five scope items (A: shop-msg CLI surface; B: templates rewrite; C: catalog schema decoupling from bd; D: template-language decoupling from bd; E: spec edits to sections 4 and 5). Sequencing: A+C+E parallel; B+D wait on lead-kq0 (PDR-001) closing. Scenario authoring deferred until Architect verifies BC pre-state and picks vehicles. Artifact: briefs/001-inter-shop-messaging-encapsulation.md.
notes:
2026-05-12 (PO subagent, brief 001 items B+D, round 3 follow-up) — BC clarify on lead-e4g (request_bugfix carrying scenario 46) confirmed the regression vulnerability is present in three sister subsections that scenario 46 does not pin: lead-po '### Responding to a clarify via the shop-msg CLI' (line 158), bc-implementer '## Your job' (line 26), bc-reviewer '## What you read' (line 15). The Implementer's textual FIX extended across all four templates correctly, but coverage only pinned one. Without parallel scenarios, future edits to the other three subsections could regress to bare-verb 'Read the inbox YAML' (or similar) and only the template-wide encapsulation scenarios (6adb97ba0d0498c5, ff97230bf1e36a81, f279d66b8313e3fc) would gate them — and the BC's clarify text correctly notes those are too weak for per-step discipline (a bare 'Read' step coexists with 'shop-msg read inbox' elsewhere in the same template, satisfying template-wide substring presence while leaving the procedural step ambiguous).

Scope decision (router): option (a) accepted — three parallel scenarios mirroring 46's shape. Not (b) (BC's textual fix needs scenario coverage to be load-bearing). Not (c) (tightening the template-wide encapsulation scenarios with per-step granularity would lose the presence/absence checks they currently make; per-subsection scenarios are the cleaner shape). Outline-form consolidation deferred — scenario 46 is already dispatched + hashed; a brief revision can fold all four into a Scenario Outline if the pattern continues to recur.

Authored: features/templates/47-lead-po-responding-to-clarify-subsection-names-shop-msg-read-outbox-per-step.gherkin; features/templates/48-bc-implementer-your-job-subsection-names-shop-msg-read-inbox-per-step.gherkin; features/templates/49-bc-reviewer-what-you-read-subsection-names-shop-msg-read-inbox-per-step.gherkin. Each mirrors scenario 46 exactly: locate the subsection by heading boundary (## or ### until next heading), three Then conditions — (i) every step whose action is reading the mailbox names the literal CLI substring on the same step, (ii) no bare-verb regression mode, (iii) no path-shape regression mode. Vary only by template name, subsection heading text, and target substring (read outbox vs. read inbox).

Pre-state verification of BC's sketch: subsection headings as sketched (verified by grep). Two asymmetries noted but kept out of scope of this dispatch:
- bc-implementer 'Your job' step 1 (line 28) names BOTH 'shop-msg read inbox' (reading) AND 'shop-msg pending inbox' (listing). The new scenario 48 pins only the read-inbox action, parallel to scenario 46's narrow target. The pending-inbox-listing action is already covered template-wide by scenarios 17/18 (uses-shop-msg-cli-for-mailbox-access). Same shape applies to bc-reviewer 'What you read' step 1 (lines 17-20) — scenario 49 pins only read-inbox.
- bc-implementer 'Your job' is H2 (## Your job); lead-po's target is H3 (### Responding to a clarify via the shop-msg CLI); bc-reviewer's target is H2 (## What you read); lead-architect's target (scenario 46) is H3. Scenario 46's locator clause 'ends at the next heading of depth two (##) or depth three (###)' handles both depths uniformly; new scenarios reuse the same clause verbatim. Consistent.

Round-4 outlook (flag): With round 3 closing the four-template per-subsection-CLI-naming regression mode, the remaining brief-001 B+D regression-mode surface is:
- Procedural CLI naming for the OTHER mailbox actions inside the same/adjacent procedural subsections. Specifically: writing the outbox response. lead-architect '### Responding to a BC clarify via shop-msg respond' step 4 cites 'shop-msg respond clarify'; lead-po '### Responding to a clarify via the shop-msg CLI' step 4 cites the same. But no scenario currently pins per-step CLI naming for the RESPOND action. Same regression mode: a future edit could regress 'respond via shop-msg respond clarify' to bare 'send the response' and only the template-wide scenarios would gate it. If round 4 is desired, the same Scenario-Outline-amenable shape applies across all four templates' write-outbox / write-inbox actions.
- Procedural CLI naming for 'shop-msg respond work_done' (Implementer/Reviewer) and 'shop-msg respond mechanism_observation' (Implementer). bc-implementer 'Your job' step 4 names 'shop-msg respond work_done'; 'Surfacing mechanism observations' section names 'shop-msg respond mechanism_observation'; bc-reviewer 'Outcomes' names both 'shop-msg respond work_done' and 'shop-msg respond clarify'. None of these are pinned by per-step scenarios.

Recommendation: defer round 4 to a deliberate brief revision rather than chasing it dispatch-by-dispatch. The pattern is structural — every per-mailbox-action step in every procedural subsection of every role template wants the same discipline — and the cleanest pin is one Scenario Outline with columns for (template, subsection heading, mailbox action, target CLI substring). That can supersede 46/47/48/49 cleanly. Until then, the four parallel scenarios are correct and load-bearing.

Did NOT dispatch. Did NOT compose shop-msg send. Architect opens a request_bugfix dispatch bead and sends the three new scenarios as additive to lead-e4g's coverage. (Open question for Architect: dispatch as a separate work_id, or amend lead-e4g? The BC's clarify is open against lead-e4g; the cleanest closure is one new request_bugfix work_id whose description references lead-e4g's clarify as the trigger.)

title: Decision-coherence defect: ADR-025 supersedes ADR-023 + ADR-024 but both remain 'accepted' with no superseded-by back-edge (prose-only supersession)
description: Surfaced empirically by the progressive-disclosure experiment's coherence gate (findings/progressive-disclosure/07-experiment.md, Goal 7) against the REAL repo. ADR-025 declares it supersedes ADR-023 and ADR-024, but the supersession lives ONLY in prose: neither ADR-023 nor ADR-024 carries a superseded-by back-edge, and both are still status: accepted. An agent consulting ADR-023/024 today has no machine-visible signal they are retired -> exactly the contradictory-active-decision failure the epic exists to prevent. Fix (hygiene, independent of the epic tooling): flip ADR-023 + ADR-024 status to superseded and add superseded-by: ADR-025 (once a frontmatter/edge convention exists, or as prose now). Note this is the concrete latent case the eventual coherence gate must catch. Ref lead-x7bp, 07-experiment.md.

title: Formalize the research-and-experiment protocol as a first-class, repeatable lead process (research -> notes -> synthesize -> experiment/validate -> recommend implementation path)
description: Requested by David 2026-07-04 alongside the progressive-disclosure epic (lead-x7bp), which is to be the current proving case. The lead shop already RAN this protocol ad-hoc for the fabro integration and the lead-odqd spikes (agent-vault, fabro 2PC, substrate comparison) — see findings/fabro-spike/ (numbered recon->targets->experiment->harden notes + plan.md), findings/substrate-candidate-comparison-vs-fabro.md (research-only multi-agent Workflow eval -> scorecard + follow-up beads), findings/iterative-experimentation-capability.md, and PDR-016 (spike lifecycle: spike -> learn real limits -> throw away -> graduate via PDR-014 findings-driven tail; verdict vocabulary CONFIRM/go-with-caveats/REFUTED; isolation contract = throwaway /tmp + spike- containers, never committed, torn down at verdict). GAP: PDR-016 formalizes the SPIKE lifecycle as intent, but the broader RESEARCH-AND-EXPERIMENT PROTOCOL the operator wants ('research available materials, take lots of notes, synthesize reports, run experiments as necessary to validate goals can be met, recommend implementation path') is not yet a named, invokable, repeatable process (skill/runbook) that future epics are routed through BEFORE any PDR/ADR is authored. DELIVERABLE (intent): capture the protocol as a first-class process — likely a lead skill + canonical runbook that graduates PDR-016 — with explicit phase gates (research fan-out -> synthesis/scorecard -> experiment-to-validate -> verdict + implementation-path recommendation -> Phase-2 graduation), the no-PDR-before-research rule as an enforced gate, and the multi-agent Workflow as the standard execution vehicle. Cross-links: PDR-016, PDR-014, lead-odqd, lead-gkhk, findings/iterative-experimentation-capability.md. Proving case in flight: lead-x7bp.

title: Feature/scenario/BC ownership model: features are customer-value units that may span multiple BCs; the single @bc owner belongs on the SCENARIO, not the feature
description:
## Context
Related to lead-bh2m (DDD bounded-context review). Surfaced 2026-07-04 by David while reviewing the lead-holds-all restore (commit 5b0530e / epic lead-vzxd): features/ is currently laid out one .feature-file-per-BC with a feature-level @bc tag inherited by every scenario (ADR-056 three-dimension schema). Not urgent — tracking only, do not act yet.
design: Target model (David's framing): a FEATURE is a higher-level piece of functionality that delivers value to the customer; a larger feature (e.g. ecommerce checkout) can span activities in multiple BCs. SCENARIOS break the feature into behaviors, and each SCENARIO is assigned to exactly ONE BC. Therefore a single feature may legitimately contain scenarios owned by different BCs. If a scenario represents an interaction between 2+ BCs, a single BC owns it as the integration point. Implication for ADR-056: @bc should be a per-SCENARIO owner tag, not a feature-level tag inherited by all scenarios; the current feature-level @bc forces one-feature-per-BC and is a likely driver of BC bloat. Work would revisit ADR-056 (schema: move/allow @bc at scenario level, keep @origin at feature level), the scenarios validate/--aggregate rules, and the features/ layout (group by customer-value feature, not by BC).
acceptance_criteria: An ADR decides where @bc lives (scenario-level vs feature-level) and how cross-BC features are represented; the features/ layout and scenarios validate schema are reconciled to that decision; BC-bloat impact assessed.

title: canonicalization not fully formatting-robust: same scenario body in differently-formatted files (lead hand-authored vs BC helper-generated) hashes differently (guard scn1: e612d2bc vs bc4faf07) — @scenario_hash identity fragile across copies
description: Observed reconciling lead-vzxd.3: the scenarios BC's guard-file copy validates green with e612d2bc while the lead's copy validates green with bc4faf07 for the SAME scenario. Under v0.3.1 validate==CLI==producer (single-sourced per TOOL), but the canonical hash still varies with file FORMATTING (whitespace/structure the canonicalizer does not fully normalize) so the 'same' scenario in two differently-formatted files gets two hashes. Bounded today (each scenario has ONE owning BC whose file is authoritative; beads deauthorized reduces wire-ref risk), but for @scenario_hash to be a robust cross-copy identity the canonicalization should be formatting-robust OR all files must be normalized through scenarios consolidate/create (standard formatting). Tail follow-up (not blocking the green gate). Route to shopsystem-scenarios when prioritized. Parent lead-vzxd.
notes: CONFIRMED + given a fix target by templates (lead-vzxd.10): scenarios v0.3.1 _iter_scenario_blocks folds an inter-scenario COMMENT block into the preceding scenario's block-only hash -> false E_HASH_MISMATCH on comment-adjacent scenarios. templates' bc-emit gate WORKS AROUND it via a producer/wire cross-check (on-disk tag reproduces the block-only hash -> not a real mismatch). Proper fix = scenarios v0.3.2 (make _iter_scenario_blocks not fold trailing comments into the preceding block; part of the formatting-robustness pass). Route to shopsystem-scenarios. Still P2 (workaround in place, gate functional).

title: shop-msg: clarify vehicle work_id pattern ^[a-zA-Z0-9-]+$ rejects dotted child-bead work_ids (lead-vzxd.5) — BCs can't clarify on dotted work_ids, fall back to blocked-work_done
description: Found dispatching the scenario-integrity sweeps on dotted child-bead work_ids (lead-vzxd.N). The clarify message_type schema rejects the dot (pattern ^[a-zA-Z0-9-]+$), so a BC needing to clarify on a dotted work_id is forced to route via blocked-work_done + mechanism_observation (bc-launcher did this on lead-vzxd.5). Fix in shopsystem-messaging (shop-msg): relax the clarify work_id pattern to accept dotted child-bead ids (align with request_maintenance/assign_scenarios which accept them). Low-pri workaround: use non-dotted work_ids, or BCs fall back to blocked-work_done (works). Parent lead-vzxd.

title: shopsystem-scenarios: 'scenarios hash' must OWN scenario-block extraction (accept a file, extract canonical Scenario:->EOF block, hash) — single source of truth, no caller reimplements
description: PRODUCT DIRECTIVE 2026-06-30 (David): scenario hashing must be done in ONE place — the scenarios BC tool — so there is never confusion on how to do it. ROOT CAUSE it fixes (vglj round-trip, 2026-06-30): scenarios.hash.compute_scenario_hash already centralizes the CANONICALIZATION (strip whitespace, drop blanks + @scenario_hash: lines), but it takes raw text — so every CALLER does the BLOCK EXTRACTION (Scenario:->EOF, drop Feature/@lead_integration) ad hoc: lead-po used an awk-from-@lead_integration pipeline (over-included), the architect hashed the whole Feature-wrapped file (over-included differently), both wrong. FIX: the scenarios tool exposes ONE operation that takes a scenario FILE (Feature-wrapped OR bare block) and returns the canonical block-only hash, owning the extraction so NO caller ever extracts by hand. All callers (lead-po authoring, architect verify, shop-msg send, bc-emit Check-3) delegate to it. Likely warrants an ADR (single-source-of-truth for scenario hashing, refines ADR-018 D2). ROUTE: lead-po authors the contract scenarios; lead-architect verifies shopsystem-scenarios pre-state + dispatches. SUPERSEDES lead-agyy (the lead-po procedure fix becomes 'call the tool'). See memory scenario-hash-is-block-only-calibrate-on-anchor.

title: request_scenario_register (+ any post-v0.4.2 message type) unusable against a BC until that BC's shop-msg is upgraded — BC shop-msg propagates via bc-launcher image bake
description:
Post-v0.4.2 message types are unusable against a BC until that BC's installed shop-msg is upgraded. Confirmed empirically by the lead-igzz clarify (2026-06-30): the shopsystem-templates BC's installed shop-msg does NOT recognize `request_scenario_register` — its supported INBOUND types = {request_maintenance, assign_scenarios, request_bugfix, request_completion_journal, nudge} (older than v0.4.3). `shop-msg read inbox --work-id lead-igzz` fails validation BC-side, so the BC cannot route/validate the message through any role path nor emit a valid `request_scenario_register_response`. The register vehicle works LEAD-side (send wired in v0.4.4, consume v0.4.3+) but is UNUSABLE end-to-end against a BC until that BC's shop-msg is upgraded.

IMPACT / WORKAROUND: until BC-side shop-msg propagation lands, register-pull from a BC must use `request_completion_journal` — the universally-supported (pre-v0.4.3) register-adjacent vehicle. Caveat (from lead-igzz): `request_completion_journal` returns a BARE set of block-only canonical hashes (features/messaging/27,29) with NO per-entry text/title/file_location — it can confirm live-vs-retired of a hash set but cannot deliver text/locations for IMPORT. For pure live-vs-retired reconciliation (as in lead-igzz/oxd8) that is sufficient; for importing pin TEXT it is not.

BC-SHOP-MSG UPGRADE DELIVERY PATH (determined 2026-06-29/30 from the al1r release chain): a BC's shop-msg is delivered baked into the bc-launcher image (bc-base/bc-lead) — BCs run bc-launcher containers that clone inside the container; the shop-msg package version is whatever the image baked. Propagating a new shop-msg/catalog version to BCs therefore requires a bc-launcher image bump that bakes the newer messaging package, then the BC re-pulls that image (the same mechanism used for shop-templates v0.47.0 -> bc-launcher v0.3.38 in lead-al1r/lead-2qno). A standalone BC dependency bump is NOT the live path today; the image bake is. ACTION when register-pull-by-vehicle is next needed against a BC: cut a bc-launcher image bump baking shop-msg >= v0.4.4 (with lead-jo9p --hash fix), have the BC re-pull, THEN the `request_scenario_register` round-trip is routable BC-side.

CROSS-LINKS: lead-jo9p (--hash narrowing crash 'set is not JSON serializable' v0.4.4 — must be fixed in the same propagated version); lead-igzz / lead-oxd8 (the register-pull that exposed this gap, reconciled-via-clarify instead); lead-cl1u (PO authoring of the register vehicle intent); lead-i1we (send subparser wiring v0.4.4).
notes: RESOLVED 2026-06-30 via lead-6b53 release: bc-launcher v0.3.41 baked messaging @v0.4.4 into bc-base/bc-lead (Dockerfile pin @v0.4.0->@v0.4.4, build resolved v0.4.4=4731c99). BCs launched off the rebuilt images now carry shop-msg 0.4.4 -> post-v0.4.2 message types (request_scenario_register et al) routable BC-side. The image-bake propagation r46p tracked is DONE. (Existing running BCs get it on next relaunch off :latest.) Unblocks lead-7njh mirror-import.
close_reason: Closed

title: [bug] dstengle/shopsystem-starter is STALE — last re-rendered from shop-templates v0.28.0 (bin/bootstrap predates v0.46.0); re-render the starter from v0.46.0
description: Empirical (2026-06-29): the dstengle/shopsystem-starter template repo's last commit is e0f0ebe 2026-06-25 'chore: re-render starter from shop-templates v0.28.0'. Adopters create their lead from this starter (gh repo create --template), so they get a v0.28.0-era bin/bootstrap (+ README). The image-float on :latest means the actual lead RENDER uses the in-image shop-templates (0.46.0 when the image is current), so starter staleness is not the agent-vault blocker (that was a stale IMAGE, see the bin/bootstrap-version-verify bug). BUT a v0.28.0 bin/bootstrap may carry old logic (e.g. --shop-name "${PRODUCT}-product" naming, image-pull handling) that diverges from current. FIX: re-render dstengle/shopsystem-starter from shop-templates v0.46.0 (refresh bin/bootstrap + README) and establish a process to re-render the starter on each shop-templates release (so the starter never lags the framework). Owning: shopsystem-templates (starter content) + a release-time re-render step. Related: the bin/bootstrap stale-image-verify bug.

title: [bug] shop-templates update does not render bin/ops-coordinates (bootstrap-only) — existing repos cannot adopt the canonical ops-coordinates-sourcing scripts via update; blocks lead-q14e re-pour
description: Found 2026-06-28 running the lead-q14e re-pour after upgrading shop-templates to v0.46.0. 'shop-templates update --shop-type lead' delivered the bring-up-bc SKILL.md fix and ADVISED the 4 drifted shop-owned ops files (shop-shell/agent-vault-provision/agent-vault-check/compose.yaml) but did NOT render bin/ops-coordinates — and 'shop-templates show ops-coordinates' errors ('no template named ops-coordinates'; only the 4 agent role templates are show-able). Per scenario 211/lead-0act, bin/ops-coordinates is rendered by 'shop-templates bootstrap' (cli.py render-tokens), NOT by update. CONSEQUENCE: an EXISTING repo (the lead, and any adopter past their first bootstrap) cannot obtain bin/ops-coordinates via update — so the canonical v0.46.0 shop-shell (zero-literal, 'source bin/ops-coordinates', references OPS_FRAMEWORK_IMAGE with NO fallback) cannot be adopted via update without either a risky re-bootstrap or hand-creating the artifact. This blocks the lead-q14e re-pour (the lead's drifted shop-shell can't be reconciled to canonical because its sourced artifact has no update-path renderer). FIX (templates): teach 'shop-templates update' to render/refresh bin/ops-coordinates (the bootstrap render-tokens path) for existing repos — new shop-owned bootstrap artifacts must be deliverable via update, not bootstrap-only; pin with a scenario. Owning BC: shopsystem-templates. BLOCKS lead-q14e. Related: lead-0act/211 (render), lead-ow4d, ADR-043 D2.
