title: PO: re-author scenario 211 (d5d65b9cfedc24c1) — pin OPS_BC_BEADS_REPO_FMT {bc}-placeholder integrity after sourcing + relax the default universal so FMT uses a placeholder-safe overridable form
description: Resolves the lead-ow4d BC clarify (shopsystem-templates, bin/ops-coordinates render). ARCHITECT DECISION = {bc}-placeholder integrity REQUIRED in Phase-1 (ADR grounding below). Scenario 211 as authored has a DESTRUCTIVE interaction the BC correctly flagged and the architect EMPIRICALLY CONFIRMED in a bash shell: line 38's universal 'each OPS_* assignment is written as OPS_X=${<OVERRIDE>:-<rendered-default>}' applied to OPS_BC_BEADS_REPO_FMT yields OPS_BC_BEADS_REPO_FMT=${OPS_BC_BEADS_REPO_FMT:-shopsystem-{bc}-beads}, and bash closes the parameter expansion on the FIRST } (after '{bc'), so sourcing resolves to literal 'shopsystem-{bc-beads}' — the {bc} placeholder (which a future consumer fills via ${FMT/\{bc\}/<bc>}) is DESTROYED and the substitution silently no-ops. 211 pins FMT as defined-non-empty (line 37) but does NOT pin its resolved value, so the corrupt default would land in every bootstrapped+forked shop. PO RE-AUTHOR (lead-side block-only authoring; architect verifies, does not introduce hash): (a) ADD a Then pinning that after sourcing with no override, OPS_BC_BEADS_REPO_FMT resolves to '<slug>-{bc}-beads' with the literal {bc} placeholder INTACT, and ideally demonstrate ${OPS_BC_BEADS_REPO_FMT/\{bc\}/<some-bc>} substitutes to '<slug>-<some-bc>-beads' end-to-end; (b) RELAX line 38's universal so FMT may use a placeholder-safe yet still environment-overridable form (render FMT OUTSIDE the ${:-} default position, brace-escape, or any shell-correct form that preserves {bc} AND honors ADR-038 D3 override precedence) — the universal currently FORCES the corrupting form and must be reframed as 'environment-overridable (ADR-038 D3) via a shell-correct form' rather than the literal ${:-} default-position when the default contains brace syntax; (c) RECOMPUTE the block-only @scenario_hash via 'scenarios hash'. ADR GROUNDING: ADR-043 D5 (FMT is the single canonical per-BC beads-naming RULE '<product>-<bc>-beads' whose {bc} placeholder is load-bearing — a corrupt placeholder defeats the rule), D1/D6 (every coordinate computed ONCE and re-used, never re-derived/hardcoded-wrong; a corrupt default IS the hardcoded-wrong-value failure ADR-043 is the durable fix for), and Consequences ('expensive to reverse once the coordinates-source contract ships and adopters FORK it' — baking a corrupt FMT now propagates to every fork). ADR-038 D3's intent is OVERRIDABILITY, satisfied by any shell-correct overridable form — NOT specifically the ${:-} syntax that corrupts the value. Do NOT author BC source or edit the dispatched block here; PO authors the lead-held features/templates/211 block only. After re-hash, ARCHITECT re-dispatches corrected 211 to shopsystem-templates (see the paired re-dispatch bead).
close_reason: PO re-authored scenario 211 (new block-only @scenario_hash 0a3a8267109b5792, supersedes d5d65b9cfedc24c1): added the {bc}-intact Then (OPS_BC_BEADS_REPO_FMT resolves to <slug>-{bc}-beads with placeholder intact after sourcing) + relaxed the ${:-} universal to a placeholder-safe overridable form (per ADR-038 D3 overridability-not-syntax). Other 211 pins preserved. Architect re-dispatches under lead-0act.

title: [bug] shop-msg respond work_done accepts empty --scenario-hash '' (stores ['']) and prints no stdout confirmation -> malformed-but-valid complete lands silently (foot-gun)
description: mechanism_observation from lead-37zx (bc-launcher, 2026-06-28). 'shop-msg respond work_done ... --scenario-hash ""' produced NO stdout (looked like a no-op) yet created an outbox work_done with status:complete, scenario_hashes:[''] (empty-string member), no summary. Two issues: (1) empty-string --scenario-hash accepted+stored as a list member rather than rejected or coerced to []; (2) successful respond emits nothing to stdout, so an operator gets no confirmation. Together: a fat-fingered flag silently lands a malformed-but-schema-valid complete, then blocks the corrected emit as a collision (recoverable only via --force). On a P1 delivery (lead-37zx) the lead nearly reconciled a complete with a bogus empty hash and no evidence summary (caught by reading deeper). FIX (shopsystem-messaging): reject/drop empty-string --scenario-hash values; print a one-line confirmation (work_id + status) on successful respond. Related: lead-mab1 (emit-fidelity), lead-ay7j (consume-enum). Could bundle with messaging emit-hardening.
notes: dispatch request_bugfix -> shopsystem-messaging (lead-7w0w)
close_reason: Reconciled work_done from shopsystem-messaging. Scenario 35 (a2d05a918db02efd) block-only recompute-verified. Both-defect teeth bind (independent reverts: blank-rejection->exits-nonzero leg, stdout removal->confirm leg). empty-list-vs-empty-member must-preserve holds; whitespace-only rejected. Coexistence pins preserved (file32 6c23bb777bab93df + family 19/22-25/29/34); nothing retired. Outbox row consumed. Reachable f610247.

title: [bug] bc-emit work-done wrapper clean-tree carve-out is narrower than work-done-gate Check 1 deliverable-scope — false-refuses emits on harness/config churn, forcing reviewers onto the bare primitive (companion to lead-mab1)
description: mechanism_observation from lead-b6f2 (templates, 2026-06-28). The executable bc-emit work-done (src/shop_templates/bc_emit.py _CARVE_OUTS) discounts only ambient carve-outs (.specstory, .claude/scheduled_tasks.lock, .beads/issues.jsonl). But the work-done-gate skill Check 1 is DELIVERABLE-SCOPED: blocks only on dirty paths under features/ src/ tests/, explicitly naming .claude/canonical/bc-primer.md and .claude/settings.json as NON-deliverable (do not block). The wrapper and its own gate spec DISAGREE: on lead-b6f2 the BC tree carried in-flight harness edits to bc-primer.md (router session-start rewrite) + settings.json (SessionStart hook reorder) — not the deliverable, not safe to commit under this work_id nor revert (another role's in-flight work) — and the wrapper REFUSED the sign-off even though all 5 gate checks pass and features/src/tests is clean. LOAD-BEARING: the harness rewrites these at session-start routinely, so the wrapper false-refuses a large fraction of reviewer emits, forcing reviewers onto the bare shop-msg respond escape valve and thereby BYPASSING the wrapper reachability + scenario-hash re-validation (opposite of intent; likely a contributor to the work_done re-emit churn seen on lead-yyr9/lead-jonx). PROPOSED: reconcile the wrapper clean-tree precondition to the gate deliverable-scope model (block only on dirty features/ src/ tests/, not a carve-out allowlist); _CARVE_OUTS is a narrower instance of deliverable-scope and should be replaced by it. Owning BC: shopsystem-templates. COMPANION to lead-mab1 (template directs reviewer to the wrapper) — both are needed for wrapper-use to actually work: lead-mab1 makes reviewers USE the wrapper, this makes the wrapper USABLE on a churned tree. Related: lead-8vny (gate tightening), lead-oyaj.
notes: DISPATCHED 2026-06-28 -> shopsystem-templates via request_bugfix (work_id lead-3rda). Vehicle: request_bugfix (discriminator: clean-tree precondition EXISTS + pinned by 176 @242c4de927d64339; behavioral tightening of existing wrong model -> bugfix, not assign_scenarios). Carries scenario 212 @cba037e97c6a8325 (deliverable-scope clean-tree). SUPERSEDE: retire 176 @242c4de927d64339 (land-then-retire ADR-036 D4; direct contradiction on .claude/settings.json+bc-primer.md dirty case). COEXIST (not retired): 177 @461d6066ef7dca0a, 178 @12c98d2f7e5259a9, 179 @ea9c1bbd9be87d72, 180 @4a6133f7b5f061a2, 181 @f81ee56bc163934b, 208 @0b48508e40fdde18, 209 @7bcfc89161c0b2ee, 210 @35d3af0c79b55fbf. Pre-state verified on artifact surface (ADR-018 D1): all hashes reproduced block-only via scenarios hash. Read-back OK (non-empty @scenario_hash + @bc tag). Companion to lead-mab1. Delivery rides templates release lead-kdsq (gated on lead-ow4d). shop-msg v0.4.2.
close_reason: bc-emit clean-tree precondition now deliverable-scoped at templates origin/main 1b8b34f: _is_deliverable_path refuses only on dirty features/|src/|tests/, replacing the narrow _CARVE_OUTS allowlist. Scenario 212 @cba037e97c6a8325 landed (teeth: red wrongly refused bc-primer.md/settings.json), 176 @242c4de927d64339 retired, 177-181/208-210 green (513 passed). Dogfood CONFIRMED: clean-tree did NOT refuse on non-deliverable-only dirt (.claude harness churn). Delivery rides templates release lead-kdsq. NOTE: dogfood surfaced a NEXT wrapper-usability issue (Check 3 global STALE scan) -> filed separately.

title: [bug] bc-container launch uses cached :latest image — a new release's images aren't pulled, so delivered fixes silently don't take effect until manual docker pull
description: Found 2026-06-28 delivering lead-boa3 (bc-launcher v0.3.34). After upgrading the bc-container CLI to v0.3.34 and the release republishing bc-base/bc-lead :latest on GHCR, 'bc-container launch' still ran the CACHED local :latest (digest 3cd3de9a, the v0.3.33 image) — docker does not re-pull :latest when present locally. The launch therefore used the OLD image (F2 Dockerfile fix absent), and the fix only took effect after a manual 'docker pull ghcr.io/dstengle/shopsystem-bc-base:latest' + bc-lead. So a release's image-layer fixes (e.g. lead-uiwu F2/F3) silently do NOT take effect until the operator manually pulls. FIX (bc-launcher): bc-container launch should  the :latest (or version-pinned) bc-base/bc-lead image before run (or pin the image to a version the manifest/coordinates declare and pull that), so a freshly-released fix is actually used — never a stale cached image. Owning BC: shopsystem-bc-launcher. Related: lead-boa3/lead-uiwu (delivery), lead-z0v2 (F3, will need the same pull after v0.3.35).
notes:
PO authoring (2026-06-30): NO NEW SCENARIO AUTHORED — ko2v is SUBSUMED, not a distinct gap.

The corrected launch-time-pull behavior ko2v asks for is ALREADY pinned by existing
features/bc-launcher/39-launch-pulls-current-latest-not-stale-local-cache.gherkin
(@scenario_hash:af2f03d3ac519cb5, pins ADR-021 D3 "Freshness at launch"). Scenario 39
Then-steps: "launch resolves the bc-base 'latest' tag against the registry and pulls
digest D_new before starting the container ... started container is running from image
digest D_new rather than the cached D_old." That IS ko2v's fix (launch pulls fresh
:latest, never a stale cached image) — same BC (shopsystem-bc-launcher), same path
(bc-container launch), same observable.

Scenario 39 was DISPATCHED via request_bugfix lead-yy30 (pinned set included
af2f03d3ac519cb5) and RECONCILED COMPLETE 2026-06-03 via lead-yk3o demonstration
(work_done hashes == scenario_hashes_pinned, ADR-018 D2). ko2v was observed 2026-06-28
in v0.3.34 — i.e. the bug RECURRED despite 39 being demonstrated complete. That makes
ko2v a REGRESSION / demonstration-fidelity problem against scenario 39, NOT a missing
scenario. Authoring scenario 73 would duplicate 39 (anti-build-trap: no duplicate
scenario for an already-pinned behavior).

Distinct from this session's lead-5xnd (provenance: scenarios 71/72 — VERIFY which
version you got) and lead-b2iz (bootstrap version-guard) — those are the VERIFY axis;
39/ko2v are the REFRESH axis. Not subsumed BY those; subsumed BY 39.

RECOMMENDED HANDLING (Architect): re-dispatch as request_bugfix re-pinning
af2f03d3ac519cb5 to shopsystem-bc-launcher as a regression, with a stronger
demonstration requirement (must exercise an actual registry republish of :latest at a
new digest and prove the running container uses D_new), rather than assign_scenarios.
No new feature file created.
dispatch request_bugfix -> shopsystem-bc-launcher (lead-ko2v)
close_reason: Regression re-pin reconciled (ADR-018). Scenario 39 (af2f03d3ac519cb5) hash UNCHANGED — block-only recompute reproduces. Demonstration-fidelity fix: hardened demo genuinely exercises D_old-cached/D_new-republished divergence (FakeDockerDriver real cache, distinct sha pins, pull-before-run via operation_log, container serves D_new). WOULD-HAVE-CAUGHT proven by independent reverts: resolve-but-skip-pull (exact v0.3.34 symptom) -> RED; run-cached :latest -> RED; restore -> GREEN. Mechanism real (controller.launch resolves digest -> driver.pull before driver.run). Scope: bc-container launch path only; lead/bootstrap untouched. 264 passed/1 skipped. Commit 83d2569 reachable from origin/main 52e8adc. Scenario 39 stays pinned at af2f03d3ac519cb5 (re-affirmed, NOT superseded).

title: [templates] bc-reviewer sign-off emit directs reviewer to BARE shop-msg respond work_done, bypassing the bc-emit work-done wrapper (179 orphan-refusal) — wrapper-use enforcement gap behind lead-jonx typo
description: mechanism_observation from lead-jonx (templates, 2026-06-28). The BC emitted work_done with a typo'd hash 'cc52003444ee7' (real pinned hash for scenario 161 is 'cc52003444ea63f7'); shop-msg ACCEPTED and persisted the bad hash into the lead inbox without complaint — caught only by post-emit re-read + --force re-emit (this is the lead-jonx re-emit the lead consumed as redundant). The work-done-gate skill's Check 3 (recompute hashes + git grep features/ for each + enforce subset) is documented as the REVIEWER'S MANUAL responsibility, but the emit CLI performs NO cross-check of --scenario-hash against the committed features/ tree, even though it runs in-repo with features/ available. Load-bearing: every reviewer work_done emit across every BC routes through this CLI and currently trusts caller hashes verbatim; a single transcription slip writes an ADR-010-violating hash to the lead inbox, mis-attributing a pass to a scenario never pinned (or, with a prefix-style typo, the wrong scenario). PROPOSED (BC): shop-msg respond work_done validates each --scenario-hash against the committed features/ tree at emit time (git grep '@scenario_hash:<h>' features/), refusing on any hash not pinned — promoting Check 3's subset rule from reviewer-manual prose to a CLI hard-refuse (mirroring existing schema/collision refusals). Owning BC: shopsystem-messaging (shop-msg CLI). Related: lead-cw7 (placeholder-summary emit-fidelity class), work-done-gate skill Check 3, ADR-010.
notes:
2026-06-28 DISPATCHED (lead-architect). request_bugfix -> shopsystem-templates, work_id lead-mab1, via shop-msg send (shopsystem-messaging v0.4.2). Deposited + read-back confirmed in BC inbox.

PRE-STATE VERDICT (ADR-018 D1/D2 artifact surface; no BC source read/run): `shop-templates show bc-reviewer` shows the "## Outcomes" Sign-off bullet instructs the BARE `shop-msg respond work_done --scenario-hash <h>` while the FIRST-ACTION prose claims the bc-emit work-done wrapper enforces preconditions — so the wrapper is never invoked and 179's orphan-refusal is dead on the sign-off path. Existing-but-mis-stated template prose, unpinned as to which command is instructed -> request_bugfix (tightening). Confirmed.

HASH REPRODUCTIONS (block-only via `scenarios hash`): 210=35d3af0c79b55fbf MATCH (PO-authored, verify-only); 179=ea9c1bbd9be87d72 MATCH; 181=f81ee56bc163934b MATCH; 189=942895d284cf0ec7 MATCH. Dispatched ScenarioPayload hash=35d3af0c79b55fbf (read-back confirmed).

189 RESOLUTION = COEXIST (NOT amended). 189 scopes its respond-CLI assertion to NUMBERED steps; bc-reviewer "## Outcomes" is bullet-form (vacuous for the per-step rule); clarify + work_done(blocked) bullets retain `shop-msg respond`; only the sign-off bullet changes to `bc-emit work-done` (the wrapper over shop-msg respond work_done, a concrete named CLI, not a bare prohibited verb). BC instructed to confirm 189 suite stays green after 210 lands.

COEXISTENCE: 210 additive, retires nothing. 179/181/189 all unchanged. Scenario 35 already retired/deleted (absent from features tree). Fold-in guidance carried: work-done-gate SKILL.md prose correction folded into this bugfix, BC pins against in-repo skill source (skill not on lead-readable artifact surface).
close_reason: bc-reviewer wrapper-use fix complete at templates origin/main c189216: the Outcomes sign-off bullet now names 'bc-emit work-done' as the concrete sign-off command, forbids hand-invoking bare shop-msg respond on the sign-off path, scopes bare --force to forced-recovery only; work-done-gate SKILL.md gains an additive wrapper-emit section. Scenario 210 @35d3af0c79b55fbf verified (teeth: red names bare primitive); 189 integrity intact (clarify/blocked bullets unchanged), 179/181 green, nothing retired. NOTE: the BC again had to use the bare escape valve for THIS emit because the wrapper false-refused on non-deliverable .claude harness churn — CONFIRMS lead-3rda is recurring; lead-mab1 (use the wrapper) + lead-3rda (make the wrapper usable) are both needed. Delivery rides templates release (lead-kdsq).

title: [bug] shop-msg consume outbox --message-type rejects request_completion_journal_response (only accepts clarify/work_done/mechanism_observation) — lead cannot drain that row
description: Found 2026-06-28 reconciling lead-glel. The architect dispatched request_completion_journal to bc-launcher and got a request_completion_journal_response in the lead outbox, but 'shop-msg consume outbox --bc <bc> --work-id <id> --message-type request_completion_journal_response' is REJECTED — the --message-type enum only accepts {clarify, work_done, mechanism_observation}. So the lead cannot consume/drain a completion-journal-response row via the documented CLI; lead-glel stays OPEN with its outbox row orphaned-pending. FIX: add 'request_completion_journal_response' (and audit for other BC->lead response types, e.g. any request_shop_card response) to the shop-msg consume outbox --message-type enum. Owning BC: shopsystem-messaging (shop-msg CLI). BLOCKS lead-glel closure. Discovered-from lead-glel.

title: Lead-mirror reconcile: bc-launcher network-naming family {19,21,22,23,24,25,26} — confirm retire vs realign against BC committed BODIES (lead-glel follow-up)
description:
FOLLOW-UP from lead-glel reconciliation (2026-06-28). lead-glel retired the two BC-CONFIRMED phantom lead-mirror scenarios 18 (c1eac3b07f198049, network-naming-01, manifest product->network derivation) and 20 (384b6296b6779419, network-naming-03, no-manifest error path) — both reported ABSENT from the BC features/src/tests by lead-ngzl work_done, and superseded by scenario 63 (5a1fc25a7823b268, on-disk-config network resolution) + the BC's NARROWED error-path f1c3a6ca6b64b713.

OPEN: the REST of the lead-held network-naming family — 19 (3c50a40bbc918f8e slugify product field), 21 (8a26d4aa1da12870 create network if absent), 22 (c06b181e342f5191 no-create if exists), 23 (9378f92b158b4ffc explicit --network overrides derived), 24 (593d25ed835942ed explicit --network suppresses creation), 25 (5e76f9229443bd89 same-product shared network), 26 (f70e4ad40470198c create-once idempotency) — are ALL ABSENT from the bc-launcher request_completion_journal_response (lead-glel) set, BUT cannot be retired on journal-absence alone. EVIDENCE OF AMBIGUITY: scenario 63's own authoring header asserts '21/22/25/26 (network creation/idempotency) COEXIST UNCHANGED', and lead-ngzl reported the BC feature diff was ADDITION-ONLY with no hash-changes to existing BC-own blocks. So the completion JOURNAL is NOT a faithful full owned-register (it omits the BC's-narrowed-error-path replacement context and conflicts with the coexist note); journal-absence != phantom. Only 18/20 had explicit BC 'absent from features/src/tests' confirmation.

ACTION FOR PO/ARCHITECT: obtain the BC's ACTUAL committed scenario BODIES for the network-naming subsystem (request_shop_card or a body-bearing register, NOT the hash-only completion journal). For each of {19,21,22,23,24,25,26}: classify (a) genuinely orphaned at BC -> retire from lead mirror; (b) reworded/consolidated at BC under a different hash -> REALIGN the lead body and re-pin block-only; (c) still committed unchanged -> keep. Also decide whether the lead should MIRROR the BC's narrowed error-path f1c3a6ca6b64b713 (replacement for retired 20). 19 in particular is tightly coupled to retired-18's manifest-product derivation that 63 supersedes, so it is the strongest realign/retire candidate. Do NOT blind-delete; this is body-level register-divergence reconciliation. Precedent: lead-y6gu (messaging-registry mirror drift), lead-glel.

title: shopsystem-bc-launcher: switch _resolve_shop_network() from interim compose.yaml/name.md to the ratified bin/ops-coordinates artifact (lead-7wta/lead-ngzl source-leg follow-up)
description: lead-ngzl shipped (bc-launcher origin/main e10c915, scenario 63 @5a1fc25a7823b268): bc-container launch resolves the shop docker network from on-disk config (compose.yaml networks:name + name.md slug) via _resolve_shop_network() with NO --network flag — value resolves to 'shopsystem'. That on-disk read is the INTERIM source; lead-ngzl deferred source-artifact pinning to ADR-043 D2 (this turn). NOW D2 FINALIZED (lead-7wta): the canonical source is bin/ops-coordinates (OPS_NETWORK, plus OPS_BROKER_ADDR / SHOPMSG_DSN coordinates). FOLLOW-UP: bc-launcher's _resolve_shop_network() (and broker/dsn/image resolution) should switch to reading the ratified bin/ops-coordinates artifact as the single source, replacing the interim compose/name.md read, once the artifact RENDER lands (lead-ow4d). Behavior + value ('shopsystem') unchanged; this swaps the SOURCE to the single artifact, preserving ADR-038 D3 precedence (explicit --network > artifact > nothing). Vehicle: request_bugfix to shopsystem-bc-launcher once lead-ow4d delivers the artifact. GATED on lead-ow4d (artifact must exist first). ADR refs: adr/043 'D2 — FINALIZED', scenario 63.

title: [lead-side config] add product: shopsystem to bc-manifest.yaml (ADR-038 D1/D3) — declares the single identity root so launch derives network=shopsystem
description: Lead-side config follow-up to lead-ngzl. ADR-038 D1 makes the manifest top-level 'product:' field the canonical product-identity declaration (value = the product SLUG, e.g. 'shopsystem'); ADR-038 Consequences explicitly calls for 'population of the live manifest with product: shopsystem'. The live lead bc-manifest.yaml currently carries NO product: field, so bc-container cannot derive the network from it and falls to the scenario-20 error path (forcing manual --network). Adding 'product: shopsystem' (single-token slug; slugify -> 'shopsystem' = compose network) is the immediate lead-side unblock via the EXISTING slugify(product) mechanism (scenario 18), complementary to the durable bc-launcher fix dispatched under lead-ngzl (scenario 63 + amend 20/18). DO NOT edit until the bc-launcher schema accepts product: (ADR-038 D1 schema addition) OR confirm the current launcher tolerates an unknown top-level key — sequence with the lead-ngzl work_done. ACTION (router): add a top-level 'product: shopsystem' line to /workspace/bc-manifest.yaml. NOTE: canonical value is the slug 'shopsystem', NOT 'shopsystem-product' (the shop NAME) and NOT 'shopsystem product' (scenario 18's non-canonical example). Related: lead-ngzl, ADR-038, ADR-043 D1.
close_reason: Done: added 'product: shopsystem' (ADR-038 D1 single-token slug) to bc-manifest.yaml; bc-container manifest validate passes (rc=0). This is the on-disk derivation root for the shop network. End-to-end flagless launch is fully delivered once the lead-ngzl bc-launcher bugfix lands (fixes the derivation + absent-default).

title: [templates skill-doc] bring-up-bc overstates BCLAUNCHER_HOST_HOME as universally required — contradicts canonical scenario 44 (no host-home for clone-path)
description: Defect (3) surfaced by lead-ngzl. The canonical bring-up-bc skill (shipped by shopsystem-templates; lead-poured at .claude/skills/bring-up-bc/SKILL.md lines 41-57) states BCLAUNCHER_HOST_HOME is UNIVERSALLY required — 'Always set BCLAUNCHER_HOST_HOME ... this is the single most common launch-time miss' — and that without it the container 'comes up with a home missing credential-helper config, git identity, and agent-vault client material'. This CONTRADICTS canonical bc-launcher scenario 44 @scenario_hash:d6296d959f851be5 ('bc-container launch does not require BCLAUNCHER_HOST_HOME to resolve a credential mount source') and the agent-vault brokered-credential model (ADR-026 / scenarios 44-46/50: a launched container carries NO host-filesystem credential mounts; credentials arrive via the agent-vault proxy on the wire). For a CLONE-path BC (no --workspace-mount) the host-home requirement is spurious; it is relevant only to workspace-mount/lead launches. FIX: amend the bring-up-bc skill body to scope BCLAUNCHER_HOST_HOME to workspace-mount/lead launches only (drop the universal 'always/single most common miss' framing). This is a TEMPLATES skill-doc change (canonical skill data shipped by shopsystem-templates), NOT a bc-launcher scenario. Owning BC: shopsystem-templates. Route: PO authors/sharpens the skill-body expectation, then architect dispatches to shopsystem-templates. Related: lead-ngzl (parent finding), scenario 44, ADR-026.

title: [bug] bc-container launch does not resolve shop config (network/broker/dsn) from on-disk configuration — forces manual --network; BCLAUNCHER_HOST_HOME spuriously required for clone-path BCs
description: Empirical finding (product authority, 2026-06-28, while bringing up shopsystem-templates). bc-container launch (shopsystem-bc-launcher) fails to start a registered fleet BC from KNOWN ON-DISK CONFIGURATION; the operator had to reverse-engineer params from another running container. DEFECTS: (1) --network is HARD-REQUIRED ('no network: bc-manifest.yaml not found and --network not provided') even though the shop network name 'shopsystem' is declared on disk in compose.yaml (networks: name: shopsystem) and is the product slug, derivable without a flag. bc-manifest.yaml is scoped (per its own header) to 'remote URLs' only and carries NO shop-level launch config (network/broker/dsn/image). (2) Broker/dsn were passed defensively but were NOT actually required (the only launch error was about network), so bc-container DOES already resolve AGENT_VAULT_ADDR (.env: http://agent-vault:14321) + SHOPMSG_DSN from env/defaults — confirming the principle that on-disk/env config CAN drive launch; network is the gap. (3) BCLAUNCHER_HOST_HOME: the bring-up-bc skill states it is universally required ('single most common launch-time miss'), but for a CLONE-path BC (no --workspace-mount) the BC clones its repo inside the container and gets credentials via the agent-vault PROXY on the wire, NOT a host-home bind mount — so the host-home requirement appears spurious for clone-path launches (it is relevant only to workspace-mount/lead launches). EMPIRICALLY: launching shopsystem-templates worked with 'bc-container launch shopsystem-templates --network shopsystem --shopmsg-dsn ... --agent-vault-broker ...' + BCLAUNCHER_HOST_HOME=/home/vscode; container came up healthy. FIX DIRECTION: bc-container launch must resolve the shop network (and broker/dsn/image) from on-disk configuration with NO per-launch flags for a standard fleet BC — ideally the ADR-043 single-source ops-coordinates artifact (the SAME source shop-shell/provision migrate to via lead-ml51/lead-7wta), or the existing compose-network/slug + .env in the interim; and NOT require BCLAUNCHER_HOST_HOME for clone-path launches. Owning BC: shopsystem-bc-launcher. RECONCILE with ADR-043 ops-coordinates (artifact bin/ops-coordinates does NOT exist yet — sequencing dependency on lead-7wta). Skill follow-up: bring-up-bc skill (canonical, shipped by templates) must update its launch instructions + drop the host-home overstatement once the contract lands. Related: lead-7wta, lead-ml51, and the bc-status-unreliable observation (reports healthy/working BCs as offline).

title: TEMPLATES: tighten work-done-gate skill — orphaned-OPEN-sub-issue detection + decomposition durability (committed/reachable from origin/main); needs PO pinning scenario
description: SOURCE: mechanism_observation from shopsystem-messaging on lead-yyr9 (2026-06-27). The work-done-gate skill (vendored, shipped by shopsystem-templates; bc-implementer invokes it via the Skill tool) has a gap: on lead-yyr9 the Implementer decomposed TWICE — an abandoned first pass (3vx.1/.2/.3 left OPEN) and a real TDD-ordered second pass (3vx.4/.5/.6 closed RED->GREEN->refactor) — closing only the second set and never committing .beads/issues.jsonl. The current gate misses this: Check 4 ('>=1 RED sub-issue exists and all sub-issues are closed') is silent on orphaned OPEN duplicates, and Check 1 (clean tree) is evaluated independently so the failure reads as a generic dirty tree rather than 'the bd decomposition+closures are non-durable / unreachable from origin/main'. Orphans surface in 'bd ready' and wedge the router idle-detection (item 2) into re-picking completed work. PROPOSED TIGHTENING (Implementer-proposed): Check 4 additionally requires (i) no OPEN sub-issue remains under the work_id umbrella bead (orphaned-duplicate detection), and (ii) the closed-decomposition state is COMMITTED and reachable from origin/main (mirror Check 2's work_id-reachability discipline for the bd registry). VALIDITY: valid against the artifact surface — the failure mode (abandon-then-recreate under a bd hiccup) is a natural recurring Implementer failure mode; the lead-yyr9 implementation itself was faithful/correct (all scenario/source/teeth probes pass, 444 passed) yet the gate mis-reported the bd-bookkeeping non-durability as a generic dirty tree. DISCRIMINATOR VERDICT (lead-architect): the work-done-gate skill capability EXISTS (Check 1/Check 4 present and executing — the BC self-blocked on them); the specific invariants (orphan detection, decomposition durability) are NOT currently enforced/pinned -> tightening of existing-but-unpinned behavior. The change is BEHAVIORAL (the gate will now FAIL cases it previously mis-passed/mis-reported), NOT flat -> NOT request_maintenance. VEHICLE = request_bugfix to shopsystem-templates, carrying a PO-authored pinning scenario. CAVEAT: final assign_scenarios-vs-request_bugfix confirmation requires verifying the shopsystem-templates pre-state register (whether Check 4/Check 1 are already pinned by a templates scenario) — the work-done-gate skill body + templates features/ register are NOT on the lead host (ADR-018); inspect via 'shop-templates show' + a request_scenario_register/work_done from shopsystem-templates before dispatch. WHAT A PO SCENARIO MUST PIN: (a) given a work_id umbrella bead with >=1 OPEN sub-issue at emit time, the work-done-gate FAILS naming the orphaned-open-sub-issue case (distinct from generic dirty-tree); (b) given the closed decomposition (incl. RED/GREEN/refactor sub-issue closures) exists only in an uncommitted .beads/issues.jsonl (not reachable from origin/main), the gate FAILS naming non-durable/unreachable decomposition (distinct from generic dirty-tree). NEXT STEP: PO round-trip REQUIRED to author the pinning scenario(s); then architect verifies templates pre-state + dispatches request_bugfix. Do NOT dispatch until the PO scenario exists.

Durability-surface guidance: implement 209 'reachable from pushed remote' as bd-dolt push of closure STATE, NOT git-tree clean of .beads/issues.jsonl (carved out by 176, non-idempotent) -> ADR-036 implementation guidance. @bc:shopsystem-templates tag confirmed on both scenarios in read-back. Awaiting BC work_done.

Gated on --plan-umbrella so non-plan emits untouched; 176/177/180/154/181 + beads-health 05 stay green, nothing retired; teeth-verified (orphan-open refuses; durability-unreachable refuses). Fixes the gate-churn class (cf. lead-yyr9 3-pass). Delivery rides the templates release (lead-kdsq).

title: Interim lead-side hardening: bake capture/restore of 4 identity fields into request_bugfix dispatch SOP (or a shop-msg send wrapper) until lead-rqox source fix lands
description: Independent of the source fix (owned by shopsystem-messaging, see lead-rqox). Until the shop-msg bd-integration is fixed to do an additive-only patch, the architect must stop relying on ad-hoc manual capture/restore on every dispatch. Recommendation: a thin LEAD-SHOP wrapper around 'shop-msg send' (or a named SOP step) that, when the work_id bead pre-exists, captures title/type/priority/description before the send and restores+verifies (bd show) after. Belongs LEAD-SIDE, NOT in shopsystem-messaging: the durable fix goes into messaging via request_bugfix, so we should not add clobber-compensation logic to the messaging BC that would be ripped out once fixed. This wrapper is disposable interim mitigation; retire it when lead-rqox closes.
notes:
RETIREMENT GATE (lead-architect, 2026-06-27): keep this interim capture/restore SOP IN FORCE. The source fix is on messaging origin/main (0329a2b) but NOT yet in the lead's running shop-msg (installed v0.4.0 still clobbered during the lead-yyr9 dispatch). Retire ONLY after lead-zqub (messaging release containing 0329a2b + lead-side shop-msg upgrade) lands and no-clobber is confirmed live on the artifact surface.
2026-06-28 lead-zqub upgrade LANDED (shop-msg now v0.4.2). This interim capture/restore SOP becomes RETIRABLE once the next real dispatch confirms native no-clobber (see lead-rqox). Keep applying it as a safety net on the very next 'shop-msg send'; retire immediately after that send confirms identity fields survive without it.
close_reason: RETIRED. Interim lead-side capture/restore SOP no longer needed: lead-rqox source fix is live in shop-msg v0.4.2 and confirmed no-clobber on the lead-ngzl dispatch. Dispatches no longer corrupt work_id identity fields.

title: ADR-043 D2 FINALIZE: decide the single ops-coordinates artifact shape/path (env-file vs manifest [product] block) + sharpen lead-ml51 scenarios 204/205 source-target leg (PO re-author + re-hash)
description: Follow-up to lead-ml51 dispatch. ADR-043 D2 left the ops-coordinates artifact concrete shape/path OPEN ('an ops/coordinates env-file OR a [product] block of the manifest'). lead-ml51 scenarios 204 (@scenario_hash:b7ea0de32ef49854) and 205 (@scenario_hash:1885dea2b4550fde) were dispatched deliberately filename-agnostic: they pin that shop-shell SOURCES the single artifact by a 'source '/'. ' directive and carries references, WITHOUT pinning the artifact filename/format. ARCHITECT LEAN (recorded in ADR-046 Open-dependency section): the scenarios' own source/. mechanism requires the artifact to be DIRECTLY SHELL-SOURCEABLE, which a manifest YAML [product] block is NOT — so D2 should finalize toward a RENDERED, shell-sourceable ops/coordinates env-file DERIVED at bootstrap from the manifest product: root (consistent with ADR-043 D1, ADR-038). Once D2 finalizes the concrete path: amend ADR-043 D2 to ratify the shape, then PO RE-AUTHORS 204/205 to sharpen the source-target leg to the concrete artifact path and RE-COMPUTES block-only hashes; architect re-dispatches the sharpened scenarios (clarify-correction-chain re-enumeration applies). Spans all bin/ scripts (footing/provision/check/approve-claude/shop-shell), not just shop-shell.
notes:
2026-06-29 D2 RECONCILED-TO-IMPLEMENTATION + CLOSED (lead-architect, ADR work; no BC dispatch). The 2026-06-28 finalization is confirmed against the shop-templates scenarios that SHIPPED + reconciled this session, on the contract/artifact surface (ADR-018 D1/D2): 211 (@scenario_hash:0a3a8267109b5792, bootstrap renders bin/ops-coordinates KEY=value env-file + slug contract _ops_slug strips -product + placeholder-safe OPS_BC_BEADS_REPO_FMT=shopsystem-{bc}-beads + OPS_FRAMEWORK_IMAGE), 213 (@4c646ae20a1540e3, update create-if-absent), 228 (@8e5955d5fb5bb9c8, update refresh-drifted-in-place byte-equal to bootstrap render). All three hashes reproduce under the installed `scenarios hash` tool over lead-held block-only Gherkin (verified this turn).
ONE DIVERGENCE corrected in ADR-043 D2: the 2026-06-28 text predicted update does NOT overwrite (advisory-on-drift, mirroring compose.yaml 139/140). The shipped contract chose the OPPOSITE: bin/ops-coordinates is a derived single-source MANAGED-RENDER artifact that update OWNS (re-pours like managed agent files 35/36 + lead skill group 162-164), NOT 139/140 drift-advisory. ADR status header, the D2 RECONCILED note, PATH paragraph, DERIVATION paragraph, and OPS_SLUG/OPS_BC_BEADS_REPO_FMT key descriptions all updated to record implemented reality. Artifact SHAPE (env-file/bin/ops-coordinates/OPS_* keys/slug contract) unchanged from 2026-06-28 + confirmed by 211.
NOTHING GENUINELY-OPEN REMAINS for D2 — no product-judgment sub-decision; settled by implementation. CLOSING lead-7wta.
NOTE (reconciliation hygiene, NOT a D2 blocker; surfaced to router/PO): scenario 228's internal cross-reference to scenario 213 cites a STALE hash @dd82193e56e52d95, but 213's actual block-only hash is @4c646ae20a1540e3. PO/reconciliation matter for the 213/228 scenario text, not an ADR edit.
Downstream (tracked separately): lead-ow4d ✓ DONE (bootstrap render shipped); lead-py3y OPEN (PO sharpen 204/205/63 source-target leg to concrete path + re-hash); lead-5lqg OPEN (bc-launcher switch _resolve_shop_network to ratified artifact).
close_reason: Closed

title: PO re-author: re-pin bin/shop-shell SHAPE legs lost when 172/134 retire under lead-ml51 (shebang, exec-bit, compose up -d postgres agent-vault, --env-file, -it, --workspace-mount, --mount-docker-socket, --startup-prompt, bc-container attach, and the NEGATIVE legs: no proxy URL / no CA fetch / no SHELL_IMAGE+docker build / no host-cred mounts)
description: Follow-up to lead-ml51 dispatch. The request_bugfix to shopsystem-templates retires 172 (@scenario_hash:725562869d9df919) and 134 (@scenario_hash:a3b723341d9f2872) because their image-literal / SHOPSYSTEM_DATA-literal legs contradict the new parameterization (203/204/205). But 172/134 ALSO carried non-contradicting SHAPE pins that 203/204/205 do NOT restate and that would become unpinned: shebang #!/usr/bin/env bash, owner-exec bit, 'docker compose ... up -d postgres agent-vault', --env-file assembly, launcher 'docker run --rm -it', socket+repo mounts, inner 'bc-container launch --workspace-mount --mount-docker-socket', --startup-prompt lead override, 'bc-container attach', and the NEGATIVE/absence legs (no 14322/HTTPS_PROXY, no 'agent-vault ca fetch'/'agent-vault-check', no SHOPSYSTEM_SHELL_IMAGE/'docker build', no host $HOME/.claude or .gitconfig mounts). PO authors a successor scenario (block-only @scenario_hash) re-pinning the surviving shape so the wrapper contract is not permanently lost; architect verifies+dispatches. Scenario 198 (socket-GID symmetry) and 203/204/205 already cover their slices and need not be duplicated. NOTE: keep the shape scenario product-literal-free per 203 (no 'shopsystem' in the wrapper).

title: [bug] shop-shell: attach docker run missing --group-add $SOCKET_GID (asymmetric with launcher run) — verify pin-status vs stale-render before dispatch
description: Empirical finding (product authority, 2026-06-27). bin/shop-shell computes SOCKET_GID at runtime (stat -L /var/run/docker.sock, correct product-neutral pattern) and passes --group-add $SOCKET_GID to the LAUNCHER docker run (step 3) but the ATTACH docker run (step 4, exec) was MISSING it. The attach run invokes 'bc-container attach' which needs docker-daemon access, so the asymmetry is a genuine defect; operator hand-patched the attach run to add --group-add (+ a debug 'set -x' on line 2 that must be removed). NOT a hard-coding/identity issue — SOCKET_GID is runtime-derived. OPEN QUESTION (product authority hypothesis): is this asymmetry just a STALE RENDER (canonical shopsystem-templates already symmetric, this lead copy not re-poured) vs a live template bug? ADR-018: lead cannot read canonical templates source. ARCHITECT ACTION: during pre-state verification check features/ + mailbox history for an existing scenario pinning symmetric docker-socket-GID grant across BOTH shop-shell docker runs. If ALREADY pinned => stale render; advise operator re-pour (shop-templates update), NO new dispatch. If NOT pinned => PO pins 'both shop-shell docker runs grant the socket GID' + architect dispatches request_bugfix to shopsystem-templates. Owning source: shopsystem-templates (rendered bin/shop-shell). Related: lead-ml51 (shop-shell parameterization, same script).
notes: 2026-06-27 ARCHITECT PRE-STATE FINDING (ADR-018 artifact surface; lead host has no repos/ BC source). VERDICT: STALE RENDER, not a live template bug — NO dispatch. The symmetric docker-socket-GID grant across BOTH shop-shell docker runs IS ALREADY PINNED by scenario 198 (@scenario_hash:84a32b05666d9e82, verified block-only via `scenarios list` over features/templates/198-bootstrap-shop-shell-grants-docker-socket-group-symmetrically-to-launch-and-attach.gherkin). 198 pins: resolves SOCKET_GID via stat -L -c %g /var/run/docker.sock AND every socket-mounting docker run (launcher bc-container launch AND attach bc-container attach) carries --group-add. So canonical shopsystem-templates already renders the attach run with --group-add; the operator local bin/shop-shell that was missing it is a stale (un-re-poured) copy. NEXT STEP (operator action, no dispatch): re-pour via `shop-templates update` to regenerate bin/shop-shell from current canonical, which restores the symmetric --group-add on the attach run. OPERATOR HAND-EDITS TO DISCARD on re-pour: (a) the debug `set -x` on bin/shop-shell line 2, and (b) the manual --group-add patch on the attach run — both are operator hand-edits, NOT committed by us, and the re-pour supersedes them. ORTHOGONAL to lead-ml51 parameterization dispatch: 198 STAYS (not retired); the lead-ml51 dispatch explicitly instructs the BC not to retire 198.
close_reason: Resolved: lead repo's bin/shop-shell now carries symmetric --group-add $SOCKET_GID on both docker runs (committed 39c3bc8). Confirmed stale-render — canonical v0.45.0 (scenario 198 / lead-ddb2) already symmetric; shop-templates update is drift-protected so the fix was applied by hand (kept operator --group-add, dropped debug set -x). Broader ops-file drift reconciliation tracked in lead-q14e.

title: Pin gh + agent-vault presence-on-PATH in bc-base (regression guard) — re-scoped from retired lead-qi0q, docker EXCLUDED
description:
Re-scoped residue of retired lead-qi0q (which was overtaken by PDR-020 Addendum II's bc-base/bc-lead split). SCOPE (current-design-consistent): pin that the bc-base image resolves gh and agent-vault on PATH, as a regression guard. gh is present but UNPINNED (incidental from the upstream base image, could regress); agent-vault is installed but only incidentally asserted (lead-side scenario 51, 938342272de4e38a, in bootstrap-entrypoint mode).

EXPLICITLY OUT OF SCOPE: docker. Per PDR-020 Addendum II (366f82e), docker is bc-LEAD-only by deliberate security/footprint decision (docker/bc-lead/Dockerfile:49); it must NOT be pinned on bc-base. If docker presence wants a regression guard, that is a SEPARATE bc-LEAD-targeted pin, not this bead.

PRE-STATE TO VERIFY BEFORE DISPATCH (lesson from lead-qi0q): the BC's lead-qi0q clarify reported the bc-base toolset is pinned BC-LOCALLY by scenario d9909f38 (bc_base_framework_cli_pins.feature) WITHOUT docker. Those files are NOT on the lead host (ADR-018; BC-local scenarios). So BEFORE authoring/dispatching a new lead-side scenario, the architect must reconcile: does BC-local d9909f38 already pin gh + agent-vault presence? If YES, this bead is satisfied (record + close, possibly promote/adopt the BC-local pin per lead-ogky). If only the framework CLIs (shop-templates/shop-msg/bc-container) are in d9909f38 and gh/agent-vault are NOT, then a lead-dispatched presence pin is genuinely needed. Confirm via a BC nudge (the only in-protocol way to read BC-local scenario contents) rather than assuming.

Refs: retired lead-qi0q, PDR-020 Addendum II, lead-ogky (BC-LOCAL unpinned scenario enumeration gap).
notes:
2026-06-27: Pre-state confirmation nudge SENT to shopsystem-bc-launcher (shop-msg send nudge --reason general --work-id lead-qi0q, msg id 1065). Two payloads: (1) WITHDRAWAL of retired lead-qi0q assign_scenarios (scenario 63 docker-on-bc-base) — BC told do-not-implement, scenario 63 + ADR-044 retired, lead-qi0q closed; (2) PRE-STATE QUESTION asking whether BC-local scenario d9909f38 (bc_base_framework_cli_pins.feature) or any other bc-base scenario already pins gh presence-on-PATH AND agent-vault presence-on-PATH as a regression guard, or whether they are only incidentally present (gh unpinned/incidental from upstream base; agent-vault only incidentally asserted by lead-side scenario 51). AWAITING BC ANSWER before authoring/dispatching any lead-side presence pin. Next reconciliation: if BC confirms d9909f38 already pins both, record + close (or adopt BC-local pin per lead-ogky); if not, a lead-dispatched presence pin (gh + agent-vault, docker EXCLUDED) is genuinely needed.

title: BC agent over-defers: pauses for operator permission on irreversible push/emit despite bc-primer full-autonomy mandate (observed: templates agent on lead-20bt)
description: 2026-06-24, shopsystem-templates agent processing lead-20bt: instead of acting per its bc-primer autonomy contract, it surfaced an interactive permission MENU ('How should I handle the irreversible push to origin/main + work_done emit?' options: 1 Full autonomy (bc-primer mandates) / 2 push+emit only / 3 hold / 4 type / 5 chat) and IDLED awaiting operator selection — blocking the queued lead-20bt + lead-62sy. The agent itself labeled option 1 'what the bc-primer mandates' yet still paused. Root cause is the same class as lead-o6tp: the autonomy mandate is PROSE the agent may not follow; an over-cautious permission reflex (likely the Claude Code irreversible-action confirmation) overrides it. Required a lead-side unblock (select option 1 + inject a full-autonomy directive). IMPACT: BC dispatches can stall indefinitely on a self-generated permission pause that no automated signal surfaces (the lead only saw it by inspecting the container pane). CANDIDATE FIXES: (a) the bc launch/startup-prompt should assert non-interactive autonomy more forcibly / disable the irreversible-action confirmation in the brokered BC context; (b) treat a BC agent idling with a pending inbox + no work_done past a window as a stall the lead can detect (mechanism_observation/heartbeat). Cross-ref lead-o6tp (prose vs enforcement), lead-8aqh (false-offline made this hard to detect). Route: lead-architect / bc-launcher (startup-prompt) + shopsystem-templates (bc-primer).
notes:
SECOND occurrence same session: after completing lead-20bt the templates agent IDLED instead of draining the next already-queued inbox item (lead-62sy), despite an explicit 'drain to completion' directive. So the over-deferral has two faces: (1) permission-pause on irreversible actions, (2) stops after ONE dispatch rather than draining the full pending inbox. Both required lead-side inject to advance. Strengthens the case for a forcible non-interactive autonomy assertion + a lead-detectable 'idle-with-pending-inbox' stall signal.
2026-06-28 CONCRETE RECURRENCE on lead-kdsq (templates release dispatch). The templates BC agent PAUSED on an interactive permission prompt ('lead-kdsq asks me to cut & publish shop-templates v0.46.0 ... How should I proceed?' — option 1 Cut & publish / 2 bump-no-publish / 3 hold) instead of proceeding under the request_maintenance that already authorized the release. lead-kdsq sat IN_PROGRESS in the templates inbox with no work_done because of this. Router unblocked it via 'bc-container inject shopsystem-templates 1' (twice: select Cut & publish, then Submit). NOTABLE: the agent also set a SESSION CADENCE 'Auto-drain bugfixes, pause on release/publish' — so EVERY future release/publish dispatch to a BC will stall on this prompt until the router injects the go-ahead. The durable fix is this bead (bc-primer full-autonomy: a lead request_maintenance/release dispatch IS the authorization; do not pause for operator permission on the outward publish). Workaround until fixed: router watches for stalled release dispatches (in inbox, no work_done) and injects the go-ahead. Owning BC: bc-primer ships from shopsystem-templates.

title: Gap: bc-launcher self-pin in bc-base Dockerfile is NOT auto-bumped by poll-bc-base-deps (bc-launcher code fixes don't reach the image without a manual bump)
description: poll-bc-base-deps.yml (centralized check-bump-rebuild, ADR-022) bumps ONLY shop-templates/shop-msg/scenarios/beads pins. The bc-base Dockerfile ALSO pins shopsystem-bc-launcher itself (the 'bc-launcher self-pin', line 49 @v0.3.6) — and that pin is NOT in the poll's DEPS list. Consequence: a bc-launcher CODE change (e.g. lead-cw7m readiness fix) does NOT propagate into bc-base/bc-lead images on the daily poll; it requires a MANUAL Dockerfile self-pin bump + a v* tag (see release bead). This is a release-propagation gap of the same family as lead-c713 (things that 'should be automatic' aren't). FIX OPTIONS: add bc-launcher self to the poll's DEPS (resolve its own latest release vs the self-pin and bump), OR make the publish-bc-base tag-push path bump the self-pin to the tag automatically. Route: lead-architect / bc-launcher. Cross-ref lead-c713, ADR-021/022.

title: bc-container v0.3.1 pre-release validation findings (start-agent readiness false-timeout; bc-status false-offline; list silent-empty without docker)
description: Live validation 2026-06-23 by restarting shopsystem-scenarios to exercise bc-container v0.3.1 (lead host AND inside bc-lead:latest — same version). Environment: lead shell (vscode) lacks docker-group access (socket root:984) so bc-container needs sudo; the operational launch path is bc-container inside bc-lead (has docker CLI) per bin/shop-shell. PASSES: CLI surface complete (launch/attach/inject/monitor/stop/status/start-agent/list/manifest + launch flags --image/--network/--workspace-mount/--mount-docker-socket); 'manifest list' + 'manifest validate' work; 'launch' correctly detects an already-running container ('bc-shopsystem-scenarios is already running'); manifest resolves via --manifest default relative to CWD (needs --network when manifest has no product/network field). FINDINGS (release-relevant): (1) start-agent (lead-lp74 recovery subcommand) RAN the recovery path (no re-clone, tmux 'agent' session) BUT its readiness probe waits for marker 'Accessing workspace:' and TIMED OUT at 60s even though the container had a HEALTHY live agent (claude proc + shop-msg watch armed + idle prompt) -> 'startup prompt NOT injected'. So start-agent's readiness detection is unreliable (false not-ready), leaving the agent un-prompted. (2) shop-msg bc-status reports EVERY BC 'offline' even when agents are demonstrably live and processing work (scenarios agent up since Jun22, watch armed, actively reconciled lead-vusv) -> the fleet online-presence signal is unreliable/stale; an operator following the bring-up skill's DoD ('verify online via bc-status') would wrongly conclude bring-up failed. (3) 'bc-container list' WITHOUT docker access (non-sudo from lead shell) SILENTLY prints 'No BC containers found' instead of erroring on the inaccessible socket -> misleading false-empty. Recommend fixing readiness-marker detection in start-agent, the bc-status presence heartbeat, and the list no-docker error path before relying on bc-container for fleet recovery. Cross-ref lead-lp74 (start-agent), lead-3nf7 (bring-up skill).
notes: BOTH LEGS RECONCILED+COMPLETE: bc-launcher own-status/list/start-agent (lead-pixf, main 43fab9a) + messaging fleet-presence false-offline root-cause (lead-bppa, main e253bb9). All 3 bc-container UX defects + the session-long false-offline closed.

title: Reviewer-gate false-negative: stale non-editable scenarios wheel shadows src/, yields 6 spurious BDD failures on clean main (scenarios lacks messaging's c1f shadow-guard / editable-install guarantee)
description: mechanism_observation from shopsystem-scenarios during lead-vusv review (origin/main 6de77b4), 2026-06-23. On a clean checkout, bare 'python3 -m pytest tests/' = 6 FAILED/19 passed, ALL 'ModuleNotFoundError: No module named scenarios.outstanding' (and scenarios.journal) at tests/conftest.py:1083 — because bare 'import scenarios.*' resolves to a STALE NON-EDITABLE wheel /usr/local/lib/python3.11/site-packages/scenarios-0.2.0.dist-info predating journal.py/outstanding.py, while /workspace/src/scenarios/ has both. With PYTHONPATH=/workspace/src: 25 passed/0 failed. HAZARD: the BDD re-run is the reviewer's primary signal; a reviewer reading the bare-pytest 6-failure result as an implementation gap emits a SPURIOUS work_done(blocked) on landed, correct work — a reviewer-gate FALSE-NEGATIVE. (This lineage nearly hit it; the dispatch's 'expect 25/0' was correct, the environment masked it.) FIX (proposed): the canonical-managed pour/install must guarantee the BC's OWN package is installed EDITABLE (__editable__ .pth or 'pip install -e .'), never a frozen wheel that drifts behind src/ as new modules land. Note messaging ALREADY ships a collection-time shadow-guard against stale site-packages (work_id lead-ym8f, 'c1f'); scenarios appears to LACK the equivalent. So: (a) port the c1f shadow-guard to scenarios (and audit other BCs), and/or (b) make editable-install a canonical pour invariant. INTERIM: reviewers set PYTHONPATH=src (or reinstall -e) before trusting a non-green suite; treat 'ModuleNotFoundError: scenarios.*' as an environment-staleness artifact, not an implementation gap. Same gate-reliability cluster as lead-63mg. Route: lead-architect (canonical pour invariant + cross-BC shadow-guard parity); cross-ref lead-ym8f, lead-63mg.
notes: CLEARED via lead-hk2a (scenarios main d5e1290): editable-install shadow-guard landed (mirrors messaging's c1f). Reviewer-gate false-negative from stale non-editable wheel is fixed.
close_reason: Closed

title: SYSTEMIC work-done-gate deadlock: closing the plan bead (Check 4) dirties .beads/issues.jsonl, failing clean-tree (Check 1) — BC self-blocks and escalates an un-lead-performable 'router action'
description: Surfaced via lead-e36e (shopsystem-scenarios) repeated blocked work_done, 2026-06-23. The canonical work-done-gate (skills/work-done-gate/SKILL.md, shipped via shopsystem-templates, canonical-poured to all BCs) has two checks in tension: Check 4 requires the work_id's bd PLAN BEAD to be closed/reconciled before emit; Check 1 requires a CLEAN working tree at emit. Closing the plan bead writes .beads/issues.jsonl -> non-empty git status -> Check 1 FAILS. The scenarios BC closed shopsystem_scenarios-bb3 in its local .beads working set but its loop did not commit+push the closure before re-review, so it deadlocks: it cannot pass Check 1 (dirty) and cannot pass Check 4 without dirtying. It then emitted a blocked work_done asking the 'router' to commit+push .beads/issues.jsonl — but that is the BC's OWN internal registry inside its container; per ADR-018 the lead has NO BC checkout and cannot perform it. So a BC can escalate an un-lead-performable action and stall. IMPACT: any BC whose work closes a plan bead (the normal pattern) can hit this. SUBSTANTIVE WORK WAS CORRECT regardless (scenario f49eaa943ed4fb3b on scenarios main 91cfbf9). CANDIDATE FIXES (gate-design): (a) sequence the gate to commit the plan-bead closure as a gate step, THEN check clean tree; or (b) exempt .beads/issues.jsonl from the Check-1 clean-tree assertion; or (c) close+commit the plan bead atomically before the tree check. Same genre as lead-o6tp (move pre-emit checks from template prose to CLI enforcement) and lead-rwtz (pre-emit hygiene). ROUTE: a fix to the canonical work-done-gate SKILL via shopsystem-templates (then re-pour). Cross-ref lead-e36e, lead-o6tp.
notes: CLEARED via lead-20bt (templates main 6618f3c): work-done-gate prose deadlock dissolved (Check 1 carve-out parity). The close-plan-bead-vs-clean-tree churn (which hit scenarios this session) is fixed.
close_reason: Closed

title: assign_scenarios dispatch must verify scenario-file block hash via 'scenarios hash' pre-send; forbid # provenance comments inside the committed block
description: SYSTEMIC bug caught on lead-k6xq->lead-ignt/lead-n8pf/lead-ucpj (2026-06-23). The architect embedded ADR-022 retirement/supersession instructions as '#' comment lines INSIDE the assign_scenarios --scenario-file gherkin block. ADR-019 compute_scenario_hash drops ONLY @scenario_hash: lines + blanks; it does NOT drop '#' comments or @bc: lines -> the provenance comments were hashed as scenario body, so the pinned @scenario_hash (e.g. 4e3510142e9cd6fb) did not match the clean block hash (f49eaa943ed4fb3b). Both shopsystem-scenarios and shopsystem-messaging correctly clarified (recompute-equality would fail). DOCTRINE TO ENFORCE: (1) dispatch/provenance/retirement/supersession context belongs in assign_scenarios --description or message fields, NEVER inside the committed --scenario-file gherkin block; (2) the committed block is the pure tagged scenario (Scenario:+steps; @scenario_hash dropped by canonicalization; no '#' comments; @bc handling explicit since @bc IS hashed); (3) MANDATORY pre-send: pipe the exact shipped block through 'scenarios hash' (present on lead host /usr/local/bin/scenarios) and confirm it equals the pinned @scenario_hash. Same genre as lead-o6tp (move procedural pre-emit checks from template prose to CLI enforcement) — candidate: bake the hash-equality check into shop-msg send assign_scenarios as a pre-emit gate, and/or into the lead-architect template. Cross-ref ADR-018 D2 (scenarios hash is the admissible contract tool), ADR-019 (canonicalization ownership).

title: shop-msg dispatch sequencing footguns: --queue-on-dependency needs a prior bd-dep edge; no lead-side inbox-deposit retraction
description: Two messaging-contract gaps surfaced driving lead-fuh4 (2026-06-23). (1) FOOTGUN: 'shop-msg send <verb> --depends-on X --queue-on-dependency' only defers the postgres deposit if a 'bd dep add <dependent> X' EDGE already exists (strict/queued mode consults the bd edge per scenario 120); the --depends-on FLAG is metadata-only and does NOT create the gating edge. So a queued send issued before 'bd dep add' deposits a LIVE inbox row instead of deferring — silently violating the intended sequencing. Fix options: --depends-on should auto-create the bd edge before gating, OR --queue-on-dependency should hard-error when no edge exists rather than depositing live. (2) NO LEAD-SIDE RETRACTION: once a lead->BC dispatch is deposited, there is no way to retract/cancel it (consume/respond/--force operate BC-response->lead-inbox direction only; sweep is wrong-direction; registry remove is for shops). A premature or erroneous dispatch cannot be undone without hand-editing mailbox storage (forbidden). Need a 'shop-msg retract'/'cancel' lead-side capability for an un-consumed BC-inbox row. Route: request_bugfix/assign_scenarios -> shopsystem-messaging (owns shop-msg). Discovered via lead-fuh4 row 742.
notes: CLEARED via lead-9xrd (messaging main 33cbe22): shop-msg retract inbox primitive shipped. The lead can now retract a still-pending mis-vehicled/superseded dispatch before the BC consumes it — the footgun that forced superseding-dispatch races this session is closed.
close_reason: Closed

title: doctrine: gate artifact-derived release pre-states on publish-run completion (or stamp run-id+timestamp)
description: MECHANISM_OBSERVATION from shopsystem-bc-launcher (observed_during lead-cek6, consumed 2026-06-23). When a lead authors a request_maintenance whose pre-state is derived from a CI/registry artifact surface (ADR-018 D1 — no BC source on lead host), the artifact can LAG the just-dispatched release run. cek6's pre-state asserted 'v0.3.6 did NOT move :latest' and ':latest lacks the new launch flags'; in reality the :latest snapshot cek6 sampled (built 2026-06-22T17:03) was the PRIOR v0.3.5 build (run 27969925553) while the v0.3.6 publish run (27991566447) was still in flight. v0.3.6 completed SUCCESS at 23:41Z and DID move :latest. Result: a request_maintenance whose entire premise was already false on arrival, costing a verify-and-no-op BC round trip. PROPOSED (BC): for artifact-surface-derived pre-states tied to a release, gate the pre-state read on the relevant publish run reaching 'completed' (gh run watch / poll conclusion) BEFORE authoring the maintenance dispatch, OR annotate the pre-state with run-id + observation-timestamp so the BC can detect the lag immediately rather than re-deriving it. Disposition: lead-architect pre-state discipline (NOT an ADR/contract change — this is lead-side authoring hygiene). Adopt as a checklist item when authoring release-coupled request_maintenance. Evidence: .github/workflows/publish-bc-base.yml:46,107 (bc-launcher). Independently confirmed by lead: docker manifest inspect ghcr.io/dstengle/shopsystem-bc-base:{latest,v0.3.6} => byte-identical manifests (sha fbadab68...) => :latest IS v0.3.6 now.

title: [mechanism] read outbox masks coexisting rows: outbox keyed by (work_id, message_type), read outbox shows only one row -> false-overwrite premise + risky --force restore
description:
MECHANISM FINDING from shopsystem-bc-launcher (mechanism_observation, provenance shopsystem_bc_launcher-jje, observed_during lead-k4k7, 2026-06-19). Consumed by router into this bead; no re-dispatch (observation, not a contract change against an open work_id).

EMPIRICAL CLI BEHAVIOR (BC-reported, against shop-msg storage; NOT BC code):
- The outbox is keyed by (work_id, message_type), NOT a single slot per work_id. shop-msg dump --bc shopsystem-bc-launcher --direction outbox showed TWO coexisting rows under work_id lead-k4k7: (1) work_done status=complete created_at 2026-06-19 20:43:12 (original, intact); (2) a later mechanism_observation.
- shop-msg read outbox --work-id <id> takes ONLY --work-id (no message-type selector; confirmed: read outbox --help has no --message-type) and surfaces exactly ONE row (apparently most-recent-by-created_at). So when a mechanism_observation coexists with a work_done under the same work_id, read outbox shows only the observation and the work_done LOOKS gone.
- The write guard keys correctly on (work_id, message_type): shop-msg respond work_done ... lead-k4k7 exited 1 with 'refusing to overwrite existing work_done response for work_id=lead-k4k7 (use --force to replace)' — confirming the original work_done was still present.

WHY LOAD-BEARING:
1. A router reading outbox state via read outbox can wrongly conclude a response was overwritten and dispatch an unnecessary 'restore' repair (this exactly happened this session — the lead-k4k7 repair-dispatch premise that the observation had clobbered the work_done was FALSE).
2. Anyone acting on that false premise might --force a re-emit, REPLACING a genuine intact work_done with a reconstructed copy — net data degradation for zero benefit.

CORRECT PRACTICE (interim, doctrine): when read outbox 'hides' a work_done, inspect via shop-msg dump BEFORE forcing; do NOT --force when the original is intact; verify overwrite premises with dump before issuing any 'restore' repair.

PROPOSED FIX (BC's proposed_action): (a) make read outbox able to list/select by message_type so callers see ALL rows under a work_id; OR (b) document that outbox is keyed by (work_id, message_type) and that read outbox shows one row, directing premise-checks to dump before any --force restore.

DISPATCHED 2026-06-23 to shopsystem-messaging on work_id lead-0lml (request_bugfix, additive/observability). RELATES: lead-q616, lead-qbbk, lead-o6tp.
close_reason: RECONCILED 2026-06-23. work_done (merge 15ac790, RED 514707e -> GREEN 3ecb741) demonstrated all 3 criteria: (1) read outbox with no --message-type now surfaces EVERY coexisting (work_id,message_type) row in created_at order; optional --message-type narrows to one (non-zero + lists present types on no-match); (2) same-(work_id,message_type) write-guard refusal UNCHANGED, pinned by regression test; (3) storage key unchanged, single-row work_ids render identically, existing readers unaffected; 410 passed. Provenance work_done consumed (rc=0, pending outbox empty). Accepted against contract/artifact surface per ADR-018 (BC demonstration; no BC source on host). NOTE: installed host shop-msg is STALE — read outbox --help still lacks --message-type, so fix is on origin/main but not yet poured to this host.

title: Architect supersede-dispatch: enumerate BC-LOCAL unpinned scenarios too (not only lead-pinned hashes); + option-(b) dropped-member skill auto-cleanup gap
description: From lead-1e8d mechanism_observation (2026-06-19). (1) DOCTRINE: when composing a supersede/request_bugfix that changes a contract, the conflicting-@scenario_hash enumeration covers only LEAD-HELD PINNED scenarios — but the target BC may carry a LOCAL UNPINNED feature scenario encoding the same (old) contract that the change regresses. On lead-1e8d the BC found+amended features/cli_repours_skills_on_update.feature ('update removes a managed skill file...') which option-(b) contradicted. FIX: architect supersede-dispatch discipline should account for BC-local unpinned scenarios (or explicitly delegate their reconciliation to the BC). Fold into findings/scenario-supersession-and-dispatch-discipline.md + the lead-architect role template (request_maintenance -> shopsystem-templates). (2) SIDE-EFFECT of lead-1e8d option-(b) (never prune a non-member): orphaned once-managed skill dirs (a member the package LATER drops) are no longer auto-pruned by 'shop-templates update'; auto-cleanup of dropped members would need a current-vs-historical-membership discriminator the carried scenario does not provide. Low-pri unless dropped-member cruft becomes a problem.
notes:
THIRD EMPIRICAL INSTANCE → FIFTH overall blind-spot occurrence (lead-5k8c mechanism_observation, 2026-06-22, provenance shopsystem_bc_launcher-t6k). The lead-5k8c request_bugfix dispatch attributed BC-side @scenario_hash:251984e3ac55e8f9 to bc-launcher's register as the k4k7 warn-and-continue pin. That hash does NOT resolve in the BC register — lead-sltr ALREADY re-pinned it to db11ca7b46dd12a4 ("a failed skill-refresh warns and proceeds to agent-start...") earlier THIS session (hash-integrity recompute). PHANTOM/STALE hash: architect cited 251984e3 from stale session memory; lead cannot see BC register (ADR-018) AND BC-side hashes go stale on re-pin. The other two (f4ebaa3f/1f1d178b) are lead-held + correctly labeled.

SHARPENED RULE (BC proposed_action ADOPTED): a dispatch's conflicting-coverage enumeration MUST NEVER cite a specific BC-side @scenario_hash BY VALUE. (a) lead structurally cannot verify it resolves (ADR-018); (b) BC-side hashes are MUTABLE — any re-pin silently invalidates a remembered value. THEREFORE: enumerate LEAD-HELD hashes by value (lead can grep+verify); refer to BC-SIDE coverage by CLASS-NAME/DESCRIPTION only, never by hash value. This is the actionable convergence of the §2 blind spot (lead cannot see BC pins) with its mutability corollary (even a correctly-remembered BC value goes stale on re-pin). Fix is a FLAT PROHIBITION, not "reconcile harder": keep BC-side hash VALUES out of dispatch prose entirely.

FOLDED into findings/scenario-supersession-and-dispatch-discipline.md §6. Still to fold into lead-architect role-template enumeration step (request_maintenance → shopsystem-templates).

title: Re-run adopter cold-walkthrough E2E on a skew-free Docker host (this host's ZFS bind-mount write-visibility skew blocks on-host auth-gate observation)
description: From lead-e8hk final re-validation (2026-06-19): the adopter bootstrap is GREEN(product) — both defects fixed+delivered, footing render proven on a skew-free (no-bind-mount) path. But the FULL chained walkthrough (bin/bootstrap renders bin/footing into a bind-mounted dir via docker run, then host-side guard checks/invokes it) cannot complete ON THIS HOST: proven ZFS dataset-relative bind-mount skew — files WRITTEN inside a container into a bind-mounted host dir are invisible/stale on the host side (minimal repro: container wrote sentinel.txt+bin/footing, container's own view showed them, host view EMPTY). So the host-side '[[ ! -f bin/footing ]]' guard reads the rendered footing as missing. ACTION: re-run the E2E cold-walkthrough to the human auth-gate boundary on a normal (non-ZFS-skewed) Docker host, OR change bin/bootstrap's render transport to avoid host-side bind-mount visibility (e.g. render+invoke within a single container, or docker cp instead of bind-mount). Relates host↔container split issues (lead-mf15 /workspace root-owned, lead-b14a CA truncation). Non-blocking: product is validated correct.

title: Reconcile this lead instance's drifted ops files (compose.yaml, bin/shop-shell, bin/agent-vault-provision, bin/agent-vault-check) against canonical v0.12.0
description: 'shop-templates update' (2026-06-15) advised these 4 shop-owned ops files have DRIFTED from the current canonical template body and did NOT modify them (they're shop-owned). Consequence surfaced in the INSTALL review: a fresh-eyes reviewer reading the LOCAL bin/agent-vault-provision saw the OLD dashboard-based Claude-OAuth flow, not the canonical v0.10+ PROPOSAL-based flow — i.e. this instance's provision script is stale. Reconcile each against 'shop-templates show <name>' and adopt the canonical body (proposal-based provision, vault-scope fix, current compose/shop-shell). Per doctrine these are POURED instance copies; adopt canonical rather than hand-editing genericity. Lower priority for adopters (who bootstrap fresh from the image = canonical) but matters for THIS shopsystem-product lead instance's own correctness. Relates the v0.10/v0.11/v0.12 provision releases.
notes:
INVESTIGATED 2026-06-19 (lead-architect) — LEAVING OPEN, flagged needs-user/needs-router-decision. Drift CONFIRMED authoritatively: 'shop-templates update --target . --shop-type lead' (v0.14.0) emits drift advisories for all 4 ops files (compose.yaml, bin/shop-shell, bin/agent-vault-provision, bin/agent-vault-check) and, by design, does NOT auto-modify shop-owned files. The stale-provision concern is REAL: local bin/agent-vault-provision uses the OLD DASHBOARD-step Claude-OAuth flow ('do NOT use static token'), while canonical v0.14.0 uses the proposal-based scriptable OAuth-slot flow (the lead-yrex correction). 574 diff lines.

WHY NOT A CLEAN RE-POUR (the risk): 'shop-templates show <name>' emits RAW templates with {{OPS_SLUG}} placeholders (not pourable directly). The slug-substituted render comes from 'shop-templates bootstrap --shop-name shopsystem-product', which I ran into a scratch dir to inspect. CRITICAL FINDING: the canonical render derives OPS_SLUG = 'shopsystem' and produces container/network names 'shopsystem-postgres' / 'shopsystem-agent-vault' with deterministic slug-derived ports. But THIS live instance's compose.yaml/shop-shell use a DIFFERENT, hand-customized scheme: container_name 'shopsystem-messaging-postgres-1' and 'shopsystem-agent-vault-1' with hardcoded ports 5432:5432 — and those names MATCH the actually-running containers (docker ps: shopsystem-messaging-postgres-1, shopsystem-agent-vault-1). A blind canonical adopt would point shop-shell at 'shopsystem-postgres' (not running) and try to start a second postgres on a colliding host port — breaking bring-up of the LIVE fleet.

So lead-94mn is NOT a clean instance-local edit: it requires a deliberate reconciliation decision — either (1) adopt canonical naming AND migrate the running containers + pgdata to the new names, or (2) cherry-pick ONLY the canonical content deltas (proposal-based provision flow, vault-scope fix) onto the local container-naming scheme. Both touch live infrastructure / data root. This is a router+user judgment call (running-fleet migration), not an unattended re-pour. FLAGGED — leaving OPEN.
close_reason: RESOLVED this session: the 4 drifted ops files were re-poured to current canonical (>=0.17.0) during the shop-shell/compose convergence work. Verified bin/agent-vault-provision is the proposal-based flow (40 'proposal' refs, 0 'dashboard') — the specific stale-dashboard-OAuth concern is gone. compose.yaml re-poured (0.16.0 /data fix + slug-scoped names), shop-shell re-poured (and further converging via lead-4c3z to the thin wrapper). The 'differ vs PKG' is {{OPS_SLUG}} template-vs-rendered, not drift. v0.12.0 baseline is obsolete.

title: Validate AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM path-vs-inline form against BC launch (lead-8jar residual) — iter-7 live proof
description: lead-8jar mechanism_observation residual. lead-8jar's .env writeback (lead-71tq fold-in) chose PATH form: provision runs 'agent-vault ca fetch > agent-vault-ca.pem' and writes AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM=agent-vault-ca.pem (a file path), because multi-line PEM is unsafe inline in .env. shop-shell forwards AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM to the BC container as an ENV VAR ('-e AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM' on docker run). OPEN QUESTION: does the BC/launcher consume AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM as a file PATH or as inline PEM CONTENT? If a path, the file (written on the lead/scratch host) must be reachable INSIDE the container (mounted) — a host path passed as an env var won't resolve in-container otherwise. This is BC-launch behavior, not verifiable from the lead artifact surface (ADR-018); the dummyco spike iter-7 live BC launch is the empirical proof. If iter-7's BC launch fails on CA verification, switch the writeback to inline-with-escaping OR ensure the CA file is mounted into the container. Owner: shopsystem-templates (provision ops) if a fix is needed; validated by lead-jdfb iter-7. Relates lead-8jar, lead-jdfb.
notes: ITER-7 VALIDATED: AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM carried INLINE (PEM text, not path) into bc-dummyco-greeter; brokered TLS through dummyco-agent-vault:14322 verified (curl to api.anthropic.com returned HTTP 404 model-not-found, NOT a TLS/cert error -> CA trust resolved). Inline form does NOT wall BC launch. Residual resolved to inline. Closeable.
close_reason: VALIDATED in iter-7: AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM inline PEM form works; brokered TLS verified end-to-end; does NOT wall BC launch. No template change needed.

title: WS-2: dummyco .env carries AGENT_VAULT_ADDR/TOKEN but not AGENT_VAULT_VAULT or AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM -> launch needs both sourced out-of-band (PDR-018 doc gap, iter-6)
description: Empirically surfaced by dummyco spike iter-6 (lead-jdfb). The provision-written .env has AGENT_VAULT_ADDR + AGENT_VAULT_TOKEN (the minted fleet token) but NOT AGENT_VAULT_VAULT (the launch vault selector) nor AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM (the broker CA the BC needs to trust the MITM proxy). For the BC launch both had to be supplied out-of-band: AGENT_VAULT_VAULT=dummyco by hand, and AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM via 'agent-vault ca fetch' from the broker container. The documented bring-up path / provision should write AGENT_VAULT_VAULT and AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM into .env (or bring-up-bc should fetch the CA via 'agent-vault ca fetch' as a documented step) so a plain launch from .env has the full triple+CA. Owner: shop-templates (provision .env writeback) + bring-up-bc skill doc. Worked around in-spike.
notes: FOLDED INTO lead-8jar dispatch (request_bugfix -> shopsystem-templates, 2026-06-15): provision .env writeback must include AGENT_VAULT_VAULT + AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM. Reconciles+closes with lead-8jar. Rides the templates release.
close_reason: RECONCILED via lead-8jar (folded, origin/main 4797c35): provision .env writeback now adds AGENT_VAULT_VAULT=$VAULT + AGENT_VAULT_CA_PEM (path form, ca fetched via 'agent-vault ca fetch' to agent-vault-ca.pem since multi-line PEM is unsafe inline); .env.example ships matching placeholders. Rides templates v0.9.0.

title: WS-2: agent-vault fleet-agent vault grant 'dummyco:proxy' vs the plain vault name needed for HTTPS_PROXY userinfo -> brokered clone 404 until corrected to AGENT_VAULT_VAULT=dummyco (PDR-018 doc gap, iter-6)
description: Empirically surfaced by dummyco spike iter-6 (lead-jdfb). The dummyco-fleet agent is granted vault 'dummyco:proxy' (per agent list). When AGENT_VAULT_VAULT was set to that literal 'dummyco:proxy', the launcher derived HTTPS_PROXY userinfo '<token>:dummyco:proxy@host:14322' and the brokered git clone failed 'CONNECT tunnel failed, response 404'. Setting AGENT_VAULT_VAULT=dummyco (the PLAIN vault name, matching the live fleet which uses AGENT_VAULT_VAULT=fleet not fleet:proxy) made the clone succeed. The ':proxy' suffix is the agent's grant/role notation, NOT the proxy userinfo vault selector. Owner: docs/bring-up — Brief 011 / bring-up-bc skill must state that the launch's AGENT_VAULT_VAULT is the plain product vault name (e.g. 'dummyco'), and provision/launcher should not surface the ':proxy' grant form as the launch vault value. Low-priority doc/UX gap; worked around in-spike.
