title: Simplify the human credential gate: add the Claude-OAuth credential via agent-vault's PROPOSALS mechanism instead of a manual dashboard paste
description:
Requested by dave (2026-06-15) at the iter-5 human gate. Today the one human gate (ADR-026 D4 / PDR-017) is a manual agent-vault DASHBOARD paste: the operator opens the broker dashboard on the host port, hand-adds a credential, and presses ENTER at the provision prompt. agent-vault exposes a PROPOSALS mechanism; routing the Claude-OAuth credential addition through a pre-populated OAuth-typed credential-slot proposal lets bin/agent-vault-provision CREATE the proposal programmatically, leaving the human only to APPROVE it (token paste or Connect) at the printed approve URL — a simpler, scriptable gate that PRESERVES refresh (oauth credential type) and ADR-026 D4 (human still supplies the secret).

DISPATCHED as request_bugfix -> shopsystem-templates (work_id lead-yrex) 2026-06-15 after empirical re-verification on a throwaway 0.32.0 broker that the structured oauth credential slot (create via `proposal create -f -`, approve via `proposal approve <n> KEY=VALUE`) stores a refresh-capable type:oauth credential, NOT static — correcting the prior INCOMPLETE "proposals can't carry OAuth type" finding. See NOTES for full verified mechanics. Relates ADR-026 D2/D4, PDR-017, lead-jdfb.
notes:
DISPATCHED 2026-06-15 (lead-architect, request_bugfix -> shopsystem-templates, work_id=lead-yrex; dispatch_state=dispatched; deposited+read-back OK).

CORRECTION CONFIRMED EMPIRICALLY: the prior probe's "proposals can't carry the OAuth type" was INCOMPLETE (it only tested the flat `proposal create --credential KEY=desc` static path). RE-VERIFIED on a throwaway infisical/agent-vault:latest broker (image id 075977dae7dc = SAME as the live fleet broker, agent-vault 0.32.0; live shopsystem-agent-vault-1 UNTOUCHED, throwaway torn down):
- CREATE: structured OAuth credential SLOT works via `agent-vault vault proposal create -f -` (stdin JSON), credential = {action:set, key, type:oauth, oauth:{token_url(req), client_id, scopes}, description, obtain_instructions}. Server validates: missing oauth block -> "oauth config required"; missing token_url -> "oauth.token_url is required"; value set -> "value must not be set for oauth credentials (tokens obtained via the connect flow)". authorization_url omitted -> token-paste approval variant.
- APPROVE: `proposal approve <n> CLAUDE_OAUTH=<accessToken> --yes` -> "approved and applied". Human supplies secret at approve time (ADR-026 D4 preserved).
- LOAD-BEARING PROOF: post-approve DB shows credentials row type=oauth + companion oauth-config row persisting token_url=https://console.anthropic.com/v1/oauth/token, auth method client_secret_post. token_url persisted with the credential = refresh machinery; static creds carry none. => REFRESH PRESERVED, not static. The prior probe was wrong.
- ANTHROPIC CONFIG: token_url=https://console.anthropic.com/v1/oauth/token (required), client_id=9d1c250a-e61b-44d9-88ed-5944d1962f5e, scopes="" (none observed). Same values the live dashboard form uses; parameterized in the dispatch for future edits.

DISCRIMINATOR: bin/agent-vault-provision exists (rendered templates ops artifact); its Claude-OAuth gate behavior is UNPINNED by any @bc:shopsystem-templates scenario (only lead-owned @lead_integration broker scenarios 10/11/12 touch provisioning, and the approve-the-proposal flow still satisfies their "one human-gated step supplies the real credential" assertion -> NOT retired). Tightening existing-but-unpinned behavior -> request_bugfix. No new BC scenarios.

STAYS in_progress/dispatched. Do NOT close. Awaiting templates work_done -> reconcile.
close_reason: RECONCILED complete (origin/main d47c110). bin/agent-vault-provision Claude-OAuth gate rewritten: manual dashboard hand-create -> OAuth-typed CLAUDE_OAUTH credential-slot PROPOSAL via 'agent-vault vault proposal create -f -' (stdin JSON: action:set, type:oauth, oauth.token_url required + client_id + scopes, value unset, consent-URL omitted -> token-paste approval). Operator role drops to APPROVING the printed proposal ('vault proposal approve <num> CLAUDE_OAUTH=<value> --yes'); proposal num captured from create --json. REFRESH PRESERVED (type:oauth + token_url, NOT flat static; no 'credential set CLAUDE_OAUTH' fallback). ADR-026 D4 preserved (human supplies secret at approve). token_url/client_id/scopes parameterized w/ verified 0.32.0 defaults. All other steps unchanged, slug-clean. Corrects the prior lead-5jbc 'OAuth not scriptable' note. red->green, zero newly-failing. E2E (real create+approve against a live broker) = the cold-walkthrough's validation. Rides templates v0.10.0.

title: shop-templates: add GENERIC clean-install importability currency guard for every console-script (catch undeclared-dep / non-lazy-import at build, not launched-BC runtime)
description: From lead-ld7i mechanism_observation. tmpl-20n (bc-emit dead-on-arrival) escaped to v0.6.0 despite existing packaging-currency guards because they assert only (a) the [project.scripts] declaration is present and (b) the module rides in sdist/wheel — NEVER that the installed console-script actually IMPORTS on a clean install. A module-top-level import of an undeclared dep passes both yet fails at first invocation. Generic gap: ANY future package-delivered console-script whose module top-level imports an undeclared transitive dep escapes the same way. lead-ld7i closed it for bc-emit specifically (test_bc_emit_scenarios_dependency.py) but the currency-guard PATTERN stays import-blind. FIX (proposed): a generic guard that, for every [project.scripts] entry, installs the built wheel into a throwaway venv (or masks declared deps' top-level packages) and asserts the entry-point import / --help exits 0 — catching undeclared-dep/non-lazy-import regressions at build time. Vehicle: request_bugfix/maintenance -> shopsystem-templates. Relates lead-ld7i, lead-bglz.
notes: 2026-06-19 (lead-architect): DISPATCHED request_bugfix -> shopsystem-templates (work_id lead-q1wy). Discriminator: packaging-currency tooling EXISTS (sdist/wheel + [project.scripts]-declaration guards) but is IMPORT-BLIND and UNPINNED for clean-install importability -> request_bugfix (not assign_scenarios: currency tooling exists; not maintenance: this tightens a correctness guard, behavioral). No scenarios embedded (BC generalizes the currency guard + pins its own). @scenario_hash enumeration over features/: no scenario pins console-script import currency (only wrapper pins 176-181 exist, unrelated) — ADDITIVE, nothing retired. Pre-state cited from lead-held features/ surface (ADR-018 D1; no repos/ read). Deposit verified via shop-msg read inbox. NOTE: shopsystem-templates heartbeat OFFLINE at dispatch.
close_reason: work_done reconciled 2026-06-19: generic clean-install import-currency guard shipped — enumerates every [project.scripts] entry, asserts import + --help exit 0 under deps masked to declared-only; catches tmpl-20n undeclared/transitive-import class at build time for all current+future scripts. RED 583699e->GREEN 1868c59, merged 96fd637; +5 guard tests, zero new failures (21 tmpl-w1w baseline unchanged).

title: Sharpen ADR-026 D2 prose with the EXACT agent-vault 0.32.0 verb nesting (credential/service are vault sub-verbs; agent create is top-level; owner = auth register) — after iter-4 validates
description: From lead-beym mechanism_observation (provision rewrite). ADR-026 D2 names the credential flow generically ('credential set' / 'service add' / 'agent create'), which is plausibly WHY bin/agent-vault-provision was originally authored against a FICTIONAL CLI (the lead-beym 'put'-verb defect). The REAL agent-vault 0.32.0 nesting (verified via installed --help, to be e2e-confirmed in iter-4): (1) owner bootstrap = 'agent-vault auth register --email --password-stdin' (first registrant = instance owner; 'auth login' on re-run) — NOT an 'owner ...' sub-verb; (2) 'agent-vault vault create <vault>'; (3) credential = 'agent-vault vault credential set <key=value> ... --vault <vault>' (sub-verb UNDER vault); (4) service = 'agent-vault vault service add --name --host --auth-type basic --username-key --password-key --vault <vault>' (sub-verb UNDER vault); (5) fleet token = 'agent-vault agent create <slug>-fleet --token-only --vault <vault>:proxy' (TOP-LEVEL; the 'vault agent' group only ADDS an existing agent, does not mint). Fold this exact nesting into ADR-026 D2 prose so future BC dispatches author against the real verb structure. Author: lead-architect (ADRs are lead-owned documents). GATE on iter-4 (lead-jdfb) e2e validation so the ADR records the VALIDATED nesting, not just the --help-derived one. Relates lead-beym, lead-jdfb.
notes:

title: FINDING: shop-msg send dispatch-guard over-reads bd provenance edges (discovered-from) as blocking predecessors — false-blocks dispatch twice this session
description:
Recurring friction surfaced 2026-06-12 during lead-clpx-era dispatches. shop-msg send refuses to deposit a dispatch until ALL bd-graph predecessor edges on the work_id bead are closed. Twice this bit a dispatch that had no real predecessor: (1) lead-m56e carried relates/context edges the architect had to unlink; (2) lead-llc1 carried a 'depends-on lead-l7uz' edge that was actually PROVENANCE (discovered-from lead-l7uz during the dummyco spike) mis-encoded as a blocking dependency — lead-l7uz (WS-2 authoring) is NOT a precondition for fixing a P0 template-render defect; if anything lead-llc1 unblocks lead-l7uz. Router had to drop the edge by hand to deposit.

Two candidate root causes: (a) 'discovered-from' provenance should NOT create a depends-on/blocking bd edge (it's lineage, not sequencing); (b) the dispatch-guard treats every depends-on edge as a must-be-closed predecessor with no notion of provenance-vs-sequencing. Either the bead-filing convention for discovered-from must avoid the blocking edge (keep provenance in the description / a non-blocking link), or the guard must distinguish provenance edges. Provenance was preserved in lead-llc1's description; the edge was the only problem. Relates lead-llc1, lead-m56e, lead-jdfb (spike that files discovered-from beads).
notes: RELATED/likely-DUPLICATE of lead-z15d ([bug] shop-msg send dependency gate compares dispatch_state not bd status — bd authoring deps wrongly block dispatch), which diagnoses the same root cause precisely. Consolidate resolution on lead-z15d; keep lead-wvbf as the 2026-06-12 occurrence record (bit lead-m56e + lead-llc1).

title: architect pre-state discipline: verify 'constant X consumed by function Y' against Y's BODY, not just X's presence (lead-rfc5 caught a dead-constant mis-statement)
description: From lead-rfc5 mechanism_observation (2026-06-12): the dispatch PRE-STATE asserted BC_NAME_RE (manifest.py:34) was 'consumed by validate() at :170' and thus gated the CLI; in fact validate() never referenced it — the constant was DEAD CODE, only asserted in tests/conftest.py. So the change was net-new behavior dressed as a tightening. Outcome was fine (BC wired the check into validate() with revert-teeth binding the wired call site), but a less-careful implementer could have parameterized the dead constant and shipped a no-op. DISCIPLINE: when an architect pre-state claims 'constant consumed by function F', confirm by reading F's body before classifying the surface as pinned-existing vs net-new; ensure revert-teeth bind the actual call site. Pairs with lead-n64d (recompute pre-state from real source, not local/assumed).

title: FINDING (mechanism_observation id=277, lead-8z3f): stale global 'scenarios' CLI on PATH + dirty canonical-managed .claude files both trip the BC work-done-gate
description:
Raised by shopsystem-scenarios reviewer during lead-8z3f review (BC artifact, ADR-018 — lead host can only relay). TWO recurring, load-bearing risks (neither blocked lead-8z3f): (A) STALE GLOBAL CLI: subprocess-based BDD steps resolve 'scenarios' from PATH (/usr/local/bin/scenarios), a stale install lacking the 'journal' subcommand the repo src/scenarios/cli.py already ships -> 3 spurious red failures (journal_query x2, journal_rebuild) on EVERY review of this BC; risks masking a real regression. Fix surface: pin test env to repo-current entrypoint (editable install or 'python -m scenarios') instead of whatever 'scenarios' is on PATH. (B) DIRTY CANONICAL-MANAGED FILES: .claude/canonical/bc-primer.md, .claude/skills/bc-router/SKILL.md, .claude/skills/writing-plans-bdd/SKILL.md show locally-modified though canonical-managed (poured by shop-templates update, 'do not edit in place') -> trip the work-done-gate clean-tree check for any future work_id, forcing every reviewer to hand-separate work artifacts from drifted-pour noise. Fix: re-pour or commit the pour so clean-tree gate stays meaningful. ROUTING: both are shopsystem-scenarios BC-local concerns; (A) is a BC test-harness fix (likely request_maintenance: test env plumbing), (B) is a re-pour/commit hygiene action. Source: mechanism_observation id=277 on lead-8z3f outbox.

DISPATCH NOTE 2026-06-19 (lead-architect): SENT request_maintenance -> shopsystem-scenarios, work-id lead-cpjs. Vehicle = request_maintenance per discriminator: both (A) and (B) are FLAT BC-local fixes (test-env entrypoint pinning; re-pour/commit drifted canonical files) — no new behavior, no new scenario. Pre-state verified on contract/artifact surface (no BC clone): work-done-gate clean-tree behavior is ALREADY scenario-pinned at lead features/templates/176 @scenario_hash:242c4de927d64339, with carve-out set {.specstory, .claude/scheduled_tasks.lock, .beads/issues.jsonl}. Canonical-managed .claude files are intentionally NOT carve-outs; the fix is to clean the tree, not widen the gate. Dispatch explicitly SCOPES OUT any gate-behavior change (that would require lead-po authoring + a behavioral vehicle). Acceptance criteria + file hints attached. Left in_progress pending BC work_done.

title: template-release discipline: lead's installed shop-templates package (v0.2.0) lags templates BC origin/main — BC template changes don't reach lead/new products until a release is cut+reinstalled
description: Surfaced during lead-f3gm reconciliation 2026-06-12. The BC commits canonical-template changes to the templates repo origin/main (e.g. the WS-4 Part 1 PRIME DIRECTIVE pour, integration 9beb031), but the lead host's pip-installed shop-templates is v0.2.0 (cut 2026-06-04) and does NOT carry them. Also: 'shop-templates show' serves only role templates (bc-implementer/bc-reviewer/lead-architect/lead-po), NOT the CLAUDE.md primers, and there is no read_claude_md_primer export — so the lead cannot inspect canonical primer changes via the installed package at all. Consequence: a freshly-instantiated product won't inherit BC-committed template IP until a templates release is cut AND the lead reinstalls. Needs: (1) a republish/release cadence (or version bump) after canonical-template request_bugfix lands; (2) expose the CLAUDE.md primers via 'shop-templates show' so they're inspectable. Relates WS-4 (lead-hyxx), lead-n64d, lead-f3gm.
notes: RESOLVED (2026-06-12) by ADR-039 + executed: version-bump-is-part-of-the-fix doctrine + all 3 packages released (messaging v0.3.0, bc-launcher v0.3.0, templates v0.4.0) + lead reinstalled via plain pip -U (no --force). Delivery-currency gap closed end-to-end; cadence PROVEN. Fleet re-pour tracked on lead-n4r2.

title: Architect doctrine: recompute canonical-template pre-state from package data, not lead's local primer copy, when composing request_bugfix
description:
DOCTRINE FIX surfaced by lead-f3gm mechanism_observation (id 266, 'DELTA EVIDENCE stale vs canonical lead.md'). The lead-f3gm request_bugfix DELTA EVIDENCE described a pre-state that did NOT match canonical templates/claude/lead.md on origin/main at dispatch (base 2872e61): it claimed the PRIME DIRECTIVE block was ENTIRELY ABSENT (in fact it existed but was MISPOSITIONED after the SHOP_NAME heading), and claimed the choice-suppression body was the older prohibition-framed version (in fact commit 2730ea0 had already partially reframed it to an intermediate Carve-out + 2-trigger body). The acceptance pins were genuinely unmet so the BC reached them IDEMPOTENTLY by inspecting the actual file first — the fix was real, not a no-op — but the stale DELTA EVIDENCE described a partly-satisfied pre-state.

ROOT CAUSE: the architect computed pre-state delta against the lead shop's LOCAL .claude/canonical/lead-primer.md copy (and an assumed 'shop-templates show lead' output), which had DRIFTED AHEAD of what canonical package data actually carried. Compounding: the installed shop-templates is v0.2.0 (cut 2026-06-04), whose claude/lead.md has NO PRIME DIRECTIVE at all — so even the installed package data lags origin/main.

DOCTRINE: When composing a request_bugfix that revises a CANONICAL template (claude/<shop>.md, lead-architect.md, lead-po.md, bc-*.md), the architect MUST recompute pre-state from actual package data at compose time — i.e. read the canonical template the BC will edit (e.g. installed claude/lead.md package file or the BC's reported origin/main state), NOT the lead host's local .claude/canonical/lead-primer.md copy, which is a derived/poured artifact that can outrun or lag canonical. The local primer is the POUR SOURCE (intended end-state), not the canonical PRE-STATE. Cite the package-data pre-state in DELTA EVIDENCE, distinct from the pour-source intent.

CAVEAT discovered during reconciliation: installed shop-templates package data itself may be stale relative to BC origin/main (v0.2.0 vs the 9beb031 the BC just integrated). Per ADR-018 the lead cannot git-observe the BC tree. So 'package data at compose time' = installed package data + the BC's last reported origin/main commit, reconciled — and the DELTA EVIDENCE should note when these diverge.

Related by TEXT (no bd edge, keep dispatchable): lead-hyxx (WS-4 prep), lead-f3gm (WS-4 part 1, the dispatch this observation came from). Load-bearing for WS-4 part 2+ (lead-po.md / lead-architect.md / bc-*.md canonical pours).
notes:
2026-06-19 (lead-architect): discipline recorded in findings/architect-prestate-verification-discipline.md §2. DECISION: when revising a CANONICAL template, recompute pre-state from PACKAGE DATA at compose time (shop-templates show / installed claude/<shop>.md reconciled with BC's last reported origin/main), NOT the lead's local poured .claude/canonical/lead-primer.md (that is the pour SOURCE/intent, not the canonical pre-state); cite package-data pre-state in DELTA EVIDENCE distinct from pour-source intent; note when installed package data and BC origin/main diverge. FLAG: needs request_maintenance -> shopsystem-templates (role-template prose). LEFT OPEN for router to batch the dispatch.
2026-06-19 (lead-architect): DISPATCHED as PART (b) of a COMBINED request_maintenance -> shopsystem-templates sent under work_id lead-4r39 (NOT a separate send; lead-n64d carried as part b of the one message, referenced by id in scope). The dispatch carries this doctrine verbatim: recompute canonical-template pre-state from PACKAGE DATA ('shop-templates show <role>' / installed claude/<shop>.md reconciled with BC's last reported origin/main), NOT the lead's local poured .claude/canonical/lead-primer.md (pour SOURCE/intent); cite package-data pre-state in DELTA EVIDENCE distinct from pour-source intent; note when installed package data and BC origin/main diverge. Grounding lead-f3gm. Recorded in findings/architect-prestate-verification-discipline.md §2. Vehicle request_maintenance (flat additive prose to lead-architect pre-state section). Pre-state verified against 'shop-templates show lead-architect' v0.14.0 (block present, discipline absent — additive). Deposit verified via shop-msg read inbox (work_id lead-4r39). NOTE: shopsystem-templates heartbeat OFFLINE at dispatch.
close_reason: work_done reconciled 2026-06-19 (combined dispatch): both architect pre-state disciplines added to lead-architect.md — (a/4r39) verify 'constant X consumed by Y' against Y's BODY not X's presence (dead-constant => net-new not tightening; grounding lead-rfc5); (b/n64d) recompute canonical pre-state from PACKAGE DATA (shop-templates show / installed claude.md) not the local poured primer (grounding lead-f3gm). Flat prose, merged 81e009f, all 42 lead-architect tests pass, zero new failures.

title: bd-dolt .beads/issues.jsonl created_by re-export normalization (source-side fix for the non-idempotent clean-tree churn) — bd-fork track, NOT shopsystem-templates
description: Split off from lead-bdqm prong (A) during 2026-06-11 triage (lead-architect). The bd-dolt backend re-exports the tracked .beads/issues.jsonl on every bd-op and the created_by metadata flips (e.g. 'David Stenglein' -> 'unknown'), so a committed tree re-dirties immediately and git status --porcelain is never clean. This is the SOURCE of the non-idempotent churn that the pre-emit clean-tree gate trips on (lead-bdqm). Two complementary durable fixes; this bead owns the SOURCE prong: either (i) normalize created_by deterministically so it does not flip, or (ii) gitignore / .gitattributes-filter the volatile export field so a committed tree stays clean across bd-ops. Coordinate with bd-fork tracking (cf lead-vlsu upstream bd work). NOT shopsystem-templates scope — the templates BC owns the gate, but the gate carve-out (prong B) is already folded into lead-m56e; THIS bead is the orthogonal source-hygiene fix on the bd/.beads surface. Routing: bd-fork track owner, not a BC dispatch via shop-msg.

title: FINDING: bd-dolt .beads/issues.jsonl created_by re-export churn perpetually re-dirties the tracked file, tripping the BC pre-emit gate clean-tree (Check 1) — a verified-COMMITTED fix cannot satisfy the gate
description:
RECURRING TRAP, surfaced on lead-rply (bc-shopsystem-bc-launcher) reconciliation 2026-06-11; also seen on lead-if8q (lead-vhfa defect 2). Would hit EVERY BC.

SYMPTOM: After a BC commits its work product, `git status --porcelain` keeps showing `M .beads/issues.jsonl`. The bd-dolt backend re-exports the tracked .beads/issues.jsonl on every commit/bd-op, and the created_by metadata flips (e.g. 'David Stenglein' -> 'unknown'), so the tracked file re-dirties immediately after each commit. The BC Implementer pre-emit gate's clean-working-tree precondition (Check 1) therefore trips PERPETUALLY — even though the actual fix IS committed and reachable on origin/main. The BC emits work_done(status:blocked) for a deliverable that is genuinely done and green. On lead-rply this forced a lead steer (bc-container inject) + a false-block loop before the BC self-resolved; the deliverable (adf7b2d) was verified-green (167 passed) and reachable the whole time.

WHY IT IS A REAL GATE BUG, not just BC discipline: the file CANNOT be made clean by committing it — the next bd-op re-dirties it. So the BC cannot satisfy Check 1 by self-resolving in the normal commit-your-own-state way; the churn is non-idempotent. The clean-tree gate must treat .beads metadata-export churn as ambient (carve-out), OR the .beads created_by re-export must be normalized/gitignored so it stops re-dirtying.

DECIDE THE DURABLE TARGET (two complementary fixes):
  (A) bd / .beads hygiene: stop the created_by re-export churn at the source — either normalize created_by deterministically (don't flip David Stenglein->unknown), or gitignore/.gitattributes-filter the volatile export field so a committed tree stays clean across bd-ops. (Coordinate with bd-fork tracking, cf. lead-vlsu upstream bd work.)
  (B) pre-emit gate carve-out: the clean-working-tree precondition (Check 1) must add .beads/issues.jsonl metadata-export churn to the ambient-artifact carve-out set (cf. lead-8lm .specstory / .scheduled_tasks.lock precedent named in lead-vhfa). Feeds the bc-emit work-done precondition wrapper (lead-m56e / ADR-036) and the gate-redesign (lead-o6tp).

LINKS: relates lead-vhfa (same clean-tree false-block class; its defect 2 already names the .beads/issues.jsonl carve-out need), lead-oyaj (self-resolve doctrine — but note: this churn is NOT self-resolvable by commit, so it specifically needs a carve-out, not just discipline), lead-m56e/ADR-036 (precondition-wrapper target), lead-o6tp (gate redesign). References BC-side bclaunch-27p (the launcher BC flagged + closed its instance on origin/main 18ecee4).
notes:
TRIAGE 2026-06-11 (lead-architect). Discriminator vs contract surface (ADR-036 D1, features/templates/105-112, ADR-018). DISPOSITION = TWO-PRONGED, NEITHER prong is a clean bugfix to shopsystem-templates today: (A) bd/.beads created_by re-export churn fix is a bd-dolt / .gitattributes hygiene change at SOURCE — NOT in shopsystem-templates scope; it belongs with the bd-fork tracking (cf lead-vlsu upstream bd work), routed to whichever surface owns the bd-dolt backend / repo .gitattributes, not to the templates BC. Recommend a separate bd-hygiene bead/dispatch off the bd-fork track. (B) pre-emit gate clean-tree carve-out for .beads/issues.jsonl metadata churn = a wrapper-scenario requirement folded into lead-m56e step 1 (same carve-out set as lead-vhfa (b); cf lead-8lm .specstory/.scheduled_tasks.lock precedent). KEY: this churn is NON-idempotent, so it is NOT self-resolvable by commit (distinguishes it from lead-oyaj) — it specifically needs the carve-out (B), authored by lead-po into the wrapper scenarios. Both prongs tracked; (B) consolidated into lead-m56e, (A) needs its own bd-hygiene routing.
Prong (A) source-fix split off to lead-kf36 (bd-fork track). Prong (B) gate carve-out folded into lead-m56e wrapper scenarios. This finding stays open as the umbrella until both land.

title: [bug] bd init-safety guard deadlock: empty local Dolt + configured sync.remote has NO non-destructive prefix-adoption path (all 5 recovery commands mutually block)
description:
DESIGN GAP in the bd (beads) tool itself, surfaced by the shopsystem-templates mechanism_observation on lead-2bdq. Distinct from lead-rply (which fixes OUR launcher so the wedge never occurs); this bead captures that bd offers NO non-destructive escape once the wedge HAS occurred.

STATE THAT DEADLOCKS: a local embedded-Dolt bd DB that is (a) empty in the working set, (b) has NO issue_prefix set, but (c) HAS a configured sync.remote pointing at a -beads repo whose committed issues.jsonl carries a definite prefix. In this exact state bd v1.0.3 blocks ALL five documented recovery commands:
  1. bd init --prefix X            -> 'already initialized'
  2. bd init --force/--reinit-local-> refuses ('remote origin already has Dolt history; would orphan-push')
  3. bd bootstrap                  -> 'database exists'
  4. bd config set issue_prefix    -> 'issue_prefix cannot be set via bd config set'
  5. bd rename-prefix X            -> 'failed to get current prefix: <nil>' (no current prefix to rename FROM)

So a user with a perfectly recoverable situation (committed jsonl + remote both name the prefix) has no documented forward path. This is upstream bd behavior, NOT shopsystem code — route as an UPSTREAM bd issue / our own bd-fork tracking, NOT to a BC dispatch. Lead-level finding; do not assign_scenarios to a BC.

EMPIRICAL NOTE / the accidental escape hatch: 'bd config set issue_prefix tmpl' FAILS its stated operation but as a SIDE EFFECT triggers bd's 'auto-import-on-empty-database', which imported the 23 committed issues AND adopted prefix 'tmpl' from them — leaving the DB fully operational. So path 4 is a de-facto (undocumented, side-effect) recovery. The proper fix is for bd to make empty-local + configured-remote NON-destructively adopt the remote/committed prefix via an explicit command (e.g. bd bootstrap should detect empty working set and import+adopt rather than erroring 'database exists').

DOWNSTREAM: once lead-rply (launcher provisioning) lands, this wedge state should never arise from our launch path; this bead remains as the upstream-robustness backstop.
notes:
RESOLVED-BY-UNDERSTANDING (lead empirical proof, 2026-06-11): bd is NOT broken — it already provides the non-destructive adoption path. PROVEN: on a fresh BC clone, reset .beads to committed state (issues.jsonl present, NO bd-created dolt working set), then 'bd bootstrap' -> 'Bootstrap plan: import from git-tracked JSONL ... Imported 21 issues' -> embeddeddolt/ created -> bd create/ready WRITE-READY, fully non-destructive. The 'deadlock' was SELF-INFLICTED: the launcher ran 'bd dolt pull' FIRST (creating an empty local DB) which made bootstrap say 'already exists, nothing to do'. FIX = launcher provisions via 'bd bootstrap' on the fresh clone, NOT dolt-pull-then-import (lead-kjv7). No bd code change needed. Downgrading from P0. Residual nit (NOT a blocker): bootstrap derives new-issue prefix from db-name ('scenarios') not the committed ids ('shopsystem-scenarios') — minor consistency, bd still functional; track separately if it matters.
Re-confirmed RESOLVED-BY-UNDERSTANDING and the fix is now in flight as lead-ezzr (request_bugfix -> bc-launcher, provision via bd bootstrap). bd is NOT broken; the deadlock was self-inflicted by launcher's bd dolt pull. Stays P2 as upstream-robustness backstop; not a BC dispatch.
close_reason: RESOLVED by v0.2.8: bd bootstrap IS the non-destructive prefix-adoption path; deadlock was self-inflicted by dolt-pull-first. Launcher now provisions via bd bootstrap as vscode. Premise dissolved.

title: bc-container launch: configured beads issue_prefix does not take in the container — forces manual bd init/bootstrap on every bring-up
description:
CONFIRMED TWICE 2026-06-09 (bc-launcher + templates bring-ups). bc-container launch prints 'Configured beads issue_prefix

in container', but inside the container 'bd' reports 'database not initialized: issue_prefix config is missing'. Every fresh bring-up therefore stalls the BC at a beads-registry gate until the lead steers it to run bd bootstrap / bd init --prefix. The launch DOES run 'bd dolt pull' (so the dolt data is present) — only the local issue_prefix config doesn't persist/take. Blocks autonomous bring-up + inbox drain. FIX: bc-container launch must actually establish a working bd config (bootstrap from the pulled remote, or set+verify issue_prefix) before injecting the startup prompt, and verify 'bd ready' works as a readiness gate. Relates to bring-up reliability (lead-n5r1 lineage) and the recurring BC self-resolve gap (lead-oyaj).
notes: 2026-06-19 lead-architect: NOT DISPATCHED — RECOMMEND CLOSE (already satisfied at contract surface, ADR-018 D1). The failure this bead describes (launch's 'bd config set issue_prefix' does not take -> manual bd init) is EXACTLY the mechanism features/bc_container_beads_usable.feature @scenario_hash:2904f3a905567b48 (lead-ezzr) already SUPERSEDED: its header says the launcher must NOT 'bd config set issue_prefix' and must run 'bd bootstrap' so the BC boots WRITE-READY. controller.py L933-995 implements bootstrap-as-vscode. The pinned scenario IS the durable fix; re-dispatch would duplicate an already-pinned contract. Residual recurrence belongs to the conftest/wedge-heal class (lead-rply/lead-vlsu/lead-80t0, durable fixes landed). Router: close as superseded-by-2904f3a905567b48, or reopen with NEW post-bd-bootstrap evidence.

title: BC container launched via bc-container lacks GitHub push auth — blocks pre-emit push-to-origin/main gate
description: SURFACED 2026-06-09 (agent-vault lead-v4ih). bc-container launch copies host gitconfig (identity) into the container but NO push credential — 'no gh, no token, no SSH key' in the container. The BC committed its green agent-vault work locally (208c6b1) but cannot push to origin/main, so it cannot satisfy the pre-emit 'commit reachable from origin/main' gate and blocks at work_done. Meanwhile the LEAD host has working gh auth (gho_ token, gh auth git-credential). The bring-up skill mounts host ~/.config/gh per its docs, but either the host /home/dstengle lacks gh auth or the mount didn't carry the token; only gitconfig landed. IRONY: this is exactly the credential-coupling agent-vault (lead-mdng) eliminates — but agent-vault isn't deployed yet, so BC push auth is currently unprovisioned. FIX OPTIONS: (a) bring-up must provision a working push credential (mount/copy gh token or configure a git credential helper) as part of launch readiness; (b) interim: lead supplies an ephemeral token to the container for the push. Relate lead-mdng (agent-vault is the durable fix) and the bring-up skill / lead-n5r1 readiness lineage.
notes: 2026-06-09 CLOSE — FIXED + VERIFIED END-TO-END. bc-base v0.2.3 bakes gh 2.93.0. In the relaunched templates container, as the vscode user (real BC context): gh auth status = logged in (dstengle, repo+workflow scopes) via mounted ~/.config/gh; git credential.helper = 'gh auth git-credential'; 'git ls-remote --heads origin' EXIT=0 returning real refs. Authenticated remote ops work → BCs can push. (Earlier ls-remote 'failure' was a root-vs-vscode safe.directory artifact, not auth.) FLEET NOTE: bc-launcher/messaging/scenarios stay on the old image until their next relaunch; they pick up gh automatically on relaunch (no forced churn). messaging already had working push (fnj5/mcps commits landed).

title: agent-vault design decisions before implementation: server topology, zero-bind-mount, two-server bring-up
description: Resolve BEFORE lead-v4ih authoring. (A) SERVER: broker must run as a container on the shopsystem network (spike-proven); decide owner/lifecycle (compose service like postgres vs launcher-managed) + master-password-on-restart + one-time human secret-load. (B) ZERO BIND MOUNTS (dave's hard goal): bake non-secret static config (placeholder .credentials.json, .claude.json, gitconfig) into bc-base; route gh creds through an agent-vault GitHub service; CA fetched at runtime by 'agent-vault run' (spike: self-bootstraps, no mount); inject ONLY AGENT_VAULT_* env -> delete _resolve_host_path/BCLAUNCHER_HOST_HOME. (GAP) TWO SERVERS: postgres + agent-vault broker arranged as one lead-shop supporting-services compose on shopsystem net w/ persistent volumes + restart; bc-launcher gains agent_vault_reachable readiness gate; bin/shop-shell brings both up. See findings/agent-vault-credential-spike.md.
notes: DECISIONS LOCKED 2026-06-09 (dave). ZERO MOUNTS: gh token via agent-vault GitHub service (NOT treated as needing proof); gitconfig via GIT_AUTHOR_*/GIT_COMMITTER_* env sourced from a lead .env file; placeholder .credentials.json + .claude.json BAKED into bc-base; CA fetched at runtime by 'agent-vault run'. Launch injects ONLY AGENT_VAULT_ADDR/TOKEN/VAULT env -> delete _resolve_host_path + BCLAUNCHER_HOST_HOME. SERVER: broker is a SUPPORTING-SERVICES COMPOSE service (extend compose.yaml alongside postgres), NOT bc-launcher-owned (launcher is a tool to start BCs, not a service owner). AGENT_VAULT_TOKEN = launch env var (MVP). Master password = auto-unlock from a .env file (MVP). SCOPE: BCs only; lead host stays launched as-is. PREREQUISITE: bc-base rebuilds MUST be solved first (gates the baking).
close_reason: Design decisions resolved (see notes). lead-v4ih authoring is design-unblocked but SEQUENCED AFTER bc-base rebuild is solved (dave: before any other work).

8. features/spike-lifecycle/08-new-hazards-surface-as-findings-and-beads.gherkin — LEAD-PROCESS (ADR-030 consequences / synthesis fabro silent-success; findings+registry discipline, lead-internal).

title: shop-msg send dependency gate compares dispatch_state, not bd status — bd authoring deps wrongly block dispatch
description:
Observed 2026-06-08 during lead-csas dispatch. shop-msg send refused on a depends-on edge (lead-csas -> lead-o0b5) even after lead-o0b5 was bd-CLOSED. The send gate compares shop-msg dispatch_state, which an authoring bead that was never a shop-msg dispatch can never satisfy (error read: dispatch_state='closed', not 'closed'). Workaround was removing the satisfied authoring-sequencing edge. Real gap: the gate conflates bd status with dispatch_state, so any bd authoring/sequencing dependency will spuriously block a downstream dispatch.

RECURRENCE 2026-06-15: bit AGAIN on lead-9qdn + lead-7if5 — their 'discovered-from:lead-l7uz' provenance edges were over-read as blocking ('predecessor lead-l7uz is at dispatch_state=in_progress, not closed') because lead-l7uz is in_progress. ~6 occurrences now. The discovered-from provenance edge should NEVER gate dispatch.

DISPATCHED 2026-06-19 (lead-architect): request_bugfix -> shopsystem-messaging, work_id lead-z15d. Fix gate to read predecessor bd ISSUE STATUS (not dispatch_state); provenance/discovered-from edges never gate; preserve --queue-on-dependency/promote path. Unpinned existing gate logic -> request_bugfix. Left in_progress pending work_done.

description: Doctrine decision (operator 2026-06-05, from first dispatch lead-y8rz). Red-before-green commit-ordering gate stays HARD (not softened). But the BC must SELF-RESOLVE fixable procedural gate failures (close own sub-issues, insert/reorder test(red) commit, clean tree) BEFORE emitting work_done — never emit work_done(blocked) for self-fixable process artifacts. work_done(blocked) reserved for things needing lead input (scope/contract/architecture/vocabulary). Authoring: PDR + bc-implementer/bc-reviewer Gherkin scenarios -> assign_scenarios to shopsystem-templates. See memory feedback_bc_self_resolves_procedural_gates. 2026-06-11 FOURTH live instance (bc-launcher lead-rply beads-provisioning bugfix): BC emitted work_done(blocked) for a fully GREEN, committable fix (167 passed) whose ONLY blocker was its own uncommitted/unpushed worktree — entire fix dirty, local HEAD==origin/main==5ac19e9. DISPOSITION: the bead's own ASK is correct and already names the vehicle — 'Authoring: PDR + bc-implementer/bc-reviewer Gherkin scenarios -> assign_scenarios to shopsystem-templates'. This is NEW capability framing (self-resolve discipline as authored scenarios/prose), so the vehicle is assign_scenarios, which requires lead-po authoring FIRST — STOP, not architect-dispatchable today. RELATIONSHIP TO WRAPPER: post-ADR-036 the mechanically-checkable preconditions move to the bc-emit wrapper (lead-m56e); the self-resolve DISCIPLINE (close own sub-issues, reorder red commit, commit/push own green fix before emitting blocked) is a JUDGMENT behavior that STAYS template-prose (ADR-036 D1 prose-layer: not mechanically checkable). So lead-oyaj is the prose-layer cousin: lead-po authors a bc-implementer/bc-reviewer prose pin (+ optional scenario) that blocked is reserved for genuine lead-input needs, never self-fixable commit/push/sub-issue steps. title: Architect design: BC-local architect role + three-tier ADR mechanism (system-global + local + periodic review)
description: NEXT thread, lead-architect. ADRs for: the BC-local architect role (activities, posture, vs Implementer/Reviewer, bc-architect template); the system-global ADR home/convention in the lead repo (distinct from framework adr/); BC-local ADR convention + periodic system-architect review cadence. Extends PDR-001 BC-template-coverage open question.
notes:
2026-06-10 DESIGN LANDED (lead-architect). Authored 3 system-global ADRs (next-free numbers 033-035; 029-032 = spike-lifecycle, verified via ls adr/):

- ADR-033 — BC-local architect role: design-sensibility-up-front posture distinct from Implementer (make-it-pass) and Reviewer (adversarial-after); owns the lead-5hm1 decomposition discipline + internal structure + BC-local ADRs; escalates STRUCTURE to lead architect, SCOPE/INTENT to lead PO. NO BC-local PO (product intent stays at lead per §3.1/§4.4 — verified against spec; the architect-yes/PO-no asymmetry is principled: design != intent).
- ADR-034 — system-global ADR home. EMPIRICAL CORRECTION: PDR-013's "new home needed (distinct from framework adr/)" premise is FALSE on pre-state — the lead repo's existing adr/ tree is ALREADY the per-product/system-global home (majority of the 30 ADRs are cross-BC product-realization decisions). The real distinction the bead wanted is "distinct from the framework SPEC" (the 01-..-06-.. section files), which the layout already provides. Decision: NO new directory; record tier via a lightweight **Tier:** header tag (framework/system-global), backfilled opportunistically at the periodic review.
- ADR-035 — three-tier model umbrella (framework / system-global / BC-local) + BC-local-tier definition + periodic system-architect review cadence. BC-local ADRs are BC-OWNED artifacts in the BC repo (never on lead host per ADR-018/§4.3), authored by the bc-architect. Review is event-anchored (reconcile a structural work_done) with optional scheduled sweep, conducted RECONCILE-ON-EMISSIONS (shop-card/work_done/clarify) NOT clone read. Tier promotion (D5) is the relief valve for mis-tiering.

THREE-TIER MODEL: kept as 3 tiers, NO collapse (each earns distinct home/owner). But authored as 3 ADRs not by mechanical 1:1 with the bead's 3 scope items — ADR-035 is the umbrella tying ADR-033 (produces bc-local tier) + ADR-034 (homes the lead-resident tiers) together and adding the bc-local tier + review (the one piece neither sibling covers). One decision per ADR, no padding.

VERIFICATION RESULTS (artifact surface, ADR-018; no repos/): §4 names only Implementer+Reviewer, no architect (ADR-033 new role confirmed); §3.1/§4.4 keep product intent at lead (no-BC-PO confirmed principled); existing adr/ already system-global home (ADR-034 correction); no separate decisions/ dir exists; framework SPEC = 01..06 section files.

FOLLOW-UPS FLAGGED (NOT dispatched — templates BC flaky/offline per lead-yxsr):
1. bc-architect.md role template -> shopsystem-templates (role-complete per PDR-001: posture, ADR-033 D2 activities, structure-vs-intent discriminator, "records BC-local ADRs" per ADR-035 D3). PO authors pinning Gherkin; rides the held templates queue (146-158).
2. Re-ground held features/templates/154-158 so the decomposition discipline's OWNER is bc-architect (currently role-agnostic). PO/Architect follow-up.
3. Minor framework-tier spec edits: name the 3rd BC role in §4.1/§4.2; add "BC-local ADRs" to the §4.3 artifact list. Flagged, not actioned.
4. Tier-tag backfill + whether to immediately tag the 3 new ADRs (ADR-034 Open question).
5. Multi-product future: framework-tier ADRs may warrant physical separation (ADR-034 Open question) — revisit at periodic review.

Cross-linked to PDR-013 (§3 S2/S3, O3) and PDR-001 (BC-template-coverage open question). No Gherkin, no dispatch, no @scenario_hash retired (all 3 enumerations empty). Working tree left for router (no commit per instruction).

title: Spike: fabro as alternable orchestration substrate (BC launch + Monitor loop)
description:
Back-burner exploration (user: keep on backlog, 2026-06-04). Evaluate running a shop on the fabro orchestration platform (docs.fabro.sh) while conforming to the SAME shopsystem execution contract, changing only how a BC is launched and replacing the Monitor system with a fabro looping workflow.

VERDICT from initial analysis (workflow, 11 agents): directionally right but 'leaks-materially'. Reframe as TWO seams of replacement bounded by THREE invariant-surfaces fabro must NOT touch.

SEAM (a) BC launch -> fabro run: CLEAN. bc-container launch -> POST /api/v1/runs (RunManifest) + /start; BC_IMAGE -> [environments.<slug>]; container-init -> [run.prepare]+[run.clone]; lead->BC dispatch -> parent/child runs via fabro_run_create; PO-authors/Architect-verifies discriminator -> child approval_required gate.

SEAM (b) Monitor -> fabro loop: PARTIAL / refuted as drop-in. All 15 fabro hook events are INTERNAL lifecycle points; fabro has NO native primitive for an EXTERNAL async arrival (postgres LISTEN/NOTIFY inbound BC response). fabro supplies loop/retry/checkpoint rails AROUND the event source but cannot BE it. shop-msg watch survives as a command node inside a fabro loop. checkpoint/resume does subsume the session-start drain.

THREE LEAKS (stay shop-msg/bd-owned): (1) bd is state authority; fabro checkpoint (durable store + fabro/run/{id}, fabro/meta/{id} branches) is a COMPETING authority, must be demoted to run-resume-only (PDR-010). (2) name registry + from/to addressing (ADR-006/020) has no fabro analog; lead still runs shop-msg registry add. (3) empirical-verification-is-contract-surface (ADR-018): lead must NOT harvest work_done by reading fabro child outputs; keep shop-msg read outbox + scenarios hash.

SHARPEST RISK (adversary): shop-msg respond is a bd-first fsync'd transactional bd write (ADR-016) under ADR-012's 3-step protocol. fabro checkpoints on EVERY node and the BC tier HAS a git repo, so fabro will checkpoint-commit the BC worktree on the SAME node that runs shop-msg respond -> active ordering hazard against ADR-012 at the BC tier (phantom postgres row / bd-postgres divergence on retry).
design:
FIRST SPIKE (smallest kill-or-confirm, one node, no topology): single fabro box+parallelogram node, working dir = git repo with bd init + SHOPMSG_DSN; node runs shop-msg respond work_done; assert (a) BC bead transitioned via bd-first path, (b) postgres row exists exactly once (uniqueness intact), (c) FORCE mid-node kill + retry_policy=standard retry -> no interleaved partial bd state, no duplicate deposit. If this survives, fabro can slot below bd; if not, the premise dies cheaply.

THEN full e2e: fabro server on same Postgres; one [environments.bc] -> bc-base; lead-side unchanged (shop-msg registry add, create lead bead = work_id); minimal BC .fabro graph (prepare clone+bd-init+venv; parallelogram node CALLING shop-msg respond, NOT fabro structured output); launch child run carrying work_id as label. PASS: work_done round-trips with hash reconciliation entirely over shop-msg+bd while fabro owns only launch+loop. FAIL: fabro boots stale image, OR can't run shop-msg from a node, OR checkpoint forces competing write, OR message only harvestable via fabro outputs (ADR-018 violation).

KEY UNKNOWNS to resolve empirically: does fabro pull current :latest digest vs stale cache (ADR-021 D3)? fabro_run_events ordering/delivery guarantees? can a node block-and-wait on external signal vs poll? can one node emit multiple shop-msg messages (only final JSON validated)?

BLOCKER: bc-base currently un-rebuildable (ADR-022: scenarios CLI not baked, pdr/002 pin 404s) -> resolve before the e2e leans on it.

Full workflow output: task wzcm397gc / run wf_ce7f1f1d-3e2.
notes: GRADUATED 2026-07-01: fabro spike graduated to canonical. ADR-048 (substrate) supersedes this origin bead; realized by ADR-049/050/051 + features/fabro-orchestration/01-04.
close_reason: Closed

title: [reliability] BC agents go idle-at-prompt after finishing work and don't return to react loop — dispatches sit unprocessed despite 'online' + armed watcher
description: Observed on shopsystem-messaging (lead-kll4): the claude agent finished prior work and parked at its prompt, never returning to its inbox-react loop. Result: a dispatched request_maintenance sat unprocessed ~1d16h while bc-status showed 'online' and the shop-msg watch child was running. A mailbox nudge would NOT have woken it (agent wasn't reacting to watcher events); required a tmux session-start nudge. This affects ANY BC — after completing work, the agent must reliably return to idle-watch posture or dispatches stall silently. Related: ~371 defunct zombies from BDD-fixture inbox noise on messaging (project_test_fixture_inbox_pollution). IMPLICATION for this project: before dispatching the pin-fix (lead-po0j) to bc-launcher, verify bc-launcher's agent is live (not idle-parked); same for any BC dispatch. Possible fixes: BC primer/loop must re-arm and idle-watch after each completion; or a liveness check + auto-nudge in the launcher; or zombie reaping.

title: assign_scenarios from lead (lead-yy30)
notes:
Lead work_id: lead-yy30
clarify: ARCHITECT RULING (lead-yy30, grounded in ADR-021 + ADR-018 + the committed scenario-40 declarative-shape precedent). Per-scenario deliverable artifact, tracked path, and pin layer follow. The governing precedent is ALREADY committed in scenario 40 (lead-usc7/40c, clarify lead-jx4u): a no-Actions-runner, in-memory-fake BDD harness cannot exercise a LIVE workflow run or a LIVE ghcr registry, so infra/CI behavior is pinned by inspecting the committed workflow/Dockerfile as a DECLARATIVE ARTIFACT (structural file-tree + YAML inspection), with live-registry/live-Actions/credential-permission state explicitly OUT-OF-BAND and NOT asserted in-suite. ADR-018 forbids any pin that requires executing live infra. Apply that precedent uniformly.

(1) d9909f38 (Dockerfile repo-artifact; = scenario 36). Deliverable: an ACTUAL committed Dockerfile at a tracked path in the bc-launcher repo that builds shopsystem-bc-base. Pin layer: STRUCTURAL file-tree inspection. Steps inspect (a) the file exists at the tracked path, (b) it installs the framework CLIs in the "github.com/dstengle/<utility> @ vMAJOR.MINOR.PATCH" VCS/published-package pin shape (not editable-from-clone). No registry, no build execution. This is the same shape as your existing shared-postgres scenarios bb070f75/8ad37d1a that conftest tests structurally.

(2) b688a5fe (publish-to-ghcr; scenario 37), 4e470f7 (rebuild-on-repository_dispatch; scenario 38), be11d615 (rollback re-tag; scenario 41). Deliverable: committed .github/workflows/*.yml in the bc-launcher repo. Pin layer: STRUCTURAL YAML inspection — NOT a live-registry assertion and NOT a new in-src registry-driver fake. Do NOT stand up an in-src registry seam to simulate digest/tag resolution for these three; that would be testing a fake's own arithmetic, not the deliverable. Instead pin: (37) a publish workflow triggered on push of tags matching "v*" that builds and pushes to ghcr.io/dstengle/shopsystem-bc-base tagging both the version and "latest" with public visibility declared; (38) the SAME (or a sibling) workflow declares a "repository_dispatch" trigger whose job rebuilds and re-pushes "latest"; (41) rollback is the already-published-digest re-tag observable — pin it as the declarative property that the publish workflow tags by version (so prior digests remain pullable) enabling a "latest"-repoint; if no rollback automation is authored, a workflow-shape/README-runbook declarative pin of the re-tag procedure suffices. The live-registry "same digest", "D_new distinct from D_old", "unauthenticated docker pull", and "successful Actions run" clauses are OUT-OF-BAND (scenario-40 precedent): the in-suite proxy is the declared workflow structure, not a live pull.

(3) af2f03d3 (launch resolves latest digest before run; = scenario 39). THIS ONE IS DIFFERENT. It is NOT infra/CI — it is in-process launch BEHAVIOR your harness CAN and SHOULD pin behaviorally. Deliverable: launch gains an explicit digest-resolution step before container start, injected via a new RegistryDriver protocol that is fakeable in tests. The fake simulates the registry resolving "latest" -> D_new; the test asserts launch calls resolve, pulls/uses D_new, and the started container runs from D_new not the cached D_old. This is a real in-src seam with a behavioral test — the right tool here precisely because the behavior lives in controller.launch, not in a workflow file.

SEAM-SHARING ANSWER (your explicit question): NO. Scenarios (2) and (3) do NOT share one RegistryDriver. (2) is workflow/CI side, pinned declaratively as committed YAML — no in-src seam at all. (3) is launch-path side, pinned behaviorally via the new RegistryDriver protocol. The RegistryDriver protocol belongs to scenario 39 ONLY. Introducing a registry-driver fake to "test" 37/38/41 is the failure mode ADR-018 warns against — it tests the simulator, not the contract artifact.

Summary deliverables: 36 -> committed Dockerfile (tracked path) + structural inspection. 37/38/41 -> committed .github/workflows/*.yml + structural YAML inspection, live state out-of-band. 39 -> launch digest-resolution step + new RegistryDriver protocol fakeable in tests, behavioral assertion. No ADR amendment needed: this is ADR-021 + scenario-40 precedent applied, not new policy.

NOTE on hash drift: the hashes you cite (d9909f38/b688a5fe/4e470f7/be11d615/af2f03d3) are the DISPATCHED bodies; lead-held bodies for 36/37/38/39/41 have since been edited and recompute to af9e04c7167018d0/82f553ba0511089b/2ba5f928d0bb5039/dd18538ac68045ce/06766294e0e9ca72. Pin against the bodies as DISPATCHED to you (the hashes in your inbox payload); a lead-side re-sync bead is filed separately. Do not re-derive from drifted lead-held text.

title: Recurring registry-reset + unset SHOPMSG_DSN drift on lead host
description: Observed during lead-ybxs dispatch (2026-06-02): the shopsystem-messaging DB registry was empty — both shopsystem-product (lead) and shopsystem-templates (bc) had to be re-added via 'shop-msg registry add' before send would work. Also SHOPMSG_DSN is unset in the lead-host (devcontainer) shell; its canonical value lives inside bin/shop-shell, which the lead host itself does not run. Symptom: shop-msg send/registry operations fail until manually re-registered and DSN exported. Decide durable fix: seed registry on postgres bring-up, persist SHOPMSG_DSN in the devcontainer env, or document the manual re-register step.
notes: RESOLVED 2026-06-19 (lead-architect). Per the 2026-06-09 re-scope, the registry-reset half is HEALTHY/not-reproducing (5 canonical rows resolve, shop-msg works without manual re-add). The remaining half — SHOPMSG_DSN unset in the devcontainer shell — is now PERSISTED durably. INSTANCE-LOCAL fix: added a containerEnv.SHOPMSG_DSN entry to .devcontainer/devcontainer.json = postgresql://postgres:postgres@shopsystem-messaging-postgres-1:5432/shopsystem. Verified the value resolves from THIS devcontainer host (the container runs --network=shopsystem, so the docker-net hostname is reachable; SHOPMSG_DSN=<value> shop-msg registry list succeeded; getent resolves shopsystem-messaging-postgres-1 -> 192.168.0.3). Takes effect on next devcontainer rebuild; current sessions still resolve via the CLI config default so no live breakage. CLOSING.

In practice the deleted existence guard in _cmd_pending_inbox was MASKING a second defect — bd_facade.bd_available(cwd) calls bd list with cwd scoped to a registry-derived shop_root; subprocess raises FileNotFoundError/NotADirectoryError (NOT the BdFacadeError callers catch) when that path is absent. Same latent crash sits on the ADR-017 BC-side-bead respond path at cli.py:1240 (_apply_bc_bead_response), not just the lead-mxxm site at cli.py:435 (_cmd_pending_inbox).

BC's in-line remediation: hardened bd_available centrally to return False when cwd is not a directory — covers both observed sites and future call sites that route through it.

Architect disposition (lead-architect, 2026-05-30):
- (a) Filed fresh bead lead-gf4h to track the proposed_action: audit other CLI paths that scope bd cwd to a registry path and confirm they route through hardened bd_available rather than calling _run_bd directly. P2, suggested vehicle request_maintenance once audit identifies stragglers.
- Did NOT extend lead-2id (respond collision) or lead-7wp (lost dispatches); the latent-crash audit is doctrinally distinct (ADR-017/ADR-018 surface coherence) and warrants its own bead trail.
- Did NOT close lead-mxxm; closure remains on its own work_done arrival per standing rule. work_done has not yet arrived.
- Did NOT dispatch a follow-up request_bugfix. Observation reports the in-line fix as already applied in the BC working tree; the lead-mxxm work_done will carry the bd_available hardening as a side-effect of the scenarios 45-47 implementation. Confirm via scenario register on reconciliation. If the work_done summary explicitly references the bd_available hardening, the audit bead lead-gf4h can proceed; otherwise file a clarify on whether the hardening landed.
- ADR-018 doctrine note: this observation strengthens the case that ADR-018's "no refusal on absent shop_root" contract surface implicates every ADR-017 side-effect site, not only the read-time warning text. Worth a one-line addendum to ADR-018 if a future revision pass touches it; not load-bearing enough to file as a standalone doc bead.
close_reason: Hash register matched (82e88384ccd417d8, dac49bb9814f7ddc, 33221aa5431226f3); origin/main landed at ef2dc83bd45bdca498ac132400064004e86beb51 (dispatch baseline 120713f); empirical re-test passed — BC-addressed shop-msg read/pending inbox ops complete cleanly with no 'registry may be stale' or 'does not exist on disk' warnings, even though lead host carries no repos/ BC source. BC summary explicitly references bd_available hardening landed via cli.py + bd_facade.py + conftest.py + feature on ef2dc83 — implicitly verifies the lead-gf4h latent crash hardening since any uncaught FileNotFoundError on the BC-addressed call paths would have surfaced during empirical re-test (it did not).

title: carrier: lead-nkd5 — upgrade installed shop-templates to >=39a64d7 before re-pour
description:
## Carrier dispatch resolving lead-nkd5 clarify

The shopsystem-docs Implementer flagged that the BC's installed
'shop-templates' package is built from commit 3f74c52 (per its
dist-info direct_url.json), NOT the lead-named canonical commit
39a64d7. Its packaged templates therefore match the lead-nkd5 documented
PRE-STATE — running 'shop-templates update' would silently no-op and
fail acceptance criteria 1 and 2. The canonical source path
/workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-templates does not exist
in the BC's container, and PDR-009 forbids hand-copying.

## Architectural answer: option (b)

Lead-side precondition: BC upgrades its installed shop-templates package
to at least canonical commit 39a64d7 (effectively, reinstall from
shopsystem-templates main / 39a64d7) BEFORE running the re-pour. PDR-009's
'use mechanism, do not hand-copy' stance is intact; the precondition is
that the installed mechanism's packaged content matches the lead-named
commit.

Verification step the Implementer can run:
- python -c "import json,pathlib; print(json.loads(pathlib.Path([p for p in __import__('sys').path if 'site-packages' in p][0]+'/shop_templates-0.1.0.dist-info/direct_url.json').read_text())['vcs_info']['commit_id'])"
  must report a commit at or beyond 39a64d7 in shopsystem-templates main.
- Equivalently, in the dist-info directory of the python env that supplies
  'shop-templates' on /usr/local/share/nvm/versions/node/v24.15.0/bin:/home/vscode/.local/bin:/usr/local/share/nvm/current/bin:/usr/local/python/current/bin:/usr/local/py-utils/bin:/usr/local/jupyter:/usr/local/share/nvm/current/bin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/local/share/nvm/versions/node/v24.15.0/bin:/usr/local/python/current/bin:/usr/local/py-utils/bin:/usr/local/jupyter:/usr/local/share/nvm/current/bin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin:/home/vscode/.local/bin, cat direct_url.json and read vcs_info.commit_id.

## Corrected acceptance criteria

1. The installed shop-templates package's dist-info vcs_info.commit_id
   is >=39a64d7 in shopsystem-templates main (precondition). If the BC's
   container ships a stale build, the BC reinstalls before re-pour
   (e.g. 'pip install --force-reinstall git+https://github.com/dstengle/shopsystem-templates@39a64d7'
   or equivalent; the exact recipe depends on how the BC's image
   provisions shop-templates).
2. After re-pour via 'shop-templates update --shop-type bc':
   - .claude/agents/bc-implementer.md: wc -l == 474; grep -c 'Pre-emit step' == 2.
   - .claude/canonical/bc-primer.md: grep -c 'YOUR FIRST ACTION' >= 1;
     grep -c {{SHOP_NAME}} == 2 (design invariant per PDR-003 alt F,
     NOT == 0; matches lead-glig carrier guidance).
   - .claude/agents/bc-reviewer.md: wc -l == 298.
   - .claude/settings.json: byte-for-byte identical to canonical (no
     re-pour needed; harmless if invoked).
3. No hand-copying or hand-substitution of any kind (PDR-009 + PDR-003
   alt F).

## Cross-links

- lead-nkd5 (original dispatch this resolves)
- lead-hgc7 (captured mechanism observation; closed as filed)

this work_id, naming the precondition explicitly and reproducing the
corrected acceptance criteria above.

title: [mechanism] re-pour installed shop-templates package commit lag breaks PDR-009 (lead-nkd5 obs)
description:
## Mechanism observation from shopsystem-docs (lead-nkd5)

Surfaced during lead-nkd5 re-pour. The docs BC Implementer observed
that the PDR-009 re-pour mechanism has no contract binding the installed
'shop-templates' package's build commit to the canonical commit the
lead names in the dispatch.

## Empirical evidence

In the shopsystem-docs container:
- Installed shop_templates dist-info direct_url.json -> vcs_info.commit_id
  = 3f74c52 (predates 39a64d7).
- Packaged templates/bc-implementer.md: 305 lines, 0 'Pre-emit step'
  (matches lead-nkd5 documented PRE-STATE, NOT target 474 / 2).
- Packaged templates/claude/bc.md: 0 'YOUR FIRST ACTION' (NOT >=1
  target for bc-primer).
- /workspaces/.../repos/shopsystem-templates does not exist in BC env;
  no 474-line bc-implementer.md anywhere on the BC's filesystem.

## Why load-bearing, not item-specific

Running 'shop-templates update' would silently no-op and exit 0. A less
careful implementer following 'use the mechanism, do not hand-copy'
would emit work_done believing acceptance was met, when in fact every
count assertion would fail. The acceptance criteria's exact wc -l /
grep -c checks are the only catch; the mechanism itself provides no
commit-provenance guard. This will recur on every future re-pour
dispatch where the BC's installed shop-templates lags the lead-named
canonical commit.

## Proposed action (carried verbatim from observation)

(a) Re-pour dispatches should pin/verify the installed shop-templates
    package commit against the message's named canonical commit before
    pour; or
(b) Lead bumps the BC's installed package to the named commit as a
    precondition; or
(c) The mechanism could surface its own build commit (e.g.
    'shop-templates version --commit') so the implementer can assert it
    matches before pouring.

## Lead-side resolution for lead-nkd5 (carrier dispatch follows)

The immediate fix for lead-nkd5 is option (b) — the BC upgrades its
installed shop-templates to >=39a64d7 (or main) before running the
re-pour. Carrier dispatch will name this precondition explicitly in
the corrected acceptance criteria. The general mechanism gap remains
open in this bead.
close_reason: Filed as captured mechanism observation. Lead-side resolution for lead-nkd5 is option (b) — BC bumps installed shop-templates to >=39a64d7 before re-pour — delivered via the carrier dispatch. The general mechanism gap (no commit-provenance contract in the re-pour mechanism) remains a follow-up: surface 'shop-templates version --commit' so implementers and dispatches can assert match before pour. Closing as 'filed'.

title: chore
description:
## Carrier dispatch resolving lead-eanv clarify

The shopsystem-devcontainer Implementer flagged that lead-eanv's
acceptance criteria #1 ('modulo {{SHOP_NAME}} -> shopsystem-devcontainer
substitution') and #2 ('grep -c {{SHOP_NAME}} returns 0') contradict
PDR-009's no-hand-copy mechanism, because shop-templates update writes
canonical files byte-for-byte WITHOUT substitution per PDR-003 alt F
(see shop_templates/cli.py:216-228 _render_claude_md comment).

Empirical: post-update grep -c {{SHOP_NAME}} bc-primer.md == 2, matching
the pre-state evidence. Prior lead-8yl re-pour landed identically.

## Architectural answer: option (a)

Acceptance #2 was misstated. The expected post-state is:
- grep -c {{SHOP_NAME}} .claude/canonical/bc-primer.md == 2
  (NOT == 0)
- No hand-substitution of {{SHOP_NAME}}.
- Shop identity remains in .claude/shop/name.md ('shopsystem-devcontainer'),
  resolved at CLAUDE.md include time per PDR-003 alt F.

PDR-009's no-hand-copy stance and PDR-003 alt F's no-substitution stance
are mutually consistent; the lead-eanv dispatch's acceptance language
imposed a substitution that neither PDR requires.

## Corrected acceptance criteria

1. bc-primer.md, bc-implementer.md, bc-reviewer.md match the canonical
   .claude/canonical/<file> and .claude/agents/<file> in
   shopsystem-templates@39a64d7 byte-for-byte (modulo none — no
   substitution required).
2. grep -c {{SHOP_NAME}} .claude/canonical/bc-primer.md returns 2 (the
   design invariant post-pour per PDR-003 alt F).
3. bc-implementer.md grep -c 'Pre-emit step' returns 2 (lead-8lm/ke1/cw7
   fixes landed).
4. bc-reviewer.md is ~298 lines (matching canonical, lead-dl6 expansion).
5. settings.json untouched (already identical pre-pour).

## Mechanism

shop-templates update --target . --shop-type bc, run from the
shopsystem-devcontainer working tree. No hand-substitution.

## Cross-links

- Resolves clarify on: lead-eanv
- Structural finding: lead-w5s5 (mechanism observation about
  {{SHOP_NAME}}-substitution discriminator)
- Design reference: PDR-003 alt F
- Mechanism reference: PDR-009
notes: Dispatched 2026-05-29 via shop-msg send request_maintenance --bc shopsystem-devcontainer --work-id lead-glig. Deposit verified via shop-msg read inbox. Resolves lead-eanv clarify with option (a) (acceptance #2 misstated; PDR-003 alt F retains {{SHOP_NAME}} post-pour).

Invariants confirmed: grep -c '{{SHOP_NAME}}' bc-primer.md == 2 (PDR-003 alt F); grep -c 'Pre-emit step' bc-implementer.md == 2; bc-reviewer.md == 298 lines; bc-implementer.md == 474 lines. title: chore
description:
## Mechanism observation from shopsystem-bc-launcher (lead-xq7z)

Surfaced during lead-xq7z re-pour. The launcher BC Implementer observed
that lead-xq7z's two acceptance requirements were mutually incompatible
by design:

1. "Use the canonical re-pour mechanism (shop-templates CLI), do NOT
   hand-copy."
2. "grep -c {{SHOP_NAME}} bc-primer.md returns 0, with {{SHOP_NAME}} ->
   shopsystem-bc-launcher substitution applied."

Per shop_templates/cli.py:216-228 (the _render_claude_md comment),
PDR-003 alt F intentionally writes the primer body byte-for-byte with
no shop-specific substitution. Shop name lives in .claude/shop/name.md,
resolved at CLAUDE.md include time. Post-pour grep -c {{SHOP_NAME}} == 2
is the design invariant, NOT a defect.

Confirmed empirically 2026-05-29:
- shopsystem-product/.claude/canonical/lead-primer.md → 2 {{SHOP_NAME}}
- shopsystem-bc-launcher/.claude/canonical/bc-primer.md → 2 {{SHOP_NAME}}

## Why load-bearing, not item-specific

This shape will recur on every primer re-pour against either shop type.
A lead-architect drafting acceptance for a re-pour is likely to read
{{SHOP_NAME}} as a placeholder needing fill-in. The implementer then
faces a contradiction between "use canonical mechanism" and "grep -c
'{{SHOP_NAME}}' returns 0", with no way to satisfy both.

Same pattern just produced contradictory acceptance in lead-eanv
(devcontainer re-pour); resolved via carrier dispatch in lead-rtf
(or whichever ID the follow-up takes).

## Proposed action

Two non-exclusive options for follow-up:

1. **Lead-architect template tightening.** Add a sentence to the
   request_maintenance vehicle guidance: when drafting a primer
   re-pour acceptance, do not require {{SHOP_NAME}} substitution out
   — PDR-003 alt F retains it post-pour. Verify pre-state by
   checking shop_templates/cli.py:_render_claude_md, not by reading
   {{SHOP_NAME}} as a placeholder.

2. **Revisit PDR-003 alt F.** If the broad expectation is that primers
   should not carry literal placeholders post-pour, the canonical
   mechanism should perform the substitution. The current asymmetry
   (shop name in .claude/shop/name.md vs. literal {{SHOP_NAME}} in
   primer header) is confusing to first-touch readers.

Either resolution would prevent future request_maintenance dispatches
from carrying contradictory acceptance criteria of this shape.

## Evidence

- shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/cli.py:216-228 (no-substitution
  design comment)
- shopsystem-product/.claude/canonical/lead-primer.md:1,3 (lead shop's
  own primer carries {{SHOP_NAME}})
- shopsystem-bc-launcher/.claude/canonical/bc-primer.md:1,3 (BC primer
  carries {{SHOP_NAME}} post-pour)

## Cross-links

- Surfaced by: lead-xq7z (launcher re-pour)
- Same shape hit: lead-eanv (devcontainer re-pour, clarify)
- Design reference: PDR-003 (alt F)
- Mechanism reference: PDR-009 (re-pour vehicle)
close_reason: Filed as captured mechanism observation. Two follow-up paths open: (1) lead-architect template guidance tightening for primer re-pour acceptance drafting; (2) reconsider PDR-003 alt F substitution stance. Closing as 'filed'; either follow-up will reference this finding when prioritized.

title: [mechanism] screen-scraped readiness markers must be unambiguous across UI states (TUI launcher discipline)
description:
Mechanism observation from lead-5ig (work_id: lead-5ig-obs-input-ready-marker-overlap).

## Observation

While implementing lead-5ig, the BC empirically verified that the bare '❯' glyph is NOT a discriminating Claude Code post-trust input-ready marker — it also appears in the PRE-trust pane as the trust-prompt selector arrow:

  Accessing workspace:
  /tmp/empirical_trust_test
  Quick safety check: ...
  ❯ 1. Yes, I trust this folder      <- '❯' here, PRE-trust
    2. No, exit

With CLAUDE_INPUT_READY_MARKER='❯' and the new PRE-trust CLAUDE_READY_MARKER='Accessing workspace:', step 4's wait would succeed trivially on the same pane content that satisfied step 2 — silently bypassing the trust-accept Enter and re-creating the lead-3oi deadlock signature in a different form.

## Resolution in lead-5ig

CLAUDE_INPUT_READY_MARKER was set to 'bypass permissions on' (footer-line text only present once trust prompt has cleared AND --dangerously-skip-permissions is active). Pinned by test_claude_input_ready_marker_is_unambiguously_post_trust.

## Load-bearing knowledge

This is a property of how Claude Code v2.1.x renders its TUI, not specific to lead-5ig. The next dispatch that introspects the same panes would reproduce the bug if it took marker suggestions at face value.

## Proposed action (lead-shop side)

Add a note to the lead-architect template's pre-state-verification guidance: when picking a screen-scraped readiness marker for a TUI-driven launcher, verify the marker token does not also appear in adjacent UI states (PRE-trust vs POST-trust panes both contained '❯').

## Evidence

- src/bc_launcher/controller.py:39 (CLAUDE_INPUT_READY_MARKER) at BC commit 69e1ee3
- Empirical pane capture 2026-05-29 (devcontainer)
- BC pinning test: test_claude_input_ready_marker_is_unambiguously_post_trust

## Cross-link

Observed during lead-5ig (closed). This bead captures the durable mechanism finding so a lead-architect template amendment can be sequenced separately from the lead-5ig fix itself.
notes: 2026-06-19 (lead-architect): DISPATCHED request_maintenance -> shopsystem-templates (work_id lead-nps). Discriminator: FLAT net-new prose note to lead-architect template pre-state-verification block — no scenarios, no behavior change -> request_maintenance. Pre-state verified against package data 'shop-templates show lead-architect' v0.14.0: pre-state block present, NO readiness-marker/screen-scrape guidance (grep returns nothing) — additive. Grounding (bare '❯' ambiguous pre/post-trust; resolved to 'bypass permissions on') carried in dispatch from lead-5ig. ADR-018 D1: no repos/ read. Deposit verified via shop-msg read inbox. NOTE: shopsystem-templates heartbeat OFFLINE at dispatch.
close_reason: work_done reconciled 2026-06-19: TUI readiness-marker discipline note added to lead-architect.md template (verify screen-scrape marker is unique to the target state, not adjacent ones; grounded in U+276F-glyph deadlock, CLAUDE_INPUT_READY_MARKER='bypass permissions on' resolution). Flat prose net-new, commit 7db2100 origin/main, zero new failures.

title: [finding] lead-primer.md is reactive-only, lacks continuous-action stance — symmetric to lead-00b
description:
Sibling finding to lead-00b. Same passivity pattern observed on the LEAD side during 2026-05-28/29 sessions.

Setup: I drove dispatches and reconciliations when user prompted ('process the work', 'launch the docs', 'what are we doing'). Between user prompts I idled even with visible next work — in-flight BC dispatches (lead-7v1, lead-gua, lead-yaj, lead-qfc all in_progress since 2026-05-22, no follow-up); pending reconciliations stuck in inbox until lead-bp3 ships (lead-0bw, lead-33r); new Monitor events processed only because they fired as task notifications. User feedback verbatim: 'It seems like this session is pretty passive as well. How can we improve the prompts to continually bias towards action.'

Root cause hypothesis: lead-primer.md (and bc-primer.md per lead-00b) has 'Standing rule: reacting to Monitor events' framing — reactive. Has no equivalent 'Standing rule: between Monitor events, drive ready work.' The Monitor arming + drain are session-start; nothing covers continuous-action stance during the session.

Three candidate amendments to BOTH lead-primer.md and bc-primer.md:

(1) END-OF-TURN CONTINUATION RULE. After completing any unit of work, the router MUST identify and start the next ready action before declaring the turn done. Idle only when no ready action exists. Equivalent prose for both primers, scoped to their respective ready-action surfaces (BC: inbox drain + work execution; lead: outbox drain + bd ready + in-flight stale-check + reconciliation).

(2) IDLE-DETECTION CHECKLIST. Concrete list to walk before declaring idle. Lead-side: shop-msg pending outbox/inbox; bd in_progress filtered by 'no activity >24h'; bd ready unblocked beads; cross-link any reconciliation marked blocked but unblocker now resolved. BC-side: shop-msg pending inbox; bd ready in-BC; in-flight work_id with no commit on origin/main.

(3) CHOICE-SUPPRESSION RULE. Standing rule: do not surface procedural choices to the user ('path 1 / path 2 / your call'). Pick the action, act, report what you did. Surface choices only for scope/vocabulary decisions that require user judgment.

Lead-00b sibling: lead-ke1 covers the bc-primer.md tightening. This bead covers the lead-primer.md tightening AND the symmetric (1)/(2)/(3) framework that should apply to both.

Live surface: every lead session that doesn't have continuous user prompting will accumulate stale dispatches and unprocessed Monitor events. The reactive-only stance can't catch its own stalls.

Recommendation: bundle with lead-ke1 as a single broader request_bugfix to shopsystem-templates covering both primers + the three new standing rules. Architect should fold or supersede lead-ke1 as appropriate.
notes: Dispatching as request_bugfix to shopsystem-templates: three amendments (end-of-turn continuation rule, idle-detection checklist, choice-suppression rule) applied symmetrically to BOTH claude/bc.md and claude/lead.md. Pre-state: grep confirms lead.md:94 'Standing rule: reacting to Monitor events' is reactive-only; bc.md has YOUR FIRST ACTION (just-landed lead-ke1) but no continuous-action standing rule. Cross-link lead-ke1 (just closed, commit 5dcc8cd) — templates BC may consolidate diff against fresh primer state.
close_reason: Closed

title: dispatch to shopsystem-templates: tighten bc-primer.md with YOUR FIRST ACTION imperative
description:
Child of lead-00b. Vehicle: request_bugfix to shopsystem-templates.
