- Lines 69-81: Session start section is descriptive ('the router must run these two orientation commands and then arm the inbox watcher') and is the LAST section, below Who-you-are, BC-inbox-protocol, Do-not, Beads-discipline.
- Missing: imperative 'YOUR FIRST ACTION on every session' framing; explicit 'drain pending inbox' step (only arm-Monitor is named).
- Scenario grep across repos/shopsystem-templates/features/ for 'primer|session.start|first.action|imperative|Monitor': only one tangential comment in brief_003_event_driven_activation_and_settings_json_bootstrap.feature. No scenario pins 'primer leads with imperative pre-user-prompt framing' or 'primer prescribes drain-pending-inbox as pre-user-prompt action.'

Behavior exists but is unpinned for the imperative-framing dimension. Vehicle: request_bugfix (tightening unpinned existing prose). No new scenarios in the dispatch; if scenario-level pinning is wanted later, lead-po authors that separately.

Change requested: lead the bc-primer.md body with imperative 'YOUR FIRST ACTION on every session' framing citing arm-Monitor and drain-inbox as the two pre-user-prompt actions. Acceptance: an LLM agent reading the primer with no prior context would interpret session-start as an action checklist to run before the first turn's response, not as background description.
close_reason: Reconciled: commit 5dcc8cd on origin/main lands top-of-document 'YOUR FIRST ACTION' imperative checklist (4 steps: shop-msg prime, bd prime, arm Monitor, drain pending inbox) with closing 'Only after all four steps complete' phrase. Acceptance criteria met. BDD 164 passed (1 unrelated pre-existing failure per lead-cw7/lead-8lm precedent).

title: dispatch to shopsystem-bc-launcher: default --startup-prompt to session-start imperative
description:
Child of lead-00b. Vehicle: request_maintenance to shopsystem-bc-launcher.

Pre-state (verified empirically):
- repos/shopsystem-bc-launcher/src/bc_launcher/cli.py:30 declares: p_launch.add_argument('--startup-prompt', help='Text to inject into tmux after start')
- repos/shopsystem-bc-launcher/src/bc_launcher/cli.py:135 wires it: startup_prompt=getattr(args, 'startup_prompt', None)
- When caller omits the flag, controller receives None and no synthetic first prompt is injected. Result: BC tmux Claude Code instance sits at empty prompt awaiting human input even with SessionStart hooks running.

Change requested: when --startup-prompt is not supplied, default it to a minimal session-start imperative (e.g., 'Run your session-start sequence per /workspace/CLAUDE.md (arm Monitor on shop-msg watch, then drain pending inbox via shop-msg pending inbox), then await user direction.') so bc-container launch <bc> injects that prompt automatically. Explicit --startup-prompt 'foo' must still override.

Acceptance:
- bc-container launch <bc> with no --startup-prompt injects the default imperative into tmux.
- bc-container launch <bc> --startup-prompt 'foo' still uses 'foo'.
- Default text vocabulary stays in lockstep with bc-primer.md (arm Monitor, drain pending inbox).

This is the load-bearing fix because Claude Code requires a user prompt to act; the launcher provides the synthetic first prompt.
close_reason: Landed adbe100 on origin/main: DEFAULT_STARTUP_PROMPT_TEMPLATE in cli.py references arm-Monitor + drain-inbox session-start sequence with {bc_name} substitution at CLI layer; explicit --startup-prompt remains total override (no concat); 71 pytest pass including 11 new unit tests covering template invariants, --help visibility, default-resolution, and total-override semantics.

title: [finding] BC agent does not autonomously arm Monitor or drain inbox at session start
description:
Observed 2026-05-28 during first live shopsystem-docs BC launch.

Setup: bc-container launch shopsystem-docs landed a healthy container with credentials, /workspace cloned, tmux 'agent' session running. User opened Claude Code inside the BC (claude in tmux). settings.json SessionStart hooks (bd prime, shop-msg prime) fired and fed orientation into Claude Code's first turn.

Expected per /workspace/CLAUDE.md (bc-primer.md canonical session-start):
1. Arm Monitor on 'shop-msg watch --bc shopsystem-docs'.
2. Drain pending inbox (shop-msg pending inbox + dispatch bc-implementer per row).
3. Then accept user direction.

Actual: agent sat at empty prompt awaiting user input. When directly asked 'have you armed the Monitor?' it answered 'No — I had not armed it yet; arming it now.' After arming it surfaced the lead-gov event already in inbox, then volunteered 'ready when you'd like me to dispatch it' — i.e., did not dispatch.

Root cause hypothesis: the bc-primer.md prose describes the session-start sequence but does not forcefully direct Claude Code to perform it as its FIRST action regardless of user prompt. SessionStart hooks deliver bd prime / shop-msg prime output as context, but Claude Code interprets the context as background, not as an imperative checklist to execute before the user speaks. The primer would need either (a) imperative MUST-DO-FIRST framing at the top, or (b) a mechanism that posts a synthetic 'first user prompt' to Claude Code instructing it to run the session-start sequence.

Three candidate fixes:
(a) Tighten bc-primer.md prose to begin with 'YOUR FIRST ACTION on every session: arm Monitor; then drain pending inbox; then await user direction. Do these before answering anything else.'
(b) bc-container launch --startup-prompt 'Run your session-start sequence per /workspace/CLAUDE.md.' (the launcher already has this flag; the docs BC was launched without it today). Adding a default startup prompt for BCs that aren't given one would force the autonomous posture.
(c) SessionStart hook posts an instruction message into the conversation directly (claude code feature?) rather than just providing context.

Live surface: any BC launched without an explicit --startup-prompt will sit idle until a human attaches and prompts. The lead-shop's reactive-router model assumes BCs autonomously process inbox; this gap breaks that assumption.

Recommendation: (a)+(b) combined. (a) makes the primer durable across launch mechanisms; (b) provides a fallback for the common case. Both small changes — (a) is in shopsystem-templates BC's bc-primer.md template; (b) is in shopsystem-bc-launcher cli.py default arg.
notes:
Pre-state verification 2026-05-29:
(b) launcher: --startup-prompt flag exists at cli.py:30 (parser) and cli.py:135 (controller wiring); default is implicit None. Repo clean on main, HEAD d02f5b9. Vehicle = request_maintenance (flat default-value change, no new capability).
(a) templates: canonical bc-primer.md at src/shop_templates/templates/claude/bc.md has descriptive 'Session start' section at line 69 with three numbered items but lacks imperative 'YOUR FIRST ACTION' framing and lacks an explicit 'drain pending inbox' step before awaiting user direction. Existing scenarios in features/ pin substring assertions on the primer body (e.g., shop-msg watch presence) but no scenario pins 'primer leads with imperative pre-user-prompt framing.' Behavior exists but is unpinned for the imperative-framing dimension. Vehicle = request_bugfix (tightening existing prose; no new capability).
close_reason: Both child fixes landed: lead-9sq (default --startup-prompt in launcher, bc-launcher adbe100) + lead-ke1 (bc-primer.md imperative YOUR FIRST ACTION + drain step, templates 5dcc8cd). Empirical proof: docs BC now autonomously runs session-start sequence (arms Monitor + drains inbox + delegates to bc-implementer) when given the default startup-prompt. Children also bridged by lead-dl6 (symmetric lead-primer.md tightening, templates 39a64d7).

title: [finding] verified-deposited lead-shop dispatch (lead-xea) lost from BC inbox between sessions
description:
FINDING — first observed case of a real, verified-deposited lead-shop dispatch disappearing from BC inbox state between sessions (not within a single session under LISTEN drop, and not a synthetic-fixture row).

TIMELINE:
- 2026-05-22: 'shop-msg send request_maintenance --bc shopsystem-devcontainer --work-id lead-xea' executed; deposit verified via 'shop-msg read inbox --bc shopsystem-devcontainer --work-id lead-xea' (see lead-xea NOTES).
- 2026-05-28: 'shop-msg pending inbox --bc shopsystem-devcontainer' returns empty; 'shop-msg read inbox --bc shopsystem-devcontainer --work-id lead-xea' returns 'no inbox message found'. lead-xea bead still in_progress P1. Devcontainer Dockerfile still lacks gh; .github/workflows/publish-devcontainer.yml still targets shopsystem-devcontainer (not shopsystem-bc-base); no ghcr.io/dstengle/shopsystem-bc-base image on the lead host. The BC never received and acted on the work — the message vanished.

DISTINCTION FROM ADJACENT BEADS:
- lead-tsj: in-session LISTEN connection drop + inbox/outbox state loss. Closest fit — same failure mode (verified-deposited rows disappear from postgres state) but the lead-xea instance crossed a session boundary, not an observable LISTEN drop within one session. It may be the same root cause (e.g., a destructive operation in the test suite or a non-transactional cleanup nuking real rows), or a second instance worth tracking.
- lead-497: BC test fixtures leak fxhyg-/test-bc-/test-lead- synthetic rows. Opposite surface (synthetic rows survive; here, real rows died).
- lead-vvz: shopsystem-messaging conftest synthetic-name fixtures lack finalizers. Same opposite surface as lead-497.

OBSERVATION VALUE:
This is the first known production-impacting instance of message loss (a real BC dispatch with downstream blockers — lead-7gt — was silently dropped, leaving the lead-shop bead and the BC repo state out of sync). Strengthens the lead-tsj root-cause case and motivates raising lead-tsj from P2 to P1 once correlated.

REMEDIATION ON THIS WORK:
lead-xea re-dispatched 2026-05-28; deposit re-verified. lead-xea bead notes updated. No further action on the work itself.

CROSS-LINK:
- Related: lead-tsj (closest fit, in-session variant), lead-497, lead-vvz, lead-xea (the re-dispatched work).
- See lead-xea NOTES for the timeline and verification commands.
notes: 2026-06-09 DEMOTE P1->P2: this is a finding record; the actionable root-cause fix lives in sibling lead-tsj (LISTEN-drop / row-loss mechanism, OPEN). Keep as finding, track the fix on lead-tsj.

title: mechanism: shop-msg respond work_done lacks CLI-layer repeat-emit collision guard
description:
Layer-correct surface for the repeat-emit collision PO deferred from lead-cw7's template scope. The bc-reviewer / bc-implementer template precondition prose (lead-cw7, and the Implementer-template follow-up) raises the bar at the role-template level, but the CLI itself (shopsystem-messaging's shop-msg respond work_done) does NOT detect or refuse a duplicate work_done emit for the same work_id.

title: request_bugfix: shopsystem-bc-launcher — assert_docker_run_includes_flag step does not assert container_name against run_cmd
description: From memory note (post-lead-90z, post-lead-cyo): the assert_docker_run_includes_flag BDD step in shopsystem-bc-launcher does not assert container_name against the recorded run_cmd. This leaves a gap in launch-command pinning — a step that names a flag without binding it to the container's identity can pass even when container_name is missing or wrong. Prerequisites are now satisfied: lead-90z (isolation tightening) and lead-cyo (bc-manifest scenarios) are both CLOSED. Filed during lead-0bw reconciliation 2026-05-27. title: BC test fixtures leak synthetic 'fxhyg-' and 'test-bc-/test-lead-' rows into shop_registry without cleanup
description:
Mechanism observation lead-6nt-mech-1 (consumed 2026-05-27) flags that while lead-6nt's six scenarios pin save/restore discipline for production canonical names in repos/shopsystem-messaging/shop_registry, the meta-tests themselves seed synthetic 'fxhyg-<uuid8>-<production-name>' rows via _fxhyg_seed_production_row (tests/conftest.py:4981) and 'test-bc-<uuid12>' / 'test-lead-<uuid12>' rows via _get_or_register_bc_name / _get_or_register_lead_name (tests/conftest.py:274, 284) — none of which are cleaned up at any scope.

EMPIRICAL CONFIRMATION (architect, 2026-05-27 post-lead-6nt suite run):
  - shop-msg registry list shows 97 fxhyg-* rows and 333 total tmp_path/test-bc-/test-lead- rows accumulated from prior sessions
  - The fixture-hygiene scenarios (which only assert invariants over production canonical names) pass cleanly — narrow scope by accident

SCOPE DECISION REQUIRED (for PO):
  (a) Tighten the fixture-hygiene feature with an additional scenario: 'no test-suite-synthesized shop_registry row survives the session, period' — requires cleanup hooks for fxhyg-*, test-bc-*, test-lead-* names.
  (b) Accept the narrow scope explicitly with a comment in the feature documenting that the invariant only covers production canonical names.

The mechanism observation's proposed_action enumerates both branches. Path (a) is more honest about the broader 'hermetic test suite over shared registry' invariant; path (b) ships scope as-is and leaves the synthetic leak as a known limitation.

LOAD-BEARING for the next BC because: a future PO writing tightening scenarios about 'no test row should survive a pytest session' will need this scope answer, and right now the answer is the narrow one by accident-of-scope.

ANCHORS:
  - Source: lead-6nt-mech-1 mechanism_observation (consumed via shop-msg consume outbox)
  - Verification anchors: tests/conftest.py:4981 _fxhyg_seed_production_row; tests/conftest.py:274,284 _get_or_register_bc_name/_lead_name
  - Related: lead-6nt (production-name hygiene, closed) and lead-0bw (inbox/outbox surface, IN_PROGRESS)
notes: 2026-06-09 RE-SCOPE: accumulated leak CLEARED — 'shop-msg registry list' now shows 0 synthetic rows (5 clean canonical) vs 97 fxhyg/333 on 2026-05-27. But the cleanup MECHANISM is unmade: core ask is the a/b scope decision (hermetic-cleanup scenario vs accept-narrow-scope-with-comment). No feature pins 'no test-synthesized row survives a session'. Narrow to the decision.

title: mechanism observation: postgres-backed shop-msg state lost across LISTEN connection drop
description:
Mechanism observation logged 2026-05-27.

SYMPTOM
'shop-msg watch --lead shopsystem-product' (in-session Monitor watcher armed
at session start) died with:

  psycopg.OperationalError: server closed the connection unexpectedly
      This probably means the server terminated abnormally
      before or while processing the request.

The last event streamed before the drop was 'lead-eqx work_done'. On
operator-initiated restart of the watcher the CLI surfaced the next layer
of damage: the messaging registry was empty (7 expected entries: 2 lead
aliases — 'shopsystem-product' and 'shopsystem product' — plus 5 BCs:
shopsystem-messaging, shopsystem-templates, shopsystem-bc-launcher,
shopsystem-devcontainer, shopsystem-docs). Inbox and outbox state were
also lost: 'shop-msg pending inbox --lead "shopsystem product"' and
'shop-msg pending outbox --lead "shopsystem product"' both returned
empty, including in-flight rows that had not yet been consumed
(lead-i18 assign_scenarios and lead-bhp request_bugfix, both addressed
to shopsystem-messaging, both verified deposited earlier in the same
session via 'shop-msg read inbox').

The 7 entries were manually re-registered and the two in-flight
dispatches were re-emitted (see lead-i18 and lead-bhp notes for
wire-hash equivalence to originals).

SUSPECTED ROOT CAUSE
Postgres instance backing shop-msg lost its volume / restarted without
persistent storage. Two candidate causes:

  (a) Postgres container restart without a persistent volume mount.
      Whatever orchestration brings the messages DB up may be using
      an ephemeral / tmpfs-backed data dir, so any restart wipes
      state.

  (b) Devcontainer attached to a *different* postgres instance than
      the one it had been using. Commit 4949706 ('chore: attach lead
      devcontainer to shopsystem docker network') changed the
      devcontainer's network topology recently and may have
      inadvertently shifted DSN resolution to a different service.

To distinguish:
  - Inspect 'docker volume ls' / 'docker inspect' on the postgres
    service the lead devcontainer's SHOPMSG_DSN points to.
  - Check whether the bound volume survives container restart.
  - Compare against pre-4949706 behavior if possible.

DISTINCT FROM lead-7v1
lead-7v1 was the silent-continue watcher bug — that bug was about
the watcher NOT exiting on OperationalError and continuing to print
nothing. The fix to that bug landed and the watcher now correctly
exits on connection drop, which is what produced the observed exit
here. The new finding is about UNDERLYING DB DURABILITY, not the
watcher's exit semantics.

OBSERVATION-ONLY
Do not dispatch. File on the lead tracker so it surfaces in 'bd ready'
for follow-up triage. Likely follow-ups (not blockers):
  - Audit docker-compose / orchestration for the postgres volume mount.
  - If the volume IS persistent and was lost anyway, escalate to
    shopsystem-messaging as a request_bugfix (the BC ought to surface
    a clearer fail-fast / recovery message rather than silently
    re-bootstrapping into an empty registry).
  - If the volume is NOT persistent, file an infrastructure brief on
    the lead tracker to add one.
notes: 2026-06-09 CLOSE (MOOT): bead's own evidence abandons the postgres-durability hypothesis — registry-loss symptom re-attributed to BDD fixture leak (lead-6nt, CLOSED); watcher-exit-on-OperationalError confirmed as CORRECT fail-fast (lead-7v1 landed). No durability fix needed.
close_reason: Closed

title: encode ADR-010 work_done.scenario_hashes pre-emit check in BC Reviewer template (shopsystem-templates)
description:
ADR-010 (2026-05-27) makes the strict-subset rule canonical: work_done.scenario_hashes MUST be a subset of the BC's pinned @scenario_hash set in features/, AND limited to hashes carried in the resolving dispatch when on a clarify-resolution bead.

The natural place to encode the BC-side pre-emit hash-presence check is the Reviewer role template carried by shopsystem-templates. The check: before approving a work_done for emit, grep features/ for each hash in work_done.scenario_hashes; abort if any hash is absent.

This bead tracks the template edit. NOT to be dispatched to templates this turn (per parent task scope guidance — ADR clarification is the deliverable; template propagation is downstream).

Companion: the lead-architect reconciliation step (grep BC features/ for each echoed hash on work_done reception) is already implicit in the lead-architect template's @scenario_hash enumeration discipline; an explicit pass on the reconciliation side may also be warranted. Capture during template edit.
notes: dispatched assign_scenarios to shopsystem-templates with 4 on-wire hashes: e0d4c445a0cd7500 (113 happy-path recompute), 50174dbb885ff5a8 (114 stale-hash block), 762e847fe873201e (115 missing-hash block), 36d22e52adcea48e (116 orphan-hash block). Feature title: 'BC reviewer template encodes ADR-010 work_done.scenario_hashes pre-emit check'. PO judgment calls addressed: scope restricted to Reviewer-emitted scenario-carrying paths (scenarios 113-115 cite assign_scenarios + non-empty request_bugfix); ADR-010 §5 origin-attribution stays architect-side (scenario 116 pins orphan-hash block as strict-subset without removed-vs-fabricated split). Pre-state: bc-reviewer.md template has zero 'scenario_hash' grep hits; BC features/ has 124 pinned @scenario_hash tags, no collisions with new on-wire hashes. Deposit verified via read inbox.
close_reason: BC dispatch landed at templates origin/main 9e7138c 'feat(lead-83l): pin bc-reviewer pre-emit scenario_hash integrity (ADR-010)'. All 4 dispatched hashes verified on-disk under features/bc_reviewer_pre_emit_scenario_hash_integrity.feature: e0d4c445a0cd7500, 50174dbb885ff5a8, 762e847fe873201e, 36d22e52adcea48e. Canonical (src/shop_templates/templates/bc-reviewer.md) and inline (.claude/agents/bc-reviewer.md) byte-identical (diff exit=0). BC's adversarial probes: literal-text shortcut, step-def silent failure, adjacent partition gap — all dismissed substantively. NOTE: deferred architectural question per lead-o6tp — these scenario-prose pre-emit checks may be retired in favor of a CLI-layer 'bc-emit work-done' wrapper. Landing as authored does not block the o6tp shift; o6tp's retirement plan explicitly covers lead-83l.

title: clarify-resolution lead-1t2: dispatch tightening scenario 38 (cross-type multi-emit) to shopsystem-messaging
description:
Lead-side clarify-resolution dispatch for lead-1t2 (shopsystem-messaging).

BC's clarify (effectively a self-answer the PO ratified): the proposed tightening scenario pins success-path cross-type multi-emit (a BC emitting both work_done and mechanism_observation for the same work_id) — a distinct third category beyond single-response routing (respond_routes_to_lead_inbox) and single-type collision (respond_work_done_collision, respond_mechanism_observation_collision). Recommended placement: NEW file respond_dual_type_same_work_id.feature.

PO ratified and authored scenario at features/messaging-registry/38-bc-emits-work-done-and-mechanism-observation-for-same-work-id.gherkin. The scenario pins: given lead+BC registered and a request_maintenance with work-id 'lead-dual-1' sent, when BC runs respond work_done then respond mechanism_observation for the same work_id, then both exit zero and 'shop-msg pending inbox --lead shopsystem-product' shows both rows.

Pre-state verified empirically (2026-05-27):
- storage.py:178,186-191 already supports allow_multi_type=True for BC-to-lead writes; insert_bc_response uses it (storage.py:485,491,503). So the success path the new scenario pins is *implemented* but UNPINNED — no existing scenario asserts it.
- grep -rn '@scenario_hash' on shopsystem-messaging/features/ shows extensive coverage of single-type respond paths and single-type collision paths, but no scenario covers the cross-type-multi-emit success path. Confirmed no conflicting @scenario_hash entries to retire/supersede.

Discriminator: capability present (allow_multi_type code path), unpinned (no scenario covers cross-type success) → request_bugfix per ADR-009 vehicle (b).

The original lead-1t2 dispatch (request_bugfix carrying the collision fix) remains in flight; per its bead it is IN_PROGRESS. This new dispatch is additive — it carries scenario 38, which is the answer to the clarify the BC raised in the course of working lead-1t2. References original work_id lead-1t2 in description.

Acceptance: scenario 38 lands as a passing BDD test in shopsystem-messaging. BC emits work_done covering hash.

Vehicle on wire: shop-msg send request_bugfix --bc shopsystem-messaging --work-id <this bead ID>.
notes: Re-dispatched 2026-05-27 after postgres state-loss event (see lead-tsj). Wire payload byte-identical to original: same scenario file (no commits between original and re-dispatch), same --feature-title ('BC emits cross-type multi-response for same work_id'), same --bc-tag ('shopsystem-messaging'); --description carries a re-dispatch marker but the body's hash is computed from the wrapped scenario only, not the description. Verified via 'shop-msg read inbox --bc shopsystem-messaging --work-id lead-bhp'. Wire hash matches original exactly: 39566e8d2d7768e4. Awaiting BC work_done as before.
close_reason: Reconciled: work_done scenario_hashes (56 entries) is a strict subset of BC-pinned set (also 56 — equal). Wire hash 39566e8d2d7768e4 pinned at repos/shopsystem-messaging/features/respond_dual_type_same_work_id.feature:3 with @bc:shopsystem-messaging. BC reports 79/79 BDD pass under canonical docker DSN. BC's hash-discrepancy investigation (resolved: wire hash matches canonical(full body incl Feature header); implementer's variant came from header-stripped canonicalization — schema validator would have rejected otherwise). No follow-up risks identified.

title: clarify-resolution lead-km9: replace b0fe0666 with content-parse-translated walkthrough end-state scenario
description:
Lead-side clarify-resolution dispatch for lead-km9 (shopsystem-docs).

BC asked (verbatim from clarify): scenario b0fe0666bcaa2398 (end-to-end adoption arrival) pins a runtime outcome — adopter follows walkthrough, lead-shop+per-BC containers run, 'shop-msg registry list' shows them, adopter dispatches assign_scenarios from a Claude Code session. Given 'an adopter has executed every step the walkthrough names...on a host with Docker installed' is not establishable inside this BC's pytest-bdd harness — no real adopter host, no Docker lifecycle, no real Claude Code session. (a) Is b0fe0666 meant to be pinned via this BC's BDD suite, or higher-level integration? (b) If pinned here, intended discharge?

PO answered: discharge (b) translate the runtime assertion into a content-parse assertion. Scenario rewritten at features/docs/02-walkthrough-end-state-is-lead-plus-bcs-ready-to-dispatch.gherkin: the walkthrough's terminal section, parsed as markdown, names the three observable end-state moves (lead-shop container, per-BC containers registered, first dispatch) AS EXECUTABLE STEPS, and states explicitly that successful completion of those three moves is what 'the framework is bootstrapped' means in v1. This pins the same intent — adopter arriving at a dispatchable state — via the walkthrough's authored content rather than a runtime drive.

Per ADR-009 vehicle (b): dispatch as request_bugfix on this new lead bead, carrying the revised scenario. References original work_id lead-km9 in description.

Discriminator: capability under change is 'walkthrough end-state intent.' Original assign_scenarios lead-km9 dispatched the intent as runtime-pinned (b0fe0666); BC observed the intent is not pinnable in BDD as written. The replacement scenario tightens the intent into a content-parse pin in the same BC's harness — same intent, testable shape. This is a behavioral tightening of unpinned behavior (the original b0fe0666 hash was dispatched but BC could not pin it; the intent it named remains unpinned).

The other four scenarios from lead-km9 (7ac28115, 6055501e, 8c55526c, 3874a7f3) remain in flight on the lead-km9 dispatch; this bugfix is additive — it carries only the revised scenario 02. Per ADR-009, the original lead-km9 row stays in the BC inbox until the BC emits work_done covering the full set (including the replacement scenario via this bead's hash).

Acceptance: scenario 02 lands as a passing BDD test in shopsystem-docs (content-parse of the walkthrough markdown's terminal section). BC emits work_done.

Vehicle on wire: shop-msg send request_bugfix --bc shopsystem-docs --work-id <this bead ID>.
notes: Reconciled 2026-05-27. BC committed only the replacement scenario 56babfaeec4514a3 to features/adoption_walkthrough_terminal_section.feature; BDD suite passes 1/1. Implementation is sound (parser at src/shopsystem_docs/walkthrough.py + step defs in tests/conftest.py with multi-substring matchers; BC explicitly noted probes considered and dismissed). BC dropped b0fe0666 per dispatch (correctly omitted from work_done). work_done landed; outbox consumed via 'shop-msg consume outbox --bc shopsystem-docs --work-id lead-b34 --message-type work_done'. Note: BC's BDD suite covers only scenario 56babfaeec4514a3; the four other lead-km9 hashes echoed in work_done are NOT actually pinned in BC features/ tree (see follow-up bead for lead-km9 four-scenario gap). BC working tree shows uncommitted work (features/, src/, tests/, walkthrough/, pyproject.toml all untracked) — BC hygiene concern, BC's responsibility to commit/push.

title: request_bugfix: bc-launcher missing-.claude.json behavior (clarify-resolution for shopsystem-templates-918)
description:
Lead-side clarify-resolution dispatch for shopsystem-templates-918.

BC asked: when host $HOME/.claude/.claude.json is absent, should bc-container launch (a) fast-fail like missing-dir scenario 4ba55450ec351623, (b) warn-to-stderr and proceed, or (c) silent skip?

PO answered (b): warn-to-stderr-and-proceed. Brief 007 minimum-friction posture + agent-consumable stderr signal. The .claude.json file is optional credential material; missing it should not block a launch, but operator must know.

Scenario authored at features/launcher-credentials/08-missing-claude-json-warns-and-proceeds.gherkin.

Pre-state verified: controller.py:208-213 issues cp exec_run unconditionally with no return-code check (silent failure today, capability exists). grep across repos/shopsystem-bc-launcher/features/ shows no scenario pins the missing-.claude.json case — only sibling scenarios 85202a4076f45fba (happy-path copy) and 4ba55450ec351623 (missing-dir fast-fail). Discriminator: capability present, unpinned → request_bugfix.

Vehicle: shop-msg send request_bugfix to shopsystem-bc-launcher carrying scenario 08.

Original assign_scenarios shopsystem-templates-918 remains in flight; this bugfix is the lead's tightening per the BC's clarify, not a replacement (per lead-d6o protocol-gap pattern).

Acceptance: scenario 08 lands as a passing BDD test in shopsystem-bc-launcher; BC emits work_done covering the new hash.
notes:
On-wire as request_bugfix to shopsystem-bc-launcher with work_id 'lead-bxl' (verified: shop-msg pending inbox --bc shopsystem-bc-launcher shows it). Carries scenario 08 (08-missing-claude-json-warns-and-proceeds.gherkin, hash 6172b02f5f57d034). Claim was missed because router was reading wrong bd (CWD drift); now corrected.
Reconciled work_done from shopsystem-bc-launcher. Empirically verified: (1) features/08-missing-claude-json-warns-and-proceeds.feature exists with @scenario_hash:6172b02f5f57d034 tag matching the dispatched body; (2) controller.py:212-222 implements option (b) — when claude_json_file.exists() is false, skips the cp exec_run and emits 'warning: .claude.json not found; skipping copy into container' to stderr (both '.claude.json' and 'not found' substrings present); (3) ran pytest tests/test_08_missing_claude_json.py — 1 passed; (4) ran pytest tests/test_bc_container_credentials.py — 7 passed, sibling hashes 85202a4076f45fba (happy path) and 4ba55450ec351623 (fast-fail) intact, no regression. Outbox row consumed. lead-gua (shopsystem-templates-918) remains IN_PROGRESS on the wire; no work_done on that work_id yet — closure of lead-bxl does not auto-close lead-gua.
Re-verified 2026-05-27 during pending-inbox drain. No new state change — bead already carried full reconciliation notes from earlier session. Outbox row remains consumed. lead-gua (shopsystem-templates-918) remains IN_PROGRESS on the wire (unrelated to this bead's closure).

title: [bug] request_bugfix: shopsystem-bc-launcher PATH-availability scenario failing
description: Scenario 'bc-container is available on PATH after installing the shopsystem-bc-launcher package' (@scenario_hash:791a18a1781a343e) has a pre-existing fragility: the @when step calls subprocess.run(['bc-container', '--help']) with no explicit env= override. It relies on ambient PATH being populated with the venv bin dir by the pytest caller. In a CI environment or when pytest is invoked without the venv on PATH, bc-container is not found (verified: 'which bc-container' returns not-found on system PATH). The step should programmatically resolve bc-container via sys.executable or importlib.metadata to locate the entrypoint in the active Python environment, not depend on ambient PATH propagation.
close_reason: shopsystem-bc-launcher work_done: PATH scenario fixed via sys.executable resolution, all 41 scenarios pass

title: [bug] request_bugfix: shopsystem-templates CLAUDE.md import-graph read failure
description: Scenario b39dedcf829e31ad (inter_shop_messaging_encapsulation.feature) asserts that repos/shopsystem-templates/CLAUDE.md contains literal substrings 'shop-msg pending inbox' and 'shop-msg read inbox'. The file is a pure @-import graph; those strings live in .claude/canonical/bc-primer.md and are only visible after import resolution. The test reads the file directly without resolving imports, so it fails. Pre-state verified: PATH=/workspaces/shopsystem-product/.venv/bin pytest gives 1 failed / 145 passed; the single failure is AssertionError: file 'repos/shopsystem-templates/CLAUDE.md' is missing required literal substring 'shop-msg pending inbox'. Discriminator: capability exists (the CLAUDE.md import-graph is present and functional) but the test incorrectly reads the raw file instead of the resolved content. Fix: either (a) update the step implementation to resolve @-imports before checking, or (b) update the scenario to check the resolved import graph, or (c) move the shop-msg CLI instruction snippets into the CLAUDE.md body directly so it passes direct-read. No scenario pins the failure mode — this is a pre-existing bug.

title: request_maintenance: wire manifest ctx keys in exits-zero step (lead-cyo blocker)
description: Shared 'Then the command exits zero' step (conftest.py:356-368) is vacuous for manifest commands. When ctx['result'] is None (as it always is for manifest commands that store results in ctx['manifest_ok'], ctx['list_exit_code'], ctx['sync_exit_code']), the fallthrough at line 367 calls ctx.get('send_exit_code', ctx.get('respond_exit_code', 0)) which always returns 0. A manifest validate() returning ok=False on a valid manifest passes 'command exits zero' without detection. Fix: add elif branches before line 367 fallthrough checking ctx['manifest_ok'] is True (for validate), ctx['list_exit_code'] == 0 (for list), ctx['sync_exit_code'] == 0 (for sync). No new scenarios needed — step definition fix only.
close_reason: work_done; 41/41 pass; manifest_ok/list_exit_code/sync_exit_code branches added to command_exits_zero

title: request_maintenance: shopsystem-messaging — docker-compose postgres, DSN via service name
description: Ephemeral /tmp cluster silently breaks shop-msg on container restart or system reboot. A compose.yml with a named postgres service gives a stable, named network endpoint reachable by service name inside Docker networks — the correct Docker Compose networking pattern. The default DSN must use the compose service name as the hostname (e.g. postgresql://postgres:5432/shopsystem if the service is named postgres), not localhost or 127.0.0.1 (which only works with network_mode: host). A devcontainer postStartCommand running docker compose up -d ensures the service is available on session start. Supersedes lead-cyc.
close_reason: superseded — compose needs host-bind volumes and attachable network

title: request_maintenance: shopsystem-messaging — docker-compose postgres for shop-msg
description: Ephemeral /tmp cluster silently breaks shop-msg on container restart or system reboot. The default DSN points to a cluster at /tmp/pgdata (started with pg_ctl on port 5433, unix socket at /tmp/pgrun) — that path is not preserved across sessions. A well-known network location with a named Docker volume is needed for reliability across sessions and CI.
close_reason: superseded — DSN must use compose service name, not localhost

title: request_maintenance: shopsystem-messaging postgres backend
description: Current file/YAML storage walks directories per-BC; as the multi-BC topology grows this approach becomes fragile and loses durability. Replacing the storage backend with Postgres simplifies pending-query logic (single JOIN vs directory walking), adds transactional durability, and enables LISTEN/NOTIFY for push-style agent wakeup.
notes: 2026-06-09 CLOSE (COMPLETE): messaging backend already PostgreSQL — ADR-006 §1 (SHOPMSG_DSN, _ensure_schema DDL); scenarios 18 (db-unreachable/DSN) + 19 (LISTEN/NOTIFY). File/YAML directory-walk storage the bead described is gone; every named benefit realized + pinned.

title: Brief 003 canonical settings.json templates use wrong Claude Code hook schema (bare type+command vs matcher+hooks wrapper)
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-13 when user attempted 'claude' session start in shopsystem-test-harness after brief 003's bootstrap update.

Claude Code rejects the canonical settings.json with validation error:
  /workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-test-harness/.claude/settings.json
   └ hooks → SessionStart → 0 → hooks: Expected array, but received undefined

Cause: brief 003's canonical templates at repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/claude_settings/{bc,lead}.json wrote SessionStart entries in this shape:
  { 'type': 'command', 'command': '...' }
But Claude Code's actual schema requires the matcher+hooks wrapper:
  { 'matcher': '', 'hooks': [{ 'type': 'command', 'command': '...' }] }

The scenarios 57-68 pinned content substrings (SessionStart, stdbuf -oL inotifywait, watch-target tokens) but NOT the structural schema Claude Code validates against. The BC's Reviewer signed off because all 12 scenarios passed against the static-content checks; nothing exercised the file against real Claude Code.

Two facets:
(a) Canonical template content correction — fix the bc.json and lead.json package data to use matcher+hooks wrappers. shop-templates update propagates to existing shops.
(b) Scenario tightening — add a new scenario pinning the actual Claude Code hook schema (e.g., 'every SessionStart entry has a matcher key and a hooks array'). Without this, future regressions of the canonical templates won't be caught.

Resolution shape: dispatch request_bugfix to shopsystem-templates carrying both the canonical fix and the scenario tightening.

Local workaround applied today: hand-patched /workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-test-harness/.claude/settings.json with the correct schema (commit 6865ff0 in the BC). Other already-bootstrapped shops will inherit the bug until canonical templates are fixed AND 'shop-templates update' is run.

Sister concern: brief 003's empirical validation focused on stdbuf-vs-buffering correctness (the lead-79q reconciliation pattern) but not on whether Claude Code itself would accept the file. The 'pre-state empirical verification' invariant from brief 002/003 should grow a hook-schema-validates-against-claude-code check for any future SessionStart-touching brief.

Related: brief 003 (lead-0h1, closed), lead-i8u (bootstrap-existing-beads gap).
close_reason: Resolved via child lead-9ht (closed). Canonical templates at repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/claude_settings/{bc.json,lead.json} now carry the matcher+hooks wrapper Claude Code requires; scenario 70 (hash f2a7de49d80332c1) is in the BC's feature set as a structural pin guarding against regression to the bare {type,command} shape. shop-templates bootstrap verified to pour the corrected canonical. Residual note (no action): test-harness BC's local settings.json was hand-patched in commit 6865ff0; matches canonical shape, so a future shop-templates update will pour idempotently. Meta-concern for the record (no action): pre-state empirical verification for SessionStart-touching briefs would benefit from a Claude-Code-binary acceptance step — invariant evolution, follow-on work.

title: Clarify protocol gap — clarify-resolution requires explicit re-dispatch carrying original scenarios
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-13 during lead-xkh→lead-5mz→lead-1pi chain. Sister concern to lead-rv9 (work_done blocked).

Pattern observed: BC clarifies on a dispatch (lead-xkh). Lead resolves the clarify by dispatching a follow-up (lead-5mz) carrying ONLY the resolution scenario (scenario 69). BC processes lead-5mz cleanly but does NOT re-process lead-xkh — because lead-xkh has a clarify in outbox, it's no longer 'pending' from BC's view. The original scenarios in lead-xkh remain unimplemented until the lead sends ANOTHER dispatch (lead-1pi) carrying them.

This is a coordination gap:
- The §4.4 closure narrative says 'terminal work_done implicitly closes the chain.'
- But that only works when the follow-up CARRIES the prior scenarios (as lead-bwu→lead-e4g→lead-14e did via accumulation).
- If the follow-up carries ONLY the resolution (e.g., a small supersession), the original scenarios are stranded.

Two design questions:
1. Should the BC's protocol re-process an inbox message after a clarify is responded to? (e.g., 'clarify in outbox does not finalize pending state; lead's next dispatch with matching work_id signals re-process')
2. Or should the lead-side protocol explicitly carry-forward prior unimplemented scenarios in every follow-up? (current de facto pattern, but easy to miss)
3. Or should we have a new message type 'clarify_resolved' (or similar) that signals 'process the originally-dispatched scenarios now'?

The current workaround (lead-1pi re-dispatches the 12 brief 003 scenarios + supersession context in description) works but produces wire-form hash drift (because the feature-title wrapping changes between dispatches) and isn't machine-readable for tracking which dispatch carries which scenarios.

Sister concerns:
- lead-rv9 (work_done blocked protocol gap)
- lead-725 (singleton-inbox presumption)
- lead-4wy (work_id pattern asymmetry)
- lead-yug (@supersedes tag convention)

All four-plus are accumulating in a 'catalog protocol gaps' cluster that would benefit from a coordinated brief revision once the test-harness or other priority work creates pressure to consolidate.

Concrete impact on lead-1pi: wire-form hashes for scenarios 57-68 differ from lead-xkh dispatch (e.g., 57 was d4b8aac19b34ea8c then; 1621b59b0ea8b20b now). Implementer's BDD attestation will use the new hashes. The brief 003 intent semantically equivalent.
notes: 2026-06-09 CLOSE (resolved): ADR-009 (accepted 2026-05-27) decides path (b) — re-dispatch via existing catalog types — as the clarify-resolution vehicle. Native clarify_response primitive explicitly DEFERRED to lead-ox8 (P3 OPEN), which carries the only residual. Dedupe into lead-ox8.
close_reason: Closed

title: Product venv installs drift from BC source after BC work lands (no auto-reinstall)
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-13 during lead-79q dispatch.

The product venv at /workspaces/shopsystem-product/.venv/ has shop_msg installed as a regular package (not editable). When BCs update their source (lead-231.1 added 'pending' subcommand; lead-bwu/14e/ijr updated CLI behavior), the venv install does NOT update automatically. Lead-shop CLI invocations against shop-msg (and likely shop-templates similarly) see the OLDER installed surface, not the BC's live source.

Concrete impact today: shop-msg pending didn't exist in the venv install despite being present in BC source. Architect for lead-79q hit this and worked around by direct ls of inbox/outbox — a violation of brief 001 invariant 1 (encapsulation; CLI is sole messaging surface) forced by tooling staleness. Re-installed manually via 'pip install -e /workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-messaging' which fixed it. shop-templates likely has the same issue (Architect for lead-79q also noted product venv shop-templates lacks bootstrap/update).

Two facets:
(a) Install mode: BCs should be installed editable (-e) so source changes flow without reinstall. Currently appears to be non-editable.
(b) Reinstall trigger: even if editable, fresh BC clones / version changes need a way to refresh. SessionStart hook in brief 003 could include 'pip install -e repos/*/' to keep the venv synced.

Adjacent / related:
- lead-zmi (shop-msg PATH ergonomics) — same theme, different facet.
- Brief 003 SessionStart hook design — should include venv-resync as part of activating each shop.

The 'ls workaround' the Architect used is a discipline concern: the lead-architect template's encapsulation guidance should be tightened to say 'if shop-msg pending fails, FIX THE TOOLING (reinstall/PATH/version) not bypass'. Worth a separate template-tightening bead if recurring.
notes: RESOLVED 2026-06-19 (lead-architect) — recorded as findings/venv-install-hygiene-and-fix-tooling-discipline.md. Both original facets are obsolete-by-topology: (a) there is NO .venv on the lead host (CLIs on PATH at /usr/local/bin + ~/.local/bin); (b) the repos/<bc> editable-clone convention is DEAD (ADR-018), so there is no editable second copy to drift from pins — pins ARE the source of truth (pins realigned to current under lead-ome3). The evergreen discipline point ('if shop-msg fails, FIX THE TOOLING, do not bypass the CLI with direct ls/db access') is already canonical in the lead-architect template's hard-stop-on-CLI-failure guidance. No BC dispatch required; if tightened further it routes to shopsystem-templates. CLOSING.
close_reason: Closed

Target BC: shopsystem-templates (the canonical bc-implementer template is the templates BC's product; messaging BC cannot self-fix).

Vehicle hint: request_bugfix (template-language tightening of unpinned existing behavior).

Natural fold-in with lead-6t3 (round-4 outlook for brief 001 — per-step CLI naming for WRITE-mailbox actions). Both items tighten the bc-implementer canonical template via additional procedural discipline; both could be carried by one brief revision that consolidates 46-49 + write-mailbox cases + hash-verify step into a coordinated dispatch. Worth considering together when the next brief revision is authored.

Also adjacent: lead-wgv (scenarios hash canonicalization ambiguity — raw vs wire form). Tightening hash-verify discipline in the template is load-bearing; ensuring the BC's bc-implementer knows WHICH form to verify against requires lead-wgv to be resolved first (or the discipline step to be explicit about it).

Related: lead-6t3 (round-4 outlook), lead-wgv (canonicalization), lead-5cv (original incident).

title: scenarios hash canonicalization ambiguity — raw body vs wrapped+tagged form
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-12 during lead-e4g dispatch (request_bugfix closing lead-bwu §4.4 loop).

The Architect observed: 'scenarios hash < features/templates/46-*.gherkin' (against the raw scenario body) produces 06328160e83d2995, while the hash 'shop-msg send' persists on the wire (validated by 'shop-msg read') is e6bdf2f33bfae0d1. The CLI computes against a wrapped+sentinel-tagged form, not the raw body.

This is the Slice-16-shaped mismatch named in the architect's role contract anti-rationalization section. Same shape almost surfaced in lead-231.2 / lead-5cv (Implementer fabricated 4 @scenario_hash values; Reviewer caught via 'cat file | scenarios hash' which DID match the post-edit canonical) — but that case's reconciliation worked because both Implementer and Reviewer used raw-body hashing on already-tagged feature files, where the @scenario_hash tag had been hand-edited. The two-canonicalization-domains problem didn't bite because both parties stayed within one domain.

For lead-e4g and any future dispatch, the canonical question matters:
- The BC's ScenarioPayload.hash == compute_scenario_hash(gherkin) enforcement uses which canonicalization?
- The 'scenarios hash' CLI computes which form?
- Implementers verifying tags need to match against what the catalog enforces.

Three resolution shapes:
(a) One canonical form across both. 'scenarios hash' wraps its input the same way shop-msg does; tag values across feature files / inboxes / outboxes are byte-identical for identical scenario semantic content.
(b) Two named forms with clear scope. 'scenarios hash --raw' and 'scenarios hash --wire' (or similar); documented when each applies.
(c) Status quo + documentation. Names the two domains and where each is canonical; templates and procedural reminders cite the right form.

(a) is the cleanest invariant; (b) is most explicit; (c) is lowest-touch but most failure-prone.

Related: lead-4wy (work_id pattern asymmetry), lead-rv9 (work_done blocked protocol gap), lead-725 (singleton-inbox presumption) — same shape of latent protocol/canonicalization constraint surfaced by real dispatches.

Adjacent: lead-5cv's Implementer-defect-and-Reviewer-catch worked because both used raw-body hashing on already-tagged files (within one canonicalization domain). A future dispatch where one party uses the wire-form hash and the other uses raw-body would silently disagree.

Concrete impact for current work: lead-e4g's @scenario_hash tag in the BC inbox is the wire form. The BC's Implementer / Reviewer must validate against the wire form, not via raw-body 'scenarios hash'. Worth a procedural reminder in the dispatch description (already included by Architect for lead-e4g).
close_reason: Already done. Canonicalization ambiguity (raw body vs wrapped+tagged) resolved at contract level by scenario 117 (exactly one block-only canonical hash text) and at implementation level by the ADR-019 fix (verified live 2026-06-08).

title: work_done(blocked) protocol gap — lead-side response shape unspecified
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-12 by lead-231.2's blocked work_done — the first natural occurrence of the work_done(blocked) branch from from-prototype-1.md finding 3 and §8 (documented in bc_reviewer_prompt.md but unexercised).

The protocol defines that the Reviewer can emit work_done(blocked) when the implementation has a gap (not a scenario gap, which would be clarify). What it does NOT define is the lead-side response. The Reviewer's report names what's wrong; what message does the lead send back to get it fixed?

Options considered for lead-231.2's specific case:
- request_bugfix: tightens unpinned behavior via scenarios. Awkward — the gap is a hash-value defect on the BC's internal feature files, not a missing scenario pin.
- request_maintenance: flat change (value-only update of 4 specific hashes). Best fit for this concrete case; chosen for lead-231.2 follow-up.
- clarify: for asking questions, not for 'redo your work'. Doesn't fit.
- No message; BC re-submits unilaterally: protocol doesn't authorize BC to re-emit work_done without lead input.

What needs designing:
1. Spec-level: does the existing 7-type catalog cover work_done(blocked) responses adequately by composition (e.g., 'always request_maintenance for value-only fixes the Reviewer specified'), or is a new mechanism needed?
2. Template-level: bc_reviewer prompt should constrain the work_done(blocked) report shape so the lead can route deterministically (Reviewer cites specific defect + canonical fix → lead dispatches request_maintenance carrying the fix).
3. Discriminator-level: 'Flat / Reviewer-cited correction' becomes an explicit pathway under request_maintenance, not just refactor/doc/value-update.

Also worth noting the substantive Implementer defect that triggered this: Implementer fabricated 4 @scenario_hash values rather than running scenarios.hash.compute_scenario_hash on the modified files. Reviewer caught it. §4.4 loop validation on a real Implementer error — the kind synthetic prototypes never produced.

Related: lead-231 (parent brief), lead-231.2 (the blocked dispatch), findings/from-prototype-1.md finding 3 / §8 (the prior 'documented but unexercised' note).
notes: RESOLVED 2026-06-19 (lead-architect): lead-SIDE response shape recorded in findings/scenario-supersession-and-dispatch-discipline.md §1. DECISION: work_done(blocked) naming a concrete value-only defect + canonical fix -> canonical response is request_maintenance carrying the fix ('Flat / Reviewer-cited correction' is an explicit request_maintenance pathway). Existing 7-type catalog covers it by composition; no new message type. CLOSED.
close_reason: Closed

title: Catalog work_id pattern asymmetry — Clarify rejects what AssignScenarios/RequestBugfix accept
description:
Surfaced by Architect during brief-001 items A+C dispatch on 2026-05-12.

During dispatch of lead-231.1 (AssignScenarios) and lead-231.2 (RequestBugfix) to shopsystem-messaging, the Architect used dotted child bead IDs as work_ids. Both schemas accepted them — neither AssignScenarios.work_id nor RequestBugfix.work_id carries a pattern constraint (just str). But Clarify.work_id has pattern '^[a-zA-Z0-9-]+$' which rejects dots. Latent failure: if the BC sends Clarify on lead-231.1, 'shop-msg respond clarify --work-id lead-231.1' fails schema validation.

What to do: align work_id patterns across catalog schemas via a single shared Pydantic type alias; admit dotted child-bead IDs uniformly. Adjacent (not scoped here): shop-msg has no recall command for in-flight dispatches. Related: lead-231; blocks lead-ox8.

DISPATCHED 2026-06-19 (lead-architect): request_bugfix -> shopsystem-messaging, work_id lead-4wy. Normalize work_id pattern uniformly across all vehicles, admitting dotted IDs. Unpinned schema-validation behavior -> request_bugfix. Left in_progress pending work_done.
close_reason: work_done reconciled 2026-06-19: work_id pattern normalized uniformly across all 8 catalog schemas (shared WorkId/OptionalWorkId Pydantic alias, ^[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9_.-]$); dotted child-bead ids round-trip through every vehicle incl clarify, path-sep/empty/whitespace still rejected (lead-008 hardening preserved). TDD red e955862->green 52d1935, 241 pass, merged 94bc094.

description: Release-propagation debt surfaced by lead-jo9p reconciliation (2026-07-04). The jo9p fix (RegisterNarrowing.hashes set[str]->list[str] + order-preserving CLI passthrough) is GREEN in the shopsystem-messaging BC repo but NOT yet released as a package version nor installed anywhere: the lead-installed shop-msg is still v0.4.4 (the buggy version), and bc-launcher's baked image carries v0.4.4 (via lead-6b53 / bc-launcher v0.3.41, which shipped v0.4.4 = the crash version, not the fix). Until a messaging release carrying the jo9p fix (v0.4.5) is (a) cut, (b) installed on the lead, and (c) re-baked into a bc-launcher image, 'shop-msg send request_scenario_register --hash' remains crash-prone from EVERY sender on v0.4.4 (incl. the lead itself — a --hash confirmation send from the lead today would hit the very crash just fixed). Steps: cut messaging v0.4.5; reinstall on lead (ADR-039 cadence); re-bake bc-launcher image (ADR-021 auto-rebuild on utility release); verify lead shop-msg --version + baked image provenance. Blocks the explicit-hash-set import path in lead-7njh (whose DEPENDS-ON lead-r46p is STALE — r46p delivered v0.4.4 which cannot run the --hash import; re-point 7njh here). Cross-links: lead-jo9p (closed, the fix), lead-r46p (closed, shipped v0.4.4), lead-6b53, lead-7njh, ADR-039, ADR-021.

title: shop-msg has no first-class BC-side blocked-retriable / re-queue primitive (mechanism gap; F2 used the nudge side-channel workaround)
description: Observed by bc-launcher during lead-i0wi F2 (mechanism_observation): once ANY outbox response is emitted for a work_id, the dispatch is permanently de-pended; there is no first-class 'blocked-retriable' / re-queue primitive in shop-msg. F2's fix worked around it via the non-consuming shop-msg-nudge side-channel (dispatch stays pending), but a proper primitive belongs in the messaging BC (shopsystem-messaging): a way for a BC to report blocked WITHOUT consuming the dispatch, so a lead re-drive doesn't need re-dispatch. Route to shopsystem-messaging when prioritized. Low-pri follow-up; the nudge workaround is functional. Parent lead-fzxt (surfaced during dagger dogfood).

title: PO: demote in-block @scenario_hash cross-references to comments (228 line 46 stale 213 ref; coupling root)
description: Root-cause follow-up from lead-320l (213 supersession dd82193e56e52d95 -> 4c646ae20a1540e3). When a scenario embeds a sibling's @scenario_hash:<hash> as a TEXTUAL cross-reference inside its OWN Scenario block (description prose or steps), that cross-ref becomes part of the embedding scenario's block-only hash. Consequence: superseding the referenced scenario silently STALES the in-block cross-ref AND any edit to fix it re-supersedes the (otherwise unchanged) embedding scenario — pure churn. Concrete instance: features/templates/228-update-refreshes-stale-ops-coordinates-artifact-against-current-canonical-render.gherkin line 46 (in-block Scenario description) cites the now-superseded OLD 213 hash @scenario_hash:dd82193e56e52d95. Lines 2 & 30 already cite it only in '#' comments (free). Per lead-320l decision, 228's own pin (8e5955d5fb5bb9c8) was deliberately NOT re-superseded just to refresh a description cross-ref — that would be churn for no functional gain. PO fix (comment-only, ZERO hash impact): rewrite cross-references that name a sibling scenario by hash so the @scenario_hash token lives in '#' comment lines, NOT inside any Scenario block; describe sibling scenarios in-block by NUMBER/title only. Apply as a convention going forward and clean up 228 line 46 when 228 is next legitimately re-authored (do not re-supersede 228 solely for this). discovered-from lead-320l.

title: footing identity dual-source-of-truth consolidation: runtime PRODUCT=${REPO_NAME%-lead} (L72) vs render-time AGENT_VAULT_VAULT={{OPS_SLUG}} (L88) -> canonical manifest product: per ADR-038 (lead-l7uz)
description: LATENT, NON-BLOCKING (product authority asked about identity derivation in multiple places). footing has a DUAL source of truth for product identity: (1) PRODUCT=${REPO_NAME%-lead} derived at RUNTIME from the fork dir basename (footing L72); (2) AGENT_VAULT_VAULT/AGENT_VAULT_CONTAINER/AGENT_VAULT_ADDR/etc. derived at RENDER time from {{OPS_SLUG}} (L86-88,126,174). They AGREE BY CONSTRUCTION (the fork is named <slug>-lead and OPS_SLUG=slug), so this is not a live bug — but ADR-038 makes the manifest 'product:' field the CANONICAL single source of product identity. Consolidation: footing should derive ALL product coordinates from one canonical source (the manifest product: field, which footing already writes per ADR-038) rather than two parallel derivations that happen to coincide. DECIDE the canonical-source design (manifest product: as the single derivation root; runtime PRODUCT and the {{OPS_SLUG}} render-time literals reconcile to it) -> likely request_maintenance (refactor to single-source, no new observable) OR request_bugfix if a tightening scenario pins the single-source invariant. Parent lead-l7uz. NOT part of the lead-h2rq vault-create blocker. Cross-ref ADR-038.
close_reason: Subsumed by ADR-043 Phase 1 (lead-0t5m, scenario p1ssot1 38c7cc83fc1bc0f0): the PRODUCT-vs-OPS_SLUG dual-source consolidation lead-yh0s identified is exactly what D1/p1ssot1 pins (manifest product: as single identity root; runtime slug reconciles to it). lead-yh0s was OPEN/never-dispatched with NO landed scenario, so this is a work-item closure, NOT a @scenario_hash retirement — nothing to retire.

title: Discipline: brokered-CLI provision-template verification needs a LIVE-broker probe, not just --help syntax — value-format constraints (key casing/value shape) are not --help-discoverable
description: From lead-l95x mechanism_observation; explains the dummyco-spike iter-2->4 grind. The BC syntax-verified every provision verb under lead-beym against 'agent-vault --help' (verb existence + flag names) and that gate passed green — yet kebab credential keys still walled the LIVE broker (agent-vault 0.32.0 enforces SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE at runtime but does NOT document it in 'vault credential set --help'). Lesson: --help/syntax verification of a brokered CLI is necessary but NOT sufficient for provision contracts; VALUE-FORMAT constraints (key casing, value shape, enum values) require a live-broker (or recorded-fixture) probe to discover. shop-templates ops templates encode such constraints by copy, silently inheriting any format rule --help omits. This validates the dummyco spike's purpose (e2e run is the ONLY way these surface). PROPOSED: future provision/credential template work (and the bc verification gate) should include a live-broker or recorded-fixture probe step for value-format constraints. Could also become a recorded-fixture test asset for agent-vault provision. Relates lead-beym, lead-l95x, lead-jdfb, lead-g19j (ADR-026 D2 sharpening).
notes: RECORDED 2026-06-19 (lead-architect) as findings/provision-template-value-format-probe-discipline.md. Captured the lesson: --help/syntax verification of a brokered CLI is necessary but NOT sufficient for provision contracts; value-format constraints (key casing e.g. SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE on agent-vault 0.32.0, value shape, enum values) require a live-broker or recorded-fixture probe to discover. This is role-template / BC-verification discipline, NOT lead-instance config — if formalized into the canonical bc-implementer gate or shop-templates ops-template authoring guidance it routes as request_maintenance to shopsystem-templates. Finding recorded; flag templates dispatch when the team decides to bake the probe into the canonical gate. CLOSING as recorded.
close_reason: Closed

title: bc-emit work-done wrapper: work_id attribution via substring grep (git log <ref> --grep=<work_id> --fixed-strings) can prefix-false-positive across work_ids
description: Surfaced by shopsystem-templates in lead-yap9 work_done (non-blocking note; BC also filed it internally). The wrapper's tag/commit reachability checks attribute a work_id to a commit lineage via substring grep (git log <ref> --grep=<work_id> --fixed-strings). A work_id that is a STRICT PREFIX of another commit's work_id could false-positive. CONSISTENCY: this is IDENTICAL to check_commit_reachable and the work-done-gate's existing canonical attribution form — so it's not a regression introduced by the wrapper, and not exploitable in the currently-pinned cases (bd work_ids like lead-xxxx/tmpl-xxxx with random suffixes rarely prefix each other). Latent correctness edge worth a future word-boundary / exact-token attribution fix across the shared attribution helper (wrapper + work-done-gate together, so they stay consistent). Relates lead-m56e (wrapper), lead-yap9.
notes: 2026-06-19 (lead-architect): DISPATCHED request_bugfix -> shopsystem-templates (work_id lead-8vwf). Discriminator: behavior (work_id->commit attribution gate) EXISTS and is pinned by features/templates/108 (0c4b28c02212d454) + 112 (bb41b17b6ebd0c81), BUT the substring-vs-word-boundary edge is UNPINNED -> request_bugfix (not assign_scenarios: capability exists; not maintenance: behavioral tightening). Pre-state cited from lead-held features/ surface (ADR-018 D1; no repos/ BC source read). Deposit verified via shop-msg read inbox. title: correct lead's own .claude/shop/name.md from display form to canonical slug after lead-yi0k advisory lands
description:
Follow-up to lead-yi0k. This lead shop's /workspaces/shopsystem-product/.claude/shop/name.md currently contains the display form "shopsystem product" (with a space); the registry slug for this shop is "shopsystem-product" (with a hyphen). Per the doctrine pinned by features/templates/130/131/132 (dispatched as lead-yi0k), name.md must carry the canonical slug; the display form belongs in primer.md.

Wait for lead-yi0k to close (shopsystem-templates work_done arrives) so the 132 advisory ships first — that advisory is the legacy-shop migration story. Then:

  1. Edit /workspaces/shopsystem-product/.claude/shop/name.md to contain exactly "shopsystem-product" + single trailing newline.
  2. Confirm no downstream agent breaks: `shop-msg watch --lead shopsystem-product`, `shop-msg pending outbox --lead shopsystem-product`, `bd prime`, session-start hooks.
  3. Verify .claude/shop/primer.md still carries (or now carries) any display-form prose the lead wants surfaced to readers — no canonical-managed file should reference the display form.

Not scenario territory — single-edit content correction in a shop-owned file. Router or architect may handle directly.

Depends on: lead-yi0k closing (shopsystem-templates lands 130/131/132 and shipss the shop-templates update with the advisory).
notes: RESOLVED 2026-06-19 (lead-architect). Gate cleared: 'shop-templates update' (v0.14.0) IS now installed on the lead host and the scenario-132 name-slug advisory FIRES against the 'shopsystem product' content (verified by running it). Verified {{SHOP_NAME}} mechanism: it is a LITERAL placeholder in .claude/canonical/lead-primer.md that nothing substitutes from name.md (CLAUDE.md @-includes are plain file concatenation). The human-facing display name is carried by .claude/shop/primer.md ('# shopsystem product'), which is UNTOUCHED. Therefore editing name.md to the slug carries ZERO display-name harm. Edited .claude/shop/name.md -> 'shopsystem-product\n'. Verified: re-running 'shop-templates update' no longer emits the name.md advisory; 'shop-msg pending outbox --lead shopsystem-product' resolves (exit 0); primer.md still carries the display form. INSTANCE-LOCAL edit. CLOSING.

title: shop-msg send <verb> lacks --description / --rationale flag for lead-side dispatch context
description:
## Empirical finding

Today's PDR-010 dispatch (5 assign_scenarios to shopsystem-messaging: lead-tuu5, lead-eow5, lead-98kk, lead-1w7r, lead-sn1e) needed to carry: (a) ADR citations (ADR-011/012/013/014/015/016/017) for each dispatched file; (b) sequencing notes (lead-tuu5 is foundation; lead-eow5/98kk/1w7r/sn1e depend on it); (c) cross-file context (e.g., 'bd_facade module introduced in 119 is reused by 121/122/123'). The current shop-msg send assign_scenarios surface has no --description or --rationale flag; the descriptive context could only land in the lead-side carrier bead's description + notes. The BC receives the scenarios and the work_id but no lead-side prose.

This was reconciled today by reading ADR-009/010 (scenarios ARE the contract; lead description is lead-side context for tracking, not BC-required input) and proceeding with the existing surface. That reading holds for *contract* purposes — scenarios are sufficient for BC implementation. But it does NOT cover the operational use cases where lead-authored prose IS what the BC needs:

- Dispatch sequencing hints ('land lead-tuu5 first; lead-eow5 depends-on it but ships with parallel inbox row')
- Cross-ADR context ('this file pins ADR-013 which assumes the bd_facade from ADR-016 is in place')
- Carrier-bead summaries that the BC's Implementer would otherwise have to assemble by reading 5 separate carrier beads

## Proposed mechanism

Add to shop-msg send {assign_scenarios, request_bugfix, request_maintenance}:

- --description <text>  (inline string)
- --description-file <path>  (path to a markdown file)

The dispatched message gains a 'description' field that flows through the postgres deposit and is surfaced via shop-msg read inbox.

## Rationale

Some BC-relevant context is lead-side authored prose that doesn't fit in scenarios — dispatch sequencing hints, cross-ADR context, carrier-bead summaries. Today this context is lead-side-only (carrier bead description + notes), unreachable by the BC Implementer reading the inbox row. The Implementer's loop currently has to either (a) work without that context, (b) request it via clarify (round trip), or (c) the lead architect has to remember to drop a parallel mechanism_observation. None of these are clean.

## Cross-links

Dispatches that surfaced this gap: [[lead-tuu5]] [[lead-eow5]] [[lead-98kk]] [[lead-1w7r]] [[lead-sn1e]].

title: mechanism: parallel in-flight dispatches to same BC create commit-sequencing deadlock
description:
## Pattern observation

Two distinct dispatches to shopsystem-messaging in close temporal proximity have now produced the IDENTICAL block reason: BC completes work and verifies ACs empirically, but cannot commit because the working tree shares files with another in-flight dispatch whose own work has not yet been signed off.

### Instances

1. **lead-2ca** (watch-reconnect tightening, BC reviewer signed off but explicitly DEFERRED commit) — blocked on lead-767 (--force respond work) entanglement: shared storage.py, conftest.py, watch_listen_drop_reconnect.feature.
2. **lead-2122** (fxhyg meta-test hygiene fix) — same blocker: shared tests/conftest.py, plus dependence on uncommitted lead-0bw ephemeral-DB infra and uncommitted lead-6nt _snapshot_production_name / _PER_TEST_MUTATED_NAMES infra. Storage.py allow_multi_type and cli.py _walk_up_resolve_shop are lead-767/lead-b3z.

### Mechanism

When the lead dispatches multiple bugfix/maintenance items to the same BC before earlier dispatches are reviewer-signed-off and committed, the BC's working tree accumulates interleaved changes across files like tests/conftest.py, src/shop_msg/storage.py, src/shop_msg/cli.py. Each new dispatch's fix lands as additional hunks; the BC cannot commit any single item because git-add-p hunk slicing leaves the resulting HEAD referencing functions/imports/features from the still-uncommitted siblings, violating 'leave main green'.

### Why the carrier pattern (lead-pfmb) is not a fix, only mitigation

lead-pfmb sequences the eventual joint push once lead-767 signs off — but it does not prevent the next dispatch (lead-Nxx) from re-entering the same trap. The carrier mounts higher each cycle.

### Candidate remediations (architect to evaluate; not in scope for this bead)

- (a) Lead-side discipline: serialize bugfix dispatches per BC; do not dispatch lead-N to BC X until lead-(N-1) is signed off AND committed.
- (b) BC-side discipline: per-dispatch branch (BC creates a branch per work_id, commits there, lead carrier merges branches in dependency order).
- (c) Architecture: split tests/conftest.py into per-concern modules so per-work hunks land in disjoint files.
- (d) Accept the carrier pattern; codify it as the canonical resolution path with a carrier-creation reflex on every blocked work_done.

### Cross-links

- [[lead-pfmb]] — carrier for lead-2ca + lead-2122 (scope updated 2026-05-29).
- [[lead-2122]] — second instance, fxhyg fix.
- [[lead-2ca]] — first instance, watch-reconnect tightening.
- [[lead-767]] — the umbrella blocking both.

### Not dispatched

This is an observation about the dispatch pattern itself; not a BC-side fix. Architect to decide whether to file an ADR, a process-discipline note, or a redecomposition PDR.
notes: RESOLVED 2026-06-19 (lead-architect): process-discipline recorded in findings/scenario-supersession-and-dispatch-discipline.md §4. Cure VALIDATED: (prevention) serialize bugfix/maintenance dispatches per BC — no lead-N to BC X until lead-(N-1) reviewer-signed-off AND committed; (recovery) carrier pattern (lead-pfmb) sequences joint recommit once blocker lands. Router end-of-turn rule already implies per-BC serialization; this records why. Open-but-non-blocking for a future PDR: optional 'shop-msg dispatch-after <work_id>' CLI affordance. CLOSED as discipline recorded.
close_reason: Closed

title: shopsystem-docs: walkthrough parser _FENCE_RE skips indented fences inside numbered list items
description:
Mechanism observation flagged by shopsystem-docs in lead-eqx work_done (2026-05-27).

OBSERVATION:
src/shopsystem_docs/walkthrough.py:_FENCE_RE uses ^``` (line-start anchor), so fenced code blocks indented inside numbered list items are NOT enumerated by the parser. The four docker fences in Move 1 of the walkthrough are skipped.

BC ASSESSMENT (within scenario tolerance):
- Scenario 4d4a451e51d9ce3b (agent-executable walkthrough structure) does not currently pin parser completeness for indented fences.
- Move 1 commands are also valid as numbered-list-item content, so under the scenario as written this is not a defect.
- BC flagged for FUTURE TIGHTENING if the docs BC ever needs to assert structural properties on indented fences (e.g., that every docker command in Move 1 must parse as an executable fence).

TRIAGE:
- Not blocking lead-eqx work_done acceptance.
- Tracking-only: if/when a future doc-structure tightening requires indented-fence enumeration, this bead is the seed for the request_bugfix or assign_scenarios that would re-pin the structure.
- No dispatch needed today.

REFERENCES:

title: mechanism_observation: cross-tracker work_id pattern (shopsystem-templates-918 from shopsystem-bc-launcher)
description:
Observed during shopsystem-templates-918 clarify drain (2026-05-22, reconfirmed 2026-05-27): the work_id 'shopsystem-templates-918' is prefixed with one BC's tracker name (shopsystem-templates) while the actual sender of the assign_scenarios was a different BC (shopsystem-bc-launcher). 'bd search 918' returns no lead-side bead; the lead-gua bead's NOTES treat 918 as a BC-side tracker ID.

This is a cross-tracker work_id pattern: the on-wire work_id is the issuing BC's bead ID, not the lead's bead ID. §6 of the spec names the lead's bead ID as the canonical work_id, so this is an asymmetry worth pinning.

Related: lead-4wy (Catalog work_id pattern asymmetry — Clarify rejects what AssignScenarios/RequestBugfix accept). That bead documents catalog-schema validation asymmetry; this bead documents the *source-of-truth* asymmetry — which tracker's beads supply the work_id when the dispatch crosses BCs without going through the lead.

In this specific case the dispatch was actually lead-side (lead-gua) but the on-wire work_id 'shopsystem-templates-918' was set to the BC-side tracker reference. Whether that was an intentional cross-reference or a misuse is unclear from the surviving evidence.

A formal mechanism_observation cannot be emitted from the lead (the respond subcommands are BC-side). This bead is the lead-side equivalent record. If a future BC re-encounters the same pattern, it should emit a mechanism_observation through 'shop-msg respond mechanism_observation' citing this bead.

No immediate action required; observation only.

title: [mechanism] bc-launcher crashes with AttributeError on non-string product field in bc-manifest.yaml
description: Mechanism observation surfaced during lead-e6j work_done (shopsystem-bc-launcher). The slugify path on the product field assumes a string; when the manifest contains a non-string YAML scalar (e.g. 'product: 123') the controller crashes with AttributeError rather than emitting a graceful 'non-string product' error message. BC dismissed it as not pinnable (not a realistic user mistake, no committed lead behavior, no existing scenario exercises it). Filed at P3 so the option is queued: if/when we tighten manifest validation broadly (schema, type-checking on string-shaped fields), add a scenario for non-string product alongside it. No dispatch yet; lead has not committed to a behavior. Source: lead-e6j work_done summary probe (6).

title: request_maintenance: shopsystem-messaging — allow multiple clarify rows per work_id in outbox
description: The postgres UNIQUE constraint on (bc, work_id, direction, message_type) prevents a BC from filing a second clarify response on the same work_id. This is an implementation artifact — §5 of the spec places no cardinality constraint on clarify messages. Multi-turn clarify exchanges (BC asks, lead answers, BC asks again) are a legitimate protocol flow but are currently blocked, forcing workarounds (mechanism_observation, fresh work_id). Fix: relax or drop the uniqueness on message_type within the outbox direction, or scope the constraint to only message types where one-per-work_id is actually required (work_done).
notes: 2026-06-09 DEMOTE P2->P3: ADR-009 fresh-work_id workaround is the sanctioned path (layer b), removing urgency; lead-8z1 only gates the DEFERRED lead-ox8 clarify_response primitive (ADR-009 path a). Couples to lead-ox8.

title: shop-templates bootstrap fails on existing .beads/ — needs idempotent or skip-bd-init mode
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-13 setting up shopsystem-test-harness as a canonical shop.

shop-templates bootstrap shells out to 'bd init --skip-agents'. When the target dir already has an initialized .beads/, bd init errors with 'Found existing Dolt database... Aborting.' Bootstrap exits 1 with no scaffold written.

This blocks bootstrap on existing shops with a beads workspace — exactly the 'wrap existing repo' case brief 002 was scoped to handle.

Workaround today: 'shop-templates update' (doesn't shell to bd init) creates .claude/agents/ + canonical settings.json; CLAUDE.md re-rendered manually via python invocation of _render_claude_md() since update is init-only on CLAUDE.md per brief 002 item D.

Resolution shapes:
(a) Bootstrap detects existing .beads/ and skips bd init (idempotent). Matches brief 002's 'wrap existing repo' invariant.
(b) Bootstrap gains --skip-bd-init flag for explicit user control.
(c) Update gains optional CLAUDE.md re-render flag for the case of already-bd-init'd shops migrating to canonical.

(a) + (c) together cover the wrap-existing case cleanly. Worth a brief revision in the bootstrap area.
notes: 2026-06-19 DISPATCHED by lead-architect: request_bugfix -> shopsystem-templates, work_id=lead-i8u. Carried scenario @scenario_hash:5786b555ee0732bf (features/templates/190). Additive (option a, idempotent skip-bd-init); enumerated conflicting set EMPTY (32/50 precondition-disjoint, not retired). Hash reproduced via 'scenarios hash' block-only before send (MATCH). Deposit verified via shop-msg read inbox. Fields restored post-send.
close_reason: work_done reconciled 2026-06-19: bootstrap idempotent on pre-existing .beads/ (scenario 5786b555ee0732bf bc+lead rows; guard skips bd-init when .beads/ present, scaffold still written byte-for-byte). Per-row two-way tamper non-vacuous (real bd-shim argv + snapshot diff); no-.beads/ happy path disjoint+green. All 5 gates pass, merged 17bdf9a origin/main, zero new failures (+2 passing rows).

title: Framework convention: @supersedes:<hash> tag for scenario replacement in inter-shop messaging
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-13 during lead-5mz dispatch (brief 003 §4.4 closure for lead-xkh).

When a new scenario supersedes a previously-dispatched one, the relationship is currently expressed only in prose (Gherkin Feature description block, dispatch description). The Architect dispatching lead-5mz noted that adding an @supersedes:<hash> tag at dispatch time would make the relationship machine-readable in the BC's scenario register, rather than buried in prose.

Concrete shape: catalog schema's @bc:<name> and @scenario_hash:<hash> are existing tag conventions injected by shop-msg send. @supersedes:<hash> would be a parallel injection at dispatch time (could be a --supersedes flag on shop-msg send, or auto-detected from scenario body, or set via PO's authoring tag).

Benefits:
- Machine-readable retire-from-feature-file instruction for the BC's Implementer.
- Audit trail in the BC's outbox when work_done attests against new scenarios.
- Potentially enables a future 'scenario register' surface (lead-otu) to track supersede chains.

Tradeoffs:
- Introduces a new tag convention; requires shop-msg schema or send-side tooling change.
- The prose mechanism works today; this is an ergonomic / discipline improvement.

Worth designing alongside the round-4 consolidation brief (lead-6t3) — that brief will already touch scenario discipline broadly. Could fold.

Related: lead-6t3 (round-4 outlook consolidation), lead-otu (scenario register surface), lead-wgv (scenarios hash canonicalization).
notes: RESOLVED 2026-06-19 (lead-architect): convention recorded in findings/scenario-supersession-and-dispatch-discipline.md §3. @supersedes:<hash> = parallel dispatch-time injection alongside @bc/@scenario_hash. DECISION: convention agreed; BUILD DEFERRED (P3) — prose mechanism works today, this is ergonomic; the build is a shop-msg schema/send-side change (shopsystem-messaging) to fold into a future scenario-discipline consolidation, not dispatched standalone. CLOSED as convention-recorded/deferred.
close_reason: Closed
